Credit limit review: if YTD DSO is trending at or below 150d and OVD is clean, prepare a peak-season credit increase proposal to support Q3 volume ramp

finance.headline: Tamkeen is technically Q1-Safe with OVD below its 35.4% baseline, but T12M DSO of 196d already breaches the 180d DPO ceiling and the May 1 OVD step-up signals fresh invoice aging that must be resolved before peak season deepens exposure.
finance.analysis:
AR health is conditionally acceptable. Current OVD of 14.3% sits well below Tamkeen's own YTD average baseline of 35.4%, and the complete absence of 60d+ receivables confirms no aged, trapped debt today. However, the May 1 step-up from 8.3% to 14.3%—with the SAR 432K 30-60d tranche now the dominant overdue slice, larger than the 0-30d bucket—indicates at least one payment cohort crossed its due date without settlement and is drifting older. If this tranche migrates into 60d+ by mid-May without payment, Q1-Safe status becomes vulnerable and the quadrant shifts.

Collection risk is moderate but structurally concerning. YTD DSO of 157d runs 67 days beyond the 90d credit term, meaning Tamkeen is operating LG's facility as a de-facto 5-month credit line rather than the contracted 3-month one. T12M DSO at 196d exceeds the 180d DPO ceiling, confirming this is a systemic payment pattern, not an episodic delay. Sell-In has eroded from SAR 21.8M in 2024 to SAR 8.0M in 2025 and SAR 4.2M through early May 2026—an ~80% volume compression over two years that raises questions about business health and forward repayment capacity. The 1.2x 2026 buy pace vs T12M average is a mild constructive signal, suggesting renewed commercial engagement as peak season opens, but volumes remain a fraction of historical levels.

Credit policy rationale supports maintaining current limits. Expanding credit into an account with T12M DSO structurally above DPO and multi-year volume contraction carries asymmetric risk—incremental sales booked at the cost of receivables that historically take 6+ months to clear. Tightening is premature given OVD is below baseline, no 60d+ exposure exists today, and the account is actively buying into peak season. The correct posture is hold-and-monitor: maintain existing limits, escalate collection on the SAR 432K 30-60d tranche immediately before it ages further, and reassess credit policy after observing payment behavior through the first two weeks of May.
finance.credit_rationale: OVD below baseline and zero 60d+ exposure rule out tightening, but T12M DSO exceeding DPO with a declining revenue trajectory disqualifies any credit expansion until structural payment behavior improves.
action: Initiate direct collection call on SAR 432K 30-60d overdue tranche this week—obtain a firm payment commitment date before it crosses into 60d+ and triggers quadrant migration.
Request written payment schedule from Tamkeen covering remaining SAR 313K 0-30d overdue; align expected cash dates to LG's May AR reporting cycle to prevent compounding aging in mid-May.

Run a commercial health check on Tamkeen's 2026 order pipeline and sell-through performance—determine whether 1.2x buy pace reflects genuine peak-season demand recovery or front-loading that masks deteriorating channel health.
finance.risk_reason: T12M DSO 196d structurally exceeds DPO 180d; the SAR 432K 30-60d bucket will enter 60d+ within two weeks absent payment, threatening Q1-Safe quadrant status at the onset of peak season.

sales.headline: 2026 buy acceleration into peak season is a positive signal, but the unexplained 63% sell-in collapse from 2024→2025 demands root cause clarity before committing resources.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is structurally ambiguous. The 2024→2025 sell-in decline of 63% (SAR 21.8M → SAR 8.0M) is too steep to attribute to market softness alone — it signals either a competitor switch (Samsung/Gree gaining shelf), an inventory overhang from an aggressive 2024 overbuy, or deliberate channel portfolio pruning by the account. The 2026 trajectory offers mild encouragement: 1.2x T12M buy pace through early May, and peak season (May–September) is just opening. However, the annualized 2026 run-rate from current YTD (SAR 4.16M over ~4 months ≈ SAR 12.5M full-year) is still just 57% of the 2024 peak, meaning the account has not structurally recovered. Critically, no sell-out data is available to confirm whether product is actually moving through to consumers — without that, the buy acceleration could reflect pre-season stocking optimism that reverses by August if channel inventory builds again.

The AR profile is mixed but not alarming. DSO structure is chronically stretched (YTD 157d, T12M 196d vs. 90d credit term) — this is not situational; it is an embedded payment pattern. That said, current OVD at 14.3% is well below the 35.4% YTD average, and there is zero 60d+ aging, indicating recent payment discipline has improved. The May 1 step-up in OVD from 8.3% to 14.3% is normal invoice aging, not deterioration. The combination of accelerating buys and below-average OVD suggests the account may be actively managing the relationship ahead of peak season — a signal of intent, not a guarantee of follow-through.

For investment, the 2024→2025 collapse is the blocking issue. If it was driven by inventory overhang from an oversized 2024 buy (common in Saudi IR), and stock has since normalized, the 2026 re-engagement could be genuine — sell-out support during June–August would then carry strong ROI given the timing. If it reflects a competitor switch or structural financial stress, any credit extension or co-marketing investment would be premature and high-risk. The correct posture is hold with targeted relationship recovery: get the sell-out story, understand the collapse, then reassess in 4–6 weeks with data.
sales.investment_rationale: 2026 buy acceleration and improving OVD are encouraging entry signals, but the unresolved 63% sell-in collapse and chronic 196d T12M DSO prevent confident resource commitment until sell-out data and root cause are confirmed.
action: Immediately investigate the 2024→2025 63% sell-in collapse — interview account to determine if inventory overhang, competitor shift (Samsung/Gree), or financial restructuring is the driver
Request point-of-sale sell-out data for Jan–Apr 2026 before any credit increase or peak-season co-marketing commitment
Hold current 90d credit terms; place the 30-60d aging bucket (SAR 432,079) on active watch through June — no expansion until DSO improvement is structurally confirmed over 2+ months
sales.channel_reason: Buy pace accelerating 1.2x into peak season but 63% two-year sell-in collapse and chronic 196d DSO create unresolved structural uncertainty without sell-out confirmation

synthesis.headline: SAR 432K 30-60d 채권이 2주 내 60d+로 이동하면 Q1-Safe 상태가 붕괴되고, 피크 시즌 수주와 회수 리스크가 동시에 확대된다 — 이번 주 컬렉션 전화 한 통이 이 계정의 방향을 결정한다.

verdict.one_line: Risk YELLOW — HOLD. 63% Sell-In 붕괴의 원인 미확인 + T12M DSO 196d 구조적 초과 상태에서 피크 시즌 투자 확대는 선(先)회수·원인규명 이후로 유보.

summary_3lines: Tamkeen은 기술적으로 Q1-Safe이나 T12M DSO 196d가 DPO 한도 180d를 이미 초과했고, SAR 432K 30-60d 트랜치가 5월 중순 60d+ 진입을 앞두고 있다.
2024→2025 Sell-In 63% 급감(SAR 21.8M → 8.0M)의 원인이 재고 과잉인지, 경쟁사(Samsung·Gree) 전환인지, 재무 구조 악화인지 아직 미규명이며, 셀아웃 데이터가 없어 2026 1.2x 구매 가속이 진정한 수요 회복인지 선취재고인지 판단 불가하다.
이번 주 SAR 432K 회수를 실패하면 60d+ 발생 → Q1→Q3 사분면 이동 → 피크 시즌(5~9월) 내내 신규 수주와 여신 리스크가 동시 확대되는 최악의 타이밍에 빠진다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 컬렉션 콜에서 SAR 432K 30-60d 트랜치 확약(Committed Payment Date)을 받아내지 못할 경우, 피크 시즌 신규 오더 출하를 일시 게이팅(Hold-to-Pay) 할 것인가, 아니면 출하·회수 병행(Ship-and-Collect)으로 볼륨 리스크를 감수할 것인가.

options.option: Option A — 선(先)회수·후(後)출하 (Hold-to-Pay)
options.consequence: SAR 432K 확약 수령 전까지 신규 출하 게이팅. 여신 건전성 보호, DSO 개선 신호 확보. 단, 피크 시즌 진입 시점 공백으로 경쟁사(Samsung/Gree)에 선반 자리 내줄 위험. Tamkeen이 강하게 반발할 경우 계정 관계 악화.

options.option: Option B — 출하·회수 병행 (Ship-and-Collect)
options.consequence: 현 여신 한도 내에서 피크 시즌 수주 허용하며 동시에 컬렉션 압박. 볼륨 기회 보존. 단, SAR 432K가 60d+로 진입하면 Q3-Watch 이동 + 총 AR 잔액 추가 증가로 피크 시즌 말(8~9월) 대규모 회수 압박 집중. 2024 과잉구매 반복 위험.

timeline.step: SAR 432K 30-60d 트랜치 직접 컬렉션 콜 — 확약 납기일 서면 확보 (미이행 시 Option A 자동 발동)
2024→2025 Sell-In 63% 급감 원인 규명 + 2026 Jan~Apr 셀아웃 데이터 요청 — 영업팀 계정 인터뷰
원인 규명 + 5월 결제 실적 기반 피크 시즌 여신 정책 및 투자 등급 재결정 (Invest / Hold / Exit)

finance.headline: Clean current AR masks a 420-day T12M DSO — a recovering account on probation, not cleared for credit expansion.
finance.analysis:
Current AR of SAR 496,736 carries zero overdue across all aging buckets, and the 6-snapshot OVD trend holds a consistent 0.0% — a textbook clean read. However, this must be placed against a T12M DSO of 420 days, which means the account spent the bulk of the last 12 months in severe collection arrears. The 94% Sell-In collapse in 2025 (SAR 131K vs SAR 2.36M in 2024) almost certainly reflects a prior credit restriction or forced payment settlement, and the 2026 rebound (SAR 431K YTD, 2.9x buy pace vs T12M avg) signals active volume recovery — likely post-settlement. YTD DSO of 144 days still overshoots the 90-day credit term by 60%, confirming the payment pattern remains structurally slow even in the current clean phase.

Collection risk in the near term is watchable, not alarming, but not dismissible. The SAR 497K balance is composed of recent, pre-season invoices that have not yet matured to due date — the 0% OVD figure may simply be a timing artifact rather than proof of behavioral change. Given the historical OVD baseline of 35.4% and T12M DSO of 420 days, the probability of aging into the 30-60d bucket as invoices mature through May–June is meaningful. The 2.9x acceleration in purchase pace further amplifies exposure: if collection velocity fails to keep up with buy velocity heading into the May–September peak, the AR balance compounds quickly with limited early warning.

On credit policy, Grade C is consistent with this account's track record. The current Q1 Safe status is real but provisional — it reflects a clean snapshot taken immediately after what appears to be a reset, not a demonstrated pattern of on-time payment. Credit expansion here would be premature; the account must clear at least two full 90-day credit cycles with invoices maturing and settling within terms before any limit increase is warranted. Maintaining current exposure with tightened monitoring is the correct stance through the peak season.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade C + T12M DSO 420d + 35.4% historical OVD baseline require proof of sustained within-term payment before any exposure increase is justified.
action: Initiate bi-weekly AR aging watch: track whether the SAR 497K current balance migrates into the 30-60d bucket as invoices mature through May–June — first maturity checkpoint is the leading indicator of behavioral change
Set an informal soft ceiling at SAR 700K total AR exposure: do not approve incremental orders beyond that threshold until at least one full 90-day cycle clears cleanly
Direct sales to verify the 2.9x buy pace driver — pre-season inventory stocking (acceptable) vs. credit-dependent cash flow management (early OVD warning) — and report back within 2 weeks
finance.risk_reason: Zero current OVD is encouraging but T12M DSO 420d and Grade C signal structural payment slowness that is likely to re-emerge as the SAR 497K balance matures into due dates

sales.headline: 2025년 구매 94% 급락 후 2026년 재진입 중이나, T12M DSO 420일이 가리키는 과거 결제 리스크가 미청산 상태
sales.analysis:
채널 건전성 측면에서 Majid Golden은 2024년 SAR 2.35M 규모 계정에서 2025년 SAR 131K로 사실상 휴면 상태에 빠진 뒤, 2026년 5월 초 현재 SAR 431K로 재진입 흐름을 보이고 있습니다. T12M 대비 2.9x 구매 가속은 피크 시즌(5~9월) 선적재 또는 관계 복원 시도로 해석되나, 2025년 급락의 근본 원인—경쟁사 전환(Samsung/Gree 가능성), 재고 과잉, LG의 여신 제한 중 어느 쪽인지—이 확인되지 않은 상태입니다. 현재 AR은 SAR 497K 전액 OVD 0%로 표면상 깨끗하지만, 이는 최근 4주 스냅샷에 불과하며 잔액 자체가 아직 만기 미도래 구간에 있을 가능성이 높습니다.

DSO 프로파일이 핵심 리스크입니다. YTD DSO 144일(신용 조건 90일 초과)은 경계 수준이지만, T12M DSO 420일은 과거 12개월 동안 평균 결제 주기가 1년을 훌쩍 넘겼음을 의미합니다. 이는 2025년 구매 급락과 시기가 맞물리며, 여신 제한 또는 분쟁 이력이 있었을 가능성을 강하게 시사합니다. 현재의 '깨끗한' AR이 행동 변화인지, 단순히 신규 발행 청구서가 아직 만기가 안 됐을 뿐인지는 향후 30~60일 결제 추적 없이는 판단 불가합니다.

투자 결정 관점에서, 2026년 재진입 시그널(구매 가속, OVD 0%)은 고무적이지만 Grade C + T12M DSO 420일 조합은 크레딧 확대나 적극적 셀아웃 지원을 정당화하기에 불충분합니다. 현재 SAR 497K 잔액의 결제 패턴을 최소 1~2개 결제 사이클 확인 후 투자 수준을 재평가하는 것이 적절합니다.
sales.investment_rationale: T12M DSO 420일 이력이 해소되지 않은 상태에서 신규 여신 확대나 마케팅 공동투자는 시기상조이며, 현 잔액 결제 추이 확인 후 재평가가 필요합니다.
action: SAR 497K 잔액의 최초 결제 예정일(90일 크레딧 기준 7월 말) 달력에 등록하고 KAM이 2주 전 사전 리마인드 연락
2025년 구매 급락 원인 현장 확인: 담당 영업이 계정 방문하여 재고 현황·경쟁사 비중·2026 구매 의향 직접 청취
T12M DSO 개선 조건부 인센티브 설계 검토: 1~2회 정상 결제 확인 시 한정적 크레딧 한도 상향 또는 피크 시즌 추가 SKU 배정 옵션 제시
sales.channel_reason: 2026년 재진입 및 OVD 0%는 긍정적이나 T12M DSO 420일 이력과 2025년 급락 원인 미확인으로 회복 지속성 불명확

synthesis.headline: 현재 AR 0% OVD는 만기 미도래 타이밍 효과일 가능성 높음 — 진짜 테스트는 7월 말 첫 90일 만기이며, 그 전까지 AR 한도 동결이 핵심 의사결정.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. T12M DSO 420일 이력이 해소되지 않은 상태에서 2026 재진입 신호는 긍정적이나, 7월 첫 만기 전까지 크레딧 확대는 시기상조.

summary_3lines: 2025년 94% 구매 급락 후 2026년 SAR 431K로 재진입, OVD 0% — 표면은 깨끗하나 SAR 497K 잔액 전액이 아직 만기 미도래 구간에 있어 '행동 변화'가 아닌 '타이밍 효과'일 가능성이 높음.
T12M DSO 420일은 지난 12개월 평균 결제 주기가 1년을 초과했음을 의미하며, 2025년 구매 급락은 여신 제한 또는 분쟁 이후 사실상 리셋된 흐름으로 해석됨 — 현재의 깨끗한 스냅샷은 리셋 직후의 신규 잔액이지 습관 교정의 증거가 아님.
모니터링 없이 피크 시즌 주문을 계속 승인하면, 7월 말 첫 만기 도래 시 SAR 700K~1M+ AR이 동시에 aging되는 최악 시나리오 발생 가능 — 수금 압박이 피크 시즌 막바지와 겹쳐 영업 레버리지가 소멸.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 SAR 700K AR 소프트 상한선을 영업팀에 공식 통보하고, 7월 말 첫 90일 만기 결제 확인 전까지 추가 주문 승인을 동결할 것인가.

options.option: Option A — Hold Line (권고): SAR 700K 소프트 상한 설정 + 격주 AR 에이징 모니터링 + 7월 말 만기 후 재평가
options.consequence: 피크 시즌 일부 주문 마찰 발생 가능하나, 첫 만기 결제 패턴 확인 후 Grade 상향·크레딧 확대·투자 전환의 명확한 게이트 확보. 최악 시나리오(대규모 OVD 동시 발생) 차단.

options.option: Option B — Open Growth: 상한 없이 피크 시즌 주문 전량 승인, 수금은 사후 관리
options.consequence: 7~8월 SAR 1M+ AR 누적 상황에서 결제 지연 발생 시 수금 레버리지 부재. T12M DSO 420일 이력이 반복될 경우 2025년 사태 재현 위험. Grade C 계정에 Grade A 수준 익스포저 허용하는 구조적 모순.

timeline.step: KAM 현장 방문 — 2025년 급락 원인(경쟁사 전환·재고·여신 이슈) 직접 확인 및 2026 구매 의향 청취. SAR 700K 소프트 상한 내부 공유.
첫 번째 AR 에이징 체크포인트 — SAR 497K 잔액이 30-60d 버킷으로 이동 여부 확인. 이상 시 즉시 수금 접촉 + 신규 주문 보류.
첫 90일 만기 결제 확인 — 정상 결제 시 Grade 재검토 + 크레딧 한도 상향 협의 + 투자 전환(인센티브·피크 SKU 배정) 개시. 지연 시 즉시 Red 등급 상향 및 주문 전면 보류.

finance.headline: SAR 1.36M fully overdue 60d+ with zero 2026 revenue — account is commercially frozen and collection is in distress.
finance.analysis:
Alsabah Housing Co. presents a maximum-severity AR profile: 100% of the SAR 1,356,409 balance sits in the 60d+ aging bucket, meaning the entire receivable has blown past the 90-day credit term by at least two months. The OVD trend shows a flat 100% overdue line across all six observation days — no inbound payment, no partial settlement, no commercial movement. This is not a temporarily stretched account; it is a stalled one.

The absence of any 2026 sell-in is a critical secondary signal. A Grade-C IR_Others account going completely dark commercially at the start of peak season (May–September) strongly suggests the account has been placed on informal credit hold by LG's order management, or the customer is experiencing acute liquidity stress. Either way, the 2024 sell-in of SAR 4.79M now looks like a historical high-water mark rather than a repeatable baseline — and approximately 28% of that annual volume remains uncollected and aging.

Credit policy must move to decrease immediately. There is no scenario in which expanding or even maintaining the current limit is justified: zero revenue generation in 2026 means credit headroom is not being used productively, while the existing exposure continues to age and deteriorate. The account should be placed on hard credit hold, and a formal demand process initiated. If no concrete payment commitment is secured within 7–10 days, the SAR 1.36M should be flagged for doubtful debt provisioning ahead of Q2 close.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of balance is 60d+ overdue, zero 2026 activity, and no payment movement over the observed window — additional credit exposure is unjustifiable until full overdue clearance.
action: Issue formal written demand to Alsabah Housing Co. for SAR 1,356,409 with a 7-day payment deadline — copy in LG KSA Country Manager and IR channel lead
Enforce hard shipment block: zero new orders until overdue balance is fully settled or an approved installment plan is signed
Initiate Q2 doubtful debt provisioning review for SAR 1.36M — if no payment commitment by May 13, escalate to legal/external collections
finance.risk_reason: Full balance 60d+ overdue, Grade C, zero 2026 revenue, flat OVD trend — no recovery signal whatsoever.

sales.headline: Alsabah Housing Co. has gone completely dark in 2026 — zero purchases, SAR 1.36M fully overdue 60d+, with no payment movement for at least 7 consecutive days entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel is effectively dead in 2026. Despite a SAR 4.79M sell-in base in 2024, the account has recorded zero revenue in 2026 YTD — and the absence of a T12M DSO figure suggests the freeze predates 2026, meaning this account likely stopped buying in H2 2025 as well. The OVD trend is the most alarming signal: seven straight days locked at exactly SAR 1,356,409 at 100% overdue, all in the 60d+ bucket, with zero basis-point movement. This is not a seasonal slowdown or payment delay — this is a frozen account. LG has almost certainly auto-blocked new credit, which explains the zero 2026 sell-in. The account is not cycling through inventory; it is not restocking for peak season. May is peak season entry. Any account intending to participate in the May–September cycle would have placed orders in March–April at the latest.

Root cause hierarchy: (1) Financial distress is the primary hypothesis — the 'Housing Co.' designation and 90-day credit terms suggest real estate or construction-linked cash flow, a sector under significant pressure in KSA. The frozen AR balance implies the client cannot liquidate product fast enough to service debt, or the business has materially contracted. (2) Competitor displacement is a secondary hypothesis — if Samsung or a Chinese brand offered sharper terms or pricing, an IR_Others grade-C account would be highly susceptible to switching without a loyalty anchor. (3) Operational exit — account may be winding down or restructuring. The zero-movement AR over 7+ days with no partial payment is inconsistent with a functioning, actively-trading business.

Investment logic is straightforward: there is no channel left to invest in. No sell-in volume means no sell-out activity. Extending credit, co-marketing, or promotional support to this account would not unlock volume — it would compound exposure. The SAR 1.36M overdue already represents a 28% DSO risk on the 2024 base. Any further investment is capital destruction until collections recover. The only value-accretive action is collection focus and account status verification.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue with 100% overdue 60d+ AR and no payment movement means there is no active channel to invest in — recovery of existing exposure must precede any commercial engagement.
action: Escalate SAR 1.36M overdue to credit manager and legal team immediately — 60d+ bucket with zero partial payment warrants formal notice of default under 90-day credit terms
Verify business status of Alsabah Housing Co. — confirm entity is still operating; if dormant or restructuring, file claim before further deterioration

Maintain hard credit block on all new orders until SAR 1.36M is cleared or a binding payment plan (with partial upfront) is executed; do not negotiate new stock ahead of peak season as a goodwill gesture
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in, 100% AR overdue 60d+, no payment movement for 7 consecutive days entering peak season — account is commercially inactive and financially distressed.

synthesis.headline: Alsabah Housing Co. is not a delayed payer — it is a commercially frozen entity with SAR 1.36M fully overdue 60d+, zero 2026 activity, and seven consecutive days of zero payment movement entering peak season. This is a collections emergency, not a credit management situation.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Account is financially distressed with 100% AR overdue, zero 2026 revenue, and no recovery signal; every day without a formal demand accelerates write-off probability.

summary_3lines: SAR 1,356,409 — the entire receivable balance — sits 60d+ overdue with zero inbound payment across all observed days; the account has made no commercial move in 2026 and likely since H2 2025.

Root cause is financial distress, not seasonal softness: a Housing Co. on 90-day terms going dark at peak season entry strongly signals real estate-linked liquidity collapse or business contraction — competitor switching or operational exit are secondary hypotheses.

If no action is taken by May 13, the SAR 1.36M ages further into unrecoverable territory, Q2 close arrives without provisioning, and LG's legal standing weakens — a SAR 4.79M account relationship ends as a bad debt, not a managed exit.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize formal written demand + legal escalation path for SAR 1,356,409 this week — decide whether to initiate structured negotiation (payment plan with upfront) or proceed directly to external collections / legal notice of default.

options.option: Option A — Structured Demand with 7-Day Ultimatum
options.consequence: Issue formal written demand by May 8 (copy Country Manager + IR lead); offer a binding installment plan only if ≥30% upfront (SAR ~400K) is committed by May 13. If no response or partial upfront not received, trigger external collections. Preserves relationship optionality if account is temporarily illiquid but still operating — but risks delay if account is already insolvent.

options.option: Option B — Immediate Legal / External Collections Escalation
options.consequence: Skip negotiation; issue notice of default and hand to legal or collections agency by May 9. Maximizes recovery speed and legal standing, especially if entity is restructuring or winding down. Forfeits any future commercial relationship — acceptable given Grade C and zero 2026 revenue trajectory.

timeline.step: Verify business status of Alsabah Housing Co. — confirm entity is active, not dormant or under restructuring (CR check, site visit or call from IR channel lead)
Issue formal written demand for SAR 1,356,409 — copy Country Manager, IR channel lead, and Finance Director; set 7-day payment deadline
Decision gate: if no payment commitment or ≥30% upfront received, escalate to legal / external collections and flag SAR 1.36M for Q2 doubtful debt provisioning

finance.headline: Al Motawa is effectively a dormant account with SAR 129K fully overdue 60d+, zero new business in 2026, and no recovery signal — collection-only mode warranted.
finance.analysis:
AR health is at maximum stress: 100% of the SAR 129,043 balance sits in the 60d+ bucket, meaning the entire exposure has breached both the 90-day credit term and the 60d aging threshold. The OVD rate has been locked at 100% for at least six consecutive snapshot days with zero movement — no partial payments, no new invoices offsetting the balance, no trajectory improvement. This is not a temporary liquidity squeeze; this is a stalled account.

Collection risk is severe. The revenue collapse tells the structural story: SAR 609K in 2024 → SAR 95K in 2025 → SAR -1,799 in 2026 (net returns). The account has effectively stopped purchasing LG products entirely. Negative 2026 sell-in suggests the dealer is returning goods, possibly liquidating inventory rather than expanding. With no new transaction flow to signal continued engagement, the SAR 129K outstanding has no commercial relationship context that would incentivize prompt payment — the leverage of future orders is gone.

Credit policy must move to decrease/freeze immediately. There is no scenario in the current data that justifies maintaining existing credit exposure. Increasing would be indefensible given Q3 Critical status. The appropriate action is to formally block new shipments until full clearance of overdue AR, and reduce the credit limit to SAR 0 as a hard stop. Any credit rehabilitation path would require a payment plan with partial settlement first, followed by a probationary low-limit restart — at minimum SAR 60-70K payment before any credit line discussion.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of balance is 60d+ overdue with zero 2026 revenue activity, removing any commercial leverage to collect — credit exposure must be frozen until full clearance.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all new shipments/orders for Al Motawa until SAR 129,043 overdue is cleared
Escalate to senior sales contact and request formal payment commitment plan within 7 days (minimum SAR 65K immediate partial payment as condition to hold relationship)
Reduce credit limit to SAR 0 in system and flag account for legal/collections review if no payment response within 30 days
finance.risk_reason: 100% OVD in 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 revenue, multi-year declining trend, and no payment movement across 6 consecutive observation days.

sales.headline: Al Motawa has effectively ceased purchasing in 2026, with SAR 129K fully overdue and a -84% sell-in collapse from 2024 to 2025 signaling near-total disengagement.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated. Sell-in dropped from SAR 609K (2024) to SAR 95K (2025) — an 84% YoY collapse — and 2026 shows a net negative of -1,799, which is a return or credit note with zero new orders placed. The account has bought nothing during peak season preparation (March–April), which is the single clearest behavioral signal of disengagement. No sell-out data is available, but the sell-in pattern implies one of two scenarios: (1) severe inventory overhang from 2025 purchases that never cleared at retail, leaving the dealer unwilling or unable to take fresh stock; or (2) the account has switched primary supplier allegiance — likely to Samsung or a Chinese brand offering more flexible credit terms — and LG has become a residual line at best.

The AR situation confirms financial stress is compounding the commercial problem. The entire SAR 129K balance is 60d+ overdue on a 90-day credit term, meaning Al Motawa is roughly 150+ days past invoice date with no payment movement across six consecutive daily snapshots. This is not a collection lag — it is a frozen receivable. The 0-30d and 30-60d buckets are both zero, meaning there is no recent invoice activity and no partial payment progress. DSO is undefined for 2026 because revenue is zero, which is itself the most damning indicator.

Investment rationale is straightforwardly negative. With zero 2026 buy activity entering the Saudi peak season (May–September), 100% overdue AR, and a three-year sell-in trajectory pointing to exit, any credit extension or promotional co-investment would increase LG's exposure with no commercial recovery signal. The only legitimate action is aggressive AR collection before the 60d+ balance ages further and becomes a write-off risk. Relationship engagement should be limited to collection conversation and understanding whether the account intends to continue at all.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 orders entering peak season, 100% of AR 60d+ overdue, and a three-year sell-in collapse leave no basis for commercial investment — collection of SAR 129K is the only priority.
action: Escalate AR collection immediately: engage Al Motawa ownership (not buyer level) on the SAR 129K 60d+ overdue balance before it ages into write-off territory — propose structured repayment schedule within 30 days

Conduct a brief account exit interview to confirm whether disengagement is permanent (competitor switch, business contraction) or recoverable (inventory overhang, liquidity crunch) — this determines whether to formally close the account or place on 90-day probation
Place Al Motawa on credit hold with zero new order processing until the overdue balance is cleared; notify the sales team to redirect peak season allocation to active IR accounts
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 purchases, 100% AR overdue at 60d+, and three consecutive years of sell-in decline confirm the account has effectively exited the LG channel.

synthesis.headline: Al Motawa has functionally exited the LG channel — SAR 129K is 100% overdue with zero 2026 commercial activity. This is not a credit decision; it is a collection deadline.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Three-year sell-in collapse to zero, 100% AR frozen in 60d+ bucket, and no payment movement across six days remove any investment rationale; only collection remains.

summary_3lines: Al Motawa has stopped buying LG products entirely — SAR 609K in 2024, SAR 95K in 2025, net -SAR 1,799 in 2026 (returns only) — with zero orders placed during peak season preparation (March–April).

The most probable cause is dual failure: severe 2025 inventory overhang that never cleared at retail, compounding a likely supplier switch to a competitor offering softer credit terms — the 90-day credit term has become a liability anchor, not a commercial tool.

If no action is taken within 30 days, the SAR 129K balance crosses 180+ days overdue, write-off risk materializes, and LG loses both the receivable and any exit-interview intelligence needed to understand whether competitor gain is recoverable in this channel.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize immediate credit hold and direct the sales team to engage Al Motawa ownership — not buyer level — within 7 days to extract a minimum SAR 65K partial payment commitment and determine whether the account is recoverable or must be formally closed.

options.option: Option A — Structured Collection Engagement (Recommended)
options.consequence: Credit hold activated now; ownership-level meeting within 7 days; propose repayment plan (SAR 65K immediate + SAR 64K within 30 days); conduct exit interview to determine if competitor switch is reversible at all. If payment plan accepted: place on 90-day probationary status with SAR 0 credit until cleared. Best case: recover full SAR 129K and retain optionality to re-engage post-season with tightened terms.

options.option: Option B — Immediate Legal/Collections Escalation
options.consequence: Refer SAR 129K to legal or third-party collections without further sales engagement. Permanently close the account. Consequence: forfeits any chance of voluntary payment (legal process is slower and recovery rate lower), burns the relationship irreversibly, and skips intelligence-gathering on whether this is a systemic IR_Others channel risk. Appropriate only if Option A engagement yields zero response after 14 days.

timeline.step: Credit hold: block all new orders/shipments for Al Motawa in system; reduce credit limit to SAR 0
Ownership-level outreach: sales lead contacts Al Motawa principal (not buyer) with formal payment demand — minimum SAR 65K within 7 days as condition to hold relationship; conduct exit interview

Decision gate: if no payment commitment received, escalate to legal/collections and formally close account; if partial payment received, enter probationary repayment plan with 30-day clearance deadline

finance.headline: Account is in collection emergency — 88.5% overdue with YTD DSO of 1,047 days and a buy pace that has collapsed to near-zero, signaling acute financial distress.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically compromised. SAR 1,006,234 — 77.7% of total balance — sits in the 60d+ aging bucket, while the 0-30d bucket holds only SAR 38,382, confirming the account has made no meaningful recent payments. The OVD% has been locked at 87.5% for six consecutive daily snapshots with zero downward movement: this is not a temporary timing gap but a structural stall. YTD DSO of 1,047 days against a 90-day credit term represents an 11.6x breach; even the T12M DSO of 182 days exceeds both the credit term and LG's own DPO benchmark of 180 days — meaning LG is effectively financing this account far longer than LG itself is financed by its suppliers.

The revenue collapse tells the deeper story. Sell-in has fallen from SAR 9.3M (2024) → SAR 4.0M (2025) → SAR 154,588 YTD 2026, a buy pace of only 0.2x vs. T12M average. An account that has stopped buying while carrying a static overdue balance is not a slow payer — it is a distressed distributor. The pattern is a textbook precursor to a write-off: new purchases dry up, cash flow tightens, overdue ages deeper, and no resolution trigger emerges. There is no seasonality argument here; the May–September peak is beginning, yet 2026 YTD buy activity remains negligible.

Credit policy must be tightened with immediate effect. There is no revenue upside to justify maintaining existing credit exposure, let alone expanding it. SAR 1.29M is already at risk; adding any new shipment volume would deepen LG's stranded exposure without a credible repayment pathway. The credit limit should be reduced to zero new extension until the 60d+ bucket is materially cleared and a structured repayment plan is in place.
finance.credit_rationale: Account contributes near-zero 2026 revenue while holding SAR 1,006,234 in 60d+ overdue with no payment momentum — any new credit extension increases stranded exposure with no recoverable upside.
action: Impose immediate credit hold: zero new shipments until 60d+ overdue (SAR 1,006,234) is resolved or a binding repayment schedule is signed within 7 days
Escalate to collections and legal review: demand structured repayment plan from account principal; if no response in 7 days, initiate formal collection proceedings
Reduce credit limit to SAR 0 new exposure; place account on weekly DSO/OVD monitoring with automatic escalation trigger if balance increases
finance.risk_reason: 88.5% OVD rate, YTD DSO 1,047d (11.6x credit term breach), buy pace collapsed to 0.2x T12M, zero payment trend across 6-day snapshot window

sales.headline: Near-complete sell-in collapse (2026 pace = 0.2x T12M) combined with SAR 1.01M in 60d+ overdue signals structural channel disengagement, not seasonal softness.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated across every measurable axis. Sell-in declined 57% from 2024 to 2025 (SAR 9.3M → SAR 4.0M), then collapsed further to a SAR 154K YTD run-rate in 2026 — annualized ≈ SAR 444K, representing a ~89% YoY decline vs. 2025. The 0.2x buy pace vs. T12M average confirms this is not a temporary dip: the account has effectively stopped purchasing. The root cause is most likely a convergence of financial stress and competitor migration — the 90d credit term against a T12M DSO of 182d (2× term) and YTD DSO of 1,047d indicates chronic inability or unwillingness to pay, which would naturally suppress ordering. A secondary hypothesis is a quiet shift to Samsung or Gree on more favorable credit, which LG should probe directly.

The AR profile is the most urgent signal. SAR 1,006,234 sits beyond 60 days — this is not floating overdue, it is potentially impaired receivables. The OVD trend (87–89% for 6 consecutive days with zero improvement) tells us no active repayment plan is in motion. YTD DSO of 1,047 days is a mathematical expression of the same reality: for every riyal of 2026 purchases, LG is carrying SAR 8.38 in outstanding balance. The credit risk is extreme.

Investment in sell-out support, co-marketing, or credit expansion would be value-destructive at this stage. Any incremental volume shipped deepens LG's exposure without a corresponding cash recovery path. The only defensible action is collection-first: freeze new credit, escalate AR recovery, and diagnose the root cause (financial distress vs. deliberate disengagement) before any conversation about re-engagement. If the account has migrated to a competitor, the commercial conversation must start with settlement, not sell-in.
sales.investment_rationale: SAR 1.01M in 60d+ overdue, YTD DSO of 1,047 days, and a 0.2x buy pace indicate a financially distressed and disengaging account — further investment would deepen credit exposure with no recoverable upside.
action: Immediately freeze all new credit shipments until overdue balance falls below 60d threshold; escalate to KAM + Finance for formal collection notice on SAR 1.01M 60d+ balance

Schedule face-to-face meeting with account principal within 7 days to diagnose root cause: determine if this is liquidity crisis, competitor shift, or business wind-down — outcome determines whether this is a collection case or a negotiated exit
Engage LG Saudi AR collection process (legal notice / third-party agency evaluation) for the SAR 1.01M 60d+ tranche if no concrete repayment commitment is received within 14 days
sales.channel_reason: Sell-in down ~89% YoY annualized, buy pace 0.2x, 88.5% AR overdue with no collection momentum over 6 consecutive observation days.

synthesis.headline: SAR 1.01M is potentially impaired — account has structurally disengaged, OVD frozen at 88% for 6 days with zero buy activity entering peak season; every day without a formal collection notice increases write-off probability.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Account is in financial distress or deliberate wind-down: 89% YoY sell-in collapse, 1,047-day YTD DSO, and a static overdue pile with no repayment signal entering the May–September peak.

summary_3lines: Abdullah Al-Dhamin has effectively stopped buying (2026 YTD SAR 154K vs SAR 4M in 2025) while SAR 1.01M sits beyond 60 days overdue with zero payment movement across 6 consecutive daily snapshots.

Root cause is either acute liquidity crisis or quiet competitor migration (Samsung/Gree on softer credit terms) — neither scenario supports new shipments; both demand immediate face-to-face diagnosis and a signed repayment commitment.

If no action is taken this week, the 60d+ tranche ages further into write-off territory, legal recovery windows narrow, and LG enters peak season carrying a SAR 1.29M receivable with no collection pathway and no commercial upside to offset it.
synthesis.decision_required: Issue a formal credit hold + written repayment demand to the account principal by 2026-05-08 (this Friday) — either receive a signed structured repayment schedule within 7 days, or escalate to legal/third-party collection by 2026-05-15.

options.option: Option A — Immediate Credit Hold + 7-Day Ultimatum
options.consequence: Freeze all new shipments today; deliver formal written demand to account principal by 2026-05-08 requiring a binding repayment plan on the SAR 1.01M 60d+ tranche. If no commitment by 2026-05-13, escalate to legal proceedings. Protects LG from deepening exposure; creates a documented legal paper trail; forces account to surface its true financial position.

options.option: Option B — Do Nothing / Await Account Response
options.consequence: The 60d+ balance continues aging toward unrecoverable status, the May–September peak passes without recovery, and LG's effective write-off exposure solidifies at SAR 1.01M+. No commercial benefit offsets this risk — 2026 buy pace of 0.2x means there is no volume upside even under an optimistic scenario.

timeline.step: Credit hold activated — zero new shipments; KAM notified; SAP block placed on account
Face-to-face meeting with account principal + formal written repayment demand delivered; determine if distress, competitor shift, or wind-down
Repayment commitment deadline — if no signed schedule received, initiate legal notice and third-party collection agency evaluation for SAR 1.01M 60d+ tranche

finance.headline: Superficially clean AR on a structurally weak account — low current exposure masks a chronic slow-pay profile that caps credit upside.
finance.analysis:
Current AR of SAR 29,661 is entirely current with zero overdue across all aging buckets, and the six-day OVD trend confirms no active collection pressure. At face value this looks healthy. However, the absolute exposure is negligible — barely a single small delivery — which makes a clean ledger easy to maintain and uninformative about true payment behavior at scale.

The structural signals tell a different story. T12M DSO of 233 days is 43 days beyond DPO (180d) and more than 2.5× the 90-day credit term. YTD DSO of 144 days, while lower, still exceeds the credit term by 60%. The YTD OVD baseline of 35.4% confirms this account has historically run overdue more than a third of the time — the current 0% OVD is a function of tiny balance, not improved behavior. Grade D reinforces the risk profile. Sell-in collapse from SAR 270K (2024) to SAR 40K (2025) to SAR 26K (YTD 2026) is a further red flag: this account has been retreating, not growing.

The 1.6× buy pace acceleration in 2026 versus T12M average warrants monitoring rather than celebration. In a Grade D account with 233d T12M DSO, accelerating purchases into peak season could signal inventory loading ahead of a collection slowdown — a pattern that typically surfaces as aged overdue in Q3–Q4. Credit policy should remain defensive: maintain current (low) exposure ceiling, enforce 90-day terms strictly, and avoid any limit expansion until DSO consistently tracks below 120 days over two consecutive quarters.
finance.credit_rationale: T12M DSO of 233 days and Grade D rating make credit expansion imprudent despite the currently clean ledger — structural payment behavior, not balance size, governs limit decisions.
action: Hold credit limit at current level; do not approve incremental open-order requests without confirmed payment of existing balance first.
Flag the 1.6× buy-pace acceleration for monthly monitoring through Q3 — watch for balance build-up converting to overdue as peak season orders land.
Set a DSO performance gate: if YTD DSO drops below 120 days by end of Q3, revisit credit limit upgrade in Q4 planning cycle.
finance.risk_reason: Zero current overdue but T12M DSO 233d and Grade D signal the account becomes a collection problem at any meaningful exposure level.

sales.headline: 2025년 급격한 구매 붕괴(-85%) 이후 2026년 재진입 신호 감지 — 그러나 회복 속도는 아직 D등급 수준
sales.analysis:
2024년 SAR 270,913에서 2025년 SAR 39,536으로 -85% 급락은 단순 계절 조정이 아니다. 가장 유력한 가설은 2024년 피크시즌 대규모 선적재고 후 소진 부진 — 2025년 내내 재고 소화에 집중하며 신규 Sell-In을 사실상 중단한 패턴이다. T12M DSO 233일은 과거 연장 신용 또는 결제 지연 이력을 반영하며, YTD 평균 OVD 35.4% 베이스라인도 이 계정의 리스크 히스토리를 말해준다. 현재 OVD 0%·잔액 SAR 29,661은 클린하나, 이는 '거래 회복' 신호이지 '건강한 성장' 신호는 아니다.

2026 YTD SAR 25,792 (4개월) = 이미 2025 연간의 65% 달성. 월간 페이스 약 SAR 6,400으로 T12M 월평균 대비 1.6배 가속 중. 이 템포가 유지되면 2026 연간 SAR 77K 수준으로 회복되나, 2024 피크(SAR 271K)의 28%에 불과하다. 피크시즌(5–9월) 진입 직전 재진입은 의미 있는 신호지만, Grade D·IR Others 포지셔닝에서 대규모 물량 회수는 기대하기 어렵다.

투자 판단 관점에서 이 계정은 '소량 재진입 확인 → 소진 지원 → 점진적 물량 확대'의 단계적 접근이 적합하다. 2024년 과잉 선적 → 재고 누적 → 관계 단절이라는 실패 패턴을 반복하지 않으려면, 이번 시즌은 소화 가능한 수량으로 관계 복원에 집중하고, 재고 회전율을 먼저 확인한 후 크레딧·볼륨 확대를 논의해야 한다.
sales.investment_rationale: 1.6x 가속은 재진입 의지를 보여주나 D등급·소액 거래 수준에서 선제 대규모 투자보다 재고 소진 지원으로 신뢰 복원 후 볼륨 논의가 우선이다.
action: 5월 피크 시즌 전 현장 방문 — 2024 재고 소진 현황 확인 및 2026 시즌 구매 계획 청취 (과잉 선적 재발 방지 조건 합의)
소규모 sell-out 지원 제안(현장 디스플레이·인스톨러 인센티브)으로 재고 회전율 개선 → 연간 SAR 100K 회복 가시화 시 크레딧 재검토
월간 Sell-In 페이스 모니터링 — 6월 말까지 누적 SAR 40K 미달 시 exit 검토 트리거로 설정
sales.channel_reason: 2025 거의 거래 단절에서 재진입 중이나 볼륨은 2024 피크의 28% 수준으로 회복 신뢰성 검증 필요

synthesis.headline: 재진입 신호는 실재하나 신용 구조가 취약 — 피크 시즌 물량 허용 전 소진 능력 검증이 선행되어야 한다.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. 2026 재진입은 긍정적이나 T12M DSO 233일·Grade D 구조에서 피크 시즌 신용 확대는 Q3 연체 부메랑 리스크를 내포한다.

summary_3lines: 2025년 -85% 거래 붕괴 후 2026년 소량 재진입 중 — YTD SAR 25K, 월간 1.6배 가속, 현재 OVD 0%로 표면은 클린하다.
그러나 T12M DSO 233일·YTD 평균 OVD 35.4% 베이스라인은 '소액이라 깨끗한 것'이지 '결제 행동이 개선된 것'이 아님을 가리킨다 — 2024년 과잉 선적 후 재고 누적·관계 단절 패턴의 재연 가능성이 잠재한다.
아무 조치 없이 피크 시즌(5–9월) 주문을 수동적으로 받으면 Q3 말 잔액이 SAR 80K+ 수준으로 올라가고, DSO 구조상 이 중 35% 이상이 연체로 전환될 확률이 높다 — Grade D 계정의 전형적 수순이다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 피크 시즌(5–9월) 동안 이 계정의 오픈 오더를 허용할 것인가 — 허용한다면 발주 상한선(SAR)을 얼마로 설정하고 어떤 소진 조건을 연동할 것인가?

options.option: Option A — 조건부 소량 허용 (권고)
options.consequence: 현 잔액 SAR 29K 완납 확인 후 단건 발주 상한 SAR 30K 설정, 소진 확인 후 다음 발주 허용. 관계 복원 속도는 느리나 2024년 재고 누적 패턴 재연 방지. 연간 SAR 60–80K 회복 가능하며 DSO 모니터링으로 Q4 크레딧 재평가 근거 축적.

options.option: Option B — 현 상태 방치 (무조치)
options.consequence: 피크 시즌 주문량 무제한 수용 시 잔액 SAR 80K+ 도달 가능. T12M DSO 233일 구조에서 Q3 말 연체 전환 확률 35%+. 2024년 실패 패턴(대량 선적 → 재고 누적 → 장기 미수) 재연 위험이 높고, 회수 지연이 연말 재무 지표에 반영된다.

timeline.step: 현장 방문 — 2024 재고 소진 현황 확인 및 2026 시즌 구매 계획 청취, 단건 발주 상한 SAR 30K 조건 합의
6월 말 누적 Sell-In 모니터링 — SAR 40K 미달 시 Exit 검토 트리거, 도달 시 소진율 현장 확인 후 2차 발주 조건 협의
YTD DSO Q3 말 추적 — 120일 이하 달성 시 Q4 크레딧 한도 상향 및 Grade 재평가 착수

finance.headline: Current AR is spotless but YTD DSO of 143d (90d term) and T12M DSO of 237d reveal a structurally slow payer — hold credit steady and watch the accelerated 2026 buying pace.
finance.analysis:
AR health at the snapshot level is immaculate: SAR 198,500 balance, zero overdue across all aging buckets, and six consecutive days of 0% OVD. On the surface this reads as a textbook Q1 account. However, the DSO metrics expose a structural mismatch — YTD DSO of 143d is 53 days beyond the 90d credit term, and T12M DSO of 237d signals that over the trailing twelve months payments were averaging nearly eight months, well into DPO-tolerance territory but indicative of habitual late settlement. The 35.4% YTD average OVD baseline further confirms that current zero-overdue is an improvement from the norm, not the norm itself.

Collection risk in the near term is contained. The balance is modest (SAR 198.5K), no invoices are aging dangerously, and the account is actively buying — 2026 Sell-In of SAR 173K in roughly four months already surpasses full-year 2025 (SAR 131K) and is tracking toward a partial recovery from 2024's SAR 545K peak. The 1.7x buy-pace acceleration relative to T12M average is the key variable to watch: it can reflect legitimate peak-season stocking (May–September window now opening) or a sudden appetite surge ahead of anticipated credit tightening. Either way, the risk of a large outstanding balance materializing quickly is real given the DSO history.

Credit policy should be maintained, not expanded. Grade C designation and T12M DSO of 237d do not support a limit increase — the account has not yet demonstrated sustained on-time payment behavior under the current 90d term, let alone a tighter benchmark. Conversely, with zero current overdue and rising 2026 volume, there is no justification for a decrease. The correct posture is hold: protect current exposure cap, enforce the 90d term rigorously on new invoices entering the system during peak season, and revisit in August once mid-season payment behavior is observable.
finance.credit_rationale: Zero current overdue is positive but T12M DSO of 237d and Grade C status show the account has not yet earned a higher credit ceiling — expansion now would reward structure, not performance.
action: Flag all new peak-season invoices (May–Sep) for 90d hard-stop dunning — do not allow informal extensions given the DSO history
Set a DSO watch trigger: if YTD DSO exceeds 160d or any invoice crosses 30d overdue, escalate to credit review immediately

sales.headline: Dormant 2025 account re-engaging at 1.7x pace, but T12M DSO of 237d and C-grade size cap upside.
sales.analysis:
Al-Qahtani collapsed 76% in 2025 (SAR 546K → SAR 132K), almost certainly from 2024 inventory overhang — the 2024 number is 3x their apparent run-rate and looks like a bulk pre-season load that took the full year to digest. The 2026 YTD buy pace at 1.7x T12M average confirms they have worked through that stock and are re-entering the market ahead of the May–September peak, but absolute volume (SAR 173K YTD) remains 68% below the 2024 peak, meaning this is re-engagement, not a breakout.

The AR picture is split: current balance is completely clean (0% OVD, six consecutive snapshots), which is reassuring pre-season, but T12M DSO of 237d — nearly 2.5x the 90-day credit term — signals a history of very slow settlement that has only recently normalized. YTD DSO of 143d is still 59% over terms. This pattern (clean today, chronic lateness historically) suggests the account pays when it wants to, not when it should, and the current cleanliness may simply reflect a low outstanding balance, not a structural behavior change.

Grade C + IR_Others positions this account below the investment priority line. The re-engagement signal is real and worth watching through Q2, but the combination of sub-scale volume, weak payment DNA, and no sell-out visibility makes credit expansion or co-marketing premature. The right posture is disciplined hold: protect existing terms, confirm the 2024 overbuy hypothesis with a field visit, and reassess at mid-season if volume trajectory holds.
sales.investment_rationale: Re-engagement pace is encouraging but T12M DSO 237d and Grade C scale do not yet justify resource commitment — verify 2024 overbuy root cause and maintain current terms through Q2 before escalating.
action: Field visit to confirm 2024 inventory fully cleared and probe which brands filled the shelf in 2025 (Samsung/Gree displacement risk)
Hold credit line at 90d hard — do not extend given T12M DSO 237d; flag any new invoice aging beyond 90d immediately
Set a mid-season checkpoint (end of June): if buy pace sustains ≥1.5x and DSO trend continues improving, upgrade to invest + evaluate targeted sellout support
sales.channel_reason: Accelerating re-entry after 2025 collapse, but chronic payment latency and below-trend absolute volume warrant caution before committing resources.

synthesis.headline: Clean AR today masks a structural slow-payer re-entering at full peak-season pace — enforce 90d hard-stop now before a large balance forms at 237d DSO.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. Zero overdue today but T12M DSO 237d means terms will likely be breached as May–September volume accumulates; hold credit line and gate investment to a June checkpoint.

summary_3lines: Al-Qahtani has zero overdue today and is buying at 1.7x pace entering peak season — on the surface a healthy Q1 account re-engaging after a 76% 2025 collapse.

The 2025 collapse was almost certainly a 2024 overbuy hangover (SAR 546K in one year = ~3x run-rate); re-engagement is real but current AR cleanliness reflects a low balance, not a payment behavior reset — T12M DSO of 237d is the true signature.

If peak-season invoices are allowed informal term extensions, this account will accumulate SAR 400–500K+ at its historical 237d settlement pace by Q3 with no leverage to accelerate collection — the 2024 overbuy cycle repeats, this time as a credit event.
synthesis.decision_required: Confirm enforcement posture on 90d credit term for all peak-season (May–September) invoices: hard-stop with no informal extensions, or negotiate a structured term arrangement in exchange for volume commitment and sell-out reporting.

options.option: Option A — Enforce 90d hard-stop unconditionally
options.consequence: Limits collection risk and keeps DSO on a correction path; may constrain volume recovery if competitors (Samsung, Gree) offer softer terms to the same account — risk is losing shelf space at a re-engaging dealer during peak.

options.option: Option B — Allow structured term extension (e.g. 120d) conditional on sell-out data sharing and a volume commitment
options.consequence: Captures upside of 1.7x buy-pace acceleration with a contractual handle; gives us sell-out visibility currently absent; requires active monitoring or risks sliding back to 237d informal behavior with no enforcement mechanism.

timeline.step: Flag all open and new peak-season invoices for 90d hard-stop dunning — no informal extensions until a policy decision is made; set DSO watch trigger at YTD 160d or any invoice crossing 30d overdue

Field visit to Al-Qahtani — confirm 2024 inventory fully cleared, probe which brands occupied shelf in 2025 (Samsung/Gree displacement assessment), and gauge willingness to share sell-out data in exchange for term flexibility

Mid-season checkpoint — if YTD DSO trending below 130d, buy pace ≥1.5x sustained, and field visit confirms shelf recovery, upgrade to invest with targeted sell-out support; otherwise maintain hold through year-end

finance.headline: Recovering Grade-C account with manageable near-term overdue but structurally elevated DSO signals payment discipline still below credit term.
finance.analysis:
AR health is currently contained — SAR 42,506 overdue sits entirely in the 0-30d bucket with zero 30-60d or 60d+ exposure, and OVD% of 30.8% is 4.6pp below the company's YTD average baseline of 35.4%. The 6-day flat OVD trend indicates stagnation rather than active deterioration. However, the structural picture is more cautious: YTD DSO of 143d is 53 days beyond the 90d credit term, meaning the account is habitually slow-paying regardless of formal terms — a discipline gap that will compound as the peak-season order cycle (May–September) drives the balance higher.

The T12M DSO of 292d is the key historical red flag. Through 2025, this account was effectively running on 9–10 month payment cycles — the period when Sell-In collapsed to SAR 82K vs SAR 280K in 2024. The 2026 recovery (SAR 120K YTD at 2.0x buy pace) is commercially encouraging, but payment behavior has not yet normalized to match the accelerated ordering pace. Unless collection velocity improves alongside purchase velocity, the absolute overdue SAR amount will scale proportionally through peak season, creating a larger exposure event in Q3.

On credit policy, maintaining current limits is the appropriate stance. The account does not qualify for expansion: Grade C classification, 53-day DSO overshoot vs credit term, and a 2025 history of near-dormancy with extreme DSO all argue against increasing credit exposure without payment normalization evidence. Conversely, Q1 Safe quadrant placement, early-bucket-only overdue, and the 2026 commercial recovery provide no basis for immediate tightening — a reactive cut would penalize a recovering account unnecessarily. The governing condition for any future credit expansion should be demonstrated DSO reduction toward 120d or below.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade C with 143d YTD DSO against a 90d credit term and a T12M history of 292d requires payment normalization evidence before any limit expansion is warranted.
action: Issue immediate collection call on SAR 42,506 (0-30d) to prevent aging into 30-60d bucket before peak-season order acceleration raises the baseline balance
Set internal monitoring alert: escalate if YTD DSO exceeds 160d or OVD% crosses the company baseline of 35.4% — both would signal the 2026 recovery story is breaking down
Condition any peak-season incremental orders above current credit limit on partial settlement of outstanding overdue, enforcing payment-before-shipment discipline without blocking commercial recovery
finance.risk_reason: YTD DSO 53d above credit term with T12M history of 292d; overdue is early-bucket-only today but 2.0x buy-pace acceleration will grow absolute exposure materially through Q3 peak season.

sales.headline: 2025 급락 후 2026 재진입 중 — 계절 전 재고 확보 재개, 그러나 T12M DSO 292일이 잠재 리스크
sales.analysis:
2024 SAR 280K → 2025 SAR 82K(−71% YoY)는 단순 부진이 아니라 사실상 거래 단절에 가까운 수준이다. 가장 유력한 원인 가설은 2024년 대량 구매 후 재고 소화 지연(inventory overhang)으로, T12M DSO 292일이 그 시기의 현금 흐름 압박을 방증한다. 부분적인 경쟁사 전환(Samsung/Hisense)도 배제할 수 없으나, 2026년 2.0x buy pace 재가속이 확인되므로 완전 이탈보다는 소화 후 재진입으로 해석하는 것이 합리적이다.

2026 YTD SAR 120K는 회복 신호지만 2024 피크 대비 43% 수준에 불과하다. 긍정적인 점은 YTD DSO 143일로 T12M 292일 대비 현저히 개선됐고, OVD 30.8%가 YTD baseline 35.4%를 하회한다는 것이다. 5~9월 피크 시즌을 앞두고 IR_Others 채널에서 선제 재고 확보를 시작한 패턴으로, 타이밍 자체는 맞다. 다만 2026 SAR 120K가 이미 2025 연간치(SAR 82K)를 초과했다는 점은 과거 대비 이례적으로 이른 구매 속도를 시사하며, 재고 소화 속도(sell-out 데이터 없음)가 검증되지 않은 채로 추가 확대 시 2025 패턴 반복 위험이 있다.

투자 결정의 핵심 딜레마: 2.0x 가속과 시즌 타이밍은 '투자' 신호이나, Grade C + T12M DSO 292일 + 미검증 sell-out은 신용 확대에 브레이크를 건다. 현 42.5K 연체를 정상화한 후 sell-out 지원(installer 프로모, 전시 강화)으로 재고 회전율을 먼저 올리는 것이 credit 증액보다 위험 대비 효과가 높다.
sales.investment_rationale: 재진입 모멘텀은 실재하나 T12M DSO 292일 + Grade C 조합에서 신용 확대는 2025 재고 압박 재현 위험이 있어, 연체 정상화 + sell-out 회전율 개선을 선행한 뒤 Q3 결과로 투자 등급 재평가가 합리적이다.
action: SAR 42,506 연체(0-30d) 즉시 수금 액션 — 90일 크레딧 조건 내 정상화 기한 명시 후 영업 담당 직접 접촉
2026 구매 내역 SKU 분석 — 어떤 모델/용량을 재매입했는지 확인하여 sell-out 가능성 및 경쟁 모델과의 가격 경쟁력 점검
7월 이전 미니 sell-out 리뷰 일정 잡기 — 2026 재고 소화 속도 확인 후 Q3 신용 한도 재설정 및 co-marketing 투자 여부 결정
sales.channel_reason: 2026 buy pace 2.0x 재가속은 긍정적이나 T12M DSO 292일 + sell-out 미검증으로 2025 재고 압박 재현 가능성 배제 불가

synthesis.headline: 재진입 모멘텀 실재하나 연체 정상화 없이 피크 시즌 주문 확대 허용 시 2025 재고 압박 재현 위험 — 이번 주 수금 액션이 투자 여부 결정의 분기점

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. 2.0x 재가속은 긍정적이나 T12M DSO 292일 + Grade C 조합에서 SAR 42,506 연체 정상화가 투자 확대의 선행 조건이다.

summary_3lines: 2025년 거래 사실상 단절(-71% YoY) 후 2026년 2.0x 속도로 재진입 중이며, 현 연체는 0-30d 단일 버킷에만 SAR 42,506 집중 — 구조적으로 아직 통제 가능한 수준
T12M DSO 292일은 2024 대량 구매 후 재고 과잉으로 인한 현금흐름 압박의 잔상이며, YTD DSO 143일로 개선 중이나 90일 크레딧 조건 대비 여전히 53일 초과 — 결제 기율 정상화 미완
지금 연체를 방치한 채 피크 시즌(5~9월) 주문을 그대로 수용하면 절대 연체 금액이 Q3에 비례 증가하고, sell-out 데이터 없이 재고가 쌓일 경우 2025년 패턴(구매 급감 + DSO 폭등)이 반복된다
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 SAR 42,506 수금 액션을 영업 담당이 직접 착수하고, '연체 정상화 완료 전 추가 신용 확대 없음' 원칙을 계정 담당 영업에게 공식 전달할 것인가

options.option: Option A — 연체 정상화 선행 후 조건부 확대
options.consequence: SAR 42,506 수금 + YTD DSO 120일 이하 도달 시 Q3 신용 한도 재검토. 재고 소화 sell-out 리뷰(7월 이전)를 통해 co-marketing 투자 여부 결정. 위험 통제하면서 회복 모멘텀 유지 가능.

options.option: Option B — 현 모멘텀 유지하며 주문 그대로 수용
options.consequence: 피크 시즌 매출 단기 확대 가능하나, 연체 정상화 없이 잔액 증가 시 Q3 OVD% 35.4% 기준선 돌파 위험. T12M DSO 292일 전력 감안 시 대규모 미수 발생 가능성 배제 불가.

timeline.step: 영업 담당 직접 접촉 — SAR 42,506 (0-30d) 수금 요청 및 정상화 기한 명시 (90일 조건 내)
2026 YTD 구매 SKU 분석 — 모델/용량 재매입 내역 확인, 경쟁사 가격 대비 sell-out 가능성 점검
Sell-out 미니 리뷰 및 Q3 신용 한도·투자 등급 재평가 — 연체 정상화 + 재고 소화 속도 확인 후 결정

finance.headline: Grade-D account in full collection crisis: entire overdue concentrated in 60d+ bucket, DSO 3x the credit term, and new goods released while the account was 100% delinquent.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired. The SAR 123,734 overdue sits entirely in the 60d+ aging bucket — zero recovery in 0-30d or 30-60d — indicating no partial settlement and a fully matured delinquency. The April 30 balance surge from SAR 0.12M to SAR 0.26M is a red flag, not a positive signal: new sell-in was released while the account was 100% overdue (per the April 29 snapshot). The resulting drop from 100% OVD to 48.1% is a dilution effect, not a collection improvement. Fresh exposure was layered on top of unresolved debt — a process control failure that must be investigated immediately.

Collection risk is severe across every metric. YTD DSO of 277 days is 3.1x the 90-day credit term and exceeds even LG's own DPO benchmark of 180 days by 54%. The T12M DSO of 333 days confirms this is a structural payment behavior problem, not a one-quarter anomaly. The account's YTD OVD baseline is already elevated at 35.4%, and current OVD at 48.1% is running above that baseline. Sell-in has collapsed 43% from 2024 to 2025, with 2026 on a further declining trajectory — this is a shrinking revenue relationship with deteriorating payment discipline, not a temporarily stressed but recoverable partner.

Credit policy must move to decrease. There is no scenario that justifies maintaining current exposure: revenue is contracting, DSO chronically breaches the credit term by a factor of three, and the most recent overdue tranche has fully aged into the 60d+ bucket with zero settlement movement. The April 30 release to a 100%-delinquent account is the clearest possible signal of a broken credit gate. Further sell-in must be blocked until the overdue is cleared, and the credit limit should be formally reduced to reflect the account's actual payment capacity.
finance.credit_rationale: DSO at 277d (3.1x the 90d credit term), 100% of overdue aged beyond 60 days, and new goods released on a fully delinquent balance — no basis for maintaining current exposure entering peak season.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all further sell-in until SAR 123,734 (60d+ overdue) is fully settled — escalate to account manager and sales head today with written confirmation
Process audit: identify who approved the April 30 order release while the account was 100% delinquent; close the credit gate gap to prevent recurrence across other Grade-D accounts
Issue formal overdue demand notice with a 14-day hard deadline; if no response or partial payment, initiate legal/collection escalation — Grade D + 333d T12M DSO leaves no room for soft follow-up
finance.risk_reason: All overdue in 60d+ bucket, DSO 3x credit term, declining sell-in trend, and new credit extended on a 100%-delinquent account — multi-dimensional collection failure.

sales.headline: Sell-in in structural collapse (-43% YoY) with DSO 3× credit terms and fresh 60d+ overdue sitting unpaid while the account placed new April orders — collection emergency, not a growth account.
sales.analysis:
Sell-in has contracted sharply: SAR 289K (2024) → SAR 166K (2025) → SAR 116K YTD 2026. The 1.2× T12M buy pace reads as peak-season front-loading, not a recovery signal — the absolute annualized run-rate still trails 2025. For a Grade D IR_Others account, this pattern typically reflects a dealer who has narrowed LG to a secondary or fill-in supplier, buying opportunistically around Ramadan/summer rather than committing to planned purchase cycles. No sell-out data is available, but declining Sell-In at this magnitude in a rising-season market strongly implies the account is bleeding share to competitors (Samsung, Gree) or reducing overall AC SKU depth.

The April 29→30 balance move is the sharpest red flag: balance jumped from SAR 0.12M at 100% OVD to SAR 0.26M at 48.1% OVD, meaning ~SAR 134K in new invoices were posted while the account was fully overdue. The 60d+ bucket (SAR 123,734) has not moved across six consecutive daily snapshots — signaling either genuine cash-flow inability or deliberate payment deferral ahead of peak season. YTD DSO of 277d and T12M DSO of 333d on a 90d credit term means LG is involuntarily extending 6–9 months of additional financing to a D-grade counterparty with no compensating volume or strategic value.

There is no investment case here. The account is leveraging LG's credit line to self-fund inventory it cannot finance independently, while volume erodes. Injecting sell-out support, co-marketing, or a credit increase would compound exposure on a counterparty already demonstrating structural payment dysfunction. With peak season beginning now (May), the risk of the balance ballooning further — and DSO widening toward 400d — is acute. The only correct posture is collection-first, conditional supply, and no incremental investment until the aged overdue is cleared and payment behavior normalizes.
sales.investment_rationale: DSO at 3× credit terms, sell-in down 43% YoY, and static 60d+ overdue despite new orders means any further investment amplifies bad-debt exposure with no credible volume or loyalty upside.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all new shipments until SAR 123,734 (60d+ overdue) is fully collected — do not release even partial peak-season orders without cleared balance
Joint sales + finance visit this week: demand a signed payment commitment schedule for the overdue, and tie any peak-season supply resumption to first installment receipt
If supply resumes post-collection, cap credit ceiling at SAR 150K and reduce term from 90d to 45d cash-against-delivery or PDC; review quarterly against DSO trend before any credit restoration
sales.channel_reason: Sell-in -43% YoY, DSO 277d on 90d terms, 60d+ overdue static across 6 days, new order placed while 100% overdue — disengagement pattern with active collection risk

synthesis.headline: New shipment released on a 100%-delinquent Grade-D account — SAR 123,734 fully aged into 60d+ with zero movement, DSO at 3× credit terms, sell-in down 43% YoY. This is a bad-debt trajectory, not a peak-season opportunity.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Dealer is self-financing peak inventory on LG's credit line while deliberately deferring payment and shrinking volume commitment.

summary_3lines: SAR 123,734 has sat frozen in the 60d+ bucket across 6 consecutive daily snapshots while a new April order — released on a 100%-delinquent balance — inflated total exposure from SAR 0.12M to SAR 0.26M.

The account is structurally dysfunctional: T12M DSO of 333 days on a 90-day credit term means LG is involuntarily carrying 9 months of dealer financing for a Grade-D partner whose sell-in has contracted 43% since 2024 and whose OVD baseline was already elevated at 35.4% before this episode.

If no action is taken this week, the balance will balloon further into peak season (May–September), DSO will approach 400d by Q3, and SAR 257K converts from a recoverable overdue into a bad-debt write-off with no compensating volume or strategic value.
synthesis.decision_required: Impose an immediate credit hold on all new shipments and decide this week whether to escalate to formal legal/collection proceedings or accept a signed PDC-backed settlement schedule — delay beyond Friday converts optionality into loss.

options.option: Option A — Hard Credit Hold + 14-Day Ultimatum
options.consequence: Block all shipments now. Issue formal demand notice with a 14-day hard deadline for full settlement of SAR 123,734. No response or partial payment triggers legal/collection escalation. Preserves LG's legal position and signals zero tolerance to other Grade-D accounts watching this precedent.

options.option: Option B — Conditional Supply with PDC Settlement Schedule
options.consequence: Allow limited peak-season orders ONLY upon receipt of signed post-dated cheques covering the full SAR 123,734 overdue in two tranches (7d + 7d). Future credit capped at SAR 150K, term reduced from 90d to 45d cash-against-delivery. Higher collection probability than hard block, but requires immediate joint sales+finance field visit this week — if PDCs are not secured by Friday, revert to Option A automatically.

timeline.step: Issue written credit hold notice to account manager and sales head; freeze all pending order releases — also audit who approved the April 30 shipment on a 100%-delinquent balance
Joint sales + finance field visit to account: present formal overdue demand, obtain signed PDC settlement schedule or confirmed hard-date payment commitment as condition of any peak-season supply

If SAR 123,734 not cleared or PDCs not received by deadline, escalate to legal/external collection and formally reclassify account as non-active; review all other Grade-D IR accounts for the same credit gate gap

finance.headline: Terminal-risk account: 76.5% of exposure is 60d+ overdue, YTD DSO at 613 days signals near-total collection failure on a rapidly shrinking revenue base.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critical. The entire overdue balance (SAR 107,142) sits exclusively in the 60d+ bucket — zero in 0–30d or 30–60d — meaning this is not a timing or liquidity lag but a structural collection breakdown. The apparent OVD% improvement from 100% on Apr-29 to 76.5% from Apr-30 onward was not driven by any payment; the balance actually grew from SAR 0.11M to SAR 0.14M. The ratio dilution came from new invoice issuance (SAR 28,570 YTD sell-in), which added current receivables on top of already-stalled 60d+ overdue — effectively compounding exposure without any recovery signal.

Collection risk is at maximum severity. YTD DSO of 613 days is 6.8x the 90-day credit term and 3.4x the company's own DPO ceiling of 180 days. Even the trailing 12-month DSO of 222 days is 2.5x the credit term, indicating chronic latency predating 2026. The 2026 buy pace at 0.4x the T12M average confirms the account is commercially disengaging — likely reflecting financial distress or deliberate withdrawal, either of which severely degrades future collection probability. Revenue has collapsed from SAR 428K (2024) → SAR 201K (2025) → SAR 29K (2026 YTD), a 93% three-year drawdown.

Credit policy must be tightened to zero immediately. Grade C classification, Q3 Critical quadrant, a terminal revenue trajectory, and zero evidence of repayment intent or capacity collectively disqualify this account from any further credit exposure. Continuing to ship would convert a difficult collection into a probable write-off. The strategic posture shifts entirely from account development to damage containment and recovery.
finance.credit_rationale: 613-day YTD DSO, 100% of overdue concentrated in the 60d+ bucket, and a buy pace collapsing to 0.4x T12M average leave no justification for any open credit — limit should be reduced to SAR 0 until overdue is fully cleared.
action: Immediately block all shipments and freeze open orders — no further goods to move until SAR 107,142 overdue is cleared in full

Issue formal written demand notice within 48 hours for SAR 107,142 with a 14-day deadline; if no payment commitment received, escalate to legal collections and flag for potential write-off provisioning

Engage account principal directly for a structured repayment plan (e.g., 3 equal monthly installments) secured by personal guarantor signature — reduce credit limit to SAR 0 in ERP pending confirmation of first payment receipt
finance.risk_reason: SAR 107K fully aged into 60d+ (76.5% of balance), YTD DSO 613d vs 90d credit term, revenue in freefall to SAR 29K YTD, and no payment activity detected across the last 6 OVD snapshots.

sales.headline: Account in structural collapse — buy activity down 93% from 2024 peak, SAR 107K aged 60d+ debt static, and a suspicious small recent purchase signals access-maintenance rather than genuine recovery.
sales.analysis:
Sell-in trajectory tells a clear disengagement story: SAR 428K (2024) → SAR 201K (2025, -53%) → SAR 28K (2026 YTD, -86% vs same-period implied run-rate). Buy pace at 0.4x T12M average confirms this is structural, not seasonal — peak season (May–Sep) is arriving but zero pre-season stocking has occurred, which in a normal account would have triggered orders by April. Root cause hypotheses in priority order: (1) financial distress / cash crisis preventing meaningful purchasing, (2) competitor switch — Samsung or Gree may have captured shelf space with better credit terms, (3) inventory overhang from 2025 under-sell-through. YTD DSO of 613 days is not a payment delay metric — it reflects near-zero purchasing activity against a fixed aged receivable balance, making the ratio explode. T12M DSO of 222 days (vs. 90d term) confirms chronic payment stress predating 2026.

The OVD snapshot sequence is the most telling signal: SAR 107K in 60d+ overdue has sat completely static across every observed date. The balance increase from SAR 110K (Apr 29) to SAR 140K (Apr 30+) with OVD% dropping from 100% to 76.5% is not a collection success — it means the account made a small new purchase (~SAR 30K) without clearing any aged debt. This 'buy small, pay nothing old' pattern is a textbook supplier-access tactic: keep the relationship nominally alive to preserve supply optionality while not committing to debt clearance. It warrants immediate escalation rather than accommodation.

From an investment lens, this account offers no credible upside scenario. Grade C in IR_Others, declining for two consecutive years, entering peak season with zero stocking momentum and SAR 107K of critically aged AR. Any credit extension or promotional investment would increase LG's exposure without a recovery path. The correct posture is a hard collection focus with supply freeze until the 60d+ balance is materially cleared — and a parallel assessment of whether the account relationship should be formally wound down post-season.
sales.investment_rationale: 613-day YTD DSO, 93% sell-in collapse, and static 60d+ overdue with no collection progress make any further investment an exposure increase with no credible return scenario.

finance.headline: C-grade account running 104-day DSO against a 60-day term with 63.4% OVD and a textbook selective-pay posture — structural liquidity stress, not a seasonal blip.
finance.analysis:
AR health is materially impaired: SAR 178,642 (63.4%) of the SAR 281,845 balance sits overdue — nearly double the account's own YTD OVD baseline of 35.4%. The aging profile reveals the key behavioral signal: overdue is concentrated almost entirely in the 0-30d bucket (SAR 162,449 of SAR 178,642), while 60d+ remains clean at zero. This is a classic selective-pay posture — the customer pays the most recent invoices to keep the supply line open while systematically deferring older balances, artificially preventing 60d+ escalation but sustaining a permanently elevated overdue stock. The partial OVD improvement from 70.5% (Apr 29) to 63.4% (Apr 30) looked promising, but six consecutive flat days with no further movement confirms payments have stalled again.

DSO of 104 days against a 60-day credit term represents a 44-day structural overshoot — 73% beyond contractual terms. Critically, T12M DSO equals YTD DSO at 104 days exactly, confirming this is not a seasonal anomaly but the account's embedded operating norm. The account has normalized paying LG on 104-day cycle while holding a 60-day credit line, effectively extracting 44 days of unsanctioned financing at LG's cost.

Revenue trajectory compounds the risk calculus: Sell-In fell 34% from SAR 1.25M (2024) to SAR 820K (2025), and while 2026 buy pace is tracking 1.0x T12M, that reference average already reflects a contracting business. Entering May-to-September peak season — where new inventory orders will drive fresh AR creation — on top of an already overdue-heavy balance for a C-grade account creates asymmetric downside. If this customer faces cash flow stress mid-season, LG's exposure could pyramid rapidly with limited recourse.
finance.credit_rationale: A C-grade account running 104-day DSO, 63.4% OVD, and a confirmed selective-pay pattern on declining revenue has no basis for credit maintenance — tightening exposure before peak-season volume build is the only defensible posture.
action: Issue formal OVD notice for SAR 178,642 today and demand a written payment commitment within 7 business days — require full clearance of the 30-60d bucket (SAR 16,193) as the minimum proof-of-intent milestone
Place account on shipment hold effective immediately; release only upon OVD ratio falling below 40% or execution of a signed repayment schedule with upfront partial payment
Reduce credit limit by 35-40% and restructure new orders to 50% prepayment + 50% net-30 to cap peak-season exposure and break the selective-pay cycle
finance.risk_reason: Zero 60d+ aging prevents red classification, but 104-day DSO at 73% beyond credit terms, 63.4% OVD nearly double baseline, and a sustained selective-pay pattern on a shrinking C-grade account place this firmly at the upper boundary of yellow.

sales.headline: Structurally declining account with cash-flow stress — sell-in halved since 2024 peak, DSO 1.7× credit terms, overdue holding stubbornly above 60%.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is deteriorating on a multi-year arc. Sell-in collapsed 34% from 2024 to 2025 (SAR 1.25M → SAR 820K), and the 2026 run-rate of SAR 340K YTD (≈SAR 85K/month) only marginally outpaces the depressed 2025 monthly average — the account has not recovered, it has merely stabilised at a lower floor. The 1.0× T12M buy pace sounds neutral, but T12M itself is anchored in the post-collapse 2025 baseline, masking the true structural disengagement relative to 2024. Root cause is most likely a combination of inventory overhang from the 2024 overbuy and ongoing cash-flow pressure rather than a clean channel switch to a competitor — if it were a competitor switch, buy pace would be declining, not flat.

Financial risk is the dominant concern right now. DSO of 104 days against 60-day credit terms means the average invoice is settling ~44 days late. The 63.4% overdue rate, though slightly improved from 70.5% a week ago (a ~SAR 20K paydown), is still critically elevated. The overdue aging profile — SAR 162K in the 0–30d bucket and SAR 16K in 30–60d — suggests selective payment: new invoices are being serviced, old balances are being allowed to age. This is a classic early-stage cash-squeeze pattern, not a transient misalignment.

With peak season (May–September) opening now, the temptation is to push volume and absorb overdue into new invoices. That logic is wrong here. Grade C, IR_Others positioning means this account does not command strategic shelf or market-signal value. Extending credit or running promotional co-investment would deepen AR exposure on an account that is already absorbing 104 days of capital — with no sell-out data to validate downstream velocity. The priority must be AR hygiene before any volume play.
sales.investment_rationale: Elevated DSO (104d on 60d terms) and 63% overdue rate make any new credit or promotional investment a net AR risk, not a growth catalyst — stabilise cash position first.
action: Freeze new shipments until overdue balance drops below 30% of total AR; communicate this as a policy limit, not a punitive measure
Sales visit (this week) to assess true inventory position and sell-out velocity — determine whether the buy-rate stability signals genuine retail turnover or passive depletion of 2024 overstock
Negotiate structured overdue repayment schedule (SAR 30–40K/month over 5 months) tied to the next order release as a conditional unlock ahead of peak season
sales.channel_reason: Two-year sell-in down 73% from 2024 peak, DSO 44 days past credit terms, overdue persistently above 60% — financial stress outweighs stable buy-pace signal.

synthesis.headline: Selective-pay C-grade account running 44 days beyond credit terms — peak season opens now, and every new shipment without collection action deepens an already-impaired AR position with no strategic upside to justify the risk.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Hold. Structural cash-flow stress on a declining, non-strategic account demands immediate collection enforcement before any peak-season volume discussion.

summary_3lines: Al-Asma is running 104-day DSO against 60-day terms with 63.4% OVD — nearly double its own baseline — using a confirmed selective-pay tactic to keep supply flowing while deferring ageing balances.

This is not a seasonal blip: T12M DSO equals YTD DSO exactly, sell-in has contracted 73% from the 2024 peak, and the six-day OVD plateau post-Apr 30 confirms payments have stalled — the partial improvement was a one-off, not a trend.

If we enter peak season (May–September) without enforcement, new shipments stack on top of SAR 178K already overdue; a Grade C, IR_Others account with no sell-out visibility and declining buy-power can cascade into a SAR 500K+ AR write-risk by August with limited recourse.
synthesis.decision_required: Issue a formal shipment hold and OVD payment notice this week — before any peak-season order is processed — and set a concrete OVD clearance threshold as the only unlock condition.

options.consequence: Short-term order loss and account friction, but AR exposure is capped at SAR 282K; a signed repayment schedule (SAR 35–40K/month) clears overdue by September and creates a conditional re-engagement pathway for the back half of peak season with prepayment terms.

options.consequence: Peak-season revenue recognised on paper, but AR pyramids to SAR 450–550K by August on a cash-stressed C-grade account; if liquidity deteriorates mid-season, LG holds unsecured exposure on a customer with no strategic leverage and no sell-out data to demonstrate downstream health.
