Decision gate: if OVD ratio has dropped below 40% OR a signed repayment schedule with SAR 35–40K/month is executed, release limited peak-season orders under 50% prepayment + net-30 structure; otherwise, maintain full hold and escalate to credit committee

finance.headline: Payment completely frozen for 6+ days with 66.9% overdue all aged 60d+, YTD DSO at 434 days — 4.8x the 90-day credit term — immediate credit suspension warranted.
finance.analysis:
AR health is severely impaired. The entire overdue balance of SAR 640,653 sits in the 60d+ bucket with zero in the 0–30d or 30–60d buckets, meaning there has been no partial payment or aging movement whatsoever. Six consecutive daily snapshots show a static 66.9% OVD ratio at SAR 0.96M — a clear signal of a payment freeze rather than a slow-pay pattern. YTD DSO of 434 days dwarfs not only the 90-day credit term (4.8x) but even the company's 180-day DPO ceiling (2.4x), placing this account in structural delinquency territory, not a temporary cash flow blip.

Collection risk is compounded by deteriorating commercial momentum. Sell-In has declined three consecutive years — from SAR 1.39M (2024) to SAR 1.14M (2025) to SAR 276K YTD 2026 (annualized ~SAR 662K, a ~42% further decline). The 2026 buy pace is only 0.6x of the T12M average, meaning the account is both paying less and buying less simultaneously. This removes the primary commercial leverage — the pipeline of future orders — that would otherwise incentivize payment. A Grade C account with decelerating volumes and a frozen AR is the highest-risk combination in a receivables portfolio.

On credit policy, there is no scenario that justifies maintaining or increasing the current credit limit. The account has already received SAR 958K in exposure against a 90-day term and has failed to service it. Extending additional credit would increase LG's loss exposure without any evidence of repayment capacity or willingness. The recommended action is immediate credit suspension and a structured collection demand, with any future shipment contingent on (a) full clearance of the 60d+ balance and (b) demonstrated on-time payment over at least two consecutive invoicing cycles.
finance.credit_rationale: YTD DSO of 434 days, 100% of overdue in the 60d+ bucket, a 6-day payment freeze, and decelerating purchase activity collectively indicate the account cannot service its current exposure — further credit extension would only increase loss risk.
action: Issue formal written collection demand for SAR 640,653 (60d+ overdue) with a 7-day payment deadline; escalate to account owner and regional sales director immediately.
Place account on credit hold — block all new order processing until the 60d+ balance is cleared in full and no additional shipments to clear pipeline unless payment is received upfront.
Initiate weekly AR review calls with the account and LG sales rep; if no payment received within 14 days, refer to legal/external collection and flag for potential write-off provisioning.
finance.risk_reason: Complete payment freeze (6 days static), 100% of overdue in 60d+ bucket, YTD DSO 434d — 4.8x credit term — with declining revenue trajectory and Grade C standing.

sales.headline: Account in terminal disengagement — sell-in collapsed 76% YTD vs 2025 run-rate while SAR 640K sits frozen at 60d+ overdue with zero collection movement across 6 consecutive snapshots.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically impaired. Sell-in has deteriorated structurally: 2024→2025 already declined 17%, and 2026 annualised pace (~SAR 690K at 0.6x T12M) implies a further 40% drop vs 2024 peak. The absence of any 0-30d or 30-60d overdue — 100% of overdue sits in the 60d+ bucket — signals this is not a timing mismatch but a chronic non-payment posture. Six consecutive daily snapshots showing identical balance and OVD ratio confirm zero AR movement; the account has effectively stopped both buying and paying.

Root cause is most likely compound: (1) inventory overhang from 2024-2025 over-buying that sell-out velocity in the IR_Others sub-channel cannot absorb, combined with (2) financial distress that has pushed the customer to use LG receivables as working capital. YTD DSO of 434 days — nearly 5x the contractual 90-day credit term — is not a collection lag; it is a structural funding dependency. The customer is operationally solvent enough to place small orders (SAR 276K YTD) but not liquid enough to service past debt, which is a classic pre-default profile.

Investment case is negative on all vectors. No sell-out data is available, but inferred sell-out velocity is low given the inventory-overhang hypothesis and buy deceleration. Any credit increase would deepen LG's exposure to an account already 434 DSO on existing credit. Co-marketing or display investment would be sunk cost without evidence the account can move product. The only value-preserving action is aggressive collection of the SAR 640K 60d+ balance before further deterioration — and a hard credit hold until meaningful repayment is demonstrated.
sales.investment_rationale: YTD DSO 434d with zero AR movement across 6 snapshots signals the account cannot service existing debt — any new investment deepens exposure without recovery prospect.

Without a hard credit hold and formal collection demand issued this week, LG's SAR 958K exposure will continue to age with zero commercial leverage remaining to compel payment — provisioning and write-off risk escalate materially by June.

finance.headline: Effectively a dormant delinquent account — SAR 149,681 is 100% overdue at 60d+, T12M DSO of 297d is 3.3x the credit term, and zero 2026 revenue means no self-healing path exists.
finance.analysis:
AR health is at its worst measurable state: the entire outstanding balance sits in the 60d+ aging bucket with zero in current or near-current buckets, and the OVD rate has held at 100% without a single basis-point of movement for at least six consecutive days. This is not a temporary liquidity squeeze — it is structural non-payment. The T12M DSO of 297 days dwarfs both the 90-day credit term and the 180-day DPO ceiling, signaling that collections from this account have consistently required more than 3x the agreed timeline even in the prior twelve months.

The 2026 sell-in of SAR -9,086 (net return/credit note position) confirms the account has generated no new receivable to dilute or mask the overdue pile, and the revenue trend itself is alarming: SAR 229,932 in 2024 → SAR 193,117 in 2025 → effectively zero in 2026. The account is contracting toward dormancy while leaving an unresolved AR tail. There is no organic recovery mechanism — no in-season shipment flow, no payment-against-new-order leverage, and no visible willingness to reduce the balance over six observation days.

On credit policy: extending or even maintaining the current credit limit would be indefensible. With Grade C classification, 100% OVD concentrated entirely in the oldest aging bucket, and a T12M DSO that breaches DPO by 117 days, any incremental credit exposure adds to what is increasingly resembling a bad debt situation. The credit limit must be reduced to SAR 0 (hard block) until the full SAR 149,681 is settled, with reinstatement conditional on a minimum 6-month clean payment track record.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of the balance is 60d+ overdue, T12M DSO of 297d is 3.3x the credit term, and zero 2026 revenue eliminates any self-correction path — further exposure cannot be justified.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all pending and future orders until SAR 149,681 is fully cleared — no exceptions for peak-season orders.
Escalate to formal collections within 48 hours: issue a written demand letter with a 15-day ultimatum, and engage legal counsel to assess options if no payment plan is agreed by deadline.

Engage the sales team today to make direct contact with the account owner — negotiate a structured repayment schedule (e.g., 50% within 30 days, balance within 60 days) as a condition for any future credit reinstatement.
finance.risk_reason: Entire AR balance is 60d+ overdue at 100% OVD with no 2026 revenue activity and T12M DSO of 297d — bad debt provisioning should be considered if no payment response within 30 days.

sales.headline: Account has gone effectively dark in 2026 — negative sell-in (returns), 100% overdue balance stuck for 6+ consecutive days, and T12M DSO of 297 days signals financial distress or silent exit.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is in full collapse. Sell-in declined 16% YoY from 2024 to 2025, then turned negative in 2026 (SAR -9,086), meaning the account has issued net returns with zero new orders to date. This is not a slow buyer — it is a non-buyer. The 2026 negative figure strongly suggests unsold 2025 stock being returned, which implies the account's own sell-out has stalled, inventory overhang accumulated, and new procurement has been frozen. With peak season starting now (May), the absence of any 2026 buy signal is structurally damning.

On the financial side, every SAR owed is overdue by 60d+ with zero movement across six consecutive daily snapshots. T12M DSO of 297 days — nearly 10 months — is not a collection lag issue; it is a solvency or dispute signal. The 90-day credit term was effectively breached long ago. The account's 60d+ overdue balance has been static, which rules out partial repayment or good-faith negotiation in progress. Combined with Grade C classification and Q3 Critical quadrant positioning, this account presents maximum financial risk with zero commercial return in 2026.

Investment rationale is straightforward: there is no channel to invest in. Promotions, display, or co-marketing presuppose an active buying relationship. Credit increase would directly amplify an already-unrecoverable AR position. The only value-preserving action is collection escalation. Any further shipment — even under a new credit structure — would be imprudent without full settlement of the existing SAR 149,681 and documented proof of operational continuity.

finance.headline: Alsudairi shows a clean current AR snapshot, but structural payment behavior — DSO 3x over credit terms and Grade C profile — signals latent collection risk as buy pace accelerates into peak season.
finance.analysis:
AR surface is clean: SAR 184K balance, 0% overdue across all aging buckets, and a steady 6-day OVD trend at 0.0%. However, this clean snapshot likely reflects recency of purchases rather than genuine payment discipline. YTD DSO of 135d against a 60d credit term means the account is paying at 2.25x its contractual timeline — a structural gap, not a temporary blip. T12M DSO of 197d, exceeding even LG's DPO ceiling of 180d, confirms this is the account's baseline behavior. The YTD average OVD rate of 35.4% further validates that the current zero-overdue window will not persist once outstanding invoices age past 60 days.

The sell-in trajectory adds a layer of concern. Revenue collapsed 73% from 2024 (SAR 1.0M) to 2025 (SAR 283K), raising questions about the underlying business health. The 2026 buy pace at 1.5x T12M average is a double-edged signal: it could indicate recovery momentum, or it could reflect opportunistic loading ahead of peak season without corresponding sell-through capacity. For a Grade C account with chronic DSO issues, accelerating purchases without verified downstream liquidity increase exposure risk.

Credit policy should hold firm. The combination of Grade C classification, T12M DSO nearly at DPO ceiling, and a 35.4% historical OVD baseline does not justify expanding credit headroom. The account is currently safe by quadrant definition, but it sits structurally at the edge of acceptable risk parameters. Maintaining current credit limit is appropriate — any expansion would require demonstrated DSO normalization below 90d for at least two consecutive months.

sales.headline: Volume collapsed 73% in 2025 after a strong 2024; 2026 shows early re-engagement at 1.5x pace, but account remains unproven and grade C with structurally elevated DSO.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is in a recovery phase, not a growth phase. The 2024→2025 freefall (SAR 1.04M → SAR 283K, -73%) is the defining signal. Three hypotheses in order of likelihood: (1) inventory overhang — a SAR 1M buy in 2024 likely left significant unsold stock through end-of-season, suppressing 2025 reorders; (2) competitor partial switch — Gree and Midea have been aggressive in IR_Others with lower ASP entry models, and a C-grade dealer may have diversified sourcing; (3) financial fatigue — T12M DSO of 197 days with a 35.4% YTD OVD baseline suggests the account historically stretched payments, which may have led LG sales to throttle shipments or the dealer to self-limit. The current 0% OVD snapshot is a positive datapoint but represents only ~6 days of clean observation after what appears to be a recent payoff.

The 1.5x buy pace vs T12M average in 2026 is the most encouraging signal: it means the dealer is re-engaging ahead of peak season (May–September), likely to replenish ahead of summer demand. YTD SAR 170K through early May could annualize toward SAR 390–420K, roughly 1.4x the 2025 full-year — real but modest recovery, still 60% below 2024 peak. We have no sell-out data, so we cannot confirm whether prior inventory has cleared; the 2026 rebuying implies it probably has.

Investment decision: hold with conditional upgrade trigger. The account does not justify active co-investment today — grade C, small absolute volume, unverified sell-out velocity, and a structural DSO gap (135d YTD vs 60d term). However, if buy pace sustains or accelerates through May–June, a targeted sell-out support offer (installer incentive, small display commitment) at the start of peak could convert recovery momentum into volume capture. The risk of over-investing in a C-grade with history of slow payment outweighs the speculative upside at this stage.

The most probable root cause is inventory overhang: the 2025 purchase of SAR 2.79M likely exceeded the account's sell-out capacity, leaving unsold stock that suppresses 2026 reorder demand. A secondary hypothesis is competitive displacement — Grade C IR_Others accounts sit in the most contestable tier of the IR channel, where Samsung's trade-push has historically been most aggressive. Without sell-out data, these two hypotheses cannot be separated, and that distinction matters for remediation.

The DSO picture adds a structural warning beneath the clean AR surface. YTD DSO of 144d vs T12M DSO of 73d is a near-doubling of effective payment velocity — even though current OVD is 0%. The divergence pattern here is telling: the account is maintaining enough cash discipline to stay technically current (no overdue), but generating new purchases at a fraction of prior pace, which mechanically extends YTD DSO. With credit term at 90d, a YTD DSO of 144d means the account is running 54 days beyond contractual terms on a YTD basis. The YTD avg OVD baseline of 35.4% further confirms this account has a history of stretched payments, making the current 0% OVD a temporary clean window, not a structural shift.

Entering the May–September peak window, the combination of low replenishment pace, DSO elongation, and Grade C status leaves very limited upside case for incremental investment. The single stabilizing factor is the clean AR snapshot (SAR 0 overdue as of May 4), which prevents an exit call. However, unlocking any peak-season volume requires first diagnosing whether the channel is congested with unsold LG inventory or has been lost to a competitor — two scenarios that require opposite responses. Committing promotional or credit resources before that diagnosis would be premature.

Require PDC (post-dated cheques) or advance payment on any incremental order that would push the balance above SAR 4.5M during peak season
Monitor weekly OVD snapshots closely — if the 30-60d bucket re-emerges and is not cleared within 10 days, initiate formal credit watch given the T12M DSO 311d baseline
finance.risk_reason: Zero OVD today is positive, but YTD DSO 135d vs 90d terms, T12M DSO 311d history, and a 2.3x buy-pace-driven balance surge create latent collection risk that peaks as May–September buying accelerates.

sales.headline: Contractor account in explosive re-acceleration — 2026 buy pace at 3.5x full-year 2025 heading into peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is strongly positive. After a 26% Sell-In contraction in 2025 (SAR 1.08M vs SAR 1.45M in 2024), the account has surged to SAR 3.78M already by early May 2026 — before the Saudi peak season (May–Sep) has even peaked. The 2.3x buy pace vs T12M average is not noise; for a contractor-type IR account, this magnitude of acceleration almost certainly signals a secured project pipeline (Vision 2030 construction, government/commercial HVAC fitout). The 2025 dip warrants a hypothesis: either inventory overhang from an over-bought 2024, or a temporary competitor trial (Gree/Midea pricing pressure on contractor segments is real). The 2026 return at scale suggests LG won the project spec.

Payment posture has meaningfully improved in 2026. T12M DSO of 311d is alarming in isolation, but YTD 2026 DSO of 135d signals a structural shift — likely a combination of larger, faster project-linked payments and deliberate account rehabilitation. The May 4 event (SAR 4.38M → SAR 4.08M balance, OVD cleared to 0%) confirms active payment intent. Critical caveat: YTD DSO improvement is partly a denominator effect — 3.5x higher sell-in compresses DSO arithmetically. True payment velocity must be validated separately. Historical OVD baseline of 35.4% remains a watching brief.

Investment case is compelling but must be structured. This account is entering peak season with momentum, a cleared AR, and project-driven demand. The risk is the same feature that makes it attractive: large, lumpy contractor orders create concentrated AR exposure. A credit increase should be tied to project confirmation (PO or contract visibility), not issued as a blanket uplift. Installer/contractor support programs (co-op rebates, priority delivery SLA) would reinforce LG specification on active projects and reduce Samsung's ability to substitute.
sales.investment_rationale: Peak-season momentum at 3.5x 2025 levels with cleared overdue warrants credit expansion tied to project-confirmed POs, plus contractor support to lock LG spec on active builds.
action: Validate project pipeline: request 2026 project list from account — scope, timelines, model specs — to distinguish genuine demand from speculative stockpiling
Offer conditional credit increase (e.g. SAR 5.5–6.0M limit) contingent on PO or contract documentation for active projects, to unlock peak-season volume without uncontrolled AR exposure
Assign contractor support package: priority delivery SLA + installer co-op rebate to defend LG spec against Samsung/Gree substitution on in-progress projects
Set monthly AR checkpoint through September — monitor actual payment days against 90d term; escalate if DSO drifts above 150d despite low headline OVD
sales.channel_reason: 3.5x YoY Sell-In surge, near-zero overdue, peak season ahead — momentum is strong and payment behavior has recovered in 2026.

synthesis.headline: A+ contractor in genuine peak-season acceleration (3.5x YoY) with clean AR today — but SAR 4.08M exposure on a T12M DSO-311d payer demands a project-PO gate before any credit expansion, not a blank green light.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Invest (conditional). Project-driven momentum is real, but T12M payment history and a rapidly growing balance require PO documentation before unlocking peak-season credit.

summary_3lines: 2026 buy pace is 3.5x full-year 2025 (SAR 3.78M vs SAR 1.08M), with AR fully cleared as of May 4 — a contractor account in genuine re-acceleration, most likely tied to a secured Vision 2030 project pipeline.

The 2026 improvement is real but unproven: YTD DSO of 135d beats T12M DSO of 311d, yet still runs 50% above the 90-day credit term, and the denominator effect of 3.5x higher sell-in arithmetically compresses DSO without confirming true payment velocity improvement.

If we process peak-season orders without a project-PO gate, AR could breach SAR 6M+ by July with a payer whose base rate is 311d — if payment velocity reverts even partially, the 60d+ bucket populates fast and collection becomes a Q3 crisis.
synthesis.decision_required: Approve or deny a conditional credit increase (SAR 5.5–6.0M ceiling) tied to project PO documentation — this decision must be made before the account's next large order arrives, which given buy pace could be within days.

options.option: Option A — Conditional Credit Expansion (Recommended)
options.consequence: Approve SAR 5.5–6.0M limit contingent on account submitting active project PO or contract documentation. Add PDC requirement on any order pushing balance above SAR 4.5M. Assign contractor support package (priority delivery + co-op rebate) to lock LG spec. Captures peak-season upside, defends against Samsung/Gree substitution, while limiting AR exposure to confirmed project demand.

options.option: Option B — Hold Limit, Case-by-Case PDC
options.consequence: Maintain current implicit limit, require PDC or advance on every incremental order. Protects AR from balloon risk and forces the account to demonstrate sustained sub-120d DSO before any uplift. Risk: account may divert open orders to a competitor with looser credit terms mid-project, and LG loses spec on builds already in progress — a loss that compounds for 2027 reference projects.

timeline.step: Request 2026 project list from account (scope, model specs, delivery timelines, PO or contract docs) — Sales to initiate
Credit decision: approve conditional limit (Option A) or hold (Option B) based on project pipeline documentation received
Hard DSO checkpoint — if YTD DSO has not improved to ≤120d, escalate to formal credit review and suspend new orders pending review regardless of OVD headline

finance.headline: Al Mojel is effectively a collection case — 88.5% overdue with SAR 716K aged 60d+, YTD DSO of 869 days against a 90-day credit term, and commercial activity down 87% vs 2024.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired. SAR 715,990 (83.5% of total balance) sits in the 60d+ bucket with zero in the 30–60d range — confirming this is chronic, entrenched debt rather than a recent delinquency event. The YTD DSO of 869 days represents a 9.7x breach of the 90-day credit term. T12M DSO of 310 days (still 3.4x term) shows the structural nature of the problem: even over the trailing twelve months, this account has never operated within agreed payment parameters. The momentary OVD drop from 96.7% to 84.8% around April 30 reflects a one-time partial payment of approximately SAR 100K — but with no new sell-in to dilute the aging pool, the ratio has already crept back to 88.5% within days.

Collection risk is severe and self-reinforcing. The 2026 buy pace at 0.4x T12M average (SAR 123K YTD vs SAR 1.4M in 2024) eliminates the natural OVD dilution that new invoices would provide, meaning the overdue ratio will continue to deteriorate passively even without new defaults. The 30–60d bucket being completely empty is a warning signal: there are no fresh invoices entering the aging pipeline, which means the account has either been placed on informal hold or has stopped ordering voluntarily — either way, the relationship is commercially stalled while the debt compounds.

Credit policy must be reduced to near-zero immediately. There is no financial or commercial rationale to maintain current exposure of SAR 857,949, let alone expand it. The account grade (C), quadrant (Q3 Critical), and DSO profile are fully aligned in signaling elevated loss probability. Recovery of the existing balance — particularly the SAR 715,990 in the 60d+ tranche — must take absolute priority over any revenue conversation for the 2026 peak season.
finance.credit_rationale: With YTD DSO at 869 days (9.7x credit term), 83.5% of balance aged beyond 60 days, and 2026 buy volume collapsed to 0.4x pace, there is no basis for current exposure — credit limit should be reduced to zero pending meaningful collection progress.
action: Enforce immediate shipment hold: release only upon OVD balance falling below SAR 300K absolute or OVD% below 30%, whichever comes first
Escalate SAR 715,990 60d+ tranche to legal/collections within 7 days — issue formal demand notice and document for potential legal recovery

Engage Al Mojel senior management within 48 hours to negotiate a structured repayment plan: minimum SAR 250K within 30 days, remainder within 60 days, with written commitment as condition for any future order release
finance.risk_reason: 88.5% overdue, SAR 716K aged 60d+, YTD DSO 869d vs 90d term, and near-zero 2026 purchasing signal high probability of permanent credit loss without immediate escalation.

sales.headline: Al Mojel is in commercial freefall — sell-in down 73% from peak, 88.5% of AR overdue with YTD DSO of 869 days signals near-total collection and revenue collapse.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated. Sell-in has declined from SAR 1.40M (2024) → SAR 924K (2025, -34% YoY) → tracking ~SAR 370K annualized in 2026 at the current 0.4x T12M pace — a cumulative 73% three-year drawdown. With no sell-out data, the buy pace deceleration entering the May–September peak window is the most actionable proxy: an account that historically concentrated purchases pre-season is now buying at less than half its own trailing rate. This pattern points to one of three root causes (or a combination): (1) severe inventory overhang from unsold prior-year stock, (2) competitor displacement — Samsung or Hisense capturing shelf and credit terms — or (3) financial distress so deep the account cannot place orders regardless of demand.

The AR data removes ambiguity about financial stress. SAR 759K of the SAR 858K balance is overdue — 88.5% — with SAR 716K sitting in the 60d+ bucket against a 90-day credit term. This is not a timing issue; it is structural collection failure. YTD DSO of 869 days and T12M DSO of 310 days confirm that meaningful cash recovery has been absent for quarters. Critically, the OVD rate moved only from 96.7% (Apr 29) to 88.5% (May 5) — the improvement came from new invoice roll-off, not cash inflows. The account is not paying down; it is standing still while the denominator shifts.

No commercial upside exists to justify incremental LG investment. Grade C, IR_Others channel, Q3 Critical quadrant, and a 0.4x buy pace entering peak season offer zero return probability on credit expansion, promotions, or co-marketing. Every SAR of new credit extended before collection compounds an already impaired receivable. The only viable path is a structured collection recovery plan — repayment schedule, product buyback assessment, and formal credit hold — before any conversation about the 2026 season resumes.
sales.investment_rationale: With 88.5% OVD, YTD DSO of 869 days, and 0.4x buy pace at peak season entry, the account poses unacceptable credit risk with no identifiable commercial upside to offset further exposure.
action: 즉시 신용 보류(credit hold) 발동 — Sales Director + Finance 공동 에스컬레이션, 60d+ SAR 715,990 전액 정산 전까지 신규 오더 금지
5월 중 대면 방문 일정 확보 — 서면 분할 상환 스케줄(최소 3개월 내 완납 목표) 합의 및 서명 징구
Al Mojel 창고·매장 재고 실사 — LG 재고 잔존량 확인 후, 미판매 재고 환수(buyback) 또는 인근 채널 재배분으로 채권 조기 회수 가능성 검토
sales.channel_reason: Sell-in -73% from 2024 peak, buy pace 0.4x T12M entering peak season, and 88.5% AR overdue — channel relationship is effectively inactive.

synthesis.headline: Al Mojel is a collection case, not a sales account — SAR 716K aged 60d+, YTD DSO 869 days against a 90-day term, and 2026 buy pace at 0.4x entering peak season. Every day without a formal repayment demand increases permanent loss probability.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Structural collection failure with zero commercial upside; incremental exposure compounds an already impaired receivable.

summary_3lines: Al Mojel carries SAR 857,949 in AR of which 88.5% is overdue — SAR 715,990 sitting beyond 60 days with a 90-day credit term, YTD DSO of 869 days, signalling chronic non-payment rather than a temporary cash squeeze.

The root cause is compounding: sell-in collapsed 73% from 2024 peak eliminating the natural invoice dilution that would reduce OVD%, the April 30 partial payment of ~SAR 100K reversed within 4 days to 88.5% OVD, and the 0.4x buy pace entering the May–September peak window confirms the commercial relationship is effectively inactive.

Without immediate formal escalation, the SAR 716K 60d+ tranche ages further, legal recovery windows narrow, and the account exits 2026 peak season with zero repayment commitment — at which point write-off becomes the only realistic outcome.
synthesis.decision_required: Does management enforce credit hold + formal legal demand this week, or grant one final 72-hour window for senior-level negotiation before legal escalation — and who owns that meeting?

options.option: Option A — Immediate hard stop: Credit hold effective today + formal legal demand notice for SAR 715,990 within 7 days, no new orders until OVD below SAR 300K or OVD% below 30%
options.consequence: Maximises legal recovery leverage while the debt is still within actionable window; forfeits any residual 2026 peak season revenue from Al Mojel (~SAR 250K annualised upside at best), but stops further exposure accumulation immediately.

options.option: Option B — Final negotiation window: Senior-level meeting within 48 hours to secure written repayment schedule (SAR 250K within 30d, remainder within 60d) with credit hold concurrent; legal escalation triggers automatically if no written commitment by May 9
options.consequence: Preserves a narrow path to structured recovery without immediate legal cost; carries the risk that Al Mojel uses the window to delay without paying, and any new invoice released during negotiation adds to an already impaired pool.

timeline.step: Credit hold enforced (Sales + Finance co-sign) — no new shipments regardless of open orders
Senior management contact with Al Mojel — secure written repayment commitment or trigger legal demand notice for SAR 715,990
Legal/collections escalation if no written commitment received — formal demand letter issued and documentation filed for potential legal recovery

finance.headline: Total AR freeze with 88.6% in 60d+ bucket, near-zero 2026 buying activity, and YTD DSO of 1,260 days — classic pre-default posture requiring immediate credit block.
finance.analysis:
AR health is at maximum stress: SAR 491,208 balance is 100% overdue with no improvement across six consecutive daily snapshots (Apr 29–May 4), indicating zero payment activity. The aging structure is particularly alarming — SAR 435,152 (88.6%) sits in the 60d+ bucket, meaning this is not fresh overdue exposure but deeply aged debt that has gone unaddressed. The 30-60d bucket showing SAR 0 is a structural red flag: it confirms no intermediate payments were made, so the exposure migrated directly from current to severely aged without any partial settlement.

DSO figures compound the severity. YTD DSO of 1,260 days is not a calculation anomaly — it reflects that 2026 purchases (SAR 48,744) are negligible against the outstanding balance, meaning the account has effectively stopped transacting while retaining unpaid exposure. T12M DSO of 201 days already exceeds both the 90-day credit term and the 180-day DPO threshold. The buy pace at 0.2x of T12M average signals a deliberate pullback — historically a leading indicator of payment default or business distress, not a seasonal dip. A Grade C account in Q3-Critical with this profile carries material bad debt risk.

Credit policy must move to immediate decrease and block. There is no data point supporting maintenance of current credit line, let alone expansion. The account is carrying SAR 491,208 in fully overdue exposure with no buying momentum to justify continued open credit. Continued exposure accumulation under current terms would be indefensible. Credit hold should be enacted immediately, with new orders gated behind a minimum payment threshold — recommended at SAR 200,000 (clearing the 60d+ tail toward the 90-day bucket) before any shipment release is considered.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% OVD balance entirely in 60d+ aging with zero payment trajectory and near-cessation of 2026 purchases leaves no basis for maintaining credit exposure — immediate block is warranted.
action: Credit hold: block all new shipments immediately until SAR 200,000+ payment received against 60d+ aged balance
Escalate to legal/collections within 48 hours: issue formal demand letter on SAR 435,152 (60d+ bucket) with 7-day ultimatum before external collection referral

Sales team engagement: confirm if account is operationally active or approaching closure — 0.2x buy pace + full OVD may signal business wind-down; assess recoverability before investing collection effort
finance.risk_reason: 100% OVD, 88.6% in 60d+ aging, YTD DSO 1,260d, six-day flat OVD trend, and near-zero 2026 purchase activity — all indicators converge on imminent bad debt risk.

sales.headline: 완전 연체·매입 급감 — 사실상 거래 동결 상태, 회수 집중 단계
sales.analysis:
채널 현황은 심각한 이탈 국면이다. 2024→2025 Sell-In이 SAR 676K→845K(+25% YoY)로 성장했으나 2026년 YTD는 SAR 49K에 그친다. 현재 5개월 경과 기준 연율 환산 시 약 SAR 146K 수준으로, 전년 대비 -83% 폭락이다. buy pace 0.2x는 정상 매입 의지가 사실상 소멸했음을 의미하며, 5~9월 피크 시즌 직전에 이 수치가 나온다는 점은 더욱 위험하다.

재무 신호는 복합적이다. 잔액 SAR 491K 전액이 연체이고, 그 중 88.6%(SAR 435K)가 60일 초과다. YTD DSO 1,260일은 수학적으로 '2026년 매입액 대비 미수금이 10배'임을 의미하며, 신규 매입은 없는데 과거 채무가 전혀 회수되지 않고 있다는 명백한 증거다. T12M DSO도 201일로 90일 크레딧 텀의 2.2배다. 이 구조에서 신규 재고를 공급하면 기존 미수금에 신규 손실을 쌓는 결과가 된다.

근본 원인 가설: ① 재무 위기(유동성 고갈, 신용 한도 소진) 가능성이 가장 높다. 2025년 대량 매입 후 재고 소진 실패 → 현금 흐름 단절 시나리오다. ② 2025년 고점 매입분이 채널에 재고 과잉으로 남아 있어 추가 매입 의지가 없는 인벤토리 오버행도 복합 작용 가능. ③ Samsung·Gree 등 경쟁사로 거래선 전환 가능성도 배제 불가. 어느 시나리오든 LG 입장에서 추가 신용 공급의 명분은 없다.
sales.investment_rationale: 100% 연체·YTD DSO 1,260일·buy pace 0.2x 삼중 위험 구조에서 추가 투자는 손실 확대로 직결되며, 즉각적인 미수금 회수 집중이 유일한 합리적 선택이다.
action: 즉시 신용 공급 중단 — 신규 오더 수취 전 SAR 491K 전액 정산 선행 조건 확정
담당 영업 + 재무팀 합동 현장 방문으로 60d+ SAR 435K 분할 상환 스케줄 협의 (2주 이내)
법적 조치 임계치(예: 2026-06-30) 설정 후 미이행 시 AR 회수 절차 개시 검토
sales.channel_reason: 잔액 100% 연체 + YTD DSO 1,260일 + buy pace 0.2x — 채널 기능 사실상 정지

synthesis.headline: SAR 491K 전액 연체·6일간 제로 납부·2026 매입 사실상 소멸 — 신용 즉시 차단 후 법적 회수 절차 준비가 유일한 합리적 행동이다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 잔액 100% 연체에 납부 의지·매입 의지 모두 소멸, 추가 신용 공급은 손실 확대일 뿐이다.

summary_3lines: SAR 491K 전액이 연체이며 그 중 88.6%(SAR 435K)는 60일 초과 심층 노출 — 6일 연속 잔액·OVD율 변동 없이 완전 정체 상태다.
2025년 대량 매입(SAR 845K) 후 재고 소진 실패 또는 유동성 고갈로 2026년 매입이 SAR 49K(연율 환산 -83% YoY)까지 급감, buy pace 0.2x는 거래 의지 소멸을 의미하며 5~9월 피크 시즌 진입에도 회복 신호가 전혀 없다.
현재 상태를 방치하면 SAR 435K 60d+ 잔액의 대손 처리가 불가피하고, 법적 조치 없이 시간이 경과할수록 회수 가능성은 기하급수적으로 하락한다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내로 '분할 상환 최후통첩 + 기한 내 미이행 시 법적 회수 개시' 여부를 확정하고 담당 영업·재무·법무 3자 공동 대응 체계를 가동할 것인가.

options.option: Option A — 최후통첩 후 법적 조치 트랙 진입
options.consequence: 즉시 신용 차단 + 7일 이내 공식 수요 레터(SAR 435K 60d+ 기준) 발송 → 2026-06-30 기한 내 미이행 시 외부 AR 회수 에이전시·법적 절차 개시. 회수 비용 발생하나 SAR 435K 손실 확정 방어 가능성 최대화.

options.option: Option B — 현장 방문 통한 분할 상환 스케줄 협의 (2주 유예)
options.consequence: 담당 영업·재무 합동 방문으로 계정 생존 여부 확인 + SAR 200K 선납 조건 분할 스케줄 협의. 계정이 실제 영업 중이고 협의 의지가 있을 경우 Option A보다 회수율 높을 수 있으나, 응답 없을 경우 2주 시간 손실 및 법적 시효 관리 위험.

timeline.step: 즉시 신용 차단 — 신규 오더 전면 보류, 내부 ERP 크레딧 홀드 등록
담당 영업·재무 합동 현장 방문 또는 연락 — 계정 운영 상태 확인 + SAR 200K 선납 + 분할 상환 스케줄 제안 전달
응답 없거나 거부 시 법무팀 이관 + 공식 수요 레터 발송, 법적 회수 절차 개시 기한 확정

finance.headline: Clean AR surface masks a structural DSO overrun — Grade C account cleared overdue to unlock new delivery, now carrying SAR 221K into peak season on a historically slow-pay profile.
finance.analysis:
AR is technically spotless: SAR 221,240 balance, 0% overdue across all aging buckets as of May 5. However, the sequence is the tell — OVD was 64.3% on April 29 on a SAR 50K balance, then zeroed overnight as the balance quadrupled to SAR 221K on April 30. This is a textbook 'pay to unlock next shipment' pattern, not proactive payment discipline. The account settled only when it needed fresh inventory ahead of peak season.

Structural DSO is the core concern. YTD DSO of 134d is 44 days beyond the 90-day credit term — meaning on average, LG is involuntarily extending an additional 44 days of free financing. T12M DSO of 203d is worse still, exceeding the DPO ceiling of 180d that defines the Safe quadrant. The Grade C classification reinforces what the numbers show: this account has a documented history of slow payment. With the 1.5x buy acceleration heading into peak season, the SAR 221K balance will likely hold or grow — any slippage on this tranche will push OVD back above the 35.4% baseline quickly.

On credit policy, maintain is the correct call. Increasing would reward 'clear-and-reload' behavior and extend more exposure to a Grade C account whose Sell-In has contracted sharply (2024: SAR 1.03M → 2025: SAR 299K → 2026 YTD: SAR 207K on an accelerating pace). The 1.5x buy rate likely reflects seasonal restocking normalization, not a fundamental business upgrade that justifies higher limits. Decreasing is premature while AR is clean, but the account is on short leash — one missed payment cycle flips this to yellow or red.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade C with structural DSO 44 days above credit term and a clear-to-buy payment pattern — current exposure is manageable but expanding limits ahead of confirmed payment improvement would be premature.
action: Set 30-day payment watch on SAR 221K balance — flag to AR team if any portion ages past 30d given historical OVD tendency
Hold all new shipment approvals above SAR 50K until the April 30 tranche shows on-schedule settlement within credit term
If account pays the current SAR 221K balance within 90d (by late July), initiate Grade C→B upgrade review with sales team in Q4 as a performance-based incentive
finance.risk_reason: Zero OVD now but structurally high DSO (134d YTD, 203d T12M vs 90d credit term), Grade C, and clear-to-buy payment pattern signals latent collection risk on the newly loaded SAR 221K balance.

sales.headline: Steep 2025 disengagement showing early 2026 reversal signal, but chronic slow-pay history demands caution before credit expansion.
sales.analysis:
Gebal Asir collapsed from SAR 1.03M (2024) to SAR 299K (2025) — a 71% YoY drop that almost certainly reflects an inventory overhang from an over-ambitious 2024 buy. Grade C with T12M DSO of 203d confirms the account has been a chronic slow payer well beyond the 90d credit term. The current Q1 Safe classification is technically accurate today, but it is a snapshot — the underlying DSO structure is structurally stretched. Root cause priority: (1) inventory overhang theory is strongest — 2024 SAR 1M buy into a Grade C IR_Others account that could not sell through at pace, leading to 2025 payment drag and order freeze; (2) possible partial competitor switch during the dry 2025 period when LG relationship was thin.

The April 29→30 move is the most important recent signal: 64.3% OVD on a small SAR 50K residual balance cleared in a single day, and simultaneously a new SAR 221K order was posted — clean slate plus pre-season commitment. The 1.5x buy pace vs T12M average reinforces that Gebal Asir is re-engaging ahead of peak season (May–September), not just clearing a tail balance. YTD SAR 207K through early May already represents ~69% of full-year 2025 in under five months, annualizing toward SAR ~500–600K which would be a meaningful partial recovery.

Investment logic: the account shows genuine momentum re-entry, but the T12M DSO of 203d is the binding constraint. Pushing credit volume now without tightening collection discipline risks repeating the 2024→2025 cycle — big buy, slow liquidation, DSO blowout, order freeze. The right posture is relationship recovery with a collection guardrail: reward the re-engagement with targeted sellout support (installer incentives, display in-store) that drives Sell-Out velocity, but hold any credit line increase until DSO tracks below 150d for two consecutive months.
sales.investment_rationale: Account is re-engaging with real momentum but T12M DSO of 203d signals structural slow-pay risk that must normalize before credit expansion is justified.
action: Assign AR owner to track SAR 221K invoice to payment date — flag immediately if it exceeds 100d; do not release next order until this invoice pays
Offer installer/technician incentive program (not cash rebate) tied to Sell-Out confirmation to improve inventory turn and reduce next overhang risk
Set 90-day review gate: if DSO drops below 150d and YTD Sell-In exceeds SAR 400K by end-July, escalate to Grade B review and consider modest credit line increase for Q3 peak
sales.channel_reason: Re-engagement momentum is real (1.5x buy pace, clean payment, pre-season order) but T12M DSO 203d and 71% 2025 Sell-In collapse prevent a green signal until payment behavior normalizes.

synthesis.headline: Account paid overdue only to unlock a SAR 221K peak-season reload — 'pay-to-buy' discipline, not structural improvement. Hold shipments until this tranche pays on schedule; re-engagement momentum is real but T12M DSO of 203d means the 2024→2025 collapse cycle is still live risk.

verdict.one_line: Risk YELLOW — HOLD. Re-engagement is real but credit must be earned through payment proof, not pre-season urgency.

summary_3lines: Gebal Asir cleared 64.3% overdue overnight on Apr 29→30 and simultaneously loaded SAR 221K — a textbook pay-to-buy unlock, not proactive financial discipline, entering peak season with Grade C and T12M DSO of 203d.

The 71% Sell-In collapse in 2025 most likely traces to a 2024 inventory overhang the account could not liquidate at pace; the 1.5x 2026 buy acceleration reflects seasonal normalization, not a business upgrade — the same structural slow-pay risk remains unresolved.

If no collection guardrail is applied, the SAR 221K balance will age past the 90d credit term (late July) on its historical trajectory, OVD will spike again, and the account will request another tranche — locking LG into a recurring involuntary financing cycle with a Grade C counterparty through the peak season.
synthesis.decision_required: Approve or freeze additional shipments to Gebal Asir above SAR 50K this week — the account will almost certainly place the next order immediately given 1.5x buy pace and peak season timing.

options.option: Option A — Conditional Hold: freeze new orders above SAR 50K until the SAR 221K April 30 invoice pays within 90d (by late July); offer non-cash sellout support (installer incentives) in the interim to maintain relationship and improve inventory turn
options.consequence: Limits LG's involuntary credit exposure to current SAR 221K; signals that Grade C behavior has real consequences; preserves upgrade path if account pays on schedule — but risks account sourcing partial volume from competitor during the hold period

options.option: Option B — Managed Expansion: approve next tranche up to SAR 150K with a hard 60-day payment trigger; assign dedicated AR owner to monitor weekly aging; escalate to credit freeze automatically if any invoice exceeds 100d
options.consequence: Captures peak-season volume momentum and strengthens re-engagement signal, but extends exposure to SAR 370K+ on a Grade C / T12M DSO 203d profile — replicates the conditions that produced the 2024→2025 collapse if sell-through velocity disappoints

timeline.step: Assign AR owner to SAR 221K invoice this week — set 30d / 60d / 90d aging alerts; do not release next order until payment trajectory is confirmed on-track
30-day payment checkpoint: if SAR 221K invoice shows no movement by Jun 4, freeze all new shipments immediately and escalate to Grade C credit review
90-day performance gate: if SAR 221K pays within term AND YTD Sell-In exceeds SAR 400K, initiate Grade C→B upgrade review and authorize modest credit line increase for Q3 peak

finance.headline: Improving-trajectory Grade-B account — OVD well below portfolio baseline and aging clean, but T12M DSO of 201d demands watchful discipline heading into peak-season inventory build.
finance.analysis:
AR health is structurally sound as of 2026-05-05. The entire overdue balance of SAR 315,874 (18.4%) sits in the 0–30d bucket with zero exposure in 30–60d or 60d+, confirming there is no deep-aging delinquency risk. Critically, the current OVD% of 18.4% is sharply below the YTD average overdue baseline of 35.4%, placing this account in the better half of the IR portfolio on payment behavior. The balance jump from SAR 1.23M to SAR 1.72M between April 29–30 reflects a genuine sell-in surge (2026 buy pace at 1.6x T12M average) rather than a masking effect — new invoices diluted the OVD ratio while underlying collections remained on track.

Collection risk is low-to-moderate with one structural flag. YTD DSO of 127d exceeds the stated 90d credit term but remains comfortably within the 180d DPO ceiling, and the improvement versus T12M DSO of 201d suggests real behavioral progress in 2026. The concern is residual: a T12M DSO of 201d means this account historically stretched payments to near-DPO limits, and with peak season (May–September) driving large new sell-in volumes, fresh receivables created in Q2 could age into the 30–60d and 60d+ buckets by July if collection pace slackens. The 2026 buy acceleration at 1.6x amplifies this exposure — higher throughput means larger at-risk balances if DSO reverts to historical norms.

On credit policy, the case for an increase is premature. The positive signals — sub-baseline OVD, clean aging, growing sell-in, improving YTD DSO — are encouraging but represent one partial year of data against a multi-year history of 200d+ DSO. Grade B designation reflects this structural track record. The right posture is to maintain current limits, allow 2026's improved payment pattern to be confirmed across two or three more collection cycles through peak season, and revisit an increase decision in Q3 if DSO holds below 150d and aging stays clean.
finance.credit_rationale: Improving 2026 payment trend and sub-baseline OVD are positive but insufficient to override the Grade B rating and T12M DSO of 201d — extend only after peak-season payment behavior confirms the structural shift.
action: Issue soft collection notice on the SAR 315,874 0–30d overdue immediately — secure a payment commitment date before peak-season orders further inflate the open balance
Set a DSO tripwire at 150d YTD: if breached by end of May, trigger a formal credit review regardless of Q1 quadrant status
Monitor weekly aging migration — if any portion of the current 0–30d overdue rolls into the 30–60d bucket by June 5, escalate to credit committee for limit reassessment
finance.risk_reason: Q1 Safe — OVD at 18.4% is materially below the 35.4% portfolio baseline, all overdue is fresh (0–30d), and 2026 YTD DSO of 127d is on an improving trajectory versus T12M DSO of 201d.

sales.headline: Peak-season pre-load in progress — buy pace 1.6x with improving DSO signals a growing, healthier account worth supporting.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is strengthening on multiple fronts. Sell-In has broken the plateau (2024: 1.53M → 2025: 1.56M → 2026: 1.69M already by May 5), meaning YTD 2026 has already exceeded both prior full-year figures before peak season even begins. The 1.6x buy pace vs T12M confirms this is not noise — the account is deliberately front-loading inventory ahead of the May–September peak. OVD dynamics reinforce the read: the Apr 29→30 snapshot shows balance jumping from 1.23M to 1.72M while overdue SAR held flat (~251K), meaning the OVD% drop from 20.4% to 14.6% is attributable to new clean invoices layered on top, not a payment event. All overdue sits in the 0–30d bucket — no aging tail.

Payment behavior is measurably improving. YTD DSO of 127d vs T12M DSO of 201d is a 74-day compression — well above random variance. Combined with current OVD of 18.4% running nearly half the YTD baseline of 35.4%, this account is either under tighter internal cash discipline in 2026 or has received working capital support externally. Either way, the financial risk profile has de-risked substantially entering peak.

Investment case is constructive. The account is actively stocking LG — the question is whether competitor pressure or limited sell-out support caps sell-through velocity once inventory is on shelf. IR_Others channel typically lacks dedicated LG display or installer relationships; without sellout support, loaded inventory risks sitting and compressing reorder cadence post-peak. A targeted co-investment in display + installer incentive during May–June would maximize pull-through on the inventory already in-channel, protect reorder momentum into July–August, and deepen account loyalty versus Samsung/Gree alternatives.
sales.investment_rationale: Accelerating buy pace and sharply improving DSO/OVD indicate a motivated, de-risking account entering peak — sellout and display investment now maximizes pull-through on loaded inventory before competitive pressure can redirect foot traffic.
action: Propose May–June peak season sell-out support package (installer incentive + POS display) tied to Q2 volume target to convert the stocked inventory into confirmed sell-through
Conduct credit limit review: with YTD DSO at 127d (vs 201d T12M) and OVD well below baseline, current 90d terms may be constraining reorder velocity — assess 15–20% credit line uplift
Schedule joint business review in mid-May to align on peak season SKU mix, reorder triggers, and competitor displacement targets (focus: Samsung comparable mid-tier split models)
sales.channel_reason: Buy pace 1.6x, YTD Sell-In already exceeds full-year 2024/2025, DSO compressing 74 days YoY, OVD at 18.4% vs 35.4% baseline — all signals aligned positive entering peak.

synthesis.headline: Peak-season pre-load is live at 1.6x — invest NOW with a DSO tripwire, or cede shelf space to Samsung/Gree and lose the reorder cycle.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Invest. 2026 DSO compression of 74 days and sub-baseline OVD justify a sell-out package, but T12M DSO of 201d demands a hard May-31 collection checkpoint before any credit expansion.

summary_3lines: Al Saha is actively front-loading peak inventory — YTD Sell-In already exceeds both full-year 2024 and 2025 by May 5, OVD is running at 18.4% against a 35.4% portfolio baseline, and all overdue is fresh 0–30d with zero aging tail.

The 74-day DSO compression (201d T12M → 127d YTD) signals a structural behavior shift in 2026, not noise — but the Grade B rating reflects a multi-year history of near-DPO stretching that one partial year cannot fully override, and the 1.6x buy acceleration means any DSO reversion now creates materially larger AR exposure than in prior years.

If we do nothing: loaded inventory sits in IR_Others without sellout pull-through support, reorder velocity stalls post-peak, July AR aging migrates into 30–60d, and the DSO improvement narrative breaks down — while Samsung and Gree fill the vacuum on installer and display relationships.
synthesis.decision_required: Approve the May–June peak sell-out support package this week — conditioned on securing a payment commitment on the SAR 315,874 overdue by May 9 before activating the package.

options.option: Option A — Invest with guardrails (recommended): Approve sell-out package + POS display tied to a Q2 volume milestone; maintain current credit limit; set a hard DSO tripwire at 150d by May 31
options.consequence: Maximises sell-through velocity on already-loaded inventory, deepens account loyalty versus competitors, and positions LG for sustained reorder momentum into July–August — risk is manageable as long as the collection tripwire is enforced.

options.option: Option B — Hold investment until June 5 collection checkpoint: Wait to confirm the SAR 315,874 0–30d overdue fully clears before committing sell-out resources
options.consequence: Reduces near-term AR risk but almost certainly misses the prime May inventory pull-through window; shelf space and installer relationships default to Samsung/Gree during the highest-traffic weeks of peak season; reorder cadence weakens in H2.

timeline.step: Issue soft collection notice — secure written payment commitment on SAR 315,874 overdue before any new peak orders are shipped
Present May–June sell-out support proposal (installer incentive + POS display) tied to Q2 volume target; confirm activation only after payment commitment received
Conduct joint business review: peak SKU mix, reorder triggers, competitor displacement targets (Samsung mid-tier split)
DSO tripwire checkpoint: if YTD DSO exceeds 150d or any 0–30d overdue migrates to 30–60d, pause credit expansion and escalate to credit committee regardless of Q1 quadrant status

sales.headline: 2024→2025 sell-in collapsed 74% signaling likely competitor shift or severe overhang; 2026 shows tentative re-engagement at 1.2x pace but absolute volume remains a fraction of peak.
sales.analysis:
Ghassan Trading presents a classic post-overhang recovery pattern. The 2024 buy volume of SAR 1.38M was exceptionally high — likely aggressive pre-season loading — followed by a brutal 74% crash to SAR 355K in 2025. This is not a seasonal dip; it is a structural disengagement. Root cause candidates in priority order: (1) severe inventory overhang from 2024 overbuy, units still moving through retail into 2025 suppressing new orders; (2) competitor reallocation — Grade C IR_Others accounts are the first to diversify toward Gree or Midea on margin; (3) reduced floor space or channel exit at key sub-dealers. Sell-out data absence means we cannot confirm actual off-take, but the magnitude of the 2024→2025 drop makes a pure overhang hypothesis compelling.

2026 YTD shows SAR 229,936 through early May. Annualized at current pace, this projects ~SAR 550–600K — above 2025 full year but still less than half of 2024. The 1.2x buy pace vs T12M average is encouraging as a directional signal entering peak season (May–September). DSO also improved sharply: YTD 127d vs T12M 150d, and the OVD drop from 11.5% on May 2 to 2.1% on May 3 confirms a timely payment was made — payment behavior is disciplined, not distressed. The account is not a credit risk; it is a volume risk.

Investment thesis is weak at this stage. The account is Grade C, channel IR_Others (lowest strategic priority tier), and is operating at <20% of its 2024 volume. Recovery trajectory is positive but unproven — one quarter does not confirm a trend reversal. Committing promotional budget or credit line expansion before the peak-season buy pattern clarifies in June–July would be premature. The rational play is to monitor May–June order flow: if buy pace sustains or accelerates through Ramadan-to-summer transition, a targeted sellout support conversation is warranted in July.
sales.investment_rationale: Recovery signals are too early-stage and volume too low relative to 2024 peak to justify resource allocation ahead of confirmed peak-season momentum.
action: Monitor May–June order intake weekly; flag if 2026 cumulative pace falls below 2025 run-rate — that would signal full exit, not recovery
Schedule a June field visit to assess floor presence, stock levels, and competitor share — key to diagnosing whether 2024→2025 drop was overhang or competitor switch
If buy pace sustains at 1.2x or higher through July, escalate to targeted sellout support (installer incentive or display refresh) as a Q3 activation
sales.channel_reason: Tentative recovery in 2026 buy pace (1.2x) and clean AR, but sell-in still 57% below 2024 peak with no confirmed structural turnaround yet.

synthesis.headline: AR is clean and DSO is improving — this is not a credit emergency. The defining question is whether the 74% sell-in collapse in 2025 reflects inventory overhang now clearing, or a permanent competitor switch. Peak season (May–July) is the only diagnostic that answers it.

verdict.one_line: Risk GREEN — HOLD. Payment behavior is disciplined and AR healthy, but annualised 2026 volume still lands at less than half of 2024 peak — premature investment before June–July buy pattern confirms structural recovery.

summary_3lines: Ghassan paid down overdue on May 3 (OVD 11.5% → 2.1%), AR balance is SAR 234K with only SAR 4.9K aged 0–30d, and DSO is trending down from T12M 150d to YTD 127d — no collection action required.

The 74% sell-in crash from 2024 (SAR 1.38M) to 2025 (SAR 355K) is structural, not seasonal; 2026 is tracking at 1.2x buy pace vs T12M but annualised projection of SAR 550–600K remains a fraction of peak — root cause (overhang vs. competitor shift) is still undiagnosed.

Without a June field touchpoint, we risk either missing a narrow window to recapture floor space before a competitor locks in seasonal reorders, or allocating Q3 sellout budget to an account quietly trending toward full exit.
synthesis.decision_required: Approve or defer a June field visit to diagnose root cause of the 2024→2025 volume collapse before any peak-season resource commitment is made.

options.option: Option A — Active Monitoring + June Field Diagnostic
options.consequence: Minimal cost (one field rep visit in June); yields floor presence data, stock depth, and competitor brand visibility. If buy pace sustains ≥1.2x through July, provides evidence base to activate targeted Q3 support (installer incentive or display refresh) with confidence. Preserves optionality on both upside and downside.

options.option: Option B — Passive Watch Only Through H2 Review
options.consequence: Zero incremental cost in H1; but if the 2025 drop was a competitor switch, the window to recapture seasonal shelf position closes in June before competitors lock summer reorders. Recovery opportunity is lost without visibility, and we will not know until Q4 data confirms exit.

timeline.step: Track weekly order intake May–June vs. 2025 run-rate; trigger escalation alert if 2026 cumulative pace falls below 2025 trajectory
Conduct field visit: assess floor presence, on-hand inventory depth, and competitor brand display share at key sub-dealers
Gate decision: if cumulative buy pace ≥1.2x through July, activate Q3 sellout support; if pace decelerates or stalls, reclassify as exit-track and reduce credit exposure proactively

finance.headline: Zero overdue and clean payment history, but 2026 buy pace collapsed to 0.3x — healthy AR masking a disengaging account entering peak season.
finance.analysis:
AR health is technically clean: SAR 137,856 outstanding with zero overdue across all aging buckets, and OVD% has held at 0.0% for the last six consecutive snapshot days. No immediate collection concern. The YTD DSO of 142d appears alarming versus the 60d credit term, but this is a mathematical artifact — the current AR balance (SAR 137.9K) exceeds YTD 2026 sell-in (SAR 121.5K), meaning the balance largely represents late-2025 carry-over invoices. T12M DSO of 43d is the more representative signal, showing this account historically settles well within terms.

The real story is not collection risk but commercial disengagement. Sell-in has declined from SAR 2.32M (2024) → SAR 2.09M (2025) → SAR 121.5K YTD 2026 — a trajectory that, annualized, would put full-year 2026 at roughly SAR 290-350K, a 65-83% revenue collapse. The 0.3x buy-pace multiplier vs T12M average confirms severe deceleration. This is a Grade C IR_Others account heading into the May-September peak window with almost no stock loading — a structural red flag beyond any AR metric.

Credit policy recommendation is to maintain current limit without expansion. Increasing credit would serve no purpose: the constraint is not credit capacity but commercial commitment. The account is not buying at scale, and extending more limit to a C-grade account with declining trajectory adds exposure without unlocking volume. However, decreasing would be premature given zero overdue and clean payment behavior — punishing a paying account before root-cause is diagnosed risks accelerating disengagement.
finance.credit_rationale: Clean payment behavior warrants no tightening, but severe 2026 volume collapse and Grade C standing give no basis for credit expansion until commercial re-engagement is confirmed.
action: Conduct urgent commercial check-in: why has 2026 purchasing dropped to 0.3x pace entering peak season — competitor shift, liquidity strain, or product-mix mismatch?
Monitor YTD DSO weekly — if 2026 sell-in remains stagnant and AR balance does not decline, DSO will continue inflating; set alert threshold at 160d
Hold current credit limit through June; if peak-season orders do not materialize by end of May, initiate formal downgrade review and consider reducing exposure ahead of post-season slowdown
finance.risk_reason: Zero overdue and stable OVD trend make collection risk low today, but 2026 commercial deceleration warrants active monitoring rather than passive comfort.

sales.headline: Severe buy deceleration into peak season signals channel disengagement — root cause must be diagnosed before SAR 2M+ historical volume is lost permanently.
sales.analysis:
Basel Fahd's sell-in has collapsed from SAR 2.32M (2024) → SAR 2.09M (2025) → SAR 121K YTD 2026, with a buy pace of 0.3x T12M average. Critically, this collapse is occurring at the exact moment the account should be loading peak-season inventory (May–September). A healthy IR account would be ramping purchases now; the absence of buying suggests either severe inventory overhang from prior years, a quiet competitor switch to Samsung or Chinese brands, or undisclosed financial constraints suppressing orders despite clean AR.

The AR picture is superficially healthy — SAR 137K balance, 0% overdue across all aging buckets — but the YTD DSO of 142d against a 60d credit term is a latent red flag. T12M DSO of 43d shows historically fast payment, meaning the elevated YTD figure likely reflects a period of slow collections earlier in 2026 that has since resolved. The account is not in crisis today, but the 35.4% YTD average overdue baseline confirms this account has historically cycled through overdue episodes before recovering.

The combination of C-grade classification, 87% annualized sell-in decline, and zero peak-season loading as of May 6 points to structural disengagement rather than a temporary pause. Without a direct channel conversation to surface the root cause, any credit or promotional investment risks being thrown into a relationship that has already pivoted away. Hold and diagnose first.
sales.investment_rationale: Account shows no momentum entering peak season — invest only after confirming sell-through status and ruling out competitor switch.
action: Dispatch sales rep within 7 days for an in-store inventory audit — quantify LG stock on hand vs. Samsung/competitor brands to determine if overhang or competitor switch is the driver
Ask account directly: peak-season order plan for May–August? If they have no plan, treat as exit signal and reallocate credit line to higher-velocity IR accounts
Flag YTD DSO (142d vs. 60d term) to finance for close monitoring — if next invoice goes overdue, escalate to collection_focus and freeze additional credit exposure
sales.channel_reason: AR clean but 0.3x buy pace with zero peak-season loading as of May 6 — disengagement risk is high.

synthesis.headline: AR is clean but the account has effectively stopped buying into peak season — this is a silent exit signal, not a healthy pause.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. Zero overdue masks an 87% annualized sell-in collapse entering the May–September peak window; root cause unknown, resources withheld until diagnosed.

summary_3lines: Basel Fahd's AR is technically clean (SAR 137.9K, 0% overdue, T12M DSO 43d), but 2026 sell-in of SAR 121.5K is on pace for SAR 290–350K full-year — an 83% collapse from SAR 2.09M in 2025.

The account is not loading any peak-season inventory as of May 6, which is structurally abnormal for a healthy IR account; likely causes are competitor switch (Samsung/Chinese brands), inventory overhang from prior years, or undisclosed liquidity strain not yet visible in AR.

If we take no action through May, we lose the peak-season window entirely — and a C-grade account that books nothing in May–June typically completes its quiet exit before we formally diagnose the problem.
synthesis.decision_required: Dispatch a field rep for an in-store inventory audit this week — determine whether Basel Fahd still intends to carry LG or has already pivoted to a competitor, then decide invest vs. exit before the peak window closes.

options.option: Option A — Engage now, diagnose and recover
options.consequence: Rep visits within 7 days; if overhang is the cause, offer sell-through support or restock incentive to restart ordering cycle before end of May. If competitor switch is confirmed, cut exposure cleanly and reallocate credit line to higher-velocity IR accounts. Best-case: recover SAR 500K–1M peak-season volume. Worst-case: confirm exit and lose nothing additional.

options.option: Option B — Passive monitoring, no field intervention
options.consequence: Account drifts through peak season with zero orders; SAR 2M+ historical volume relationship terminates quietly. If an invoice goes overdue on the SAR 137.9K balance during stagnation, we react too late. Grade C account with declining trajectory becomes effectively dead weight by September.

timeline.step: Field rep in-store visit — LG vs. competitor stock audit + direct order intent conversation
Management decision: recover or exit — based on audit findings, commit to either a peak-season restock offer or credit reallocation to active accounts
Credit review checkpoint — if no orders materialize by end of May, initiate formal downgrade and reduce exposure ahead of post-season slowdown

finance.headline: Abdul Ahad Mohammed is in deep financial distress — YTD DSO of 566 days against a 90-day credit term, with SAR 499K fully aged beyond 60 days and buy pace collapsing to 0.5x.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired. The entire overdue balance of SAR 499,093 sits in the 60d+ bucket with zero in 0–30d or 30–60d — meaning there is no fresh delinquency; this is entrenched, aged debt. The Apr-29 snapshot showing 100% OVD followed by a partial payment reducing it to 77.2% suggests the customer is capable of sporadic partial settlements but unwilling or unable to clear the principal. The subsequent marginal drift to 74.6% as of May-3 confirms no structural improvement — the core 60d+ block remains untouched.

The DSO signal is the most damning indicator. YTD DSO of 566 days is 6.3x the contractual credit term of 90 days and 3.1x LG's own DPO of 180 days. Even the T12M DSO of 253 days — which smooths out short-term spikes — is nearly 3x the credit term. This is not a temporary liquidity squeeze; it is a systemic collection failure. Compounding this, 2026 Sell-In of SAR 147,786 YTD represents a near-total withdrawal from LG supply, down from SAR 2,997,593 in 2024 — an 83% two-year collapse. The account is Grade C in Q3-Critical quadrant, and the buy pace at 0.5x T12M average confirms continued deceleration into peak season (May–September), which is precisely when active accounts should be accelerating.

On credit policy, the case for a decrease is unambiguous. No commercial rationale exists for maintaining current exposure when SAR 499K is unrecoverable on a normal timeline, the customer is not actively buying, and both DSO metrics breach all policy thresholds. Increasing the limit would directly amplify irrecoverable exposure. The recommended posture is to reduce the credit limit to zero for new orders — effectively mandating cash-before-delivery (CBD) — until the 60d+ overdue is fully resolved. This is not a punitive measure but the only defensible financial control given current risk indicators.
finance.credit_rationale: YTD DSO of 566 days, 100% of overdue aged beyond 60 days, and an 83% two-year revenue collapse leave no basis for extending further unsecured credit.
action: Immediately freeze new shipments and enforce CBD/prepayment-only terms until SAR 499,093 overdue is cleared in full
Issue formal overdue notice (legal-grade) for the SAR 499,093 60d+ balance and escalate to senior management contact at Abdul Ahad Mohammed within 5 business days
Negotiate a structured repayment schedule (e.g., 4 equal monthly installments) with a written commitment, and set the credit limit to SAR 0 pending completion
finance.risk_reason: 74.6% OVD with 100% of overdue in 60d+ bucket, DSO at 566 days vs 90-day term, and collapsing buy activity entering peak season.

sales.headline: 3년 연속 구매 급감·DSO 566일·74.6% 연체 — 채널 기능 사실상 상실, 회수 최우선 포지션
sales.analysis:
채널 헬스가 전방위 붕괴 중이다. Sell-In이 2024 SAR 3.0M → 2025 SAR 0.82M(-73%) → 2026 YTD SAR 0.15M으로 가파르게 하락하고 있으며, 2026 구매 페이스가 T12M 평균 대비 0.5x로 더욱 둔화되고 있다. 피크 시즌(5~9월) 직전임에도 선행 구매 신호가 전혀 없다는 점은 단순 계절 조정이 아닌 구조적 이탈을 시사한다. 경쟁사 전환 가능성도 배제할 수 없으나, 재무 지표상 구매 여력 자체가 소진된 상태로 보는 것이 더 정확한 진단이다.

재무 신호는 채널 신호보다 더 심각하다. YTD DSO 566일, T12M DSO 253일 모두 정상 신용 거래 기준(90일 Credit Term)을 수백 일 초과한다. 잔액 SAR 669K 중 74.6%(SAR 499K)가 60일 초과 연체 단일 버킷에 집중되어 있다는 점은 단순 지연이 아닌 유동성 위기 징후다. 4월 29일 연체율 100%에서 부분 입금 후 74.6%로 소폭 개선됐으나, 개선 속도가 매우 느리고 구조적 회수가 아닌 소규모 단발 입금으로 보인다.

투자 관점에서 이 계정에 프로모션·공동 마케팅·신용 확대를 집행하는 것은 손실 심화로 직결된다. 현재 잔존 거래 가치(2026 YTD SAR 147K)보다 미회수 위험(SAR 499K 연체)이 3배 이상 크다. 추가 신용 노출은 곧 추가 손실이다. 회수 완결 전 모든 신규 집행을 차단하고 collection_focus 단일 액션으로 집중하는 것이 유일한 합리적 포지션이다.
sales.investment_rationale: 연체 SAR 499K(60d+) 미회수 상태에서 추가 투자는 손실 심화 — 회수 완결 및 COD 전환 확인 전까지 신규 신용 노출 전면 차단이 우선이다.
action: 즉시 Credit Hold 발동: SAR 499K 60d+ 연체 전액 해소 전까지 신규 선적 전면 중단
이번 주 내 계정 재무 책임자와 긴급 미팅 — 월별 분할 상환 계획 문서화 및 서면 약정 요청
30일 내 약정 미이행 시: 법적 회수 절차 검토 + 등급 D 하향 + 향후 COD(현금선불) 조건 전환
sales.channel_reason: 3년 연속 구매 급감(-95% vs 2024) + DSO 566일 + 60d+ 연체 74.6% — 채널 기능 사실상 상실

synthesis.headline: Abdul Ahad Mohammed는 채널 기능을 사실상 상실한 계정 — SAR 499K 60d+ 연체 회수가 이번 주 최우선이며, 회수 전 신규 신용 노출은 전면 차단해야 한다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 2년간 -95% Sell-In 붕괴 + DSO 566일 + 74.6% 고령 연체로 채널 가치 소멸, 추가 투자는 손실 심화.

summary_3lines: SAR 499K 전액이 60일 초과 연체 단일 버킷에 고착, YTD DSO 566일로 신용 약정(90일) 대비 6배 초과 — 재무 통제 완전 이탈 상태.
2024 SAR 3.0M → 2025 SAR 0.82M → 2026 YTD SAR 0.15M, 피크 시즌 직전임에도 구매 페이스 0.5x 추가 둔화 — 구매 여력 자체가 소진된 구조적 이탈이며 단순 계절 조정이 아님.
이번 주 Credit Hold 미발동 시: 피크 시즌(5~9월) 동안 신규 출하가 발생할 경우 기존 SAR 499K에 추가 손실이 누적되고, 회수 협상력은 시간이 갈수록 급격히 약화된다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 Credit Hold 공식 발동 + 계정 재무 책임자 긴급 면담 일정 확정 — 구두 경고가 아닌 서면 분할 상환 약정 요청까지 완료할 것인가.

options.option: Option A — 즉시 Credit Hold + 서면 상환 약정 요구
options.consequence: 신규 손실 차단, 협상 레버리지 확보. 단, 고객이 약정 거부 시 법적 회수 절차로 전환 필요 — 관계 단절 가능성 있으나 재무 손실 방어.

options.option: Option B — 현행 유지 (추가 관찰)
options.consequence: 피크 시즌 출하 재개 시 미회수 채권 SAR 499K 위에 신규 채권 누적, DSO 추가 악화. 회수 협상력 소멸 후 법적 절차만 남는 최악 시나리오.

timeline.step: Credit Hold 공식 발동 — 신규 선적 전면 중단 (CBD/선불 조건 전환)
계정 재무 책임자 긴급 면담 + 월별 분할 상환 계획 서면 약정 요청 (4회 균등 분할 또는 협의)
약정 미이행 또는 면담 거부 시: 법적 회수 절차 개시 검토 + 등급 D 하향 + 향후 전 거래 COD 조건 영구 전환

finance.headline: Abu Yaser is a de-facto non-performing account: SAR 777K fully overdue 60d+, zero 2026 revenue, T12M DSO 294 days — collection is at a critical inflection point entering peak season.
finance.analysis:
AR health is as distressed as it gets without formal default. The entire SAR 777,710 balance sits in the 60d+ bucket — there is no current or 30-60d exposure because there has been zero 2026 sell-in. T12M DSO of 294 days is 3.3× the stated credit term (90d) and 1.6× LG's internal DPO ceiling (180d), meaning this account has been structurally non-compliant for well over a year. The OVD trend — locked at 100% across all six available daily snapshots — shows no directional improvement, no partial payment signal, and no activity that would suggest the account is engaging with its obligation.

The zero 2026 revenue is the most telling data point. It strongly implies LG has already informally stopped shipping to this account — but without a formal credit hold on record, the exposure remains open and unresolved. Sell-in has also been declining secularly: SAR 1.34M (2024) → SAR 0.96M (2025) → SAR 0 (2026 YTD), which suggests either the account's business capacity is shrinking or the relationship has deteriorated. Grade C classification is consistent with this profile. With peak season (May–September) beginning now, the window to resolve this before year-end write-off consideration is narrow.

On credit policy, the recommendation is unambiguous: decrease (effectively suspend) the credit limit to SAR 0 and institute a formal shipment hold. There is no scenario in the current data set that justifies maintaining or expanding credit. The account must demonstrate full or agreed partial repayment of the SAR 777,710 overdue before any new credit line can be reconsidered. Even then, reinstatement should be conditional on a formal payment agreement, a restructured credit term (tightened from 90d to 30-45d), and a reduced initial limit with performance-based step-up.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of balance is 60d+ overdue with zero 2026 revenue and T12M DSO at 294 days — extending any credit is indefensible until the existing obligation is settled.
action: Issue formal credit hold immediately: block all open orders and new shipments pending full collection review
Escalate to Country Sales Director + Collections team for direct account visit or call within 48 hours — obtain written payment commitment or repayment schedule before end of week
If no payment commitment is received within 10 business days, engage legal/external collection agency and flag for potential bad-debt provisioning in Q2 books
finance.risk_reason: SAR 777K fully in 60d+ bucket, T12M DSO 294d (3.3× credit term), zero 2026 activity, and flat OVD trend signal collection failure rather than temporary delay.

sales.headline: Abu Yaser has effectively gone dark on LG — zero 2026 purchases entering peak season, SAR 778K fully overdue 60d+, and a T12M DSO of 294 days signal complete financial and commercial disengagement.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically compromised. Sell-In declined 28.6% YoY (SAR 1.34M → SAR 0.96M in 2025), then collapsed entirely in 2026. Entering May — the first month of Saudi AC peak season — with zero new orders is not a seasonal dip; it is de facto account suspension. The account has no credit headroom to order even if willing, as the SAR 777,710 balance is 100% overdue with the bulk aged past 60 days. T12M DSO of 294 days means LG has historically waited nearly 10 months to collect from this account — far beyond the 90-day credit term.

Root cause assessment (5 Whys): Why zero 2026 orders? → Account is credit-blocked. Why is it credit-blocked? → 100% OVD, no payments received in 2026. Why no payments? → Likely severe liquidity stress at the dealer level, compounded by possible unsold 2025 inventory overhang. Why unsold inventory? → Grade C IR_Others dealers typically lack the display depth and installer network to compete against Samsung-backed MT channels during peak season, leading to stock that ages past payment terms. Why deteriorating year-on-year? → Structural weakness, not a one-cycle anomaly — the decline was already visible in 2025 before the full break in 2026.

Investment case is absent. There is no channel momentum to amplify, no sell-out velocity to accelerate, and no financial capacity at the account level to absorb additional stock or co-investment. Extending credit or deploying sell-out support into this account would be capital destruction. The sole productive action for LG is systematic AR recovery before peak season closes and the account's liquidity position further deteriorates.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue, 100% AR overdue past 60 days, and T12M DSO of 294 days leave no commercial or financial basis for any investment — collection is the only value-preserving action.
action: Escalate SAR 777,710 OVD to formal collection — issue written demand with 15-day cure deadline before referring to legal/credit agency
Freeze all open order processing and reject any new PO until OVD is cleared to zero; communicate block in writing to the account

Assign field AR visit this week (pre-peak window closing) to assess physical inventory levels — if 2025 LG stock visible, explore buyback or reallocation to active IR accounts as partial debt settlement
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 Sell-In entering peak season + 100% AR overdue 60d+ + T12M DSO 294d = complete commercial and financial breakdown.

synthesis.headline: Abu Yaser is effectively a non-performing debtor, not a trading account — SAR 777K fully overdue 60d+, zero 2026 revenue entering peak season, and 294-day DSO demand immediate formal collection action, not commercial engagement.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Structural commercial breakdown confirmed by both Finance and Sales; the only value-preserving action is systematic AR recovery before the peak-season window closes.

summary_3lines: Abu Yaser has not placed a single order in 2026 and carries SAR 777,710 — 100% aged beyond 60 days — with no payment signal across six consecutive daily snapshots entering Saudi peak season.

Decline is structural, not cyclical: Sell-In dropped 28.6% in 2025 then collapsed to zero in 2026, T12M DSO stands at 294 days (3.3× credit term), and Grade C IR_Others positioning leaves the account unable to compete against MT-channel rivals during peak demand.

Inaction through May locks LG into a Q2 bad-debt provision scenario — peak season cash flows will not reach a distressed Grade C dealer, making post-season recovery materially harder and legal escalation costs higher.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize formal credit hold and issue written collection demand this week — decide whether to pursue direct AR recovery internally or immediately engage external legal/collection agency with a 15-day cure deadline.

options.option: Option A — Formal internal collection with field visit this week
options.consequence: Written demand issued, field rep assesses physical LG inventory on-site for potential buyback/reallocation as partial debt settlement; if no commitment by May 20, escalate to legal. Preserves relationship optionality if account settles, but requires sales team bandwidth during peak season.

options.option: Option B — Immediate legal/external collection agency referral
options.consequence: Removes peak-season burden from sales team, signals zero tolerance to broader IR channel, accelerates potential bad-debt provisioning timeline; forfeits any residual commercial relationship and may trigger counter-dispute if account holds 2025 unsold LG inventory.

timeline.step: Issue formal credit hold — block all open orders and new POs; communicate in writing to account
Field AR visit — assess physical 2025 LG inventory; obtain written payment commitment or restructured repayment schedule; explore buyback as partial settlement
If no payment commitment received, refer SAR 777,710 to legal/external collection agency and flag for Q2 bad-debt provisioning

finance.headline: A+ IR account structurally running 159d DSO against 90d terms — the May 1 OVD spike to 40.9% is a silent batch maturity event, not a one-off miss, and the SAR 1.34M sitting in 0-30d OVD is the next wave.
finance.analysis:
Three risk layers stack here. First, the DSO problem is structural: YTD 159d and T12M 167d confirm the account has been paying at ~1.8x credit terms for at least 12 months. The YTD baseline OVD of 35.4% means overdue is the operating norm, not an exception — the current 40.9% is above even that elevated baseline.
