Second, the May 1 OVD jump (13.3% → 40.9% with balance unchanged at SAR 4.32M) is a reclassification event, not a new default: a cohort of invoices crossed their 90-day mark simultaneously, most likely tied to orders placed in late January. This pattern — current balance jumps on new shipment, then OVD spikes 30-60 days later when prior invoices mature — is a textbook selective-payment cycle. The SAR 1.34M in the 0-30d OVD bucket is already staged for the next spike; if not collected by end-May, OVD ratio will push toward 55-60% of balance entering peak season. Third, the 60d+ bucket at SAR 246,689 (5.7% of balance) is today's most time-sensitive exposure — small enough to be dismissible, large enough to signal the tail of the aging curve is beginning to form. On the positive side: 2026 buy pace is 1.1x vs T12M, the account is commercially active and on trajectory to match prior-year sell-in run rates, and the A+ grade reflects a long, volume-weighted relationship.

For credit policy: increasing the limit would amplify SAR exposure at a time when existing receivables are already aged well beyond terms, with no evidence that capacity — rather than payment discipline — is the constraint on volume. Decreasing mid-peak-season would rupture a material IR channel relationship without proof of financial distress (the account appears cash-flow-managing, not distressed). Maintaining with a hard collection milestone is the defensible middle path — but only if tied to a measurable OVD reduction gate before any peak-season credit uplift is considered.
finance.credit_rationale: Hold current limit pending clearance of the 60d+ aging bucket and confirmed non-ageing of the SAR 1.34M 0-30d cohort — raising exposure now would compound a structural DSO problem entering the highest-volume quarter.
action: Demand full settlement of 60d+ bucket (SAR 246,689) within 7 business days — non-response triggers shipment hold on the next open order, communicated in writing to the account today
Issue a formal written payment schedule for the 30-60d bucket (SAR 178,077) with fortnightly milestones and account manager counter-signature — this creates legal standing if escalation is needed

Establish weekly OVD monitoring gate: if the SAR 1.34M 0-30d OVD cohort has not been partially cleared by May 31, escalate to credit committee for limit reduction and Watch → Danger reclassification before peak season shipments accelerate
finance.risk_reason: DSO is structurally 1.8x over credit terms and OVD exceeds account's own 12-month baseline, but 60d+ bucket remains contained and 2026 commercial activity is stable — elevated and worsening, not yet critical.

sales.headline: Active pre-season buyer at 1.1x pace but chronic DSO 159d and a textbook selective-payment OVD pattern demand collection-first sequencing before any credit or marketing investment.
sales.analysis:
Channel health reads superficially positive: sell-in grew 16.7% YoY in 2025, 2026 YTD buy pace holds at 1.1x the T12M monthly average, and the account is clearly stocking ahead of the May–September Saudi peak window. For an IR-channel A+ account, this level of pre-season activity is expected and healthy on the surface. However, without sell-out data we cannot confirm whether incoming stock is moving to end-consumers or accumulating in the warehouse. Given IR competitive dynamics where Samsung dominates mid-to-large format, an LG-heavy IR account stocking aggressively in April without confirmed velocity is a latent inventory overhang risk that could suppress reorders by July.

The AR data tells a more concerning story. DSO at 159d YTD and 167d T12M against a 90-day credit term means this account has never been in terms — this is structural, not cyclical. The OVD snapshot sequence between April 29 and May 1 is the diagnostic tell: balance jumped SAR 880K (new large order) on April 30, OVD% briefly compressed to 13.3%, then snapped back to 40.9% the very next day with zero change in total balance. No new invoices entered; existing invoices simply aged past the overdue threshold. This is classic selective-payment behavior — the account releases just enough cash to open credit headroom for the next order, then allows old invoices to drift. The 60d+ bucket at SAR 246K confirms the aging tail is deepening, not clearing.

Investment calculus: the account's continued buy pace means leverage is intact — they need LG supply for peak season. That leverage should be exercised on collection, not surrendered through credit expansion or promotional spend. Injecting additional investment into a 40.9% OVD counterparty in the weeks before the highest-invoice-volume period of the year amplifies downside without adding meaningful upside. The unlock condition is clear: 60d+ bucket cleared and OVD below 25% by end of May.
sales.investment_rationale: Buy-pace leverage is strongest right now given peak-season dependency — use it to normalize OVD before any credit or promotional resource is committed.
action: Issue formal written notice within 48 hours: clearance of the 60d+ bucket (SAR 246,689) is a prerequisite for maintaining current credit terms through peak season — set a hard deadline of May 13
Freeze any credit line increase and communicate the OVD threshold (≤25%) that triggers reinstatement; document this condition so there is no ambiguity if the account escalates
Set a May 15 checkpoint — if OVD remains above 35%, place a soft order hold on any single invoice exceeding SAR 500K and escalate to the IR channel KAM for a joint collection-sales call
sales.channel_reason: Buy pace stable and pre-season stocking intact, but DSO 159d and selective-payment OVD pattern signal structural liquidity stress underneath active ordering

synthesis.headline: DSO at 1.8x credit terms is structural, not cyclical — peak-season supply dependency gives us maximum collection leverage right now, and this window closes in days, not weeks.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. Selective-payment cycle is the operating norm; use pre-season supply leverage to force OVD normalization before the SAR 1.34M 0-30d cohort matures into the next spike.

summary_3lines: A+ IR account running 159d DSO against 90d terms with 40.9% OVD — the May 1 spike was a silent batch maturity event (January invoices crossing 90d), confirming a textbook selective-payment cycle, not a one-time miss.

The SAR 1.34M currently sitting in the 0-30d OVD bucket is already staged for the next reclassification; if unpaid by end-May, OVD ratio approaches 55-60% entering the highest-shipment quarter of the year — with a 60d+ tail (SAR 246K) already beginning to form.

If no formal action is taken this week, the account enters peak season (May–September) with structural DSO deepening, credit exposure at maximum, and the collection leverage window lost — making any mid-season limit reduction or hold far more disruptive and commercially damaging than acting now.
synthesis.decision_required: Issue formal written collection demand on the 60d+ bucket (SAR 246,689, hard deadline May 13) and freeze any credit expansion with an explicit OVD ≤25% reinstatement gate — or accept structural DSO risk and ship peak-season volumes into a counterparty already 1.8x over terms.

options.option: Option A — Act Now: Issue demand, freeze credit expansion, set May 31 OVD gate
options.consequence: Short-term relationship friction possible, but peak-season dependency means the account cannot walk away from LG supply; OVD normalizes before June shipment acceleration; maintains credit discipline without a mid-season rupture.

options.option: Option B — Absorb and Ship: Continue without formal collection action through peak season
options.consequence: OVD reaches 55-60% by June as the SAR 1.34M 0-30d cohort matures; maximum credit exposure coincides with maximum shipment volume; any collection enforcement during July–August peak directly disrupts sell-through and channel relationship at the worst possible time.

timeline.step: Issue written demand: 60d+ bucket (SAR 246,689) cleared in full — non-payment by deadline triggers shipment hold on next open order
Communicate credit freeze in writing: no limit increase until OVD ≤25%; document the reinstatement threshold so there is zero ambiguity if account escalates
Hard deadline for 60d+ clearance; if missed, execute shipment hold and escalate to IR KAM for joint collection-sales call

Checkpoint: if SAR 1.34M 0-30d OVD cohort not partially cleared and OVD still above 35%, soft hold on any single invoice exceeding SAR 500K and escalate to credit committee for Watch → Danger reclassification
Final gate: OVD must be below 25% to unlock peak-season credit headroom; if not met, implement limit reduction before June shipment acceleration

finance.headline: Small-balance D-grade account with elevated YTD DSO driven by volume deceleration, not payment default — overdue is fresh and improving.
finance.analysis:
AR health is technically within tolerable bounds: all SAR 20,758 overdue sits in the 0-30d bucket with zero aging beyond 30 days, and the OVD ratio improved from 50.8% (late April) to 34.4% (May 3) — now marginally below the 35.4% portfolio baseline. Absolute exposure is modest at SAR 60K. The elevated YTD DSO of 143d versus the 90d credit term warrants scrutiny, but must be contextualized: with only SAR 52.5K in 2026 Sell-In against a T12M run-rate implying ~SAR 250K+ annually, low-volume denominator effect artificially inflates YTD DSO. T12M DSO of 69d confirms underlying payment behavior is sound when volumes are normal.

Collection risk is present but not acute. The more pressing signal is behavioral: 2026 buy pace at 0.5x entering peak season (May–September) indicates demand weakness, channel disengagement, or quiet credit hesitation. A D-grade account decelerating into peak season is a pattern that escalates fast — if the current 0-30d overdue ages into 30-60d+ during May while no new purchases arrive, the risk profile shifts materially.

Credit policy should be maintained at current level. Increasing is inappropriate for a D-grade with decelerating activity — credit expansion does not unlock volume when the constraint is engagement or demand, not capacity. Decreasing is premature given zero 60d+ aging, improving OVD trend, and sub-SAR-100K total exposure. The correct posture is hold-and-monitor through peak season entry, with a hard reassessment trigger if overdue ages or buy pace stays flat into June.
finance.credit_rationale: Zero 60d+ aging and improving OVD trend justify holding current limit; D-grade with 0.5x buy pace does not support expansion.
action: Collect SAR 20,758 (0-30d overdue) by May 15 — confirm payment timeline directly with Alharkan to prevent aging into 30-60d during peak season
Monitor buy pace through May 20: no new order by that date triggers sales escalation to diagnose disengagement vs. demand weakness before peak window narrows
Schedule June credit review with hard trigger: if 0-30d overdue ages OR YTD DSO remains >120d by June 1, consider temporary shipment hold pending payment clearance
finance.risk_reason: YTD DSO 143d exceeds 90d credit term and 0.5x buy pace entering peak season signals engagement risk — offset by zero 60d+ aging and improving OVD trajectory.

sales.headline: 2025 growth momentum has stalled sharply — 2026 buy pace halved and payment speed deteriorated 2x, raising disengagement or financial stress flags entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is yellow-trending-red. Alharkan posted strong 2024→2025 growth (+29% YoY, SAR 268K), but 2026 YTD Sell-In of SAR 52.5K at a 0.5x T12M buy pace means the account is entering May — the first month of Saudi peak season — with near-zero replenishment momentum. Pre-peak stocking window (March–April) has largely passed, and there is no indication of forward orders. Two hypotheses: (1) inventory overhang from aggressive 2025 stocking that has not yet cleared to end consumers, or (2) active reallocation of open-to-buy budget toward a competitor (Gree/Midea offering extended terms is plausible in this IR_Others segment).

Payment behavior has deteriorated markedly in 2026. T12M DSO of 69d is actually better than the 90d credit term — historically a reliable payer. YTD DSO of 143d blows through that, suggesting 2026 orders were placed under stretched liquidity or that the account is deliberately slowing payments as buy commitment fades. The OVD snapshot shows 50.8% overdue as recently as May 1, recovering to 34.4% after a partial payment on May 2–3. That payment is a mild positive (intent to settle), but the current OVD of 34.4% sits right at the YTD average baseline of 35.4% — no structural improvement, just a one-off clearance.

Investment case is weak at this stage. Grade D, absolute balance small (SAR 60K), and the deceleration is structural, not seasonal. Before any volume push or credit expansion, the account needs to demonstrate: (a) sustained OVD reduction below 20%, (b) a concrete forward order for peak SKUs. Pouring sell-out support or co-marketing into an account that may already be pivoting to competition would yield negative ROI. Collection focus and watchful monitoring through June is the right posture.
sales.investment_rationale: Buy pace at 0.5x and YTD DSO at 143d (vs 90d term) signal financial stress or disengagement — clear AR first before committing any incremental investment.
action: Sales rep to contact Alharkan by May 9: confirm whether May–June peak stocking order is being planned and, if so, on what SKU mix — absence of any intent is an exit trigger.
Set OVD monitoring alert: if balance exceeds SAR 70K or OVD % rebounds above 40% before end of May, escalate to credit hold review.
Request account to reduce 0-30d overdue (SAR 20,758) to zero before approving any new shipment — leverage the small exposure as a low-risk forcing function to test commitment.
sales.channel_reason: Buy pace 0.5x T12M entering peak season + YTD DSO 143d vs 90d credit term — momentum stalled, payment discipline weakening.

synthesis.headline: Peak season starts now — Alharkan has zero replenishment momentum and a partial overdue; the May 9 sales call determines whether this is a salvageable account or a quiet exit in progress.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. Collect first, probe peak intent by May 9, then decide: structured hold or managed exit.

summary_3lines: Alharkan's 2026 buy pace has collapsed to 0.5x T12M entering May peak season, with SAR 52.5K YTD Sell-In against a ~SAR 250K annual run-rate — pre-peak stocking window has passed with no forward orders visible.

Root cause is unresolved: either 2025 aggressive stocking created an inventory overhang not yet cleared to end consumers, or the account is quietly reallocating open-to-buy to a competitor (Gree/Midea extended-term offers are plausible in IR_Others); the May 9 call must determine which.

If nothing is done this week, the 0-30d overdue ages into 30-60d+ during peak season, no replenishment order arrives, and a currently manageable SAR 60K yellow account becomes a collection drag on a commercially dead relationship by June — with no peak season upside captured.
synthesis.decision_required: By May 9, after the sales rep confirms or denies peak stocking intent: hold and require SAR 20K clearance before next shipment, OR begin managed exit by freezing new credit and prioritizing resources on active IR accounts.

options.option: Option A — Collect & Structured Hold: require overdue clearance by May 15, approve new shipment only on cleared AR, monitor OVD weekly through June
options.consequence: If Alharkan clears overdue and places a peak order, the account recovers to D-grade active status with upside in H2; if overdue ages or no order materializes, you have a hard data point for exit by June 1 with exposure still sub-SAR-100K.

options.option: Option B — Managed Exit: no new shipment approvals regardless of payment, redirect sales effort and credit capacity to higher-grade IR accounts with active peak demand
options.consequence: Forfeits any residual peak season revenue from Alharkan (likely small given 0.5x pace), eliminates collection risk escalation during peak, and frees rep bandwidth for accounts where investment yields returns — appropriate if May 9 call confirms no stocking intent.

timeline.step: Sales rep direct call to Alharkan: (1) confirm or deny May–June peak stocking order intent and SKU mix, (2) obtain commitment date for SAR 20,758 overdue clearance — no intent = trigger Option B immediately
Collect SAR 20,758 (0-30d overdue) in full — hard gate before any new shipment approval; failure to pay by this date escalates to credit hold regardless of Option A/B decision
Final credit and investment review: if OVD has not structurally declined below 20% AND no confirmed peak order on record, execute Option B and begin managed exit before peak window closes

finance.headline: Maqam Alreem is effectively a non-performing account — SAR 234K fully overdue 60d+, zero 2026 revenue, T12M DSO at 1,259 days against a 90-day credit term.
finance.analysis:
The AR position is critical by every metric available. The entire SAR 234,380 balance sits in the 60d+ aging bucket with a 100% OVD rate that has shown zero movement across six consecutive daily snapshots (Apr 29 – May 4). There is no partial payment, no restructuring signal, and no new revenue in 2026 to suggest the business relationship is still active. The account is operationally dormant from LG's side, yet the receivable remains fully exposed.

The T12M DSO of 1,259 days is the most alarming indicator: it implies that on a trailing 12-month revenue basis, LG would need over three years to collect what is owed — approximately 14× the agreed 90-day credit term. This is not a slow payer; this is a potentially uncollectable balance. The sell-in trajectory reinforces this: SAR 1.03M in 2024 collapsed to SAR 269K in 2025 (–74%) and went to zero in 2026, suggesting LG's own credit or fulfillment controls may have already tightened informally, or the account has exited the channel.

Credit policy conclusion is unambiguous: the existing exposure must be ring-fenced and escalated. Extending any further credit — even maintaining the current nominal limit — creates moral hazard and signals tolerance for non-payment. The credit limit should be reduced to SAR 0 (prepayment only) pending full settlement of the outstanding balance. No new shipment should occur under open-account terms.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% balance overdue in 60d+ bucket, T12M DSO 14× credit term, zero 2026 revenue — no basis to extend open-account credit; limit must be reduced to zero until full collection.
action: Immediately enforce hard credit hold — block all open orders and new POs pending settlement of SAR 234,380
Escalate to legal or third-party collections for the SAR 234,380 60d+ overdue balance; issue formal demand letter with 15-day cure period
Reclassify account to prepayment-only tier; any future business to require 100% advance payment before shipment
finance.risk_reason: T12M DSO 1,259d, 100% balance in 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 activity — collection risk is near-maximum with no recovery signal.

sales.headline: Maqam Alreem is functionally inactive in 2026 with SAR 234K fully overdue and zero new purchases — this account has already self-exited.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is in terminal decline. Sell-In collapsed 74% YoY (SAR 1.03M → SAR 269K) and has dropped to zero in 2026 YTD — entering peak season (May) with no purchase activity is the clearest possible disengagement signal. With a T12M DSO of 1,259 days, the 2025 purchases have not been paid for in any meaningful sense. The 60d+ overdue bucket holding 100% of the SAR 234K balance with no movement across six consecutive daily snapshots indicates this is not a timing issue — it is a structural inability or unwillingness to pay.

Root cause priority order: (1) Financial distress is the primary driver — a Grade C IR_Others account with 90d credit terms but 1,259d actual DSO is insolvent relative to LG exposure. (2) Inventory overhang from 2025 purchases likely still unsold, suppressing 2026 reorder appetite. (3) Possible competitor redirect (Midea/Gree typically offer looser credit to distressed small dealers) — but the zero-purchase posture suggests the account is shrinking overall, not simply switching brands. No sell-out data exists to confirm end-consumer pull, but the buy freeze itself is the tell.

Investment case is non-existent. Any new credit, co-marketing, or promotional support extended to this account would be capital destruction — the priority is protecting the existing SAR 234K receivable, not generating new exposure. The account's Q3 Critical classification is appropriate and understated given the 2026 zero-revenue reality.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue, 100% AR overdue at 60d+, and T12M DSO of 1,259 days make new investment indefensible — sole focus must be recovering existing receivables before any further deterioration.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all new orders until SAR 234,380 balance is cleared — no exceptions for peak season urgency
Escalate 60d+ bucket to collections/legal within 5 business days; prepare formal demand notice given T12M DSO of 1,259 days
Direct sales/finance joint visit to negotiate structured repayment plan (e.g., 3-installment over 60 days) — partial recovery is preferable to full write-off
Assess whether the 2025 SAR 269K purchase inventory is still sitting unsold at the dealer; if so, explore buyback or reallocation to active dealers to accelerate cash recovery
Flag for quarterly AR review: if no payment commitment secured within 30 days, initiate write-off provisioning process
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 purchases entering peak season, 100% AR overdue at 60d+, and T12M DSO of 1,259 days — account is effectively dormant and financially non-performing.

synthesis.headline: Maqam Alreem는 이미 사실상 퇴출 계정 — SAR 234K 전액 60d+ 연체, 2026년 매출 제로, T12M DSO 1,259일(약정 90일의 14배). 신규 투자 가치 없음, 이번 주 법적 회수 절차 개시 여부 결정 필요.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 전액 연체·거래 동결·T12M DSO 14배로 회수 가능성 자체가 소멸 직전이며 신규 투자 근거 전무.

summary_3lines: SAR 234,380 전액이 60일 초과 연체 상태이며 6일 연속 1원의 입금도 없음 — 단순 지연이 아닌 지급 의사 또는 능력 자체의 부재.
2024년 SAR 1.03M → 2025년 SAR 269K → 2026년 SAR 0: 성수기 돌입 시점에 신규 주문이 전무하다는 것은 거래관계가 이미 실질적으로 단절됐음을 의미하며, Grade C·IR_Others 소딜러가 90일 약정에서 1,259일 DSO를 기록했다는 것은 구조적 지급불능 수준.
지금 법적 회수 절차를 개시하지 않으면 SAR 234K는 시효·증거력 약화로 회수 불가 채권으로 전락할 가능성이 높으며, 잘못된 관대함이 유사 소딜러들에게 불이행 선례로 작용할 수 있음.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 SAR 234,380 전액에 대해 공식 법적 청구(Formal Legal Demand)를 발송하고 신용한도를 즉시 SAR 0(선결제 전환)으로 확정할 것인가 — 아니면 30일 분할상환 협상을 먼저 시도할 것인가.

options.option: Option A: 즉각 법적 청구 + 신용한도 0 확정
options.consequence: 15일 내 입금 미이행 시 제3자 추심/소송으로 직행. 부분 회수 가능성을 높이되 거래관계는 완전 종결. 선례 효과로 유사 연체 계정에 경고 신호.

options.option: Option B: Sales·Finance 합동 방문 → 60일 3회 분할상환 합의 시도 후 불이행 시 법적 조치
options.consequence: 부분 회수 가능성을 유지하고 자발적 정산 시 소송비용 절감. 단, 협상 기간 동안 회수 시점이 늦어지며 추가 자산 은닉 위험. 성수기 중 영업력 낭비 최소화 필요.

timeline.step: 신용한도 SAR 0 확정 및 전 오픈오더 즉시 하드블록 — Finance 실행
Sales·Finance 합동 방문 또는 서면 Demand Letter 발송 — 15일 내 전액 또는 분할상환 약정 요구
입금·약정 미이행 시 법적 청구(제3자 추심 또는 소송) 개시 + 대손충당금 설정 검토

finance.headline: Fakhamah is a de facto default: SAR 534K fully overdue at 60d+, T12M DSO 342d (3.8× credit term), zero 2026 revenue — account shows all hallmarks of business cessation or deliberate non-payment.
finance.analysis:
AR health is at maximum stress. The entire SAR 534,054 balance sits exclusively in the 60d+ bucket — not a single riyal in current or near-term aging. This is not a timing issue; it is structural. The OVD rate has held at 100% for at least six consecutive observed days with no partial payment signal, and the T12M DSO of 342 days confirms this deterioration predates 2026. The account has generated zero sell-in revenue in 2026 despite Saudi peak season (May–September) beginning now — the highest-demand window of the year — which strongly implies either LG has already imposed an informal credit stop, the account is commercially dormant, or both.

Collection risk is critical. The 100% concentration in the 60d+ bucket eliminates any seasonal-delay or cash-flow-timing narrative. Sell-in has declined 41% YoY (SAR 973K → SAR 569K) and collapsed to zero in 2026 — the revenue trajectory itself signals the business may be winding down or exiting the LG relationship. Without a concrete repayment commitment backed by assets or a personal guarantee, SAR 534K should be flagged for provisioning in the current quarter.

Credit policy is unambiguous: decrease to zero. No commercial rationale exists for maintaining a nominal credit limit on a Grade C account with 100% overdue, a DSO nearly four times the contractual term, and zero active trading. Maintaining the limit without enforcement only creates a liability illusion. A formal credit stop, demand letter, and 30-day repayment ultimatum are the minimum threshold before any future exposure is contemplated.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% AR overdue in the 60d+ bucket, T12M DSO 3.8× credit term, and zero 2026 revenue justify immediate reduction of credit limit to SAR 0 pending full settlement.
action: Issue formal demand letter immediately for SAR 534,054 with 14-day payment deadline and explicit notice of legal escalation upon non-response
Enforce hard credit stop: block all new orders system-wide until overdue balance is cleared in full or a signed repayment schedule with personal guarantee is executed
Dispatch AR team and account manager for on-site visit within 7 days to assess business operational status and asset position — determine whether Q2 bad-debt provisioning is required
finance.risk_reason: 100% AR overdue at 60d+, T12M DSO 342d vs 90d credit term, zero 2026 revenue — account meets all criteria for default classification and provisioning review

sales.headline: Fakhamah has effectively ceased purchasing from LG in 2026 while carrying SAR 534K in fully overdue AR — a textbook disengagement-under-financial-stress pattern entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is in terminal decline. Sell-in collapsed 41.5% from 2024 to 2025 (SAR 973K → SAR 569K), and 2026 shows zero purchases despite the May peak-season window now open. The absence of any 2026 buy-in is not a timing artifact — IR_Others accounts typically pre-load ahead of summer; Fakhamah's silence signals either a competitor switch (Samsung, Gree offer aggressive credit to C-grade accounts) or a liquidity freeze that prevents new orders altogether. T12M DSO of 342 days is not a DSO problem — it is a solvency signal. The 60d+ bucket holds 100% of the SAR 534K balance with zero movement across six consecutive daily snapshots, meaning no payment plan is active and no partial settlement is occurring.

Root cause hierarchy: (1) Financial distress is the leading hypothesis — a 90-day credit term account with 342d T12M DSO and zero 2026 revenue has no capacity to absorb new inventory. (2) Competitor switch is secondary — if the account is still trading, they are likely sourcing from a supplier willing to extend terms or sell on consignment. (3) Wind-down risk cannot be excluded — zero buy-in at peak season onset is atypical even for struggling accounts unless operations are contracting.

Investment rationale is unambiguous: LG is already exposed at SAR 534K with no repayment signal. Extending further credit, co-marketing funds, or promotional support would deepen a bad-debt position against an account that has not generated a single riyal of 2026 revenue. The only defensible action is aggressive AR recovery before peak season closes any leverage window (dealers typically have seasonal liquidity in May–June from sell-out proceeds). Every week of delay post-June reduces collection probability as competitor inventory replaces LG floor space.
sales.investment_rationale: 100% overdue AR with zero 2026 sell-in and T12M DSO of 342 days makes any new investment an unrecoverable capital exposure — collection of existing SAR 534K is the only viable objective.
action: Escalate SAR 534K (60d+) to formal collection — issue written demand letter with 14-day cure window before legal referral; leverage May–June dealer liquidity as collection window
Block all new orders and credit extensions until overdue balance is cleared in full — no exceptions pending CFO-level sign-off

Conduct in-person visit to verify account operational status and assess competitor inventory mix on floor — determines whether this is a liquidity issue (recoverable) or a full competitor switch (write-down scenario)
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in at peak-season onset + 100% AR overdue (60d+) + T12M DSO 342d = financial distress or active disengagement, no recovery signal present

synthesis.headline: Fakhamah은 사실상 부도 상태 — SAR 534K 전액 60d+ 연체, 2026년 매출 제로, 회수 레버리지 창(5~6월 딜러 시즌 유동성)이 4~6주 안에 닫힌다. 이번 주 법적 조치 승인 없이는 전액 대손 가능성이 높다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. SAR 534K 전액 60d+ 연체 + 2026년 구매 제로 + T12M DSO 342일 = 신용 공여 근거 소멸, 회수 집중이 유일한 목표.

summary_3lines: Fakhamah는 SAR 534,054 전액이 60d+ 연체 버킷에 고착된 채 6일 연속 잔액 변동 없음 — 분할 납부 합의도, 부분 결제 신호도 없다.
2024→2025 Sell-In 41% 급감 후 2026년 완전 중단: 피크 시즌(5~9월) 직전 구매 부재는 자금 경색 또는 경쟁사 전환(삼성·Gree 공격적 여신 제공)이 원인이며, T12M DSO 342일은 재무 건전성 붕괴를 확인한다.
6월 이후 딜러 시즌 유동성이 소진되면 회수 레버리지가 사라지고 SAR 534K의 대손 가능성이 급증 — 이번 주 행동하지 않으면 Q2 전액 충당금 계상이 불가피하다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주(2026-05-09까지) SAR 534K에 대한 공식 내용증명 발송 + 전산 오더 블록 집행을 승인할 것인가 — 아니면 최종 1회 상업적 협상(분할 납부 각서 + 개인보증) 기회를 먼저 부여할 것인가?

options.option: Option A — 즉시 법적 절차 개시
options.consequence: 14일 기한 내용증명 발송 + 전산 하드 블록 즉시 집행 → 무응답 시 법무팀 이관 및 Q2 충당금 계상. 강경 신호로 계정 대표를 협상 테이블로 불러낼 가능성 있으나, 관계 복구 가능성은 사실상 종료.

options.option: Option B — 현장 방문 후 최종 협상 선행
options.consequence: 7일 내 영업·AR팀 현장 방문으로 영업 지속 여부 및 자산 현황 파악 → 운영 중이면 개인보증 + 분할 납부 각서 조건으로 30일 유예 제안 → 불응 시 즉시 Option A로 전환. 1~2주 지연이 발생하지만, 회수 가능 사례라면 소송 비용 없이 처리 가능.

timeline.step: 현장 방문 긴급 실시 — 운영 상태·경쟁사 입고 현황·대표 접촉 가능 여부 파악 (Option A·B 공통 선행 조건)
현장 결과 기반 내용증명 발송 + 전산 크레딧 블록 집행 (14일 납부 기한 명시, 무응답 시 법적 조치 명시)
납부 미이행 시 법무팀 이관 및 Q2 충당금(SAR 534K) 계상 의사결정

finance.headline: Laith Alarab is effectively insolvent on LG terms — 75.7% overdue frozen for 6 consecutive days with YTD DSO at 239d against a 90d credit term, while business volume has collapsed 94% since 2024.
finance.analysis:
AR health is deeply distressed. Of the SAR 134K outstanding balance, SAR 101.5K (75.7%) is overdue, with SAR 53.5K sitting in the 60d+ bucket — meaning nearly 40% of total exposure has aged beyond two payment cycles with zero reduction. The OVD% has been locked at exactly 75.7% for six straight days (Apr 29 – May 4), indicating no partial payments, no good-faith clearing activity, and a static collection situation that typically precedes full default rather than gradual resolution.

The DSO picture confirms structural payment failure, not a temporary cash flow squeeze. YTD DSO of 239 days is 2.7× the contractual 90-day credit term — meaning on average, receivables from this account are sitting for over 8 months. Even the trailing 12-month DSO of 177 days far exceeds the credit term. This is not a new deterioration; it is a pattern. The YTD avg OVD baseline of 35.4% (already elevated) versus the current 75.7% shows a sharp intra-year worsening, suggesting the account's payment capacity or willingness has materially deteriorated in 2026.

The commercial trajectory reinforces the financial risk: Sell-In has collapsed from SAR 1.27M (2024) → SAR 274K (2025) → SAR 70K (2026 YTD), a 94% revenue decline over two years. The 2026 buy pace running at only 0.7× the T12M average signals continued deceleration, not recovery. A Grade C account in Q3-Critical with shrinking business, frozen overdue, and DSO at 239d presents no credible case for extended credit — only escalating collection action.
finance.credit_rationale: With 75.7% OVD frozen for 6 days, YTD DSO at 239d (2.7× credit term), and 94% sell-in collapse since 2024, any remaining credit line should be suspended until the overdue balance is materially cleared.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all open orders and new shipments until SAR 101,484 overdue is reduced below 30% of balance
Escalate SAR 53,473 (60d+ bucket) to collections/legal — issue formal demand letter with 7-day cure period before pursuing legal remedies
Negotiate a structured repayment schedule for total overdue with bi-weekly milestone payments; any future orders to be settled on Cash-In-Advance (CIA) basis only
finance.risk_reason: YTD DSO 239d (2.7× credit term), 75.7% OVD frozen for 6 days, 60d+ bucket at 40% of balance, and accelerating business contraction — active collection failure with no recovery signal.

sales.headline: Account is in full disengagement — 78% sell-in collapse over two years, DSO at 239d, and six-day frozen OVD at 75.7% signal structural financial distress with zero recovery trajectory entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated across every measurable dimension. Sell-in collapsed 78.5% from 2024 to 2025 (SAR 1.27M → SAR 273K), and 2026 YTD at SAR 70K with a 0.7x buy pace is tracking toward sub-SAR 150K — a further halving year-on-year. The fact that buy pace is decelerating in late April, precisely when IR channel accounts should be front-loading inventory ahead of the May–September peak, is a definitive disengagement signal. A Grade C IR_Others account that historically showed SAR 1.27M annual volume and is now going quiet entering peak is not a seasonal blip — it is a structural exit.

The AR structure compounds the concern. The complete absence of balance in the 30–60d bucket (SAR 0) while simultaneously holding SAR 53,473 in 60d+ overdue and SAR 48,010 in the 0–30d bucket suggests erratic lump-sum payment behavior — the account occasionally pays to avoid immediate lock but is not systematically clearing aging debt. Six consecutive days frozen at exactly 75.7% OVD with zero movement on a SAR 134K balance is the most alarming indicator: this is not a payment-timing issue, it is an account that has stopped paying. YTD DSO at 239d versus a 90d credit term means LG is effectively subsidizing 2.7x the agreed credit period with no volume return.

Root cause hypothesis: primary driver is financial stress, likely compounded by a shift in purchasing toward competitor brands (Samsung, Gree, Midea all carry competitive pricing in the IR channel with potentially softer credit enforcement). The account may be liquidating remaining LG inventory without reordering while carrying the payable it cannot clear. Any new credit extension would increase exposure into an account with no volume recovery signal and no payment momentum — the risk/return is inverted.
sales.investment_rationale: With sell-in down 78% YoY, DSO at 239d, 75.7% OVD frozen for 6 consecutive days, and buy pace decelerating entering peak season, no investment type can generate a return before credit losses materialize.
action: 즉시 신규 오더 크레딧 홀드 — 60d+ 연체 SAR 53,473 완납 확인 전까지 출하 전면 중단, 예외 없음
영업·재무 합동으로 계정 대표 직접 접촉 — 14일 내 SAR 101,484 전액 또는 구체적 분할상환 계획서 징구, 미제출 시 에스컬레이션
상환 계획 불이행 시 Credit팀·법무 경유 공식 채권 회수 프로세스 개시 검토, 동시에 해당 계정 잔여 AR을 Bad Debt 충당금 검토 대상으로 Finance에 플래그
sales.channel_reason: Sell-in -78% YoY trend continuing into 2026, DSO 239d vs 90d terms, OVD 75.7% with zero payment movement over 6 days — financial distress, no recovery signal entering peak season.

synthesis.headline: Laith Alarab has stopped paying and stopped buying — SAR 101K overdue frozen 6 days straight with 94% sell-in collapse entering peak season; this is active default, not cash-flow timing. Collect now or accept bad debt.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Account is in structural disengagement: payment frozen, volume collapsed, and no recovery signal with peak season already underway.

summary_3lines: SAR 101,484 (75.7% of balance) has sat overdue without a single SAR of movement for 6 consecutive days, with YTD DSO at 239d — 2.7× the contractual 90-day credit term.

Sell-in collapsed 94% from SAR 1.27M (2024) to SAR 70K (2026 YTD) with buy pace decelerating at 0.7× entering the May–September peak; the account is liquidating remaining LG inventory while not reordering, a classic exit pattern.

If no action is taken this week, the SAR 53,473 in the 60d+ bucket will age further with zero collection leverage, the full SAR 134K exposure converts to bad debt, and LG has effectively extended 8+ months of free credit to an account that has already disengaged.

finance.headline: White Snow is a de-facto frozen account: SAR 667K fully overdue in the 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 revenue, and a T12M DSO of 332 days — nearly 4× the 90-day credit term.
finance.analysis:
AR health is at maximum stress. The entire outstanding balance of SAR 667,004 sits in the 60d+ aging bucket with zero allocation to current or near-term buckets — meaning there is no near-term self-healing mechanism through normal payment cycles. The OVD ratio has been locked at 100% for at least six consecutive daily snapshots, and the marginal balance reduction (SAR 0.72M → SAR 0.67M) suggests a small partial payment occurred but nowhere near enough to signal a recovery trend. The account has not generated a single riyal of new sell-in during 2026, which means LG has either already blocked the credit line or the account has self-selected out of ordering — both interpretations point to a broken commercial relationship.

Collection risk is severe and structural, not transient. T12M DSO of 332 days against a contracted 90-day credit term represents a 3.7× breach — a pattern that predates 2026 and reflects entrenched payment behavior, not a one-off liquidity event. Sell-in itself declined 48% YoY (SAR 2.33M in 2024 → SAR 1.21M in 2025) before collapsing entirely in 2026, a trajectory consistent with an account that is winding down activity while carrying an unresolved legacy balance. The Grade C classification confirms this is not a surprise deterioration but a known chronic risk.

From a credit policy standpoint, there is no basis for maintaining the current nominal credit limit. With 100% of exposure overdue beyond 60 days, zero active revenue to offset the balance, and a DSO that exceeds even LG's own DPO ceiling of 180 days by 152 additional days, extending any new credit would compound exposure with no commercial return. The credit limit should be formally reduced to zero (credit block) and tied to a structured collection plan. Any resumption of credit should require full settlement of the SAR 667K plus a probationary period of on-time payment under a new, tighter term.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of SAR 667K is 60d+ overdue with zero 2026 revenue and T12M DSO of 332 days — no commercial justification exists to maintain any open credit line until full settlement is confirmed.
action: Issue formal written demand notice for SAR 667,004 with a hard settlement deadline (recommend 15 calendar days), escalating to legal/external collections if no response
Formally freeze credit limit to SAR 0 in SAP immediately — block any new open orders until balance is cleared and a structured repayment schedule is signed
Schedule weekly AR review call with local sales team to track payment progress and determine whether legal recovery or write-off provisioning is required by Q2 close
finance.risk_reason: Entire balance 60d+ overdue, T12M DSO 332d vs 90d credit term, zero 2026 revenue, no OVD improvement trend — maximum collection risk with no self-correcting signals.

sales.headline: Account effectively dormant entering peak season — SAR 667K fully overdue with zero 2026 sell-in; disengagement pattern strongly suggests financial distress or business wind-down.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated on every measurable dimension. Sell-in collapsed 48% YoY (SAR 2.33M → SAR 1.21M in 2025), and 2026 shows zero revenue as of May — the exact moment peak season stocking should be peaking. The absence of any 2026 buy is not seasonal; it signals either a credit block already in place, account insolvency, or a deliberate switch to a competing brand. Given the T12M DSO of 332 days, the most probable root cause is financial stress severe enough to halt both payment and new ordering simultaneously.

The AR profile leaves no ambiguity: SAR 667K outstanding is 100% overdue, 100% in the 60d+ bucket, with zero recovery movement over the six-day OVD snapshot. The slight balance reduction from SAR 0.72M (Apr 29) to SAR 0.67M (Apr 30) suggests a token partial payment that has since stalled, not a genuine collection trajectory. At T12M DSO of 332 days, historical recovery pace implies this balance would not clear until Q1 2027 under optimistic assumptions — and that assumes any new sell-in resumes, which generates no 2026 baseline to anchor DSO calculation.

Investment case is effectively closed. There is no channel momentum to unlock: no open orders, no sell-out data suggesting pull-through demand, and a Grade C classification that reflects a sub-scale, high-risk operator. Injecting credit, co-marketing, or sell-out support into this account at this moment would compound the SAR 667K exposure without a credible recovery mechanism. The only viable investment is collection effort — legal notice, field visit to assess operational status, and escalation to credit committee if no response within 14 days.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue + 100% 60d+ overdue balance + T12M DSO 332d = no active channel to support; all effort must go to recovering existing exposure before any new investment is considered.
action: Issue formal written collection notice immediately citing 60d+ overdue status of SAR 667,004 and credit term breach; set 14-day response deadline
Dispatch field rep to physically verify account is still operational — confirm inventory on hand, store activity, and whether competitor brands have replaced LG
Escalate to credit committee with exit recommendation: freeze credit line, flag for legal recovery if no payment plan agreed within 30 days, and reclassify account as inactive in CRM
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in during peak season entry + 100% 60d+ overdue AR + T12M DSO 332d = account functionally inactive with unresolved credit exposure.

synthesis.headline: White Snow is a dead account carrying a live SAR 667K liability — the only remaining decision is how fast to recover the money before it becomes a write-off.

summary_3lines: White Snow has not placed a single order in 2026 — entering peak season — while carrying SAR 667K fully overdue in the 60d+ bucket, locked at 100% OVD for six consecutive days with no recovery momentum.

A 48% YoY sell-in collapse (2024→2025) followed by a full 2026 halt, combined with a T12M DSO of 332 days versus a 90-day credit term, points to financial distress or business wind-down — not a transient liquidity dip.

finance.headline: United Vision is in structural default territory — DSO 227d against 90d terms, 65% OVD accelerating, and zero 2026 sell-out signal; credit hold is the only defensible position.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired across all three aging dimensions. The 60d+ bucket at SAR 358,247 (35.7% of total balance) is the dominant component — this is entrenched non-payment, not timing friction. OVD% has deteriorated 14pp in under a week (51.4% on Apr 29 → 65.0% on May 4), and the Apr 30 pattern is particularly telling: balance jumped SAR 0.89M → 1.04M while OVD simultaneously spiked to 66.3% the following day, confirming that new shipment exposure was absorbed without any corresponding settlement. The account is not paying down old invoices as new ones arrive.

Collection risk is severe on every vector. DSO of 227d is 2.5x the contracted credit term of 90 days and — critically — exceeds LG's own DPO of 180 days, meaning LG is net-financing this customer beyond what LG itself receives from its own suppliers. The YTD OVD of 65% sits 29pp above the account's historical baseline of 35.4%, confirming this is structural deterioration in payment behavior, not a transient peak-season cash crunch. The revenue trajectory compounds the risk: Sell-In has collapsed 81% from 2024 peak (SAR 2.93M) to 2026 YTD (SAR 0.55M), and Sell-Out registers 0 units in 2026 — the account is neither generating consumer cash flow nor rebuilding its balance sheet capacity to service AR.

Credit must be decreased immediately. Grade C designation, Q3-Critical quadrant, DSO exceeding DPO, and a complete absence of 2026 sell-out activity leave no analytical basis for maintaining current exposure, let alone expanding it. Entering peak season (May–September) with SAR 1.0M at risk and OVD trending upward means loss severity in a default scenario would be maximized at precisely the worst time. Any new shipment without prior overdue clearance directly increases LG's net credit loss exposure.
finance.credit_rationale: DSO at 227d exceeds both credit term (90d) and LG's own DPO (180d), 60d+ bucket is the largest aging component at 35.7% of balance, OVD is accelerating not stabilizing, and zero 2026 sell-out provides no offsetting liquidity signal.
action: Impose immediate credit hold: block all new PGI until overdue balance falls below SAR 300K or OVD% drops below 40%
Escalate 60d+ bucket (SAR 358,247) to legal/senior AR collections with a formal 14-day written repayment demand
Require a signed, structured overdue clearance schedule with weekly payment milestones as a precondition for reinstating any shipment approval before peak season
finance.risk_reason: OVD at 65% and rising, DSO 2.5x credit terms and above LG DPO, 60d+ bucket dominant, no 2026 sell-out — maximum collection and balance sheet exposure entering peak season.

sales.headline: Channel in deep freeze — 6,969u NW242H overhang plus 227d DSO signals financial distress, not seasonal softness.
sales.analysis:
Sell-in has collapsed sequentially: SAR 2.9M (2024) → SAR 1.4M (2025, -52% YoY) → SAR 552K (2026 YTD). Sell-out mirrored the decline: 51,697u (2024 peak) → 34,432u (2025, -33%) → 0u (2026 — no channel movement detected entering peak season). The decisive signal is the end-of-2025 stock snapshot: 6,969u of NW242H sitting unsold, up from 1,690u a year prior. The account over-bought against 2024 demand, couldn't clear, and is now buried under ~4x normal carry stock. Zero 2026 sell-out data going into May is consistent with a channel that stopped pulling inventory entirely.

Buy pace of 1.0x T12M average sounds neutral, but the T12M baseline is itself a collapsing series. A stable pace on a deteriorating trend line is still deterioration. The NW242H overhang likely explains the full 2026 buying freeze — the account will not absorb new stock while sitting on 7K unsold units. The SKU mix shift from 2023 (NF182C-heavy) through 2025 (NW/ND series dominance) suggests product-line transitions weren't managed cleanly, and slow-moving H-variant stock is now the core problem.

The AR picture closes the case on investment: SAR 652K overdue out of SAR 1.0M balance (65%), DSO at 227d versus 90d credit terms — the account pays at 2.5x beyond agreed terms. The 60d+ tranche alone (SAR 358K) represents 35% of total balance with no sign of resolution across six consecutive OVD snapshots (stable 65-66% since May 1). Grade C + Q3 Critical is LG's highest-risk classification. Any new credit extension or promotional co-investment at this stage would increase LG's exposure in an account that is already demonstrably unable to service existing obligations. The investment question is moot until collection stabilizes.
sales.investment_rationale: 65% OVD with SAR 358K 60d+ overdue and 227d DSO leaves no investment case — collection recovery must precede any commercial re-engagement.
action: Formal escalation on SAR 358K 60d+ tranche: demand written payment schedule within 5 business days or initiate credit hold
Suspend all new shipments until OVD falls below 30% — communicate to account that peak-season supply access is contingent on AR normalization
Physical channel visit: verify NW242H stock count, set mutual clearance target (units + timeline) before June to salvage peak-season sell-out window
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-out, 6,969u unsold overhang, sell-in down 52% YoY — channel effectively frozen entering peak season.

synthesis.headline: United Vision is in structural default — SAR 358K entrenched 60d+ overdue, DSO 2.5x credit terms, zero 2026 sell-out, and 6,969u unsold overhang entering peak season. This is a collection emergency, not a sales problem.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Account cannot service existing obligations; any new credit extension maximizes loss exposure at peak-season peak balance.

The root cause is a 2024 over-buy that left 6,969u of NW242H unsold — the account is cash-frozen, cannot clear stock, and has stopped pulling inventory entirely (0u sell-out in 2026); buy pace 'stable at 1.0x' is a statistical artifact on a collapsing baseline.

Physical channel visit: verify NW242H stock count (6,969u target), set mutual clearance milestone (units + timeline) before June to salvage any peak-season sell-out

finance.headline: C-grade account carrying 65.8% overdue at 2.35× credit terms — selective-pay pattern masks underlying cash stress as buy pace accelerates into peak season.
finance.analysis:
AR health is structurally weak. YTD DSO of 141 days against a 60-day credit term means LG is involuntarily extending 2.35× the agreed tenor with no contractual basis. T12M DSO of 173 days confirms this is not a one-quarter blip but an entrenched collection profile. The sole near-term buffer is that all SAR 191,757 overdue sits in the 0-30d bucket — nothing has cascaded into 60d+ yet. That window is narrow: at the historical DSO pace these invoices will not clear for another 50-80 days, meaning they will migrate through every aging bucket before settlement.

The OVD drop from 81.3% (Apr 29) to 65.8% (Apr 30) is arithmetically driven not by payment but by new invoice injection — balance grew from SAR 0.24M to SAR 0.29M while absolute overdue barely moved (~SAR 191-195K). Buy pace is running 1.2× T12M average heading into peak season. This is textbook selective-pay: the account absorbs fresh inventory on LG's balance sheet while aging receivables remain frozen. In a 180-day DPO environment the distributor is structurally using LG as a working-capital facility.

Sell-in has declined 29% (2024→2025) and 2026 YTD trajectory continues downward, so the recent buy acceleration looks like seasonal front-loading rather than a recovery inflection. Grade C classification and Q4 Watch quadrant are consistent: this account underperforms on both revenue contribution and payment discipline. Increasing credit rewards the selective-pay pattern; maintaining it endorses it by inaction. Decrease is the only defensible posture until overdue is resolved and DSO normalizes toward terms.
finance.credit_rationale: DSO at 2.35× credit terms with confirmed selective-pay behavior and accelerating buy pace — additional exposure increases write-off risk without improving collection probability.
action: Freeze new shipments until overdue SAR 191,757 is cleared or a signed installment plan (3 tranches over 30 days) is in hand
Reduce active credit ceiling to current outstanding balance (SAR 291K) to cap further peak-season loading
Escalate to Sales Manager for in-person collection visit within 7 days — selective-pay pattern requires direct negotiation, not email follow-up
finance.risk_reason: No 60d+ exposure yet, but DSO 2.35× terms and selective-pay pattern mean current 0-30d overdue will age further before settlement — escalation to red is likely without intervention.

sales.headline: 2-year sell-in contraction with DSO at 141d against 60d terms and 65.8% overdue frozen for 6 consecutive days — collection must precede any commercial discussion.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is structurally weakening. Sell-in fell 29% YoY in 2025 (SAR 503K → SAR 355K) and 2026 YTD continues below 2024 pace even with a 1.2x buy-pace acceleration vs T12M. That acceleration most likely reflects a rebound off the weak 2025 base rather than genuine demand recovery — with zero sell-out visibility, inventory overhang heading into peak season (May–September) cannot be ruled out. Grade C standing and IR_Others classification confirm limited shelf presence, weak sell-out pull, and low strategic leverage within the MT channel.

Financially, the risk profile has worsened materially. DSO at 141 days versus a 60-day credit term is 2.4x over limit; T12M DSO of 173 days confirms this is a structural pattern, not a transient cash event. The OVD ratio has been locked at 65.8% (SAR 191,757) for six consecutive days since the Apr 30 partial inflow — textbook 'pay new, age old' selective payment behavior. More concerning: the account is buying at 1.2x pace while carrying this overdue load, suggesting LG's credit line is being used as working capital to front-load peak-season stock. If sell-through underperforms, the overdue balance will compound into H2.

Investment case is weak at this stage. No promotional, credit, or co-marketing support should be extended until the overdue balance is resolved and DSO shows a credible move toward terms. The 1.2x buy pace preserves a narrow commercial thread worth monitoring, but deploying investment before collection resolution would add balance-sheet exposure on an account that has contracted for two consecutive years.
sales.investment_rationale: Extending any commercial investment while DSO runs 141d vs 60d terms and SAR 191K overdue sits frozen is balance-sheet exposure without upside — collection resolution is the only unlock for re-engagement.
action: Escalate collection on SAR 191,757 overdue immediately: set a 14-day hard deadline (by May 20) aligned to peak-season order cycle open — no new credit until OVD drops below 30%
Freeze credit limit; gate any new POs against confirmed incoming payment or prepayment terms until DSO shows a credible downward move

Request channel-level sell-out data or dealer POS snapshot to determine whether 1.2x buy pace reflects real demand pull or inventory build — critical input for June re-assessment before peak restock orders

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Hold. Account is front-loading peak inventory on LG's balance sheet while deliberately aging receivables; no investment unlock until OVD clears and DSO moves toward terms.

summary_3lines: Ahmed Shar Hafeez carries SAR 191,757 overdue (65.8% of balance) frozen for 6 consecutive days while simultaneously buying at 1.2× pace — a confirmed selective-pay pattern using LG's 60-day credit line as a working-capital facility heading into peak season.

Two years of sell-in contraction (−29% in 2025, 2026 YTD tracking below 2024) combined with zero sell-out visibility mean the 1.2× buy acceleration almost certainly reflects inventory front-loading off a weak base, not genuine demand recovery — overhang risk is high.

finance.headline: Dormant Grade-C account with a negligible credit balance — collection risk is near-zero, but the 99% revenue collapse since 2024 signals a dead commercial relationship, not financial health.
finance.analysis:
AR position is technically clean: the SAR -2,177 balance is a credit (LG owes the dealer, not the reverse), 0-30d bucket accounts for the entirety, and overdue exposure is zero across all recent snapshots. On pure collection optics, there is nothing to chase. However, this 'cleanliness' is misleading — it reflects the absence of trade rather than creditworthy behavior. Zero 2026 Sell-In across 125 days into peak-season buildup, combined with a collapse from SAR 443K (2024) to SAR 4K (2025), indicates the account has effectively exited the LG portfolio.

Collection risk on existing AR is negligible given the credit-balance position. The meaningful risk is forward-looking: the 35.4% YTD avg OVD baseline confirms that historically, when this account did transact, collections were strained. T12M DSO of -53 days is arithmetically produced by minimal revenue and credit notes — it is not evidence of strong payment discipline. Grade C and Q3-Critical classification reflect this underlying pattern. If any new Sell-In were to occur, 35%+ of it would statistically roll overdue based on the account's own history.

Credit policy should be tightened. There is no justification to maintain existing credit headroom for a dormant Grade-C account with a documented overdue tendency. Decreasing the credit limit sends a clear internal signal, prevents inadvertent exposure if orders resume, and aligns exposure to the account's actual current commercial weight: effectively zero.
finance.credit_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue on a Grade-C account with a 35.4% historical OVD baseline warrants shrinking credit capacity to match the de-facto dormant relationship.
action: Confirm the SAR -2,177 credit balance origin (credit memo, overpayment, return) and resolve or net-off before any new order is accepted

Conduct a commercial review call with the sales team to determine whether the account is lost to a competitor, financially distressed, or simply inactive — this informs whether to re-engage or formally downgrade

Reduce credit limit to a nominal floor (e.g., SAR 50,000 or lower) pending evidence of renewed activity and improved payment behavior; any reinstatement requires re-qualification under standard Grade-C terms
finance.risk_reason: Current AR exposure is zero, but historical 35.4% OVD rate and full commercial dormancy in peak season make forward credit exposure high-risk if trading resumes.

sales.headline: Al-Shuwaired has effectively gone dormant — 99% sell-in collapse from 2024 to 2025, zero 2026 revenue entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critical by any measure. Sell-in dropped from SAR 443K in 2024 to SAR 4.4K in 2025 — a 99% collapse — and no transactions have occurred in 2026, with May (peak season onset) already underway. The account's only current balance is a minor credit of SAR -2,177, which is residual noise, not active business. There is no sell-out data to infer pull-through, but the complete absence of replenishment orders entering the hottest months of the Saudi AC calendar (May–September) is a definitive disengagement signal. A functioning channel partner would be buying now.

Root cause is most likely a combination of financial stress and competitor migration. The C-grade classification and a 35.4% YTD average overdue baseline from prior periods indicate chronic payment discipline issues. IR_Others accounts of this profile are typically price-sensitive and susceptible to switching to Chinese OEMs (Gree, Midea, Hisense) offering longer credit or distributor-backed financing. The 2024 volume (SAR 443K) was real enough to matter, but the 2025 near-exit suggests either a credit dispute, a better competing offer, or a deterioration in the account's own business fundamentals — possibly all three.

Investment is not warranted at this stage. The SAR 4.4K 2025 buy did not seed any observable recovery momentum, and with zero 2026 activity entering peak season, there is no near-term upside for promotional or credit support to unlock. The credit balance (no receivables outstanding) means there is no immediate collection risk, but equally no basis for relationship leverage. Resources deployed here would have far higher ROI redirected to active IR accounts showing buy momentum.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero revenue entering peak season, 99% multi-year sell-in collapse, C-grade with chronic OVD history — no investable signal exists.
action: Field sales: one touchpoint call this week to confirm whether account is still commercially active (business still open, still selling AC) — minimum cost, maximum information
If account confirms active operations, probe root cause of volume collapse: credit terms, competitor switch, or portfolio mismatch — do not offer concessions without diagnosis

If no response or account confirms LG is no longer primary supplier, formally flag as dormant in CRM and reallocate any assigned display/shelf support budget to active IR accounts before peak season peaks in June
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 revenue, 99% sell-in YoY collapse, entering Saudi peak season with no buy signal — account is functionally disengaged.

synthesis.headline: Al-Shuwaired는 사실상 이탈 계정 — 피크 시즌 진입에도 2026년 매출 제로, 신규 신용공여 시 35% 연체 재현 위험.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. 현재 AR 위험은 없으나, 거래 재개 시 C등급·35% OVD 이력이 즉시 신용 위험으로 전환된다.

summary_3lines: 2024년 SAR 443K → 2025년 SAR 4.4K → 2026년 YTD 제로: 3년 연속 붕괴로 계정은 LG 포트폴리오에서 사실상 이탈.
C등급·35.4% 연체 기준선·IR_Others 가격민감 채널 특성상 중국 OEM(Gree·Midea) 크레딧 조건 이탈 또는 재무 악화가 가장 유력한 원인.
5~9월 사우디 피크 시즌에 구매 신호 전무 — 지금 아무 조치 없으면 이 계정은 경쟁사 전환 완료로 연말 공식 이탈 처리가 기정사실화된다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 단 한 번의 현장 콜로 계정 생사 여부를 확인하고, 결과에 따라 즉시 재활성화 vs. 공식 휴면 처리 중 하나를 선택한다.

options.option: Option A — 현장 콜 후 재활성화 시도
options.consequence: 계정이 아직 영업 중이고 LG 복귀 의사가 있다면 원인 진단(크레딧 조건·SKU 미스매치·경쟁사 조건) 후 조건부 재개. 단, 신용한도는 SAR 50K 이하로 제한하고 첫 2오더는 T/T 또는 단기 조건 적용 — 35% OVD 재현 방어 필수.

options.option: Option B — 응답 없거나 타사 전환 확인 시 공식 휴면 처리
options.consequence: CRM 휴면 플래그, SAR -2,177 크레딧 잔액 정산(반품/크레딧메모 원인 확인), 신용한도 명목 수준(SAR 50K 이하)으로 축소. 피크 시즌 전에 해당 예산·전시 지원을 활성 IR 계정으로 즉시 재배정하여 기회비용 최소화.

timeline.step: 현장 영업 담당자 콜 1회 — 계정 영업 여부·LG 복귀 의향 확인
SAR -2,177 크레딧 잔액 원인 규명(반품·크레딧메모·초과납입) 및 정산 또는 차기 오더 상계 처리
콜 결과 기반 최종 처리: 재활성화(조건부 T/T 첫 오더) 또는 CRM 휴면 + 신용한도 축소 + 예산 재배정

finance.headline: Cold World Est. is in acute collection crisis — 100% of SAR 2.06M balance overdue, T12M DSO at 411 days against a 90-day credit term, while aggressively accelerating 2026 purchases 2.2x above prior pace.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically compromised. The OVD rate surged from 25.5% to 100% overnight on May 1, indicating a large tranche of 2026 invoices simultaneously hit maturity and went unpaid — not a gradual deterioration but a structural payment refusal. Of the SAR 2.06M overdue balance, SAR 454,918 (22%) has aged beyond 60 days with no resolution, confirming this is not a temporary liquidity squeeze but a pattern of chronic deferral. The 0-30d bucket at SAR 1.575M is misleadingly large — it will migrate to the 30d+ and 60d+ buckets within weeks if the account's behavior continues.

Collection risk is severe. T12M DSO of 411 days is 4.6x the contractual 90-day credit term — this is not a payment timing issue, it is a structural default pattern embedded over the trailing year. The 2.2x buy pace acceleration in 2026 (SAR 1.37M YTD vs SAR 461K full-year 2025) is the most alarming signal: the account is front-loading inventory ahead of peak season (May–September) while simultaneously failing to clear existing obligations. This pattern — accelerate buying, defer payment, exploit peak-season leverage — significantly raises the probability that this account will not self-cure without external pressure.

Credit policy must be tightened immediately. Maintaining or increasing the current credit exposure with 100% OVD, a T12M DSO of 411 days, and an accelerating buy pace would be fiscally indefensible. The account's Grade B classification and Q3-Critical quadrant positioning confirm that neither commercial relationship quality nor payment behavior justifies continued open credit. The appropriate response is a hard credit freeze — no new shipments until the 60d+ bucket (SAR 454,918) is cleared and a formal PDC or payment schedule is executed for the remainder.
finance.credit_rationale: T12M DSO of 411 days (4.6x credit term) combined with 100% OVD and a 2.2x purchase acceleration signals deliberate float behavior ahead of peak season — further credit exposure is not recoverable under current conditions.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all new orders and shipments until overdue balance is formally addressed — peak-season demand gives LG negotiating leverage now, not later
Issue 7-day payment demand for SAR 454,918 (60d+ bucket) with escalation to legal/collection if not received — this aging segment is at highest impairment risk

Require execution of post-dated cheques (PDCs) or a signed payment schedule covering the full SAR 2,061,594 balance as a precondition for any credit reinstatement — document formally to protect legal recourse
finance.risk_reason: 100% OVD on full balance, T12M DSO 4.6x credit term, 60d+ bucket at SAR 455K, and accelerating 2026 purchase pace while delinquent — multi-signal acute default risk at peak season entry.

sales.headline: Cold World is aggressively stocking pre-peak on LG's balance sheet — 100% overdue with T12M DSO of 411 days signals a chronic payment default, not a growth story.
sales.analysis:
Surface numbers look like a breakout account: 2026 Sell-In already 3x full-year 2025 (SAR 1.37M vs SAR 462K), buy pace 2.2x T12M average, and the timing aligns with pre-peak stocking ahead of May–September. However, the buy momentum is entirely financed by unpaid LG receivables. The 100% OVD flag triggered May 1 — the exact moment the 90-day credit term expired on recent purchases — meaning the account made no payment at all at term. T12M DSO of 411 days on a 90-day credit term is not a timing variance; it is a structural financing dependency where Cold World is using LG as a de facto 14-month lender.

The SAR 454,918 in 60d+ overdue is chronic and predates 2026 acceleration, confirming this pattern is not new stress but an embedded behavior. The question is whether the 2.2x buy surge reflects genuine channel pull-through (rapid sell-out, strong consumer demand) or inventory accumulation without downstream velocity — and with no sell-out data, the latter must be assumed until proven otherwise. An IR_Others B-grade account loading this level of stock without paying is more consistent with financial stress or margin arbitrage than with healthy channel expansion.

From an investment standpoint, any additional support — credit, promotions, co-marketing — would deepen LG's exposure without a commensurate signal of repayment capacity. The account is already consuming resources disproportionate to its grade and collectability. The right lens here is not 'how do we grow Cold World' but 'how do we recover SAR 2.06M and stabilize the relationship on sustainable terms.'
sales.investment_rationale: T12M DSO of 411 days and 100% current balance overdue indicate the account cannot service existing credit, making any new investment a direct increase in unrecoverable exposure.
action: Immediate credit hold — zero new shipments until overdue balance falls below 50% of total; escalate to sales director + finance jointly
Prioritize SAR 454,918 (60d+) collection with hard deadline: require structured payment plan (weekly/biweekly installments) within 5 business days or initiate formal AR escalation
Conduct account visit to verify actual inventory levels on-site — if stock is accumulating without sell-out, negotiate return of slow-moving SKUs to reduce exposure before peak season ends
sales.channel_reason: 100% overdue balance, T12M DSO 411d vs 90d term, and no payment at credit maturity indicate financial distress or deliberate payment avoidance — channel health cannot be assessed independent of collection risk.

synthesis.headline: Cold World is using LG as a 14-month lender at peak season — 100% of SAR 2.06M is overdue, T12M DSO is 4.6x the credit term, and the account is accelerating purchases 2.2x while making zero payments. Credit hold must be enforced this week before peak-season leverage disappears.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Cold World has structurally defaulted on a 90-day credit term while front-loading peak inventory on LG's balance sheet; further exposure deepens unrecoverable receivables, not a commercial relationship.

summary_3lines: Cold World's full SAR 2.06M balance turned 100% overdue on May 1 — the exact moment 90-day credit terms matured — confirming a deliberate payment refusal, not a liquidity accident, while simultaneously running a 2.2x buy pace into peak season.

T12M DSO of 411 days on a 90-day credit term reveals a structural financing dependency: the account has embedded LG as a de facto 14-month lender for at least one full prior year, and the 2026 acceleration is an attempt to deepen that dependency ahead of May–September peak when LG has no choice but to supply.

If no action is taken this week, the SAR 1.575M sitting in the 0-30d bucket migrates to overdue within 30 days, total exposure could reach SAR 3M+ by July, and post-peak (October) LG loses all negotiating leverage — making partial recovery the best-case outcome instead of full recovery now.
synthesis.decision_required: Enforce immediate credit hold on all new orders and shipments, and require Cold World to execute a signed PDC or structured payment schedule for the full SAR 2,061,594 balance — this week, while peak-season demand gives LG maximum leverage.

options.option: Option A — Credit Hold + Structured Recovery (Recommended)
options.consequence: New shipments blocked immediately. Cold World faces supply disruption at peak season entry — highest pressure point to compel payment. Issue 7-day formal demand for SAR 454,918 (60d+ bucket) with legal escalation trigger. Require signed PDC covering full balance as precondition for any reinstatement. Probable outcome: partial-to-full recovery of SAR 2.06M over 60-90 days; relationship reset to sustainable terms or terminated with minimal additional exposure.

options.option: Option B — Continue Supply Under Current Terms
options.consequence: SAR 1.575M in 0-30d bucket ages into 30d+ and 60d+ over May–June. Total overdue exposure grows toward SAR 3M+ by July. Post-September, Cold World's need for LG product drops sharply and leverage inverts. Recovery probability falls below 50%. Grade B account with no sell-out data confirmation creates full impairment risk on a balance approaching annual Sell-In equivalent.

timeline.step: Issue credit hold — block all open orders and shipments; notify Cold World in writing
Deliver formal 7-day payment demand for SAR 454,918 (60d+ bucket) with explicit legal escalation clause if not received
Conduct on-site account visit — verify physical inventory levels and sell-out velocity; if stock is accumulating without downstream movement, open negotiation for SKU return to reduce exposure
Deadline for Cold World to execute signed PDC or formal payment schedule covering full SAR 2,061,594 as condition for any credit reinstatement
If no payment plan executed by deadline — escalate SAR 454,918 to legal/collection and downgrade account to credit-suspended status; re-evaluate full relationship

finance.headline: Muthaleth Al-Rwad is effectively dormant — 100% of its SAR 58,739 balance is locked in the 60d+ bucket with zero 2026 revenue, a textbook pre-write-off profile.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired. The entire SAR 58,739 balance sits in the 60d+ aging bucket, and six consecutive daily snapshots show zero movement — no partial payments, no new invoices, no sign of engagement. T12M DSO of 144 days already exceeds the 90-day credit term by 60%, and with zero 2026 sell-in there is no receivables turnover to bring the metric back toward terms. The DSO will only deteriorate further as the overdue balance ages without offset.

The revenue trajectory compounds the concern: SAR 1.47M in 2024 collapsed to SAR 149K in 2025 (−90%), and 2026 is flat at zero. This is not seasonal softness — this is a commercially inactive account. The pattern is consistent with a customer under financial stress, a relationship that has effectively ended, or business diverted to competing suppliers. Grade C classification is consistent with this risk profile.

On credit policy, the account currently consumes credit without generating revenue or repayment velocity. Maintaining current exposure is indefensible; any incremental shipment would compound an already stalled receivable. The sole priority is recovery of the existing SAR 58,739. Credit should be reduced to zero and reinstated only after full clearance and a demonstrated clean payment track record of at least two consecutive cycles.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% OVD concentrated in the 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 activity, and T12M DSO of 144d against 90d terms leave no risk-adjusted basis for maintaining current credit exposure.
action: Issue formal written demand notice for SAR 58,739 with a 14-day payment deadline, explicitly referencing escalation to external collections or legal if unresolved
Implement immediate hard credit hold — block all open orders and future shipments until overdue balance is fully settled
Reset credit limit to zero upon any partial payment; reinstate only after full clearance and a minimum two-cycle clean payment track record, subject to Finance Director approval
finance.risk_reason: 100% of balance is 60d+ overdue, zero 2026 revenue, T12M DSO 60% beyond credit term, with no collection improvement across six consecutive daily observations.

sales.headline: Near-complete channel disengagement — 90% sell-in collapse YoY, zero 2026 activity, 100% AR overdue entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critical by every measurable indicator. Sell-in collapsed from SAR 1.47M (2024) to SAR 149K (2025), an 89.9% YoY decline — well beyond seasonal noise. Zero 2026 revenue with no buy activity recorded through early May, the precise onset of Saudi AC peak season (May–Sep), confirms this is not a timing lag but structural disengagement. The account holds SAR 58,739 balance, 100% of which has been overdue 60d+ for at least six consecutive snapshot days with no movement whatsoever.

Root cause hierarchy: (1) Financial stress is the primary driver — T12M DSO of 144 days against 90d terms signals chronic payment dysfunction, not a one-time delay; the 2024 large buy may have overstretched the account's working capital. (2) Inventory overhang from the 2024 peak-load purchase (SAR 1.47M) likely suppressed 2025 reorders as unsold LG units sat in the channel. (3) Competitor migration is plausible — Grade C IR_Others accounts are price-sensitive and susceptible to switching to Gree/Midea/Hisense on softer credit terms when LG's AR relationship deteriorates. Any combination of these three creates a self-reinforcing exit spiral.

Investment case does not exist. Entering peak season with a fully overdue, non-buying Grade C account means any credit extension or sell-out support would be incremental exposure on an already impaired asset. LG's opportunity cost is real — capital and field resources redirected here come at the expense of active, growing accounts in the same IR channel. The decisive action is collection first; commercial rehabilitation is a post-recovery conversation.
sales.investment_rationale: 100% AR overdue 60d+, zero 2026 buying, and 90% sell-in collapse leave no commercial case for investment — recovery of existing exposure is the only defensible priority.
action: Freeze credit immediately: block all new order processing until SAR 58,739 is fully cleared — no exceptions given 60d+ vintage and 144d T12M DSO.
Escalate collection to account owner within 48 hours: structured payment plan with hard deadline, no later than 30 days; involve legal/finance if no commitment by Day 7.

Conduct field visit or direct call to diagnose root cause (financial distress vs. inventory overhang vs. competitor switch) — findings determine whether account is recoverable post-collection or should be formally exited from active coverage.

If full payment secured and root cause is inventory-driven, evaluate limited re-engagement on cash-only or prepayment terms post-peak season (Oct+) — do not reopen credit before T12M DSO normalizes below 100d.
sales.channel_reason: 89.9% sell-in YoY decline, zero 2026 revenue entering peak season, 100% AR overdue 60d+ with no payment movement — channel effectively inactive.

synthesis.headline: SAR 58,739 전액 60일+ 연체·2026 매출 제로·피크 시즌 진입 — 즉각 회수 조치 없으면 전액 대손 처리 수순

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 90% 셀인 붕괴·144일 DSO·100% 연체 고착으로 신규 투자 근거 없음, 회수가 유일한 우선순위.

summary_3lines: SAR 58,739 전액이 60일+ 연체 버킷에 6일 연속 고착, 2026 신규 매출·결제 이동 모두 제로.
2024 대규모 선적(SAR 1.47M)이 거래처 운전자본을 초과했거나 재고 소진 실패·경쟁사 전환이 복합 작용해 사실상 채널 이탈 상태.
지금 아무 조치도 취하지 않으면 피크 시즌(5~9월) 내내 회수 모멘텀을 잃고 SAR 58,739가 대손으로 전환되며, IR_Others 채널 내 활성 거래처에 투입될 자원도 함께 잠식됨.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 공식 지급 최고장 발송 및 즉시 신용 정지 집행 여부 — 14일 내 전액 회수 또는 법적 절차 이관 트랙 확정

options.option: Option A — 즉각 회수 트랙 (권고)
options.consequence: 14일 지급 기한부 공식 최고장 발송 + 즉시 신용 홀드 집행. 기한 내 미납 시 법적/외부 추심 이관. 전액 회수 후 최소 2사이클 정상 결제 확인 전까지 신용 재개 불가. 회수 성공 시 손실 없이 관계 청산 또는 현금 선결제 조건부 재개 여지 확보.

options.option: Option B — 현상 유지 또는 추가 신용 공여
options.consequence: 피크 시즌 내내 SAR 58,739 추가 노화, 대손 전환 가능성 급상승. 신규 선적 시 연체 잔액 누적으로 총 익스포저 확대. IR_Others 채널 내 투자 가치 없는 거래처에 자원 고착, 활성 계정 기회비용 발생.

timeline.step: 공식 지급 최고장 발송 (SAR 58,739, 14일 기한 명시, 미납 시 법적 조치 예고) + 전 채널 신용 홀드 즉시 집행
영업 담당자 현장 방문 또는 직접 통화 — 재무 압박·재고 적체·경쟁사 전환 중 근본 원인 진단, Day 7까지 결제 약정 미확보 시 법무·재무 에스컬레이션 결정
전액 회수 확인 후 신용 한도 제로 리셋 유지, 재개 조건(2사이클 정상 결제 + DSO 100일 미만) 충족 시에만 재심사 — 미회수 시 외부 추심 또는 법적 절차 공식 개시

finance.headline: Dyar Al-Fares carries SAR 140K fully overdue with zero 2026 revenue — a dormant, non-paying account requiring immediate collection escalation and hard credit freeze.
finance.analysis:
The AR position is unambiguously distressed: 100% of the SAR 140,017 balance sits in the 60d+ bucket with zero partial payments recorded across six consecutive daily snapshots (Apr 29 – May 4). There is no fresh receivable to dilute the aging — the account has placed zero sell-in in 2026, meaning LG has no leverage from an active commercial relationship to encourage payment. The T12M DSO of 178 days against a 90-day credit term signals a structural payment discipline problem, not a temporary cash flow blip; the account has historically operated at nearly 2× its agreed terms even when ordering.

From a collection risk standpoint, this is the highest-severity scenario: a static, fully-aged balance with no inbound cash signal and no new business activity. The absence of 2026 orders is itself a warning — it may indicate the account is commercially inactive, financially stressed, or has already been informally blocked by the sales team. Either way, the SAR 140K is effectively a non-performing receivable. Without a concrete payment commitment or installment plan executed within days, this balance risks migrating into doubtful/write-off territory before the peak season window (May–Sep) closes.

On credit policy, there is no scenario that justifies maintaining or increasing the credit limit. The account is a Grade-C, Q3-Critical counterparty with a T12M DSO of 178 days, 100% OVD concentration in the worst aging bucket, and zero 2026 revenue to offset exposure. The only defensible posture is a full credit freeze and exposure reduction — new orders must be blocked until the overdue balance is settled in full. Any credit extension at this stage would compound LG's collection risk with no commercial upside.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of balance is 60d+ overdue, T12M DSO is 178d vs 90d terms, and zero 2026 activity eliminates any commercial rationale for maintaining exposure — credit limit should be reduced to zero until full settlement.
action: Issue formal written demand notice for SAR 140,017 today; require payment commitment or installment plan within 5 business days
Place hard credit block on all pending and future orders until overdue balance is fully cleared
If no payment response within 15 days, escalate to legal counsel / external collection agency and initiate bad debt provision process for SAR 140,017
finance.risk_reason: 100% balance overdue in 60d+ bucket, T12M DSO 178d (2× credit term), zero 2026 revenue activity — static non-performing receivable with no collection signal.
