sales.headline: Dyar Al-Fares has gone completely dark in 2026 — zero orders, 100% AR overdue 60d+, signaling financial collapse or effective credit freeze post-overbuy.
sales.analysis:
The sell-in trajectory looks deceptively healthy on the surface: SAR 175K in 2024 → SAR 288K in 2025, a 64% YoY surge. However, 2026 YTD revenue is exactly zero, meaning this account has not placed a single order entering the peak season window (May–September). The sharp 2025 spike combined with a full 2026 stop is a classic overbuy-then-stall pattern: the account likely front-loaded inventory in 2025, couldn't move stock fast enough, and is now either cash-constrained or sitting on unsold units. The 60d+ overdue balance (SAR 140K, 100% of total AR) reinforces this — cash is frozen downstream and they cannot service the LG liability.

The financial risk profile is severe. T12M DSO of 178 days against a 90-day credit term means this account pays on average 88 days late — nearly a full credit cycle beyond terms. YTD DSO is undefined because there is no 2026 revenue at all, which itself is the loudest signal. The OVD trend has been locked at 100% for at least 7 consecutive days with no movement, suggesting no partial payment effort is underway. This is not a seasonal dip or a temporary cash delay; this is a structural payment breakdown.

From an investment standpoint, there is no case for sell-out support, credit extension, or co-marketing. All three require an operational channel that is actively buying and selling — this account is frozen on both sides. The only rational allocation of effort here is collection recovery. Continuing to hold this account in an investable category while peak season resource allocation decisions are being made would dilute support from higher-momentum IR_Others accounts. Grade C designation is appropriate and should be treated as a signal to triage down, not defend.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue, 100% AR overdue at 60d+, and T12M DSO of 178d on 90d terms make any new investment indefensible — collection recovery is the only viable action.
action: 즉시 SAR 140,017 전액 대금 회수 플랜 요청 — 2주 내 서면 분할납부 약정 확보 또는 법적 에스컬레이션 검토
신용한도 완전 동결 유지 — AR 전액 정산 전까지 신규 오더 수락 불가 (시스템 credit block 확인)
2025년 오버바이 재고 현황 파악 — 현장 방문 또는 거래처 확인으로 LG 미판매 재고 규모 추정, 필요 시 재매입 협상 검토하여 채권 회수율 극대화
sales.channel_reason: 2026 YTD 매출 제로 + 전액 60d+ 연체 = 채널 기능 정지 상태, 피크 시즌 진입 시점에 완전 이탈 리스크

synthesis.headline: Dyar Al-Fares is a non-performing receivable — SAR 140K 100% overdue with zero 2026 activity. This is a collection emergency, not a commercial relationship.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Static 60d+ overdue balance with no payment signal and no 2026 orders means the channel is functionally dead entering peak season.

summary_3lines: Dyar Al-Fares has placed zero orders in 2026 while carrying SAR 140,017 fully overdue in the 60d+ bucket — the account is commercially and financially frozen.

The 64% sell-in surge in 2025 (SAR 288K) followed by a complete 2026 stop is a textbook overbuy-stall pattern: the account front-loaded, could not move inventory, and is now cash-constrained with no capacity to service the LG liability.

If no action is taken this week, the SAR 140K migrates irreversibly into doubtful debt territory before peak season closes — recovery probability drops with each passing cycle, and LG absorbs a write-off with zero offsetting revenue.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize formal written demand notice and hard credit block this week — then decide within 15 days whether to pursue installment recovery or escalate to legal/external collection.

options.option: Option A: Demand + Structured Recovery Plan
options.consequence: Issue formal written demand for SAR 140,017 today. Require signed installment plan within 5 business days. If agreed, maintain credit freeze until plan is fully executed. Maximizes recovery rate while preserving a minimum commercial relationship if account rehabilitates post-settlement.

options.option: Option B: Immediate Legal Escalation + Bad Debt Provision
options.consequence: Skip installment negotiation, refer directly to legal counsel or external collection agency, and begin bad debt provisioning for SAR 140K. Faster resolution path if the account is financially insolvent — limits further time cost on a low-probability recovery. Closes the relationship permanently.

timeline.step: Issue formal written payment demand for SAR 140,017 and confirm system credit block is active — no new orders accepted
Receive signed payment commitment or installment schedule from account — if no response, escalate decision to Option B
If no payment received or plan breached, hand off to legal/collection agency and initiate SAR 140K bad debt provision

finance.headline: Star Appliances carries a structurally elevated AR profile — DSO at 2.3× credit terms and 23% of total balance locked in the 60d+ bucket — yet OVD is stable and below its own YTD baseline, consistent with peak-season stocking distortion rather than active default risk.
finance.analysis:
AR health is a tale of two layers. The current balance of SAR 5.74M on its face is manageable for an A+ IR account entering peak season, but the internal composition is concerning: SAR 1.33M sits in the 60d+ overdue bucket, representing 23.2% of total AR and 68.8% of all overdue. With 90-day credit terms, these invoices are 150+ days from issuance — well past any seasonal stocking rationalization. The 0-30d and 30-60d buckets (SAR 604K combined) are comparatively modest and typical of ongoing activity.

Collection risk is elevated but not accelerating. DSO at 204d runs 2.27× the contracted credit term and even exceeds LG's own DPO of 180d — the precise condition defining Q2 Anomaly. However, the OVD% has been flat at 33.7% for five consecutive days, and this figure is 170bps below the account's own YTD average baseline of 35.4%. The trend line is stable, not deteriorating. The 2026 buy pace at 1.0× T12M confirms the account is not aggressively expanding its footprint ahead of collections, which reduces the risk of runaway exposure accumulation going into peak season.

Credit policy must balance two competing realities. This account generated SAR 8.2M in 2025 (8× the 2024 base) and is pacing SAR 3.5M YTD before peak season has fully opened — it is commercially consequential. Grade A+ confirms its strategic weight in the IR channel. However, increasing the credit limit on an account whose DSO already exceeds DPO would deepen structural exposure without resolving the underlying 60d+ tail. Decreasing would likely disrupt critical peak-season stocking flows unnecessarily, given OVD is stable and below baseline. The correct posture is to hold the current limit firm while applying targeted, accountable collection pressure on the aged 60d+ bucket as a precondition for any future expansion discussion.
finance.credit_rationale: OVD is stable and below YTD baseline — tightening would penalize a strategically important peak-season account without cause, while expanding on a 204d DSO profile would compound structural overexposure.
action: Initiate formal collection call on the SAR 1.33M 60d+ bucket this week — obtain written repayment commitment with milestone dates before end of May
Gate any new shipment approvals for June onwards on 60d+ bucket reducing to below SAR 800K; communicate this threshold explicitly to the account
Set a weekly OVD monitoring trigger: if OVD crosses 38% (YTD baseline +250bps), escalate to red and pause credit line pending finance review
finance.risk_reason: 60d+ bucket at 23% of total AR and DSO at 2.3× credit terms are structurally elevated, but OVD trend is flat and below the account's own historical baseline — no imminent default signal, but aged tail demands active resolution.

sales.headline: A+ IR account with proven 8x growth sustaining stable 2026 buy pace, but DSO at 204d vs 90d terms signals structural working capital overextension requiring collection discipline before any credit expansion.
sales.analysis:
Star Appliances executed a transformational volume ramp in 2025 — SAR 1.0M to SAR 8.2M sell-in, an 8x YoY jump that elevated them to A+ IR status. The 2026 YTD pace (SAR 3.52M through May, 1.0x vs T12M) signals the account is sustaining this elevated base rather than retreating, and with peak season (May–September) ahead, buy momentum is likely to accelerate further. This is not a disengaging account — the fundamental channel posture is intact and growing.

The critical concern is structural DSO elongation: 204d actual vs 90d credit terms means LG is effectively financing Star at 2.27x the agreed tenure. The 60d+ overdue bucket at SAR 1.33M (23% of total balance) demands immediate collection action. However, the OVD% trend (33.7% stable from 05-01 through 05-04, below the 35.4% YTD baseline) and flat daily balance indicate no accelerating deterioration — this reads as peak-season inventory-stocking-induced cash cycle stretch, not a default trajectory. The Q2 Anomaly classification is consistent: DSO lagging from volume velocity, not from credit quality collapse.

The investment calculus favors hold with targeted sell-out activation. Credit expansion would deepen an already overextended AR position without addressing root cause. But withdrawing support entirely risks losing an A+ account at the highest-value moment in the LG–IR annual cycle. The optimal lever is sell-out acceleration — installer incentive, POS display, end-consumer promotion — to shorten Star's inventory-to-cash cycle, which directly resolves the DSO root cause and enables AR recovery by Q3 before year-end settlement.
sales.investment_rationale: Stable A+ buy pace entering peak season justifies continued engagement, but 204d DSO on 90d terms requires measurable AR recovery before any credit expansion — sell-out support accelerates Star's cash cycle without deepening LG's exposure.
action: Escalate 60d+ overdue (SAR 1.33M) immediately: schedule joint collection meeting with Star Appliances finance and LG credit team within 7 days — secure written payment plan with milestone dates anchored to peak-season sell-out receipts (June–August tranches)

Activate targeted sell-out support package (installer incentive program + POS display refresh) entering May peak to accelerate inventory velocity, reduce Star's working capital pressure, and drive AR recovery organically through cash generation

Enforce conditional order release: no new credit line increase until 60d+ overdue drops below SAR 600K — use peak-season order urgency as structured leverage to extract payment commitment and set intermediate DSO target of 150d by end-Q3
sales.channel_reason: Strong 2025 volume base and stable 2026 buy pace offset by 204d DSO on 90d terms and SAR 1.33M in 60d+ overdue — green upgrade conditional on collection milestone delivery in Q2.

synthesis.headline: Star Appliances is LG's highest-momentum IR account entering peak season — but SAR 1.33M sitting 150d+ past issuance means LG is financing an A+ account at 2.3× agreed tenure with no formal recovery plan. The window to extract a binding payment commitment closes the moment peak-season order urgency flips the leverage in Star's favour.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. OVD is stable and below baseline, but the 60d+ aged tail and 204d DSO demand a formal, time-bound collection gate before any further credit or investment expansion.

summary_3lines: Star Appliances is sustaining its 8× growth trajectory at a stable 1.0× T12M buy pace into peak season, yet SAR 1.33M in 60d+ overdue (23% of total AR) and a 204d DSO on 90d terms mean LG is carrying structural overexposure on its own balance sheet.

OVD has been flat at 33.7% for five consecutive days and sits below the account's own YTD baseline of 35.4% — this is Q2 Anomaly territory: peak-season stocking velocity inflating DSO, not a default trajectory — but the 60d+ bucket is too aged (150d+ from issuance) to dismiss as seasonal lag.

If no written payment commitment is secured this week, peak orders will stack new volume on top of unresolved aged AR, driving total exposure toward SAR 7–8M by August with no structured recovery path and zero leverage remaining once LG needs the account to clear quota.
synthesis.decision_required: Formally communicate a conditional shipment gate to Star Appliances this week — 60d+ overdue must reduce to below SAR 700K as a precondition for June order releases — and secure a written, milestone-anchored repayment plan (June/July/August tranches) by May 13 while LG still holds supply leverage.

options.option: Option A — Enforce the gate now (recommended)
options.consequence: LG retains supply leverage before peak demand locks in; written milestones create accountability; if Star meets targets, OVD normalises by Q3 and credit expansion conversation reopens on solid footing. Marginal friction risk with an A+ account is offset by structural AR recovery.

options.option: Option B — Defer to June review with informal collection pressure only
options.consequence: Peak-season order volume accumulates on top of the existing SAR 1.33M aged tail; total AR likely reaches SAR 7–8M by August; leverage inverts as LG needs sell-out numbers and Star knows it; Q3 becomes a forced concession rather than a negotiated recovery.

timeline.step: Joint collection meeting — Star Appliances finance + LG credit team: secure written 60d+ repayment plan with June / July / August milestone tranches and formally communicate the SAR 700K gate threshold for June order release
Activate sell-out support package (installer incentive programme + POS display refresh) to accelerate Star's inventory-to-cash cycle and reduce working capital pressure organically
First payment milestone check — confirm 60d+ bucket movement against written plan; if no progress, escalate to formal credit hold and halt new shipment approvals pending finance review

finance.headline: Grade-C account in full collection crisis — 370d YTD DSO, all overdue hardened in 60d+ bucket, and new exposure incorrectly extended to a 100%-delinquent account on Apr 30.
finance.analysis:
AR health is severely compromised: SAR 570,086 (61.1% of balance) sits entirely in the 60d+ aging bucket with zero fresh overdue — the delinquency is hardened, not transitional. More critically, the OVD ratio was 100% on April 29; the apparent improvement to 61.1% on April 30 does not reflect collections — it reflects approximately SAR 362K in new goods shipped to a fully delinquent account, inflating gross exposure from SAR 0.57M to SAR 0.93M. This is a credit control breach, not a recovery signal.

Payment behavior is structurally broken. YTD DSO of 370 days is 4x the contracted 90-day credit term, and even the T12M DSO of 194 days is more than double the limit. Revenue is in structural decline — SAR 1.8M (2024) → SAR 1.5M (2025) → 0.5x buy-pace in 2026 — signaling either a shrinking dealer operation or a liquidity implosion at the customer level. The account is no longer a growth contributor; it is a pure collection problem with worsening trajectory.

Credit policy must move decisively to protect LG's position. Extending further credit to this account under current conditions is indefensible. Every material indicator — Grade C, Q3-Critical quadrant, 4x DSO breach, fully hardened aging, decelerating buy-pace — converges on a single conclusion: credit hold until arrears are cleared. Any delay in tightening increases LG's unrecoverable exposure as the account's business velocity continues to slow.
finance.credit_rationale: YTD DSO is 4x the credit term, all overdue is hardened in the 60d+ bucket, and new shipments were incorrectly authorized while the account was 100% delinquent — credit limit must be reduced to SAR 0 (hold) until full clearance.
action: Immediate credit hold: freeze all new shipments until SAR 570,086 (60d+ overdue) is fully collected — no exceptions pending written payment commitment
Escalate to Sales Director for emergency field visit within 7 days: obtain signed payment schedule with weekly milestones tied to reinstatement of supply
Conduct credit control audit on the ~SAR 362K Apr 30 shipment authorized while account was 100% overdue — identify approval breakdown and enforce policy compliance to prevent recurrence
finance.risk_reason: YTD DSO 370d (4x credit term), 100% of overdue in hardened 60d+ bucket, structurally declining revenue at 0.5x pace, and new credit extended to a fully delinquent account on Apr 30.

sales.headline: Multi-year sell-in collapse (1.8M→1.4M→0.3M YTD) paired with 370d YTD DSO signals a financially distressed account in active disengagement — not a channel slowdown but a structural breakdown.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated across every measurable dimension. Sell-in has declined 19.5% YoY in 2025 and is tracking to roughly 750K annualized in 2026 — a 58% collapse vs 2024 peak. The 0.5x buy pace vs T12M confirms deceleration is accelerating, not stabilizing. The April 30 balance jump from 0.57M to 0.93M (while overdue held flat at 570K) indicates new shipments were extended despite an already 100% overdue position — a risk control failure that compounded exposure. Without sell-out data, channel velocity cannot be confirmed, but the sell-in trajectory alone suggests either severe inventory overhang (product sitting unsold in the channel) or active competitor substitution — possibly both. DSO of 370 days YTD against a 90d credit term means LG is effectively financing this account for over a year with no return signal.

The 60d+ overdue bucket holding 100% of the SAR 570K overdue portion, with zero movement across the past six daily snapshots, is the most concerning signal. No partial payment, no aging improvement — static. This is not a timing issue; this is a liquidity or willingness problem. The account's Grade C classification and Q3 Critical quadrant placement are accurate reflections of the risk. Root cause is most likely a combination of financial stress at the distributor level (possibly over-leveraged across multiple suppliers), inventory that is not clearing at retail, and reduced commitment to LG as a primary brand.

No investment case exists under current conditions. Extending credit, co-investing in sell-out support, or increasing marketing exposure to this account would increase LG's unsecured receivables exposure without any evidence of improved channel absorption or payment behavior. The only actionable path is collection-focused engagement, a hard credit freeze on new orders, and a field visit to assess inventory reality and counterpart financial health before any re-engagement discussion.
sales.investment_rationale: 370d YTD DSO, 61% overdue balance fully aged past 60 days, and a 0.5x buy pace with no collection movement across six consecutive days leaves no investment thesis — only credit risk reduction.
action: Immediate credit hold: freeze all new shipments until SAR 570,086 overdue is cleared or a binding payment schedule is signed — do not release based on verbal commitment only
Emergency owner-level collection call within 48 hours: establish weekly payment milestone schedule (e.g., SAR 100K/week for 6 weeks) with escalation clause; document in writing

Field visit within 2 weeks: physically assess on-hand LG inventory, observe competitor brand presence on shelf, and determine whether the account is operationally active or winding down before any re-engagement or write-off conversation
sales.channel_reason: 58% sell-in collapse vs 2024, 370d YTD DSO, 100% of overdue aged 60d+, zero collection movement — all indicators point to structural disengagement, not cyclical softness

synthesis.headline: SAR 570K 전액 60d+ 연체 고착 + 100% 연체 상태에서 신규 출하 승인된 신용통제 실패 — 즉각 크레딧 홀드 없이는 미회수채권 확대 불가피

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 370d YTD DSO(계약의 4배), 연체 전액 60d+ 고착, 6일 연속 회수 無 — 투자 근거 없고 회수만이 유일한 액션.

summary_3lines: SAR 932K 잔액 중 SAR 570K(61%)가 60d+ 연체로 완전 고착되었고, 4월 30일 100% 연체 상태임에도 약 SAR 362K 신규 출하가 승인되어 총 노출이 오히려 확대됨
YTD DSO 370일(90일 계약 대비 4배), 매출 58% 붕괴(2024 SAR 1.8M → 2026 연환산 SAR 0.75M), 0.5x 구매 페이스 — 사이클 둔화가 아닌 유동성 위기 또는 LG 브랜드 이탈로 판단
이번 주 크레딧 홀드 미조치 시 연체 잔액 추가 고착 + 신규 출하 재승인 위험 → 미회수채권 규모 확대 및 계정 자체 청산 시 전액 손실 처리 시나리오 현실화
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 SAR 570,086 완납 또는 주별 납부 스케줄(서면) 수령을 조건으로 한 즉각 크레딧 홀드 실행 여부를 확정하라

options.option: Option A — 즉각 크레딧 홀드 + 48시간 내 오너 직접 회수 협상
options.consequence: 신규 노출 차단, 주별 납부 일정(예: SAR 100K × 6주) 서면 확보 시 단계적 공급 재개 가능 — 미회수 리스크 통제 가능 구간 진입

options.option: Option B — 현행 유지(출하 지속, 회수 모니터링)
options.consequence: 100% 연체 계정에 추가 신용 노출 누적, 계정 유동성 악화 시 SAR 932K+ 전액 대손 위험 — 재무·신용통제 정책 위반 지속

timeline.step: 즉각 크레딧 홀드 실행 — 모든 신규 출하 중단, 4월 30일 SAR 362K 출하 승인 경위 감사 착수
오너 레벨 회수 협상 콜 — 주별 납부 마일스톤(서면) 확보, 미이행 시 법적 조치 에스컬레이션 조건 명시
현장 방문 — LG 재고 실사, 경쟁사 쉘프 점유율 확인, 계정 영업 지속 여부 판단 후 재계약 또는 대손처리 방향 결정

finance.headline: Air Care Co. has crossed into default-risk territory — 100% AR overdue with SAR 1.2M aged beyond 60 days and a 2026 purchase rate at near-zero signal structural inability to pay.
finance.analysis:
The AR position is critically impaired. As of May 1, the entire SAR 1,535,443 balance flipped to 100% overdue and has remained at ceiling for six consecutive days with no payment movement. The 60d+ bucket at SAR 1,215,148 (79.1% of total balance) is the most alarming component — this tranche is already 30–90 days past the 90-day credit term, meaning the debt is functionally delinquent, not merely late. The OVD trend from 94.6% to 100% is not a fluctuation; it is a hard stop in payment activity.

Collection risk is at its highest tier. YTD DSO of 1,155 days against a 90-day credit term means the account owes over 3 years of its current purchase run-rate — a ratio that is arithmetically catastrophic and practically unrecoverable under normal trading terms. Even the T12M DSO of 240 days (2.7x the credit term, which already exceeded LG's DPO of 180 days) has now been made irrelevant by the 2026 collapse: the account has purchased only SAR 166,129 in 2026 (5.8% of 2025 volume, 0.2x T12M pace). A buyer who is decelerating sharply while carrying a fully frozen 100% overdue AR balance is exhibiting a classic pre-exit or insolvency pattern.

On credit policy, there is no scenario that justifies maintaining — let alone increasing — the current credit limit. Revenue is declining YoY (2024: SAR 3.1M → 2025: SAR 2.9M → 2026: SAR 0.17M run-rate) while the AR balance remains sticky and unpaid. The account is not credit-constrained; it is liquidity-constrained or disengaging from LG. Further exposure would convert a recoverable SAR 1.5M debt into a larger write-off risk. Immediate credit suspension is the only defensible position.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% overdue balance with 79% aged 60d+, YTD DSO at 1,155 days vs 90-day terms, and near-zero 2026 purchase activity justify full credit suspension with reinstatement conditional on substantial OVD clearance.
action: Initiate credit hold immediately — block all new orders and shipments until at least 50% of overdue balance (SAR 768K) is cleared
Escalate to Country Sales Manager within 48 hours: require written payment commitment for SAR 1,215,148 (60d+ tranche) with milestone dates by May 13, 2026
Refer SAR 1,215,148 aged 60d+ tranche to legal/collections review if no signed payment plan is received by May 15, 2026
finance.risk_reason: 100% AR overdue, 79% in 60d+ bucket, YTD DSO 1,155d vs 90d credit term, and 0.2x buy pace — all four indicators simultaneously at distress levels

sales.headline: Air Care Co. has effectively ceased purchasing while carrying 100% overdue AR — this is a collection crisis, not a channel opportunity.
sales.analysis:
Channel disengagement is near-total. Sell-in declined 6.6% YoY from 2024 to 2025, but 2026 has collapsed to SAR 166K through early May — roughly 5% of the prior full-year run rate. A buy pace of 0.2x T12M average heading into peak season (May–Sep) is not a seasonal dip; it is a near-complete stop. There is no pre-season stocking signal whatsoever, which in the IR_Others channel almost always precedes exit or a competitor pivot.

The AR picture confirms financial stress as the primary driver. 100% of the SAR 1.54M balance is overdue, with SAR 1.22M aged beyond 60 days — that cohort predates the new year. YTD DSO of 1,155 days is not a ratio anomaly; it signals that LG has been effectively extending unsecured credit to a non-paying account for multiple cycles. T12M DSO at 240 days already exceeds the 90-day credit term by 2.7x. The OVD trend locked at 100% since May 1 with zero partial payment activity in the snapshot window eliminates any 'timing lag' excuse.

Investment in this account — whether sell-out support, co-marketing, or credit increase — would increase LG's exposure to an account that cannot or will not service its existing obligations. The 90-day credit term is being ignored entirely. With peak season opening, any new shipment issued without full AR clearance would compound the loss. The only rational posture is collection-first, shipment freeze until material repayment, and reassessment of the account's Grade B classification downward.
sales.investment_rationale: 100% overdue AR, YTD DSO of 1,155 days, and 0.2x buy pace into peak season indicate financial insolvency risk — any new investment deepens LG's exposure with no recovery pathway visible.
action: Freeze all open orders and new credit immediately; issue formal overdue notice citing SAR 1,215,148 in 60d+ aging and demand structured repayment schedule within 7 days
Escalate to Finance and Country Sales Director for legal/collection review — the 60d+ bucket predates Q1 and warrants formal demand letter or engagement of collection agency

Conduct in-person account visit before end of May to assess whether Air Care is operationally active; verify if inventory from prior sell-ins has been sold through or is sitting — determines whether partial debt-for-goods recovery is feasible
Reclassify account from Grade B to Grade D pending collection outcome; remove from peak season sell-in targets to avoid inflating channel forecasts
sales.channel_reason: Buy pace at 0.2x with 100% AR overdue and no pre-season stocking into May peak — account is effectively inactive and financially distressed.

synthesis.headline: Air Care Co.는 회수 불능 직전 단계 — SAR 1.54M 전액 연체, 2026 구매 사실상 중단. 이번 주 신용 차단과 법적 회수 절차 착수가 유일한 방어선이다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 100% 연체 + 0.2x 구매 속도 + 피크 시즌 무반응은 거래 관계 종료 또는 유동성 파탄 신호이며, 추가 익스포저는 손실을 키울 뿐이다.

summary_3lines: SAR 1.54M 전액 연체 중 79%(SAR 1.22M)가 60일 이상 경과했고, 5월 1일 이후 6일 연속 단 1리얄도 입금되지 않았다.
2026년 매입액은 SAR 166K — 전년 대비 94% 붕괴. 피크 시즌(5~9월) 진입에도 재고 확보 움직임이 전혀 없다는 것은 계정이 LG를 이탈 중이거나 유동성이 고갈됐음을 의미한다.
이번 주 신용 차단 없이 시즌 오더를 허용하면 현재 SAR 1.54M 손실이 SAR 2M+ 규모로 확대되며, 60d+ 트랜치는 법적 회수 가능 시효를 벗어날 위험이 높아진다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 신용 차단 공식화 + SAR 1,215,148(60d+ 트랜치) 대상 법적 회수 절차 착수 여부를 결정해야 한다.

options.option: Option A — 즉시 신용 차단 + 법적 회수 병행 (권고)
options.consequence: 추가 익스포저 차단. 5월 13일까지 상환 계획 수령 실패 시 법적 청구로 자동 전환. SAR 1.22M 회수 가능성을 최대로 보전하는 유일한 경로.

options.option: Option B — 부분 납입 조건부 소량 오더 허용
options.consequence: 계정 유지 명목으로 추가 리스크 부담. 상환 약속 없는 납입 이력 전무 — 조건 미이행 시 익스포저만 확대되고 법적 회수 시 불리한 선례 생성.

timeline.step: 신용 차단 발령 — 모든 오픈 오더 및 신규 출하 즉시 블록
공식 연체 통보서 발송 + SAR 1,215,148 분할 상환 계획 요청 (서면)
납입 계획 미수령 시 법적 청구 또는 추심 대행사 의뢰 — 계정 등급 B→D 하향 동시 시행

finance.headline: Zero-revenue 2026 account with 100% aged overdue — effectively dormant but still carrying unresolved 60d+ exposure.
finance.analysis:
AR health is structurally impaired: the entire SAR 47,017 balance sits in the 60d+ bucket, meaning no payment has been received within terms. While the absolute balance has shrunk from SAR 0.20M to SAR 0.05M between late April and early May, indicating partial recovery in progress, the OVD ratio has remained locked at 100% throughout — the improvement is in volume, not payment behavior. The T12M DSO of 28 days is deceptively flattering; it reflects historical 2025 transactions, not current behavior, and the YTD DSO is undefined due to zero 2026 sell-in.

Collection risk is elevated but not runaway given the reduced exposure. The core concern is abandonment risk: with sell-in collapsing 71% from SAR 2.1M in 2024 to SAR 618K in 2025, and zero activity in 2026, this account appears to be in a managed wind-down — or has shifted sourcing elsewhere. A Grade C rating with no current trading activity makes recovery leverage minimal; LG has less commercial pull over dormant accounts.

Credit policy must reflect the reality that this account is not active. Extending or maintaining credit limits serves no operational purpose and creates asymmetric downside: if the account re-engages without clearing the current overdue, exposure compounds. The SAR 47K must be collected in full before any credit facility is reinstated. Recommend credit block until zero overdue, then re-grade at re-activation.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% aged 60d+ overdue, zero 2026 revenue, and Grade C rating together eliminate any commercial justification for maintaining active credit — limit should be set to zero until full collection and account reactivation.
action: Immediately place account on credit hold: no shipments or orders processed until SAR 47,017 overdue is cleared in full
Assign collections escalation to responsible sales rep with 10-day deadline — leverage remaining supplier relationship before account goes fully dormant
Conduct viability review: determine if account has churned to competitor or ceased AC distribution; if churned, write-off probability assessment needed for Q2 provisioning
finance.risk_reason: 100% OVD all in 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 revenue activity, Grade C, and 71% YoY sell-in decline signal high abandonment risk with limited collection leverage.

sales.headline: AL SAFWA PLUS COMPANY has effectively gone dark in 2026 — zero sell-in entering peak season with 100% overdue AR, signaling a high-probability disengagement or business distress.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated. Sell-in collapsed 71% YoY from SAR 2.1M (2024) to SAR 618K (2025), and has dropped to zero in 2026 — with May marking the start of the Saudi peak season. Any active AC dealer preparing for peak should have been stocking since March; the complete absence of purchase activity through May 2026 is not a seasonal lag, it is disengagement. The small partial payment received between May 1–3 (balance reduced from ~SAR 200K to SAR 47K) indicates the account is still operationally alive, but it is clearing old debt rather than transacting new business. Root cause hypotheses in priority order: (1) Credit block — LG likely froze shipments due to overdue AR, making the zero buy a forced stop rather than a voluntary one; (2) Financial distress — 100% of outstanding balance sitting in 60d+ bucket, a C-grade account with no 2026 cash deployment; (3) Competitor pivot — with blocked LG credit, the account may have shifted procurement to Samsung or Gree on better terms; (4) Business contraction — retail footprint may have shrunk, reducing AC offtake capacity organically.

The investment calculus is straightforward and unfavorable. This is a Grade C IR_Others account — not a strategic anchor — with a compounding risk profile: zero revenue contribution in 2026, elevated credit risk, and no behavioral signal suggesting near-term volume recovery. T12M DSO of 28d reflects historical payment discipline that has now broken down; the current 60d+ overdue block is a structural deterioration, not a temporary blip. Extending credit or committing sell-out support into this account would burn resources with negligible probability of return during the 2026 peak window.

The sole investable action is collection. The SAR 47,017 overdue balance must be fully recovered before any commercial re-engagement is considered. Only after AR clearance and a credible forward order commitment (ideally prepaid or reduced credit terms) should a channel recovery conversation be reopened — and even then, the account would need to demonstrate structural improvement before being treated as a growth vehicle.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue entering peak season, 100% AR overdue in 60d+ bucket, and Grade C IR_Others classification offer no basis for resource deployment; full collection is the only value-recovery path.
action: Dispatch AR collection team immediately — SAR 47,017 is fully overdue 60d+; escalate to account owner with a hard deadline before May 15
Field visit to confirm operational status and root cause of zero 2026 buy — determine if credit block, competitor switch, or business closure is the driver
Block all new shipments and credit extension until AR is fully cleared; do not negotiate new terms until balance is zero
If account is operational post-collection, propose a micro-restart on 50% prepaid terms with SAR 100–200K ceiling — no return to 90d open credit until 2 consecutive clean payment cycles
Flag internally for quadrant review: if zero buy persists through June (2 full peak months), formally reclassify as inactive and remove from active pipeline
sales.channel_reason: Zero sell-in in 2026 through peak season onset, combined with 100% overdue AR and 71% YoY volume decline — all three signals pointing the same direction.

synthesis.headline: AL SAFWA PLUS COMPANY는 2026년 피크 시즌에 완전 이탈 — SAR 47K 전액 60d+ 연체, 신규 매출 제로. 수금만이 유일한 가치 회수 경로다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 2026년 매출 제로 + 100% 연체 + Grade C IR_Others = 투자 근거 없음; 수금 완료 후 관계 종결 또는 조건부 재개만 논의 가능.

summary_3lines: SAR 47,017 전액이 60일 초과 연체 상태이며 2026년 5월 현재 신규 매입 전무 — 피크 시즌 진입에도 불구하고 계정이 사실상 비활성화됨.
71% YoY 매출 붕괴(2024 SAR 2.1M → 2025 SAR 618K → 2026 SAR 0)는 크레딧 블록·경쟁사 전환·사업 위축 중 하나 이상의 구조적 이탈을 시사하며, 부분 상환(~SAR 150K 회수)은 사업 생존은 확인하나 재거래 의지는 미확인.
이 상태로 방치 시 SAR 47K 대손 처리 가능성 상승, Q2 충당금 편입 불가피, 2026 피크 윈도우 내 계정 회복 기회 완전 소멸.
synthesis.decision_required: 5월 15일 이전까지 SAR 47,017 전액 수금 강제 집행을 개시할 것인가 — 아니면 협상 여지를 주고 기다릴 것인가?

options.option: Option A — 즉각 강제 수금 + 크레딧 블록 유지
options.consequence: SAR 47K 회수 가능성 극대화. 계정 관계 종료 위험 있으나, 현재 실질 거래가 없으므로 기회비용 최소. 5월 15일 미납 시 법적 조치 또는 Q2 대손 처리로 전환.

options.option: Option B — 현장 방문으로 근본 원인 확인 후 조건부 재개 협상
options.consequence: 크레딧 블록 해제 없이 50% 선불 + SAR 100~200K 한도 미니 재시작 제안. 계정이 살아 있고 재거래 의사가 있을 경우에만 유효. 단, 현 연체 전액 완납이 선결 조건이며 협상 지연 시 수금 레버리지 약화.

timeline.step: 담당 영업 + AR팀 현장 방문 — 사업 운영 상태 및 이탈 원인(크레딧 블록 vs 경쟁사 전환 vs 폐업) 확인
SAR 47,017 전액 납부 최후통첩 발송 — 미납 시 법적 조치 또는 대손 처리 명시
수금 결과에 따라 분기: ① 완납 시 50% 선불 조건부 재개 여부 검토 / ② 미납 시 Q2 충당금 편입 + 비활성 계정 공식 분류

finance.headline: Chronic SAR 500K overdue masked by April 30 new shipment — collection has not improved, exposure has grown.
finance.analysis:
The OVD% drop from 86.4% (Apr 29) to 43.4% (Apr 30) is a statistical illusion, not a collection improvement. The absolute overdue balance barely moved (SAR 501K → SAR 499K), while total exposure doubled from ~SAR 580K to SAR 1.15M via a new ~SAR 570K shipment. LG effectively extended fresh credit to an account that was 86% overdue — expanding risk without resolving the underlying delinquency. The 60d+ bucket at SAR 481,751 (96.4% of all overdue) confirms the chronic nature: this debt has been aging without resolution for at minimum two billing cycles.

Collection risk is severe. YTD DSO of 254 days is 2.8× the credit term (90d) and 1.4× the DPO ceiling (180d), meaning LG is effectively financing this account far beyond any policy guardrail. The T12M DSO of 206d shows this is not a 2026 anomaly — structural inability to pay on time predates this year. The zero balance in the 0-30d bucket is particularly alarming: no current payments are flowing in, which rules out 'slow but steady' repayment and points to active liquidity stress on the account side.

On the credit policy dimension, the account fails every threshold: Grade C classification, Q3 Critical quadrant, 2026 buy pace decelerating to 0.8× T12M average, and DSO exceeding both credit term and DPO. The April 30 shipment should not have been released without a minimum partial collection on the existing overdue. Decreasing the credit limit is the only defensible position — any further extension amplifies LG's uncollateralized exposure to a counterparty showing clear signs of financial distress heading into peak season.
finance.credit_rationale: DSO at 2.8× credit term, SAR 481K in 60d+ aging, and new shipment extended onto an 86% OVD base indicates structural inability to pay — further credit exposure is indefensible.
action: Immediate hold on any new shipments until SAR 481,751 (60d+ bucket) is collected in full — escalate to account manager and country GM for approval exception if business pressure mounts.

Dispatch formal overdue notice to account with 14-day ultimatum; engage senior buyer contact (not just purchasing team) and request partial payment plan with weekly tranches as minimum condition for reinstating supply.
Reduce credit limit from current implied ceiling to SAR 600K (covering only the new invoice tranche); flag in SAP for automatic block if overdue exceeds SAR 250K going forward.
finance.risk_reason: SAR 481K in 60d+ aging, YTD DSO 254d (2.8× credit term), new shipment released at 86% OVD — total exposure SAR 1.15M with no collection evidence.

sales.headline: Construction contractor in chronic collection default: SAR 482K aged 60d+ persists while a fresh SAR 570K order doubled LG's exposure overnight without resolving existing arrears.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is structurally compromised. Sell-in grew +26.5% YoY in 2025 (SAR 1.47M), suggesting real project activity, but the 2026 buy pace at 0.8x T12M signals deceleration entering peak season. The April 29–30 balance surge (SAR 0.58M → SAR 1.15M) reveals a large new order placed while 86.4% of the prior balance was already overdue — a critical credit control breach that doubled LG's total exposure in a single day without settling any existing defaults. This is a project-channel buying pattern where orders spike on new contracts but payment lags indefinitely.

The DSO of 254 days against a 90-day credit term (2.8x breach) and T12M DSO of 206 days confirm this is systemic, not situational. The 60d+ overdue bucket at SAR 481,751 represents 97% of total overdue with zero inflow in the 0-30d window — no active repayment is occurring. As a Grade C IR_Others construction contractor, this account has no consistent payment cadence; LG is effectively functioning as an involuntary project financier with no recourse mechanism visible in the data.

With peak season (May–September) arriving, the risk of the new SAR 570K tranche also aging into 60d+ is high absent immediate intervention. No sell-out data is available to infer downstream velocity, but the construction channel context (B2B project installs) means settlement is entirely contingent on project completion and client payment cycles beyond LG's visibility — making forward exposure unacceptable at current DSO levels.
sales.investment_rationale: DSO 254d (2.8x credit term), SAR 482K aged 60d+, and a fresh large order layered on top of unresolved arrears make any incremental investment an unacceptable credit risk for a Grade C account with decelerating buy pace.
action: Initiate formal dunning on SAR 481,751 (60d+ bucket): official demand letter + direct sales visit within 5 business days
Place new SAR 570K order on credit hold: block PGI until 60d+ balance is fully settled or a written repayment schedule is countersigned
Escalate to Watch List: if no material collection progress within 30 days, escalate to trading suspension and AR write-off assessment
sales.channel_reason: DSO 254d vs 90d credit term, SAR 482K 60d+ unresolved, fresh order placed atop deep arrears — collection failure dominates all channel risk signals.

synthesis.headline: LG released a fresh SAR 570K shipment onto an account that was 86% overdue — total exposure is now SAR 1.15M with no active repayment, and the new tranche is already on track to age into 60d+ default.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. SAR 482K is already in chronic default and a new SAR 570K shipment has doubled exposure without resolving a single riyal of arrears.

summary_3lines: A construction contractor (Grade C, 90d term) carries SAR 1.15M total exposure: SAR 482K aged 60d+ in unresolved default and a fresh SAR 570K tranche shipped on April 30 while the account was 86% overdue — a credit control breach that doubled LG's risk overnight.

Structural inability to pay is confirmed by YTD DSO of 254d (2.8× credit term), T12M DSO of 206d, zero inflow in the 0-30d bucket, and a decelerating 2026 buy pace of 0.8× — this is a project-channel buyer leveraging LG as an involuntary financier with no visible repayment cadence.

If no intervention this week, the new SAR 570K tranche will age into the 60d+ bucket by late June, total chronic default will exceed SAR 1M entering peak season, and LG's collection leverage diminishes further as the account's project cycle extends — write-off risk escalates materially.
synthesis.decision_required: Decide this week whether to (A) place a hard credit hold on the new SAR 570K order and issue a formal 14-day ultimatum on the SAR 482K 60d+ bucket, or (B) accept the full SAR 1.15M exposure and attempt a negotiated repayment schedule — there is no middle path at this DSO level.

options.option: Option A — Credit Hold + Formal Dunning (Recommended)
options.consequence: Block PGI on new SAR 570K tranche immediately; issue formal demand letter on SAR 482K with 14-day deadline and weekly payment tranche requirement. LG caps exposure at current SAR 1.15M and regains negotiating leverage before peak season ships more inventory. Risk: account may pause buying entirely — but a Grade C contractor with 254d DSO is not a volume account worth protecting at this exposure level.

options.option: Option B — Negotiate Repayment Plan Without Hold
options.consequence: Continue supply on agreed payment schedule without blocking the new order. If the account honors tranches, DSO gradually normalizes. If it defaults again (historically likely given 206d T12M DSO), LG enters peak season with SAR 1.15M+ in uncollectable AR and zero leverage. This is the high-probability path to a write-off exceeding SAR 1M.

timeline.step: Block PGI on new SAR 570K order and issue formal overdue notice on SAR 481,751 (60d+ bucket) — sales manager + country GM alignment required
Senior buyer contact meeting (not purchasing team): present written repayment schedule demand with minimum SAR 100K first tranche as condition for reinstating supply
timeline.deadline: 2026-05-13 (14-day ultimatum clock starts from notice issuance)

timeline.step: Assessment gate: if SAR 100K+ collected and repayment schedule countersigned, move to Watch List with SAR 600K credit ceiling; if no material progress, escalate to trading suspension and initiate AR write-off assessment for 60d+ bucket

finance.headline: Negligible AR exposure with zero overdue, but structurally slow-paying account in steep multi-year revenue freefall — effectively a dormant relationship.
finance.analysis:
Current AR health is superficially clean: SAR 8,137 balance, 0% overdue across all aging buckets, and a stable flat OVD trend over the past 6 days. In absolute terms, collection risk is de minimis — even a full write-off would be immaterial. However, the structural payment behavior tells a different story: YTD DSO of 144 days sits 54 days beyond the 90-day credit term, and T12M DSO of 119 days confirms this is not a one-off anomaly but a chronic pattern of slow settlement. The account routinely takes 30–50% longer to pay than its contractual obligation, which is inconsistent with the clean OVD optics and warrants scrutiny of how invoices are being dated or settled.

The more pressing concern is the revenue trajectory, which signals a relationship in terminal decline. Sell-in collapsed from SAR 164,212 in 2024 to SAR 17,933 in 2025 (−89% YoY), and 2026 YTD of SAR 7,076 annualizes to roughly SAR 17K — flat at best, likely still falling given the 0.8x buy-pace deceleration vs. T12M average. Grade D classification aligns with this deterioration. This is not an account constrained by credit limits; it is an account that has largely exited the purchasing relationship with LG, possibly sourcing from competitors or reducing AC category exposure altogether.

For credit policy, there is no financial case for expansion: the account cannot absorb more credit productively given its buying inactivity, and the DSO overage suggests it struggles to honor even current terms. Maintaining the existing limit preserves optionality without adding risk. The practical priority is not credit management but account diagnosis — why did a SAR 164K account shrink to SAR 7K in two years? That answer determines whether this is a salvageable relationship or one to close out gracefully.
finance.credit_rationale: Exposure is already negligible and the account shows no buying capacity to utilize additional credit, making any change — up or down — commercially irrelevant at this stage.
action: Conduct account visit or call to diagnose root cause of 96% sell-in decline since 2024 — competitor switch, liquidity issue, or category exit
Flag structural DSO breach (144d vs 90d term) to sales team and require next PO to be settled within terms before new shipment approval
If no credible recovery plan emerges within 30 days, reclassify to inactive and redirect field coverage resources to higher-potential IR accounts
finance.risk_reason: Zero current overdue masks a chronic DSO overage of 54 days beyond credit term and a Grade D account in near-complete revenue collapse — low absolute risk but deteriorating relationship fundamentals.

sales.headline: AL MUTTMAKEN has effectively disengaged from LG — 89% volume collapse over two years with no recovery signal entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Sell-in tells a stark story: SAR 164K in 2024 collapsed to SAR 18K in 2025 (-89%), and 2026 YTD sits at SAR 7K with buy pace decelerating further at 0.8x the already-depressed T12M baseline. Entering May — the gateway to Saudi peak season — with this trajectory means the account is unlikely to contribute meaningfully to summer volume. The current AR balance of SAR 8,137 with zero overdue looks clean only because the account is essentially dormant, not because payment behavior has improved; YTD DSO of 144d against a 90d credit term reveals historically slow settlement.

Root cause is most likely a competitor switch or business restructuring that occurred in late 2024/early 2025. The drop is too steep and too sustained to be seasonal or inventory-driven — a dealer holding overhang from 2024 would have digested it by mid-2025 and resumed ordering. The continued deceleration into 2026 rules out a one-off disruption. Samsung, Gree, or Midea are the most probable beneficiaries in the IR_Others channel.

With Grade D classification, minimal financial exposure, no sell-out visibility, and zero momentum signal ahead of peak season, there is no investment thesis here. Resources allocated to this account — whether credit increase, co-marketing, or sell-out support — would be competing against an incumbent competitor relationship that has had 12+ months to entrench. The prudent call is a clean exit: cap exposure at current levels, do not extend additional credit, and redirect the sales team's bandwidth to accounts showing active buying intent in the May–September window.
sales.investment_rationale: 89% volume collapse over two years with continued deceleration entering peak season indicates a competitor switch already completed; no momentum exists to justify resource allocation.
action: Conduct a single direct outreach call to understand why volume dropped post-2024 — confirm whether this is a competitor switch, business downsizing, or relationship issue before finalizing exit decision
Do not extend credit limit beyond SAR 8K current exposure; collect current balance on schedule and monitor for any DSO slippage given 144d YTD vs 90d terms
If outreach confirms competitor switch, formally de-prioritize the account and reallocate visit frequency to higher-grade IR accounts ramping for peak season
sales.channel_reason: Sell-in down 89% YoY with buy pace decelerating into peak season — account has structurally disengaged from LG.

synthesis.headline: AL MUTTMAKEN is a structurally disengaged account — 89% sell-in collapse over two years with continued deceleration entering peak season; financial exposure is immaterial but the relationship has effectively ended. The only open question is whether a single outreach call reveals a recoverable cause before we formally exit.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. Account is near-dormant with negligible AR exposure, but chronic DSO overage and a 96% cumulative sell-in collapse signal a competitor switch already completed — no investment thesis exists entering Saudi peak season.

summary_3lines: AL MUTTMAKEN's sell-in has collapsed from SAR 164K (2024) to SAR 7K YTD 2026 (-96% cumulative), with buy pace still decelerating at 0.8x a already-depressed T12M baseline as Saudi peak season opens.

The pattern — steep, sustained, and accelerating on the downside — is inconsistent with inventory overhang or a seasonal pause; it points to a completed competitor switch (Samsung / Gree / Midea most probable) in late 2024 or early 2025, now 12+ months entrenched.

If no action is taken, this account contributes zero meaningful volume through the May–September peak window, consumes field coverage that should be redirected to high-grade IR accounts, and drifts into full dormancy by year-end while DSO structurally continues to breach the 90-day credit term.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize one last direct outreach call within 5 business days to confirm root cause — if competitor switch is confirmed, formally de-prioritize and reallocate field coverage to peak-season accounts immediately; if a recoverable cause is found, escalate with a concrete recovery plan before any resource commitment.

options.option: Option A — Single Diagnostic Call then Exit
options.consequence: One call to confirm root cause (competitor switch vs. business downsizing vs. relationship issue); if switch confirmed, formally reclassify as inactive, cap credit at current SAR 8K, redirect visit frequency to higher-grade IR accounts ramping for peak. Cost: minimal. Upside: bandwidth freed for accounts that can actually drive June–August volume.

options.option: Option B — Passive Hold with No Action
options.consequence: Account continues to drift toward zero with no diagnosis; DSO structural breach (144d vs. 90d) goes unaddressed; field resources remain partially allocated to a dormant account through peak season. Risk: low in absolute SAR terms but represents opportunity cost against accounts that could be activated for summer.

timeline.step: Direct outreach call or visit to AL MUTTMAKEN — confirm root cause of 96% decline (competitor switch / liquidity / category exit)
Based on call outcome: either escalate with written recovery plan to sales leadership, or formally log as inactive and reallocate visit frequency
If no credible recovery plan by this date, close out active account management, collect SAR 8K balance on schedule, and monitor DSO compliance only — no new shipments without settlement within terms

finance.headline: Air conditioning land is in full collection default — 100% of SAR 1.78M overdue, YTD DSO at 408 days versus a 90-day credit term, with zero signs of recovery.
finance.analysis:
AR health is at terminal stress. The entire balance of SAR 1,780,906 is overdue with no current portion whatsoever, and SAR 1,153,924 (64.8%) sits in the 60d+ bucket — the hardest segment to recover. The 0-30d bucket of SAR 627K is not 'new' business paying normally; it is simply the most recent layer of a fully delinquent stack. Six consecutive daily snapshots from April 29 to May 4 show a static 100% OVD rate with zero inflow — no partial payments, no aging improvement. This is not a temporary cash-flow delay; this is a frozen account.

The DSO picture makes the risk quantitative. YTD DSO of 408 days is 4.5x the contracted 90-day credit term, and the trailing 12-month DSO of 234 days confirms this is not a 2026 anomaly — the account has never truly paid within terms. The critical flag is the 2025 sell-in surge: from SAR 168K in 2024 to SAR 2.23M in 2025 (13x), with the 2026 buy pace now decelerating sharply to 0.6x the T12M average. This pattern — aggressive volume buildup followed by payment collapse and purchase pullback — is a classic precursor to a distressed or contested receivable. The account may be illiquid, may be disputing invoices, or may be re-routing sourcing away from LG while sitting on unpaid stock.

Credit policy must move to hard stop. The account cannot service its existing obligation, and extending any additional credit would compound an already difficult recovery. LG's exposure of SAR 1.78M on a Grade C / IR_Others account with 408-day DSO is already above acceptable risk tolerance. The priority is cash collection — not relationship maintenance, not volume defense. New shipments should be blocked at the system level until overdue AR falls below 30 days and DSO normalizes below 120 days. Separately, the 60d+ bucket of SAR 1.15M should be escalated immediately to the Sales Director and legal/external collection review, given the age and non-movement.
finance.credit_rationale: With 100% of the balance overdue, YTD DSO at 408 days (4.5x credit term), and zero payment movement across six consecutive snapshots, extending any credit is equivalent to writing off new exposure — credit limit must be reduced to SAR 0 pending full clearance of existing AR.
action: Block all new shipments immediately at ERP/SAP level until overdue AR is fully cleared — no exceptions pending Sales Director written override
Escalate SAR 1,153,924 (60d+ bucket) to legal/external collection within 48 hours; initiate formal demand letter with cure deadline
Schedule emergency collection call with account owner and Sales Director this week — require a signed repayment schedule with weekly milestones as condition for any future credit reinstatement
finance.risk_reason: 100% OVD on SAR 1.78M, YTD DSO 408d vs 90d credit term, 60d+ bucket at 64.8%, decelerating buy pace, and zero payment movement for 6+ consecutive days — maximum collection risk.

sales.headline: Account loaded heavily in 2025 (SAR 2.2M) but has not paid — 100% AR overdue for 6+ consecutive days with YTD DSO at 408d, now buying at 0.6x pace while debt sits unpaid.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is effectively broken. The 2024→2025 sell-in spike (SAR 168K → SAR 2.2M, ~13x) was almost certainly a large stocking order that has not turned into sell-through — evidenced by the 2026 buy pace at only 0.6x T12M average. The account is not re-ordering at the rate that would justify the 2025 load, pointing to an inventory overhang rather than genuine sell-out velocity. With 90-day credit terms and a YTD DSO of 408 days (4.5x the agreed term), the payment discipline was structurally absent from the start of the 2025 relationship. The T12M DSO of 234d confirms this is not a recent deterioration — overdue accumulation has been building for at least a year.

The AR structure is the most concerning signal: SAR 1,153,924 sits in the 60d+ bucket with zero balance in the 30-60d window. This binary distribution (either current or severely aged) indicates the account is not making partial payments to roll the balance — they are simply not paying. The 0-30d bucket (SAR 626,982) likely represents the most recent 2026 purchases still within grace period, which means new shipments are continuing to stack on top of an unresolved 60d+ exposure.

Root cause hypothesis: account used the 2025 LG inventory as working capital float — bought on 90d terms, has not achieved sell-through at the pace needed to fund repayment, and is now decelerating purchases to avoid deepening the hole. Financial stress is the primary driver; inventory overhang is the secondary constraint. Competitor switch is less likely given they are still buying (0.6x pace), but possible if they are shifting volume to Samsung or Gree for newer SKUs while sitting on unsold LG stock.
sales.investment_rationale: With 100% AR overdue, 408d YTD DSO, and decelerating buy pace, any further investment deepens LG's unsecured exposure — collection must precede any commercial relationship rebuild.
action: Immediate credit hold: block all open orders and new shipments until overdue balance is materially reduced — prioritize clearing the SAR 1.15M 60d+ bucket
Escalate to formal collection: schedule in-person meeting within 7 days to establish a structured repayment plan with milestone dates; involve finance and regional credit team
Conduct physical inventory audit at account premises to verify LG stock on hand — if inventory overhang is confirmed, explore consignment reversal or structured buyback to accelerate debt recovery
Review 2025 sell-in authorization trail: who approved the 13x scale-up on a Grade C account with 90d terms — flag for credit policy review to prevent recurrence
Set 30-day collection milestone: if SAR 600K+ not recovered by 2026-06-06, escalate to legal/collections agency and formally mark account for exit
sales.channel_reason: 100% AR overdue for 6 consecutive days, YTD DSO 408d vs 90d terms, buy pace decelerating to 0.6x — account is financially distressed and commercially disengaging.

synthesis.headline: Air conditioning land는 상업적 관계 이전에 채권 회수 문제다 — SAR 1.78M 전액 연체, YTD DSO 408일, 6일 연속 입금 제로. 이번 주 법적 에스컬레이션 여부를 결정하지 않으면 60d+ 버킷 SAR 1.15M은 사실상 대손 처리 수순에 진입한다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — EXIT. 채권 회수가 선행되지 않는 한 추가 상업 투자는 신규 대손 적립과 동의어다.

summary_3lines: 2025년 SAR 2.2M 대량 선적(전년 대비 13배) 이후 계정은 단 한 번도 정상 결제 궤도에 진입하지 않았고, 현재 SAR 1.78M 전액이 연체 — 6일 연속 100% OVD, 입금 움직임 없음.
2025 재고 과적 → 소진 부진 → 결제 재원 부족 → 2026 발주 감속(0.6x)의 악순환으로, 계정은 LG 외상을 운전자본 플로트로 활용한 구조적 신용 부실이며 일시적 유동성 지연이 아님.
이번 주 법적 에스컬레이션 없이 방치하면 60d+ 버킷 SAR 1.15M은 회수 가능성이 급격히 하락하며, 추가 선적이 계속될 경우 Grade C 계정 대 LG의 무담보 익스포저가 더 누적된다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 안에 SAR 1.15M(60d+ 버킷)을 법적 채권 추심으로 즉시 에스컬레이션할 것인지, 아니면 7일 내 대면 협상 후 상환 일정 서명을 조건으로 법적 조치를 1회 유예할 것인지 — 이 두 경로 중 하나를 형님이 직접 결정해야 합니다.

options.option: Option A — 즉시 강경 대응 (Hard Stop + Legal Escalation)
options.consequence: 오늘 ERP/SAP 선적 차단 → 48시간 내 60d+ 버킷 법적 내용증명 발송 → 30일 내 SAR 600K 미회수 시 외부 추심기관 이관. 계정과의 상업 관계는 사실상 종료되나 SAR 1.15M 회수 가능성을 극대화하고 추가 손실을 차단.

options.option: Option B — 7일 협상 창 부여 후 법적 조치 (Structured Negotiation with Hard Deadline)
options.consequence: 7일 내 대면 미팅 + 주간 마일스톤 포함 상환 계획서 서명을 선행 조건으로 법적 조치 1회 유예. 계정이 진정성 있는 상환 의지를 보이는 경우 관계 재건 여지를 남기되, 서명 불이행 시 즉시 Option A로 전환. 단, 7일 추가 지연 중 신규 선적은 완전 차단 유지 필수.

timeline.step: ERP/SAP 신규 선적 전면 차단 — 모든 오픈 오더 홀드, Sales Director 서면 오버라이드 없이 예외 없음
Option A 선택 시: 60d+ SAR 1.15M 법적 내용증명 발송 + 외부 추심 에스컬레이션 개시 / Option B 선택 시: 계정 오너 + Sales Director 대면 미팅 설정 및 상환 일정 서명 요구
30일 회수 마일스톤 점검 — SAR 600K 이상 미회수 시 추심 기관 이관 및 계정 공식 Exit 처리, 2025년 13배 선적 승인 경위 내부 감사 착수

finance.headline: Grade C account in full payment stall — 60d+ overdue dominates the AR book while 2026 purchases have essentially ceased, signaling acute collection risk.
finance.analysis:
AR health is severely deteriorated. Of the SAR 275,673 outstanding balance, SAR 164,537 (59.7%) sits in the 60d+ bucket — the most critical aging tier — while the 30-60d bucket shows zero, indicating no recent structured repayment activity. The single partial payment between Apr-29 (96.9% OVD) and Apr-30 (62.5% OVD) reduced exposure modestly but the improvement has flatlined for six consecutive days, suggesting no further collections momentum. T12M DSO of 109 days is already 1.8x the contractual 60-day credit term; the YTD DSO of 10,123 days is a mathematical distortion caused by near-zero 2026 Sell-In (SAR 3,404), but it underscores that the account is effectively dormant on the buy side while the legacy debt ages unresolved.

The 2025-to-2026 trajectory is the most alarming signal. This account accelerated from SAR 11,754 in 2024 to SAR 922,152 in 2025 — a 78x ramp — then purchased only SAR 3,404 YTD 2026 (0.0x buy pace). This pattern is consistent with an account that loaded inventory on credit in peak season 2025, failed to achieve sufficient Sell-Out velocity, and is now capital-locked. The complete purchase halt in early 2026 — well ahead of the May–September peak — further suggests the dealer is either financially distressed or has been informally blocked from new orders.

Credit policy must move to immediate restriction. Extending or maintaining credit to an account with 62.5% overdue, 60d+ aging at 60% of balance, Grade C standing, and zero 2026 purchase activity would only compound exposure with no offsetting volume benefit. The account is neither capacity-constrained nor healthy — it is in a collection workout state. The priority is recovering SAR 164,537 in aged overdue before any discussion of credit normalization.
finance.credit_rationale: 62.5% overdue rate anchored in the 60d+ bucket with zero 2026 purchase momentum leaves no justification for maintaining current credit exposure; limit must be reduced to zero until aged overdue is fully settled.
action: Initiate immediate field collection visit targeting SAR 164,537 in 60d+ overdue — written demand letter with 7-day cure period and legal escalation notice
Freeze all new shipments and credit issuance effective immediately; flag account in SAP for automatic order block

Engage IR channel manager to assess dealer's inventory position and Sell-Out capability — determine if structured repayment plan (e.g., installment against remaining stock) is viable before pursuing legal recovery
finance.risk_reason: 60d+ overdue at 60% of balance, T12M DSO 1.8x credit term, 2026 buy pace 0.0x, Grade C — collection default risk is high and worsening by inaction.

sales.headline: 2025 대량 매입 후 2026 완전 구매 중단 — 재고 소화 정체 또는 경쟁사 전환 가능성, 연체 62.5%로 신용 리스크 임계점 도달
sales.analysis:
2024년 SAR 11,754에 불과했던 매입이 2025년 SAR 922,152으로 78배 급등한 뒤 2026년 SAR 3,404(피크 시즌 진입 시점 기준)로 사실상 제로 수준으로 붕괴했다. Buy pace 0.0x는 단순 감속이 아닌 완전 구매 정지 신호다. 최소 두 가지 가설이 경합한다: (①) 2025년 과잉 매입으로 재고가 적체되어 소화가 되지 않은 상태이거나, (②) 경쟁사(삼성·Gree 등)로 공급선을 전환하면서 LG 재주문이 끊긴 경우. 피크 시즌(5월~9월) 진입을 앞두고 추가 발주가 전혀 없다는 점은 후자 가능성을 높인다.

재무 건전성은 더 심각하다. 잔액 SAR 275,673 중 62.5%(SAR 172,242)가 연체이며, 그 중 SAR 164,537이 60일 초과 — 약정 크레딧 기간(60일)을 완전히 넘긴 장기 연체다. T12M DSO 109일은 크레딧 텀 대비 49일 초과 운용 중임을 의미한다. 특히 2026-04-29(OVD 96.9%, 잔액 SAR 0.18M) → 2026-04-30(OVD 62.5%, 잔액 SAR 0.28M) 변화가 주목된다: 잔액이 ~SAR 100K 증가하면서 OVD 비율이 낮아진 것은 연체가 해소된 게 아니라 신규 인보이스가 발행된 결과다. 연체 미결제 상태에서 추가 출하가 이루어진 것으로, 신용 노출이 오히려 확대된 상황이다.

투자 관점에서 이 계정에 셀아웃 지원·마케팅 공동투자·크레딧 확대를 집행할 근거가 전혀 없다. Grade C, IR_Others 채널, Q3 Critical, buy pace 0.0x 조합은 수익 기여 가능성이 낮음을 명확히 보여준다. 현 시점에서 유일한 실행 우선순위는 SAR 164,537 이상의 연체 회수이며, 신규 출하는 즉시 동결해야 한다.
sales.investment_rationale: 피크 시즌 재진입 의지 없음(buy pace 0.0x) + 60일 초과 장기 연체 SAR 164,537 미해소 상태에서 추가 투자는 신용 손실 심화만 초래
action: 즉시 신규 출하 동결 — 2026-04-30 발행 신규 인보이스(~SAR 100K 추정) 포함, 연체 전액 해소 전까지 크레딧 라인 중단
60일 초과 연체 SAR 164,537 전액 즉시 납부 요청 및 납부 계획서 서면 징구 (미이행 시 법적 회수 절차 개시 검토)
현장 방문 통해 2025년 대량 매입 재고 실사 — 재고 소화 여부 확인 후 경쟁사 전환 여부 판단, 결과에 따라 거래 지속 또는 종결 결정
sales.channel_reason: buy pace 0.0x + 60d+ 연체 62.5% + 피크 시즌 재주문 없음 — 채널 기능 사실상 정지

synthesis.headline: 2025년 SAR 922K 매입 후 대금 미회수 상태로 피크 시즌 재진입 없음 — 즉각 신용 동결 및 SAR 164,537 법적 회수 절차 개시 여부를 이번 주 결정해야 한다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 60d+ 연체 SAR 164,537 미해소·buy pace 0.0x·피크 시즌 재주문 無 — 투자 근거 전무, 회수 집중만 남았다.

summary_3lines: 2025년 78배 급증 매입(SAR 922K) 후 2026년 구매 완전 정지 — 잔액 SAR 275,673 중 62.5%(SAR 172,242)가 연체이며 SAR 164,537은 60일 초과 장기 연체로 약정 크레딧 텀(60d)을 완전히 넘어섰다.
2026-04-30 잔액이 SAR 0.18M → SAR 0.28M으로 증가하면서 OVD 비율이 낮아진 것은 연체 해소가 아닌 신규 인보이스 발행 결과 — 연체 미결 상태에서 추가 출하가 이루어져 신용 노출이 확대된 상황이며, 이후 6일간 OVD 62.5%에 완전 고착되었다.
지금 아무것도 하지 않으면 60d+ 연체가 추가 노화되어 법적 회수 난도가 높아지고, 피크 시즌(5~9월) 내 자금 회전 기회마저 소멸 — SAR 164,537 전액을 대손으로 처리해야 하는 최악 시나리오로 직행한다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주(5월 9일까지) SAR 164,537 즉시 납부 요청 공문 발송 + 7일 내 미이행 시 법적 회수 절차 자동 개시 여부를 승인할 것.

options.option: Option A — 즉각 신용 동결 + 7일 최후통첩 + 법적 에스컬레이션
options.consequence: 신규 출하 전면 중단, 서면 납부 요청 발송, 미이행 시 법무팀 이관 — 계정 관계 종결 위험 있으나 SAR 164,537 회수 가능성 최대화. 피크 시즌 추가 노출 완전 차단.

options.option: Option B — 현장 방문 + 재고 실사 선행 + 분할 상환 협의
options.consequence: 2025년 적체 재고 소화 여부 확인 후 구조화된 상환 계획(재고 처분 연동) 협상 — 법적 비용 절감 가능하나 회수 기간 연장 및 추가 지연 위험 내포. 경쟁사 전환 확인 시 협상력 소멸.

timeline.step: SAP 즉시 오더 블록 설정 + 신규 크레딧 라인 완전 동결 (2026-04-30 발행 인보이스 포함)
SAR 164,537 즉시 납부 요청 공문 발송 (7일 큐어 기간 명시, 미이행 시 법적 회수 개시 경고 포함) + IR 채널 매니저 현장 방문 및 재고 실사
현장 방문 결과 기반 최종 판단 — 분할 상환 계획 수용(Option B 진행) 또는 법무 이관 결정

finance.headline: Zero overdue on the surface, but a 143-day DSO against a 60-day credit term signals structural slow-pay behavior in a declining-volume account.
finance.analysis:
AR is technically clean: SAR 363,943 balance, zero overdue across all aging buckets, and a stable OVD flat-line at 0.0% for the past six days. At face value this reads as a healthy account. However, the DSO tells a different story — YTD DSO of 143d is 2.4x the contractual credit term of 60d, and even the T12M DSO of 109d is 1.8x the credit term. This is not a one-time lag; it is a structural payment pattern. The account systematically pays well beyond terms, which means the current zero-overdue reading likely reflects recent invoices that have not yet matured past the 60-day threshold — not a genuine improvement in payment discipline.

Collection risk is elevated but not acute. The immediate exposure is SAR 364K, which is manageable in absolute size, and the account has no 60d+ bucket — meaning no payment is in crisis territory today. But with Grade C classification, decelerating buy pace at 0.8x versus T12M, and 2026 Sell-In at SAR 319K against SAR 1.44M in 2025 (roughly 22% of last year's pace for the same period), the business trajectory is weakening. A shrinking revenue base paired with a DSO that chronically breaches the credit term raises the question of whether the account is managing its own liquidity by using LG as a 143-day financing vehicle.

Credit policy should remain unchanged. The zero-overdue status does not justify a credit increase — buy pace is decelerating, not volume-constrained by credit availability. Conversely, a reduction is not yet warranted given no current delinquency and a small absolute exposure. The correct posture is to hold the line: enforce the 60-day term strictly on new shipments, monitor the current SAR 364K balance as it ages through June, and reassess if any portion rolls past 60d. If DSO remains above 120d through peak season (May–July), a formal credit review should be triggered.
finance.credit_rationale: No current delinquency keeps decrease off the table, but declining volume and 2.4x credit-term DSO provide zero basis to expand exposure.
action: Flag the SAR 363,943 balance for active 60-day watch — set a calendar alert for early July to verify no bucket migration past credit term
Enforce strict 60-day payment terms on any new orders this peak season; do not ship additional volume until the current balance clears or the account demonstrates on-time settlement

Initiate a sales-led conversation to understand the volume decline (2026 at ~22% of 2025 pace) — whether this reflects competitor shift, liquidity stress, or market contraction changes the risk profile materially
finance.risk_reason: Structurally pays at 143d DSO against a 60d term with declining 2026 buy pace — not in default today, but chronic slow-pay in a weakening account warrants active monitoring through peak season.

sales.headline: Al Rowad is buying less and paying slower — a Grade C account entering peak season at 0.8x pace with DSO 2.4x its credit term.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is deteriorating on two fronts simultaneously. Sell-in has collapsed to SAR 319K YTD vs SAR 1.44M full-year 2025 — even annualised, 2026 pace is tracking roughly 55% of last year. The 0.8x buy pace vs T12M average confirms this is not a seasonal timing gap but a structural slowdown. With peak season (May–Sep) now open, an account that intends to grow should be loading inventory aggressively; Al Rowad is not. Root cause hypotheses in priority order: (1) competitor displacement — Samsung or Chinese brands offering better margin at the C-grade tier, (2) sell-out congestion — prior LG stock from 2025 still sitting in their warehouse, suppressing re-order velocity, (3) financial capacity constraint — the DSO signal supports this.

The DSO picture is the most alarming dimension. YTD DSO of 143 days against a 60-day credit term means Al Rowad is structurally paying at 2.4x its contracted terms. T12M DSO of 109 days confirms this is not a 2026 anomaly — it is habitual. The current SAR 364K balance is 100% current (OVD = 0%), but only because the most recent purchase (~SAR 110K, loaded Apr 29–30) has not yet aged. The clean OVD snapshot is a lagging indicator, not a health signal. The YTD avg OVD baseline of 35.4% tells the real story: historically more than a third of this account's balance has been overdue at any given time.

Investment case is weak. A Grade C IR_Others account with decelerating volumes, a DSO nearly 2.5x its credit term, and no sell-out data to anchor upside forecasts does not justify pushing credit or promotional resources into peak season. The right posture is to stabilise the receivable, understand whether the buy-pace deceleration is inventory-driven or competitor-driven, and hold investment until evidence of re-engagement emerges — ideally a May buy that exceeds T12M monthly average.
sales.investment_rationale: Chronic DSO 2.4x credit term combined with 0.8x buy deceleration entering peak season makes incremental credit or promotional spend unjustifiable without first diagnosing whether the slowdown is stock overhang or competitor displacement.
action: Conduct a floor visit in May to physically check LG stock levels — if inventory overhang exists, co-develop a sell-out plan (bundle/promo) before any new shipment is approved
Require settlement of the current SAR 364K balance on or before the 60-day due date as a hard gate before authorising any peak-season loading beyond T12M monthly run rate

Run a competitor displacement check: ask the sales rep to confirm whether Al Rowad has added Samsung / Chinese brand shelf space in 2026 and at what terms — this determines whether this is a recoverable account or a managed wind-down
sales.channel_reason: Buying is decelerating into peak season and DSO is chronically 2.4x credit terms, but AR is currently clean and the account is still active — not yet red.

synthesis.headline: AR is clean today only because the latest invoices haven't aged yet — structurally this account pays at 2.4x its credit term while buying 78% less than last year entering peak season.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. A grade-C account in volume freefall with chronic slow-pay is not a peak-season investment target; it is a receivable to protect.

summary_3lines: Al Rowad's SAR 364K balance shows zero overdue today, but YTD DSO of 143d against a 60d term reveals a structural habit of using LG as a 143-day financing vehicle — the clean snapshot is a timing artifact, not a behavior change.

Buy pace has collapsed to 0.8x T12M and 2026 Sell-In is tracking at roughly 22% of full-year 2025 pace entering peak season, with no sell-out data to explain whether this is inventory overhang or competitor displacement — both diagnoses carry different remedies.

If we load peak-season inventory on current terms without resolving payment behavior, we risk SAR 500K+ exposure to an account with a 35.4% historical OVD baseline and no demonstrated urgency to re-engage — the downside is a red-flag account in September with summer stock we can't recall.
synthesis.decision_required: Do we authorize peak-season shipments to Al Rowad now, or gate all new orders behind (a) settlement of the current SAR 364K balance on time and (b) a field visit to diagnose the volume collapse?

options.option: Option A — Gate: No new shipments until SAR 364K settles on or before 60d due date AND field visit confirms root cause
options.consequence: Protects receivable exposure through peak season; may cost Al Rowad's incremental orders if they are ready to buy, but preserves negotiating leverage and forces payment discipline. If visit reveals competitor displacement, this becomes a managed wind-down.

options.option: Option B — Open: Continue shipping within current credit limit while monitoring OVD weekly
options.consequence: Risks adding SAR 200K–400K exposure to an account with 143d DSO and 35% historical OVD baseline. If any bucket migrates past 60d in June–July at peak season, collection options narrow while the sales team is focused on growth, not recovery.

timeline.step: Field visit to Al Rowad — physically check LG stock levels, confirm competitor shelf presence, get buy intent for May
Set 60-day payment gate alert on SAR 363,943 balance — verify on-time settlement or flag immediately if any bucket migrates

Reassess investment signal based on: (1) field visit findings, (2) whether May buy exceeds T12M monthly average, (3) whether balance settles on time — upgrade to Invest or downgrade to Exit accordingly

finance.headline: Chronically slow payer masking as peak-season anomaly: SAR 306K frozen in 60d+ bucket for 6 consecutive days while DSO runs 2.3x credit term — structural lag, not seasonal timing.
finance.analysis:
AR health is superficially stable but structurally stressed. The OVD% of 31.8% sits below the account's own YTD average of 35.4%, and the 1.2x buy pace confirms commercial activity heading into peak season. However, 96.7% of all overdue (SAR 306,512 of SAR 317,007) is concentrated in the 60d+ bucket — with zero transition from 30-60d, meaning no new overdue is forming but the aged pile is completely static. Six consecutive snapshots at identical balance and OVD% is not seasonal stocking lag; it is frozen receivable.

Collection risk is elevated on a structural basis. YTD DSO of 211d against a 90d credit term means LG is extending 121 days of unauthorized credit. T12M DSO of 250d suggests this is a chronic pattern, not a 2026 anomaly. The Q2 Anomaly classification (DSO > DPO with low OVD) is technically correct at the portfolio level, but the 60d+ aging concentration tells a more granular story: this account does not default, but it does not pay proactively either. Grade C classification aligns with this behavior profile. The absence of any collection progress over the past week, despite the account actively purchasing (SAR 592K YTD, ahead of 2025 full-year pace), suggests the buyer views extended credit as a structural operating assumption.

Credit policy should be maintained, not increased. Increasing exposure on an account running 2.3x its contracted term with SAR 306K in frozen aged AR would deepen LG's unsecured position entering peak season — precisely when collection leverage is highest. A decrease risks disrupting a commercially relevant and growing account at the worst time. The correct posture is maintain with a hard enforcement condition: the 60d+ balance must be materially reduced before any new purchase orders are released to logistics. This converts the upcoming peak-season demand into collection leverage.
finance.credit_rationale: Commercially active and growing but operating 2.3x outside contracted credit terms with frozen 60d+ AR — exposure should not increase until aged receivable is cleared.
action: Demand partial payment of SAR 150K on the 60d+ bucket by May 20 as a precondition for releasing any new peak-season orders
Issue formal credit hold: block new PGIs until 60d+ AR is reduced below SAR 150K or post-dated checks covering the full SAR 306K overdue are received
Coordinate with sales: communicate that 2026 peak-season allocation is contingent on payment compliance — use order demand as collection lever before May inventory pressure peaks
