sales.headline: Peak-season buyer with chronic payment default — strong volume momentum but SAR 306K in frozen 60d+ overdue is a structural credit risk that must be resolved before further stocking.
sales.analysis:
Channel growth trajectory is real: near-zero in 2024 (SAR 20K), then a 48x surge to SAR 966K in 2025, and SAR 592K already booked by early May 2026 — already 61% of full-year 2025 with peak season just starting. Buy pace at 1.2x T12M average confirms this is an active, growing channel. The Q2 Anomaly classification (high DSO, contained OVD%) aligns with a dealer stocking aggressively ahead of May–September peak without fully clearing prior obligations — a common IR_Others pattern in Saudi AC.

However, the payment structure is deeply concerning. YTD DSO of 211d and T12M DSO of 250d against a 90-day credit term means this account is structurally paying 120–160 days late — not a one-time miss, but embedded behavior. The 60d+ bucket holding SAR 306,512 frozen for 6+ consecutive days (zero balance movement Apr 29–May 4) signals no active repayment effort on the oldest debt. The 30–60d bucket being zero while 60d+ is large suggests selective aging manipulation or deliberate non-payment on old tranches.

The risk is compounding: if peak-season orders are fulfilled on top of an unmoving 60d+ overdue base, LG's exposure deepens while the dealer's sell-through velocity remains unverifiable (no sell-out data). Grade C combined with DSO 211d and frozen AR is a yellow-to-red credit posture. Investment now without collection progress on the 60d+ tranche effectively rewards non-payment behavior and increases LG's unsecured exposure heading into the highest-volume quarter.
sales.investment_rationale: The volume momentum justifies maintaining the relationship, but SAR 306K frozen 60d+ overdue must be cleared — or a payment schedule agreed — before any sell-out support, credit increase, or co-marketing is unlocked.
action: Immediate collection call: demand structured repayment plan on SAR 306,512 (60d+) within 2 weeks — propose 3-tranche schedule by end of May as condition to continue peak-season supply
Hard stop on new order fulfillment until 60d+ overdue drops below SAR 150K or a signed payment commitment is in place — communicate to sales rep and CS team

Re-evaluate credit limit and DSO tolerance in June mid-season review: if OVD trend improves and 60d+ clears, unlock limited sell-out support (display or installer incentive) to accelerate Q3 sell-through
sales.channel_reason: Strong buy momentum but payment behavior is structurally non-compliant (DSO 211d vs 90d term) and 60d+ overdue has been frozen for 6+ days with no resolution signal.

synthesis.headline: SAR 306K frozen in 60d+ for 6 consecutive days with DSO at 2.3x credit term — peak-season order demand is the only collection leverage LG has, and it expires in weeks.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow (trending Red) — Hold. Structurally chronic slow-payer with frozen aged AR; strong buy momentum is the collection lever, not a reason to invest further.

summary_3lines: Asemat Aljaled is a fast-growing IR_Others buyer (SAR 20K in 2024 → SAR 966K in 2025 → SAR 592K YTD by early May 2026) with SAR 306,512 completely frozen in the 60d+ bucket across 6 consecutive daily snapshots — zero collection movement while the account keeps ordering.

YTD DSO of 211d against a 90d credit term is not a peak-season timing lag — T12M DSO of 250d confirms this is embedded behavior: the dealer treats LG's credit facility as a 200d+ revolving line, and the 1.2x buy pace entering peak season will deepen LG's unsecured position further unless the aged overhang is cleared first.

If peak-season orders are fulfilled on top of an unresolved SAR 306K frozen balance, LG's total exposure could cross SAR 1.5M by July with zero collection leverage remaining — the dealer's demand for product is the only enforcement window, and it closes when peak-season stocking completes.
synthesis.decision_required: Enforce a hard credit hold this week — release conditions are either SAR 150K cash receipt OR post-dated cheques covering the full SAR 306K overdue by May 13. No new PGIs until one of these conditions is met.

options.option: Option A — Hard Hold Now: Block all new PGIs immediately; release only upon SAR 150K payment or post-dated cheques covering SAR 306K by May 13
options.consequence: Maximizes collection leverage at peak demand; non-compliance self-selects the account out before exposure deepens further. Downside: risk of losing SAR 400K–600K of remaining 2026 Sell-In volume if the dealer walks rather than pays.

options.consequence: Preserves commercial momentum and relationship trust heading into peak season; still uses order demand as leverage. Risk: dealer stalls on signature, window closes with no payment and deeper exposure.

finance.headline: Alghanim is peak-season loading aggressively with OVD improving on the surface, but the underlying 60d+ concentration and a structural 86-day DSO overshoot versus credit term demand a clearing condition before any credit expansion.
finance.analysis:
AR health is directionally improving but the mechanism requires scrutiny. The OVD rate collapsed from 71.6% (Apr 29) to 18.5% (May 4), yet the Apr 30 balance spike of approximately SAR 1.57M was driven by fresh inventory loading, not payment recovery — the overdue absolute amount held near SAR 720K through Apr 30 and only partially cleared to SAR 422K by May 4. Critically, 98.8% of that remaining overdue (SAR 417K of SAR 422K) sits in the 60d+ bucket. There is virtually nothing in the 0–30d aging band, meaning this account does not partially overshoot terms — it skips to deep aging. That is a structural pattern, not a one-time event.

Collection risk is moderate-elevated despite the Q1 Safe designation. The 'Safe' classification rests on YTD DSO (176d) sitting inside the 180d DPO ceiling — a narrow 4-day buffer. More critically, the stated credit term is 90 days, meaning the account is running 86 days beyond contractual terms on a systematic basis. The T12M DSO of 272d confirms this is not a 2026 anomaly; Alghanim has historically been a structural slow-pay partner. The mitigating factors are real — A+ grade, 1.5x buy-pace acceleration into peak season, and an active OVD clearing trend all signal commercial health and liquidity. Solvency risk is low; payment discipline risk is elevated.

On credit policy, the commercial case for increase is visible: a grade-A+ IR account accelerating purchases at 1.5x T12M during the May–September peak window, with DSO trending significantly better YoY (176d vs 272d T12M). However, expanding exposure before the 60d+ bucket is resolved would reward the slow-pay pattern and increase concentration risk precisely when peak-season balances will swell further. The correct posture is a conditional hold: maintain current limit, set explicit clearing benchmarks for the 60d+ aging and DSO trajectory, and table a formal increase review for mid-June if those benchmarks are met.
finance.credit_rationale: Account is commercially active and improving but structural 86-day DSO overshoot versus credit term and near-total 60d+ overdue concentration must be demonstrably cleared before expanded exposure can be justified.
action: Issue formal AR clearing notice to Alghanim: demand full settlement of SAR 417K in 60d+ aging within 14 days as precondition for uninterrupted peak-season supply continuity
Set DSO ceiling watch at 180d — if YTD DSO breaches DPO boundary during May–June peak loading, escalate immediately to credit hold review regardless of OVD%
Schedule conditional credit limit review for June 15: increase approved only if 60d+ bucket is fully cleared AND YTD DSO holds below 150d by that date — document as formal gated uplift
finance.risk_reason: 98.8% of overdue in 60d+ bucket and YTD DSO running 86 days past stated 90d credit term, offset by A+ grade, active purchases, and improving OVD trend

sales.headline: Pre-peak bulk load confirmed — SAR 1.57M injected in a single Apr 29-30 buy — but 272d T12M DSO signals LG is being used as a financing vehicle, not a trusted partner.
sales.analysis:
Alghanim executed a deliberate pre-peak stock build around Apr 29–30: balance spiked from SAR 1.01M to SAR 2.58M in a single day, consistent with an A+ IR account front-loading inventory ahead of the May–September peak. Buy pace at 1.5x T12M confirms accelerating channel commitment, and YTD 2026 Sell-In of SAR 1.617M through early May is tracking well above 2025's pace if momentum holds. The OVD % dropping from 71.6% to 18.5% is largely a dilution effect from the new large invoice — not a collection event — and the channel is unambiguously active and season-ready.

The structural DSO picture demands a harder look. T12M DSO of 272d versus 90d credit terms means LG is financing this account at 3x the agreed window — indefinitely. The 60d+ aging bucket (SAR 417K, ~99% of total overdue) is not a timing glitch; it reflects habitual payment behavior tolerated under an A+ relationship. YTD DSO at 176d shows marginal improvement, but the pattern has been sustained long enough to be structural. The large new order further inflates LG's exposure without resolving the underlying aged tail.

The key question is not whether to serve Alghanim — they are a core IR account entering peak season — but whether LG should extend more credit on top of a 272d DSO record. The answer is no. Sellout support (display, installer incentive, in-store activation) is the right investment vehicle: it accelerates sell-through, which is the primary catalyst for collection, without adding balance sheet risk. Any support commitment should be explicitly conditioned on clearing the 60d+ overdue.
sales.investment_rationale: A+ IR account buying aggressively into peak season warrants sell-out activation, but 272d T12M DSO and SAR 417K 60d+ aging block any credit expansion until the aged tail is resolved.
action: Issue formal collection letter on SAR 417K 60d+ overdue immediately — set 2-week resolution deadline (by May 20) as explicit condition for peak-season allocation priority
Condition any sell-out co-investment (display, installer fee, co-op) on written collection milestone: SAR 200K cleared by end-May, remaining by end-June
If 60d+ clears by June and OVD drops below 15%, escalate to 'invest' signal and propose a co-marketing activation tied to LG's peak-season campaign
sales.channel_reason: 1.5x buy pace and pre-peak loading confirm active channel engagement, but 272d T12M DSO and persistent 60d+ aging indicate LG is absorbing financing risk the credit terms never authorized.

synthesis.headline: Alghanim은 A+ 전략 계정이지만 LG를 3배 기간 무이자 파이낸싱 수단으로 운용 중 — 피크 시즌 공급 우선권을 레버리지로 즉시 SAR 417K 회수 압박에 나서야 한다.

verdict.one_line: Risk YELLOW — HOLD. A+ 계정이 1.5x 매입 가속 중이나 T12M DSO 272d·60d+ 집중 99%는 구조적 연체 패턴이며, 피크 시즌 추가 익스포저 확대 전 SAR 417K 정산 선행 필수.

summary_3lines: Alghanim은 4월 29-30일 SAR 1.57M 피크 선적재(Pre-load)를 단행한 전략적 IR 계정으로, 2026 매입 페이스는 T12M 대비 1.5배 가속 중 — 채널 커미트먼트 자체는 건강하다.
그러나 T12M DSO 272d는 계약 신용기간 90d의 3배로, 이는 LG가 계약 밖에서 최소 182일치 무이자 자금을 해당 계정에 무상 제공하는 구조이며 잔여 연체 422K 중 99%가 60d+ 버킷에 고착된 패턴은 일회성 이벤트가 아닌 반복 행동이다.
지금 아무 조치 없이 피크 시즌 공급을 계속 늘리면 5-9월 발란스가 추가 팽창하면서 LG의 익스포저는 SAR 3M+ 수준으로 확대되고, OVD 개선 수치 뒤에 구조적 연체 패턴이 고착화돼 피크 이후 회수 협상력이 급격히 약해진다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내(5월 9일까지) Alghanim에 SAR 417K 60d+ 연체에 대한 공식 정산 요청서를 발송하고 — '5월 20일까지 미정산 시 피크 시즌 공급 우선순위 제외'를 명시한 정산 조건부 공급 정책을 확정할 것인가?

options.option: Option A — 조건부 공급 정책 즉시 시행
options.consequence: SAR 417K 60d+ 정산을 5월 20일 마감으로 공식 요청. 미정산 시 공급 우선순위 후순위 전환. 정산 완료 시 셀아웃 공동 활성화(디스플레이·인스톨러 인센티브) 투자로 전환, 6월 15일 신용한도 확대 검토 게이팅. LG의 피크 노출을 통제하면서 관계를 유지하고 정산 레버리지를 극대화.

options.option: Option B — 현 상태 유지, 추가 공급 계속
options.consequence: Alghanim의 A+ 등급과 매입 가속을 이유로 공식 정산 압박 없이 피크 시즌 공급 지속. 단기 Sell-In 수치는 개선되나 5-9월 발란스가 SAR 3M+ 팽창, T12M DSO 구조적 악화 가능성 높음. 연체 패턴을 묵인하는 선례가 되어 피크 이후 회수 협상력 저하.

timeline.step: 공식 AR 정산 요청서 발송 — SAR 417K 60d+ 전액, 14일 이내(5월 20일) 정산 조건 명시 / 미정산 시 피크 시즌 공급 우선순위 유예 경고 포함
정산 이행 확인 — SAR 417K 중 최소 SAR 200K 입금 확인 시 셀아웃 공동 투자안(디스플레이, 인스톨러 수수료) 협의 개시. 미이행 시 공급 우선순위 실제 조정 집행
신용한도 확대 조건부 검토 — 60d+ 버킷 전액 해소 + YTD DSO 150d 이하 유지 시에만 한도 증액 승인. 미충족 시 현행 한도 유지 및 재검토 일정 재설정

finance.headline: SAR 1.60M balance is 100% overdue in the 60d+ bucket, T12M DSO stands at 850 days against a 90-day credit term, and zero 2026 sell-in signals the account has effectively gone dark — functional bad-debt risk.
finance.analysis:
The AR position is unambiguously distressed. The entire SAR 1,596,788 balance sits in the 60d+ aging bucket with zero current or near-term receivables to offset it. A T12M DSO of 850 days is not a collection delay — it is 9.4x the contractual 90-day credit term and indicates the account is either unable or unwilling to pay. The six-day OVD trend locked at 100% with no directional improvement confirms this is not a temporary cash-flow blip but a structural impasse.

The absence of any 2026 sell-in revenue is the most damning signal. Either LG has already placed this account on a credit hold (appropriate), or the account has self-selected out of ordering because it cannot absorb further payables. Either way, the commercial relationship is functionally frozen while the legacy receivable continues to age. With 2025 sell-in of SAR 2.85M and a current balance of SAR 1.60M, roughly 56% of last year's revenue remains uncollected — and has been sitting delinquent long enough to generate an 850-day DSO.

For credit policy, there is no scenario that justifies maintaining, let alone increasing, the credit limit. The immediate priority is collection of the existing SAR 1.60M exposure before any discussion of resuming supply. A formal demand letter, escalation to senior management at Al-Zaqzouq, and legal review of collection options should be initiated in parallel. If a payment plan is the only path to partial recovery, it must be structured with milestone-linked supply resumption — not upfront credit restoration.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of balance is 60d+ overdue with T12M DSO of 850 days and no 2026 revenue activity; extending any credit before full collection would compound unrecoverable exposure.
action: Issue formal written demand for SAR 1,596,788 immediately; set 15-day response deadline with explicit escalation path to legal proceedings if no response
Enforce hard credit block — zero new orders released until overdue balance is cleared or a legally binding payment schedule is executed
Initiate quarterly bad-debt provision review: flag SAR 1.60M for potential write-off if no payment commitment is received within 30 days
finance.risk_reason: T12M DSO 850d vs. 90d credit term, 100% balance in 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 revenue activity — meets all criteria for imminent bad-debt classification.

sales.headline: Al-Zaqzouq is commercially frozen — zero 2026 sell-in, SAR 1.60M 100% overdue (60d+), no payment movement across 6 consecutive snapshots entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is functionally dead. The account purchased SAR 2.85M in 2025 but has made zero purchases in 2026 through May — the opening month of Saudi peak season. This is not a slowdown; it is a complete cessation. The most probable sequence: LG imposed a credit hold due to payment default, supply stopped, and the account has either pivoted to competitors offering looser credit (Midea/Hisense are known for this in the IR segment) or is experiencing a cash crisis that has frozen all procurement. The static SAR 1.60M balance across all six OVD snapshots (Apr 29 – May 04) confirms zero payment activity — not even partial settlement — which rules out a temporary liquidity dip and points to structural distress or deliberate avoidance.

The T12M DSO of 850 days is a forensic red flag, not just a bad metric. It implies accumulated unpaid balances that predate the current cycle, meaning Al-Zaqzouq's payment behavior has been chronically deficient. Combined with a YTD DSO of N/A (zero 2026 revenue), the picture is clear: LG has already de-facto exited this account by halting supply, but the receivable risk (SAR 1.60M) remains fully exposed with no collection momentum. Grade B classification arguably understates the actual risk at this moment.

Investment rationale is straightforward: any credit extension, promotional co-investment, or sell-out support injected into this account would compound exposure without a credible path to recovery. Peak season resources — installer incentives, display budgets, co-op marketing — must be concentrated on accounts with active buying momentum. Al-Zaqzouq's upside scenario (payment + restart of purchase) is conditional entirely on collection resolution, not channel investment. Until SAR 1.60M is meaningfully cleared and a payment plan with collateral is agreed, this account is a receivables management case, not a sales case.
sales.investment_rationale: With 100% OVD fully aged 60d+, zero 2026 purchases, 850-day T12M DSO, and static balance across all recent snapshots, any investment beyond collection effort would increase SAR exposure with no recoverable upside during peak season.
action: Escalate to credit/legal team immediately: issue formal written demand for SAR 1,596,788 with 14-day payment deadline and explicit consequences (legal action, account suspension from LG dealer program)
Conduct field visit to verify account is operationally active — confirm store presence, LG display status, and whether competitor brands have replaced shelf space; assess if account is winding down

Negotiate structured payment plan only if account demonstrates good faith (minimum 30% upfront); condition any future supply restart on full clearance of overdue plus advance payment or bank guarantee
Flag SAR 1.60M for bad debt provision review with finance team given 850-day T12M DSO — do not carry this at face value through peak season without provisioning
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in, 100% overdue SAR 1.60M aged 60d+, no payment activity for 6+ days — account is commercially inactive entering peak season.

synthesis.headline: SAR 1.60M is 100% delinquent (60d+ bucket), T12M DSO stands at 850 days — 9.4× the contractual term — with zero 2026 revenue activity and no payment movement across six consecutive snapshots entering peak season. This is functional bad debt, not a collection lag.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. Al-Zaqzouq is commercially frozen with chronic payment failure; every SAR of new credit or sales investment compounds unrecoverable exposure.

summary_3lines: Al-Zaqzouq has made zero purchases in 2026 and zero payments since at least April 29 — SAR 1.60M sits fully aged in the 60d+ bucket with no directional movement, confirming the account is operationally dark entering Saudi peak season.

An 850-day T12M DSO is not a cash-flow delay — it reflects either structural insolvency or deliberate non-payment, compounded by the likelihood that the account has already migrated to competitors (Midea / Hisense) offering looser credit terms.

Without immediate collection action, SAR 1.60M progresses to near-certain write-off while LG's peak-season sales capacity and credit headroom are consumed by a dead account instead of being redeployed to active IR partners.

options.consequence: Issue formal written demand by May 13 with 15-day payment deadline; no response triggers legal proceedings and full SAR 1.60M bad-debt provision. Maximizes recovery pressure but extinguishes any future commercial relationship. Recommended if field visit confirms account is winding down or has pivoted to competitors.

options.consequence: Offer a payment plan requiring minimum 30% upfront (SAR ~479K) with monthly tranches and zero new supply until full clearance; condition any future orders on bank guarantee or advance payment. finance.headline: A+ grade in name only — 326-day YTD DSO at 3.6× credit term, with 96% of overdue locked in the 60d+ bucket signals structural collection failure, not a timing gap.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired. Of SAR 1.95M overdue, SAR 1.88M (96.3%) sits in the 60d+ aging bucket — this is not a collection timing issue; it is chronic non-payment. YTD DSO of 326 days stands at 3.6× the 90-day credit term and exceeds LG's own DPO of 180 days, meaning LG is financing this customer on terms more lenient than it receives from its own suppliers. The sole positive signal this week is a ~SAR 470K payment around May 4 (OVD% declined from 54.6% to 45.9%, balance from SAR 4.74M to SAR 4.26M) — directionally correct, but structurally insufficient given the remaining 60d+ concentration.

Collection risk is red and deteriorating in quality even as the headline OVD% marginally improves. T12M DSO of 252 days confirms this pattern predates 2026 — it is not a seasonal aberration. The 0.8× buy pace deceleration removes the growth-trajectory argument for extended leniency: the account is simultaneously buying less and paying slower. Entering the May–September peak season with SAR 1.88M stuck in the oldest bucket means LG is absorbing peak-season risk on behalf of a customer with no demonstrated urgency to clear aged debt.

Credit policy must contract. There is no scenario — volume, relationship, or strategic — that justifies maintaining current exposure at 326-day DSO. LG is effectively an unsecured short-term lender. Credit reduction paired with a structured repayment milestone on the 60d+ balance is the only fiscally responsible path. New shipment authorization should be hard-gated on demonstrable 60d+ principal reduction, not token payments that stall OVD% in the 45–55% band.
finance.credit_rationale: YTD DSO of 326d (3.6× agreed credit term) with 96% of overdue aged 60d+ and a decelerating 0.8× buy pace eliminates any financial basis for maintaining current exposure entering peak season.
action: Immediate shipment hold: freeze all new PGI until the 60d+ bucket (SAR 1.88M) is reduced by a minimum SAR 700K — set a hard deadline of May 20 as a pre-peak gate, not a negotiation.

Issue a formal structured repayment demand: bi-weekly payment schedule covering the 60d+ balance over 8 weeks, with an automatic credit hold trigger if any installment is missed by more than 5 business days.

Reduce approved credit ceiling to ≤SAR 1.0M (from current implied ~SAR 4–5M exposure), benchmarked against demonstrated T12M repayment pace, and reclassify the A+ grade to conditional pending DSO normalization below 150 days.
finance.risk_reason: 326d YTD DSO, 96% of overdue concentrated in 60d+ bucket, and declining buy velocity entering peak season creates acute and compounding collection risk.

sales.headline: A+ grade account in severe financial distress — decelerating buys, 326-day DSO, and SAR 1.88M aged debt signal active disengagement risk entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically compromised. 2026 buy pace running at 0.8x T12M average with YTD sell-in of SAR 1.63M against a full-year 2025 base of SAR 4.55M — even accounting for seasonality, the trajectory is well below par for a pre-peak loading period (May entry). No sell-out data available, but the deceleration in sell-in combined with mounting overdue strongly implies either inventory overhang (channel not clearing stock, so reorder appetite is suppressed) or active liquidity stress at the account level — likely both feeding each other.

The AR structure tells the real story: 60d+ bucket dominates at SAR 1.88M out of SAR 1.95M total overdue — meaning virtually all overdue debt is aged, not transitional. YTD DSO of 326 days against a 90-day credit term means this account is paying at 3.6x the agreed cycle. The recent OVD improvement (54.6% → 45.9%, approx. SAR 0.5M collected Apr 29–May 4) is a mild positive signal — the account is not completely silent — but it does not structurally change the risk profile given the size of the aged tail.

Investment rationale is negative: committing additional sell-out support, credit, or co-marketing into an account with 326-day DSO and decelerating orders would increase LG's exposure without a credible demand catalyst. The A+ grade reflects historical importance, not current trajectory. Recovery path exists only if the 60d+ bucket is substantially cleared and buy pace normalizes — neither condition is currently met.
sales.investment_rationale: Aged overdue dominates and buy pace is decelerating — no new investment until 60d+ bucket clears and DSO drops below 180d.
action: Immediate escalation call with account owner targeting SAR 1.88M aged (60d+) bucket — propose structured payment milestone plan (e.g., 50% by May 20, balance by June 5) as condition to unblock any new orders
Enforce informal credit hold on new POs until OVD drops below 25% and 60d+ balance clears — communicate clearly to sales team to avoid unauthorized commitments

Weekly AR snapshot review through June: track whether Apr 29–May 4 payment momentum continues or was a one-off; if no further movement by May 15, escalate to formal credit review and flag for Q3 re-classification
sales.channel_reason: DSO 326d vs 90d credit term, 60d+ aged overdue SAR 1.88M, and 0.8x decelerating buy pace at peak season entry — all three vectors point negative simultaneously.

finance.headline: Extra Value Est is a zero-overdue, fast-paying account, but Grade C status and a growing NW242H inventory overhang temper any case for credit expansion heading into peak season.
finance.analysis:
AR health is pristine. Balance of SAR 104K carries zero overdue across all aging buckets, and the May 4 drop from SAR 0.24M to SAR 0.20M confirms active, voluntary repayment — not just avoidance of due dates. YTD DSO of 23 days against a 60-day credit term means the account is consuming less than 40% of its permitted float, a strong behavioral signal. OVD has held at 0.0% across every tracked snapshot, with no deterioration trend to flag.

Collection risk is structurally low, but the sell-out trajectory warrants attention before any credit move. Sell-out peaked at 51,697 units in 2024 and fell 33% to 34,432 units in 2025 — broadly in line with OR channel softness, but the NW242H closing stock jumped from 1,690u (2024) to 6,969u (2025), a 4x inventory buildup on a single SKU. This signals that sell-in outpaced sell-out in 2025, and that stock is still sitting at the dealer level entering May 2026. 2026 sell-out is recorded at zero units so far — concerning as peak season (May–September) opens, though partial-year data may explain the gap. Buy pace at 0.9x T12M average is stable but not accelerating.

Credit policy recommendation is to maintain. The payment discipline justifies no tightening, but Grade C account classification combined with the NW242H overhang and unconfirmed 2026 sell-through velocity makes this the wrong moment to extend additional exposure. If the account clears its 6,969-unit NW242H stock and posts positive sell-out by mid-June, that would be a concrete trigger to revisit a limit increase aligned with peak-season momentum.
finance.credit_rationale: Exemplary payment behavior (DSO 23d vs 60d term, 0% OVD) does not justify tightening, but Grade C rating plus 4x YoY NW242H inventory buildup blocks a credit increase until 2026 sell-through is demonstrated.
action: Verify 2026 sell-out data completeness — if system shows 0u with peak season open, flag to sales team for POS sync or dealer reporting gap
Monitor NW242H stock clearance pace at Extra Value Est through May; if still above 4,000u by end of May, flag for joint sell-out support or promotional push before reordering
Set a mid-June credit review checkpoint: if sell-out recovers and NW242H stock drops below 2,000u, re-evaluate credit limit increase to support H2 peak replenishment
finance.risk_reason: Zero overdue, DSO well inside credit term, and declining balance trend — no near-term collection exposure.

sales.headline: Financially clean but sell-out is stalling — 6,969u NW242H overhang from 2025 is the likely drag on 2026 momentum.
sales.analysis:
Extra Value Est shows impeccable payment discipline: DSO 23d against a 60d term, zero overdue across all aging buckets, and a balance trending down from SAR 0.24M to SAR 0.10M in recent days. The AR dimension is textbook safe. The problem is on the demand side. Sell-out peaked at 51,697u in 2024 then collapsed 33% to 34,432u in 2025 — almost exactly reversing the prior year's surge. This pattern is classic inventory overhang: the 2024 buy-in was ahead of true end-consumer demand, and 2025 was the correction year. The smoking gun is the 2025 ending stock of 6,969u (NW242H), a 4x jump from 2024's 1,690u residual. The NW242H is sitting dead on their floor going into peak season.

The 2026 sell-out showing 0u is the sharpest risk signal here. If this reflects a reporting lag it is manageable; if it reflects actual silence on the floor in April–May — the very ramp into peak season — this account is potentially range-switching or losing shelf velocity to Samsung/Gree. The 0.9x buy pace (slightly below T12M average) is consistent with a dealer that still has unsold inventory and is rationing new orders. The SKU shift from NF-series dominance in 2023 to ND-series in 2025 suggests the account is tracking LG's portfolio migration, so brand loyalty appears intact for now.

Investment calculus: Grade C, OR_Others channel, declining sell-out trajectory, and a known overhang make this a hold rather than a push. The risk of adding sell-in volume before the NW242H clears is piling inventory on top of inventory, which eventually stresses the relationship. The right play is surgical: clear the 6,969u overhang first with a targeted sell-out incentive (installer rebate, demo unit activation, or retail staff push), validate 2026 floor sales data, then reassess for Q3 peak-season replenishment in June.
sales.investment_rationale: NW242H stock overhang of ~6,969u must clear before new sell-in investment makes sense — layering volume on unsold inventory will not grow the account.
action: Field audit NW242H stock at Extra Value Est wareroom within 2 weeks — confirm actual residual quantity and current floor price vs. competition
Activate targeted installer rebate or consumer promo on NW242H to accelerate clearance ahead of May–June peak; tie disbursement to verified sell-out scan data
Investigate 2026 sell-out data gap (0u reported) — validate whether this is a reporting pipeline issue or genuine floor silence; escalate to OR channel manager if no scan data exists by May 15
sales.channel_reason: Strong payment health offset by 33% sell-out decline in 2025, 4x inventory overhang on NW242H, and zero 2026 sell-out reported at peak season entry.

synthesis.headline: AR is pristine — but 6,969u of NW242H sitting unsold at peak season entry is the real exposure: not credit default, but inventory stagnation that kills reorder momentum through H2.

verdict.one_line: Risk Green — Hold. Flawless payment discipline is overshadowed by a 4x NW242H inventory overhang that will suppress new orders until cleared.

summary_3lines: Extra Value Est pays 37 days faster than its credit term allows, carries zero overdue, and has a declining balance — the AR book is as clean as it gets.

The sell-out side tells a different story: 2024's 51,697u peak was demand borrowed from the future; 2025 corrected to 34,432u and left 6,969u of NW242H unsold — a 4x YoY stock buildup now sitting on their floor as peak season opens.

If the NW242H overhang is not cleared by June, the account enters Q3 peak with no reorder appetite, 2026 sell-in stays depressed below 2025 pace, and a Grade C account drifts further from investment-grade without a recovery narrative.
synthesis.decision_required: Do we activate a targeted NW242H sell-out incentive NOW (within this week) — before confirming 2026 POS data — to protect the May–June clearance window, or do we wait until May 15 for data validation and risk losing three weeks of prime peak-season velocity?

options.option: Option A — Act now: Launch NW242H installer rebate or consumer promo this week, parallel to data audit
options.consequence: Maximizes clearance window during May peak; minor spend risk if 2026 sell-out gap turns out to be a reporting error rather than real silence. Upside: overhang clears by end-May, enabling June replenishment order and a Q3 sell-in recovery.

options.option: Option B — Validate first: Confirm 2026 POS data and field-audit physical stock by May 15, then activate incentive
options.consequence: Eliminates spend risk on a data artifact, but sacrifices 2–3 weeks of peak clearance window. If floor silence is real, a mid-May start may be too late to clear 6,969u before the June replenishment decision point — leaving the account stagnant through H2.

timeline.step: OR channel manager to confirm 2026 sell-out data integrity — POS sync or dealer reporting gap investigation
Field audit: physical count of NW242H at Extra Value Est wareroom + floor price vs. Samsung/Gree benchmark
Activate NW242H sell-out incentive (installer rebate or consumer promo tied to verified scan data) if stock confirmed above 3,000u
Mid-cycle checkpoint: if NW242H residual below 2,000u, initiate credit limit review and Q3 replenishment sell-in discussion

finance.headline: Grade C stocking-cycle buyer with SAR 239K locked in 60d+ bucket — improving OVD trend masks a chronic tail risk that must be resolved before peak-season credit expansion.
finance.analysis:
AR health is structurally stressed but not acutely deteriorating. The SAR 239,117 overdue is 100% concentrated in the 60d+ aging bucket, meaning these invoices are at minimum 120+ days old (60d credit term + 60d+ past-due). No fresh overdue is forming in the 0–30d or 30–60d buckets — the account IS paying on current invoices — but the old tail is stubborn and unresolved. The declining OVD% trend (27.5% → 24.5%) despite a growing balance signals simultaneous buying and partial paying behavior, which is a mild positive but insufficient to clear concern on a Grade C counterparty.

DSO tells the harder story: YTD DSO of 190d is 3.2x the 60d credit term, and T12M DSO of 280d is extreme — this is a chronically slow payer, not a seasonal blip. The Q2 Anomaly quadrant framing (high DSO, low OVD%) is partially valid for peak-season stocking context, but the 60d+ bucket concentration contradicts a clean stocking narrative. A pure stocking account would show OVD spread across 0–60d buckets as invoices age in; here the delinquency has already aged past all intermediate buckets into the hardest-to-collect zone.

Credit policy must lean conservative. The 1.5x accelerating buy pace against a backdrop of unresolved 60d+ overdue means LG is effectively extending additional unsecured exposure to a Grade C account that has not serviced its oldest obligations. Expanding the credit limit now rewards non-payment behavior and increases LG's concentration risk on a C-grade entity entering peak season — precisely when collection leverage is highest. The right posture is to use the peak-season order pipeline as collection leverage: link new shipment approvals to a clear-down schedule on the SAR 239K tail.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade C account with 100% of overdue locked in 60d+ bucket and T12M DSO of 280d — credit limit should contract until the chronic tail is retired, not expand to reward it.
action: Place new order approvals on payment-conditional hold immediately: no further shipments until a signed installment schedule for the SAR 239,117 (60d+ OVD) is received — leverage peak-season demand as collection tool.
Issue formal overdue notice to account owner this week; request full clearance of 60d+ bucket by 2026-05-20 or a structured bi-weekly payment plan through June.
Reduce effective credit exposure ceiling by SAR 200K (approx. overdue amount) until chronic OVD clears; re-evaluate credit limit upward only after two consecutive clean payment cycles post-clearance.
finance.risk_reason: OVD% declining and no new delinquency forming, but 100% of overdue is 60d+ on a Grade C account with T12M DSO 280d — chronic tail risk, not imminent default.

sales.headline: Aggressive pre-season stocking at 1.5x pace, but T12M DSO of 280d and fully aged 60d+ overdue demand collection-first posture before any credit expansion.
sales.analysis:
Channel volume momentum is real and notable for a Grade C IR_Others account: 2026 sell-in (SAR 642K YTD through early May) has already eclipsed full-year 2025 (SAR 631K), with buy pace at 1.5x the T12M average. Timing aligns precisely with Saudi AC pre-season stocking — accounts in this channel typically front-load inventory in April–May to secure shelf position before the May–September peak. The Q2 Anomaly classification is textbook for this pattern: DSO lags hard because payment follows sell-out, not sell-in, and the channel is stretching its credit cycle to maximize opening stock.

However, the financial profile demands caution. YTD DSO at 190d is 3.2x the contractual 60d term; T12M DSO at 280d (4.7x) signals this is not a seasonal artifact but a structural slow-pay pattern. Critically, all SAR 239K overdue sits in the 60d+ bucket with zero in the 0–30d or 30–60d bands — meaning no fresh overdue is forming (positive), but the aged tail is not clearing (negative). The declining OVD% trend (27.5% → 24.5% over one week as balance grew to SAR 978K) shows some cash is coming in, but the pace of collection does not match the pace of new buy. Without sell-out data, it is impossible to determine whether the 1.5x stocking acceleration reflects end-user pull or speculative channel accumulation.

Investment calculus: the account shows genuine volume upside — if it pays — but extending credit or co-investing in sell-out support before the 60d+ overdue clears would deepen LG's exposure into peak season without payment assurance. The correct posture is hold with a collection trigger: clear SAR 239K aged overdue as pre-condition for continued shipment approval, obtain sell-out velocity data through May, then reassess for targeted invest in June when peak-season performance is observable.
sales.investment_rationale: Buy momentum is real but T12M DSO of 280d and all overdue concentrated in the 60d+ bucket represent a structural payment problem that must be partially resolved before LG accepts deeper peak-season exposure.
action: Engage AM immediately: SAR 239K aged 60d+ overdue must be cleared (minimum SAR 120K by end-May) as explicit pre-condition for continued order fulfillment — document in writing
Request weekly channel sell-out data (POS or shelf-depletion report) from account to determine whether 1.5x stocking reflects real demand or inventory build-up before deciding on further shipments
Gate any new credit extension on two sequential triggers: (1) overdue tail cleared below SAR 100K, (2) sell-out velocity confirmed — reassess invest posture in June if both conditions met
sales.channel_reason: Strong buy acceleration entering peak season but chronic slow-pay profile (DSO 280d T12M) and uncleared 60d+ overdue create elevated collection risk if inventory does not convert to cash by August.

synthesis.headline: Peak-season demand gives LG maximum collection leverage right now — use it or lose it. Do NOT ship further without a signed payment schedule on SAR 239K aged overdue.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. Real volume momentum on a chronically slow-pay Grade C account; peak-season leverage must be converted to cash collection before any credit expansion is warranted.

summary_3lines: Asr AlTakyif is accelerating buys at 1.5x pace into peak season (2026 sell-in already exceeds all of 2025), but all SAR 239K overdue has aged past 60d+ with T12M DSO of 280d — a structural slow-pay pattern, not a seasonal blip.

Finance and Sales both converge on the same diagnosis: the account pays on current invoices but refuses to clear its oldest tail, and LG has accepted this behavior by continuing to ship — the Q2 Anomaly quadrant masks the underlying credit discipline failure.

If LG ships through peak season without resolving the 60d+ tail, the SAR 239K overdue will age further into near-uncollectable territory by August, while balance grows to SAR 1.2M+, locking LG into a Grade C account with 4–5x DSO exposure at peak-season maximum concentration.
synthesis.decision_required: Approve or reject the following condition: no further shipment approvals until account signs a bi-weekly installment schedule clearing the SAR 239,117 (60d+ bucket) by 2026-06-15 — decision must be made this week before next order cycle.

options.option: Option A — Payment-Conditional Hold (Recommended)
options.consequence: Freeze new shipments immediately; demand signed installment plan (minimum SAR 120K cleared by end-May, balance by June 15). Account likely complies — peak-season stock need is high and LG is the supplier. OVD tail clears, DSO normalizes, credit posture improves. Downside: account may place partial orders with competitor for the gap period.

options.option: Option B — Continue Shipping, Parallel Collection
options.consequence: Maintain order flow to protect volume momentum; pursue SAR 239K overdue through AR follow-up in parallel. High risk: collection leverage disappears post-peak (August); account history shows it does not self-correct without hard stops; LG's balance grows to SAR 1.2M+ on a Grade C counterparty with 280d DSO by end of season. Not recommended.

timeline.step: AM issues formal overdue notice and payment-conditional hold to account — no new orders processed until response received
Account returns signed installment schedule (minimum SAR 120K by May 31, balance by June 15) — or escalate to credit committee for account suspension
Confirm first installment received (SAR 120K); release held orders if paid — reassess invest/hold posture for June with sell-out data in hand

finance.headline: SAR 710K fully overdue (100% in 60d+ bucket), zero 2026 revenue, T12M DSO 272d — this account is in de facto default.
finance.analysis:
The AR picture is unambiguous: the entire SAR 710,357 balance sits in the 60d+ aging bucket with not a single riyal in current or near-term buckets. The OVD% has held at 100% for six consecutive daily snapshots with no directional improvement, ruling out a temporary cash-flow delay. The absence of any 2026 sell-in activity is the most telling signal — LG has either placed this account on credit hold or the dealer has stopped ordering, both of which point to a broken commercial relationship.

On a T12M basis, DSO stands at 272 days — three times the contracted credit term of 90 days and 92 days beyond LG's own DPO benchmark of 180 days. This is not a slow-pay account; it is a non-performing receivable. The Grade C classification and Q3-Critical quadrant assignment are consistent with the data, but the numbers suggest the account may have deteriorated beyond what a single-grade adjustment captures. With zero 2026 revenue, the 35.4% YTD OVD baseline is rendered moot — the account is simply not paying down legacy debt.

Credit policy conclusion is straightforward: there is no business case to maintain, let alone increase, the credit limit. Extending any new credit before full collection of the SAR 710K overdue would compound LG's exposure with no offsetting revenue upside. Credit limit should be reduced to zero (hard hold) and reinstated only after the overdue balance is settled and a minimum of two consecutive on-time payment cycles are demonstrated.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of AR is 60d+ overdue with zero 2026 revenue and T12M DSO at 272d — no basis for credit extension; hard hold is the only defensible posture.
action: Escalate SAR 710,357 (100% 60d+ OVD) to legal/collections immediately — issue formal demand letter with 7-day cure deadline before litigation referral
Enforce hard credit hold: block all pending and future orders until full overdue settlement or a signed, secured payment plan with post-dated cheques
Schedule C-level or owner contact within 48 hours to assess solvency and negotiate structured repayment; document outcome for potential write-off provisioning if no engagement
finance.risk_reason: SAR 710K entirely in 60d+ bucket, T12M DSO 272d (3× credit term), zero 2026 activity — account is effectively non-performing with no recovery signal.

sales.headline: Zero 2026 sell-in with 100% AR overdue at 60d+ entering peak season — this is a collection case, not a sales case.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is effectively dead in 2026. The account has placed zero orders YTD despite Saudi AC peak season opening in May — a complete reversal from SAR 953K sell-in in 2025. The buy halt is not seasonal; it is structural. A T12M DSO of 272 days means LG waited nearly 9 months on average to collect last year, and the current snapshot shows the entire SAR 710K balance has crossed 60 days with zero partial payment — the OVD rate has been locked at 100% for at least 7 consecutive days with no movement. The account is not slowing down; it has stopped.

Root cause hierarchy: (1) Financial distress is the dominant signal — a dealer that cannot service existing 60d+ debt will not be ordering fresh stock regardless of sell-out conditions; (2) Inventory overhang from 2025 purchases is a plausible compounding factor — if 2025 sell-through was weak, unsold units would explain both the payment freeze and the order freeze; (3) Competitor migration is possible but secondary — a channel switch would typically show residual partial payments, not a hard stop. The pattern here is consistent with acute cash flow stress or pre-insolvency.

From an investment lens, SAR 710K outstanding represents ~75% of the account's entire 2025 sell-in value. Extending credit, running promotions, or co-investing would compound LG's exposure with zero evidence of collection capacity. The account is Grade C, Q3 Critical, and entering peak season with nothing in the pipeline — the risk/return calculus for any incremental investment is deeply negative. All energy must go toward recovery of existing receivables before peak season closes in September and the leverage window narrows.
sales.investment_rationale: 100% AR overdue 60d+ with zero 2026 orders leaves no basis for sales investment — full credit hold and active collections is the only rational posture.
action: Escalate SAR 710,357 (60d+ overdue) to collections immediately; set hard payment plan deadline by end of May while peak season provides dealer-side urgency leverage
Place account on unconditional credit hold — no new orders processed until overdue balance is resolved or a signed payment schedule is in place
Field visit to assess dealer solvency and 2025 inventory status; determine whether unsold LG stock is sitting in warehouse and whether the account is winding down operations
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in + 100% AR 60d+ overdue locked for 7+ days entering peak season = account financially frozen and effectively disengaged

synthesis.headline: SAR 710K 전액 60일+ 연체, 2026년 매출 제로 — 영업 계정이 아닌 채권 회수 케이스. 피크 시즌 레버리지 소멸 전 이번 주 안에 법적 조치 개시 필요.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — EXIT. 계약 신용 조건 3배 초과 DSO(272일), 100% 연체 7일 연속 고착, 2026년 신규 주문 전무 — 추가 투자 근거 없음, 즉각 채권 회수 전환.

summary_3lines: SAR 710,357 전액이 60일+ 연체 버킷에 고착되어 있으며, 2026년 Sell-In은 단 한 건도 없고 T12M DSO는 272일로 계약 신용 기간(90일)의 3배를 초과한 사실상 부실 채권 상태.
2025년 SAR 953K 매입 대금을 9개월째 미결제한 채 신규 주문을 완전히 중단한 패턴은 일시적 유동성 지연이 아닌 구조적 재무 위기(급성 현금 부족 또는 사전 도산 징후)를 강하게 시사하며, 2025년 재고 소진 실패로 인한 창고 체증이 복합 요인으로 작용 중.
5~9월 피크 시즌 레버리지(딜러의 재고 확보 필요성)는 지금이 최대치이며, 9월 이후 수요가 꺾이면 상환 협상력이 급격히 소멸 — 이번 주 미조치 시 SAR 710K의 대손 처리 가능성이 현실화됨.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 SAR 710,357에 대한 법적 채권 회수 절차(공식 이행 최고 + 7일 내 변제 또는 담보부 분할 상환 계획 서명)를 개시할 것인가, 아니면 추가 상업적 협상 기간을 부여할 것인가.

options.option: Option A — 즉각 법적 조치 개시 (권고)
options.consequence: 7일 내 이행 최고서 발송 → 미응시 시 소송/채권 추심 회부. 피크 시즌 중 딜러 측 압박 최대화. 미수금 회수 가능성 최상. 단, 상업 관계는 사실상 종료.

options.option: Option B — C레벨 직접 접촉 후 담보부 분할 상환 협의 (조건부 병행)
options.consequence: 48시간 내 오너/대표 면담 → 재무 건전성 확인 → 후불 수표 담보 분할 상환 계획 서명 시 소송 유예. 회수 가능하되 9월까지 장기화 리스크 존재. 서명 불발 시 즉시 Option A로 전환.

timeline.step: 48시간 내 — 딜러 오너 C레벨 직접 면담: 재무 상태 확인, 2025년 재고 현황 실사, 분할 상환 의사 타진 (현장 방문 필수)
5월 8일 결과 기준 분기 — 상환 계획 서명 시 담보부 일정 확정 / 미응시·거부 시 공식 이행 최고서 발송 + 법무팀 소송 준비 개시
5월 말 — 피크 시즌 레버리지 마감 전 최종 회수 목표 설정: 전액 또는 최소 60% 선수금 확보 여부 확인 후 대손 충당금 설정 여부 CFO 보고

finance.headline: 100% of SAR 9,231 balance is 60d+ overdue on a 4-day credit term — account is effectively delinquent with zero 2026 commercial activity.
finance.analysis:
AR health is critically impaired. The entire outstanding balance sits in the 60d+ bucket, which on a 4-day credit term implies the obligation is at minimum 56 days past due — an egregious breach of agreed terms. The OVD rate has been locked at 100% for six consecutive daily snapshots with no movement, indicating no partial payment, no dispute resolution, and no active collection progress. This is a static, fully-aged receivable, not a transitional situation.

Collection risk is high, compounded by the absence of any 2026 sell-in activity. Zero revenue in 2026 means there is no ongoing commercial relationship to leverage for collection — no new orders to withhold, no shipment leverage, and no indication the account intends to resume business. The T12M DSO of 71 days versus a 4-day credit term reveals a structural payment behavior problem, not a one-off delay. Grade D further confirms this is a chronically weak credit counterparty. The only mitigant is the small absolute exposure (SAR 9,231), which limits systemic risk to LG.

Credit policy must be tightened immediately. There is no basis to maintain current limits — the account has demonstrated it cannot honor a 4-day credit term, has gone dark commercially in 2026, and carries 100% aged debt. Increasing credit would be indefensible. The appropriate action is to freeze all new credit extensions, place the account on cash-in-advance terms, and escalate the SAR 9,231 to legal/collections track if payment is not received within 14 days.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% 60d+ overdue on a 4-day term, zero 2026 revenue, and grade D profile leave no justification for any unsecured credit exposure.
action: Issue formal overdue notice immediately demanding full settlement of SAR 9,231 within 7 days with explicit legal escalation warning
Freeze all credit extension — move account to cash-in-advance (CIA) only; no shipments until balance cleared
Escalate to legal/collections track at day 14 if no payment received; flag account for write-off provisioning review in Q2 close
finance.risk_reason: 100% balance overdue 60d+ on 4d term, zero 2026 activity, grade D — full collection impairment with no recovery signal.

sales.headline: Account has gone dark in 2026 — zero purchases entering peak season with 100% AR overdue in the 60d+ bucket; disengagement and financial stress are simultaneous.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is effectively flatlined. Despite May marking the start of Saudi peak AC season, Wahaj AL Aseel has made zero purchases in 2026 YTD. A Grade-D IR_Others account with only SAR 47K Sell-In in 2025 was already a thin contributor, but the complete absence of pre-season loading is a disqualifying signal. The most probable root cause is credit blockage: the 100% overdue balance (entirely in the 60d+ bucket) almost certainly triggered an automatic order hold, meaning the account cannot buy even if willing. Secondary hypothesis is voluntary disengagement — the account may have shifted wallet share to Samsung, Midea, or a competing distributor that offers softer payment terms.

Financially, the picture is unambiguous. A 4-day credit term reflects a prior risk decision that was correct — T12M DSO of 71 days means the account has historically paid at nearly 18× its contractual term. The SAR 9,231 overdue is small in absolute terms but represents 100% of the outstanding balance with zero recent cash inflow. The six-day OVD trend at 100% with no movement confirms the account is not servicing even this residual debt. There is no 2026 revenue to generate a DSO calculation — a stark data point in itself.

Investment rationale does not exist at this stage. Extending credit to unlock volume would layer fresh exposure on top of uncollected legacy debt. Sell-out support and co-marketing on a channel that has placed zero orders would generate zero ROI. The only productive action is debt recovery. If SAR 9,231 clears promptly after direct contact, the account could be revisited on strict COD terms post-season — but that is a future decision, not a present investment.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 purchases entering peak season combined with 100% 60d+ overdue on a Grade-D account leaves no credible upside to justify any commercial investment ahead of full debt clearance.
action: Escalate SAR 9,231 (60d+) to immediate collection: direct call to account owner this week with written payment demand citing credit term breach
Confirm order-hold status in system and do not release until full overdue clearance — no exceptions given 4d credit term and D-grade
After collection (or failure to collect within 14 days), formally reclassify: COD-only if recovered, dormant exit if unresponsive — document decision before end of May
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 Sell-In entering peak season; 100% AR overdue 60d+; no payment movement in 6-day OVD trend window.

synthesis.headline: 계정 전체가 사실상 정지 상태 — SAR 9,231 전액 60일 초과 연체 + 2026년 매출 제로 + 피크시즌 진입. 즉각 채권 회수 또는 계정 종료 결정이 필요합니다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 4일 신용 조건 위반 60일+ 연체 100%, 2026년 거래 전무, 피크시즌에도 오더 없음 — 투자 근거 부재, 채권 회수만이 유일한 액션.

summary_3lines: Wahaj AL Aseel은 4일 신용조건 계정임에도 SAR 9,231 전액이 60일 이상 연체 상태이며, 6일 연속 OVD 100% 고착 — 일부 결제도, 협의 진행도 없음.
2026년 매출이 전무하고 피크시즌(5~9월)에도 오더가 없다는 것은 신용 차단 또는 자발적 이탈(경쟁사 전환) 중 하나이며, 어느 쪽이든 LG와의 상업 관계가 사실상 종료된 신호.
지금 아무것도 하지 않으면 SAR 9,231은 회수 불능 처리 대상이 되고, 피크시즌이 끝난 후에는 계정 재활성화 명분도 사라져 손실로 확정됩니다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 직접 연락을 통해 SAR 9,231 즉시 회수를 요구하고, 14일 내 미결제 시 법적 채권 추심 트랙으로 전환할 것인지 — 또는 소액임을 감안해 손실 처리 후 즉시 계정 종료할 것인지 결정해야 합니다.

options.option: Option A — 즉각 채권 회수 추진 (권고)
options.consequence: 이번 주 계정 오너 직접 접촉 + 7일 내 결제 요구 공문 발송. 14일 내 미결제 시 법적 채권 추심 착수 및 Q2 대손충당금 처리. 회수 성공 시 COD 조건으로 재개 검토 가능하나, 2026 피크시즌 내 실질 기여는 기대 불가.

options.option: Option B — 즉시 손실 처리 후 계정 종료
options.consequence: SAR 9,231 소액으로 추심 비용 대비 ROI가 낮을 경우, 대손 처리 후 계정을 Dormant/Exit으로 즉시 분류. 추가 리소스 낭비 없이 집중도 높은 계정에 역량 재배분. 단, 향후 재거래 시 CIA(선결제) 조건 필수 명시.

timeline.step: 계정 오너 직접 전화 연락 + SAR 9,231 결제 요구 공문 발송 (법적 에스컬레이션 경고 포함)
시스템 오더 홀드 상태 확인 및 유지 — 연체 전액 해소 전 출하 불가 원칙 재확인
결제 미수신 시 법적 채권 추심 트랙 전환 OR 대손충당금 처리 결정 + 계정 Dormant/Exit 공식 분류 기록

1120000000.name: United Electronics Company الشركة المتحدة للالكترو

finance.headline: United Electronics carries zero overdue today but its T12M DSO of 245 days — double the contracted 120-day credit term — exposes a chronic slow-pay structure that a clean snapshot obscures.
finance.analysis:
AR health at face value is excellent: SAR 74.2M balance, zero overdue across all aging buckets, and the Apr 29–May 4 trend confirms gradual active repayment (balance declining from SAR 77.98M). The account is technically current. However, the DSO lens tells a structurally different story — YTD DSO at 143 days already breaches the 120-day credit term by 23 days on just five months of data, while T12M DSO at 245 days is nearly double the credit term and well above LG's DPO ceiling of 180 days. United Electronics routinely settles late; LG is absorbing ~125 days of financing beyond what the credit agreement mandates. The OVD% staying at 0% is not evidence of disciplined payment — it signals the account clears invoices just before they age into overdue classification.

The 2026 commercial picture adds complexity. Buy pace is accelerating at 1.7x T12M average entering peak season (May–September), and YTD sell-in of SAR 64.7M is already 71% of 2025's full-year SAR 90.6M. This is commercially reasonable pre-season positioning. But sell-out trajectory is a latent risk: 2024's 51,697 units collapsed 33% to 34,432 units in 2025, and 2026 sell-out is unreported at zero. If sell-through does not recover alongside the accelerated buy pace, channel inventory will pile up, payment timing will stretch further, and the current clean AR could deteriorate rapidly as peak season invoices mature in Q3.

On credit policy: the YTD DSO improvement from 245d (T12M) to 143d (YTD) suggests tightened payment discipline in 2026 — encouraging, but one quarter of data is insufficient to call a structural inflection. Increasing the credit limit now would amplify exposure on an account that has historically taken 2x its credit term to pay. Decreasing is unwarranted given A+ grade, zero overdue, and peak-season commercial importance. Maintain current limit with a hard reassessment trigger tied to sell-out recovery and DSO normalization below 150d by end-Q2.
finance.credit_rationale: Account pays but T12M DSO at 245d reveals chronic settlement beyond credit terms; maintain current limit and reassess after Q2 sell-out data confirms whether the 1.7x buy acceleration is backed by real channel velocity.
action: Obtain May sell-out data from United Electronics by mid-May — if the 1.7x buy pace is not matched by sell-through recovery, initiate credit limit review before Q3 peak invoices mature
Set YTD DSO threshold alert at 150d: if breached, escalate to credit committee regardless of OVD% remaining at zero
Condition any peak-season supplemental order approvals on rolling DSO improvement confirmation — do not extend incremental credit until T12M DSO trends toward the DPO 180d ceiling
finance.risk_reason: Zero overdue today but YTD DSO 143d breaches credit term, T12M DSO 245d reveals habitual slow-pay, and accelerating 2026 buy pace against an unconfirmed sell-out recovery creates concentrated Q3 collection risk.

sales.headline: Accelerating 2026 buy pace (1.7x) signals recovery intent, but 6,969u NW242H overhang from 2025 and zero reported sell-out create a pending inventory crisis entering peak season
sales.analysis: United Electronics is LG's highest-grade OR account with impeccable payment discipline — OVD has held at 0% across all recent snapshots, and the 2026 buy pace running 1.7x above T12M average confirms they are restocking aggressively at peak season entry (May). On paper this looks bullish. But the channel health picture is more complex: sell-in compressed 40.7% from 2024 (SAR 152.8M) to 2025 (SAR 90.6M), driven by a demand-side failure in 2025 where sell-out fell 33.4% to 34,432u — the lowest in the three-year window — leaving 6,969u of NW242H unsold at year-end. That overhang is four times the 2024 year-end stock (1,690u), confirming the 2025 season was a structural miss, not a one-time blip. The SKU pattern reinforces this: NW242H has been dead weight for two consecutive years, while the sell-out mix has rotated toward ND-series (ND242C, ND182C dominated 2025 top sellers). The critical unknown is 2026 sell-out showing 0u — this must be verified before any investment decision is finalized. If it is a reporting lag, the 1.7x buy pace is genuine recovery momentum. If it is real, the account is loading up on inventory with no consumer movement visible, which would mean the 2025 overhang has not cleared and a second cycle is forming heading into the peak months.
sales.investment_rationale: A+ account with clean AR and accelerating purchases at peak season entry warrants proactive sell-out investment to convert inventory velocity into consumer demand — without it, 1.7x buy pace compounds the NW242H overhang and risks a hard stop in Q3
action: Verify 2026 sell-out data within 48 hours — confirm whether 0u is a pipeline reporting lag or actual zero consumer movement; if the latter, escalate to account team immediately before additional orders ship
Deploy NW242H-targeted sell-out support (consumer promotions, installer cash incentive, dedicated floor display) to liquidate 6,969u overhang before new 2026 stock compounds the burden

Set a weekly WOS guardrail review through September: if WOS exceeds 6 weeks at any point, pause new shipments regardless of 1.7x buy momentum — protect the channel from a third consecutive overhang year
sales.channel_reason: Clean AR and strong reorder pace are positives, but 33% sell-out decline in 2025, 6,969u dead stock, and unverified 2026 consumer movement make channel health conditionally stable — not safe to declare green until peak-season sell-out confirms

synthesis.headline: 2026 sell-out data must be verified within 48 hours — United Electronics is re-loading at 1.7x pace while 2025's 6,969u NW242H overhang is unconfirmed as cleared; if consumer movement is genuinely zero, peak-season shipments are building a third consecutive inventory crisis on an account that chronically settles at 2× its contracted credit term.

verdict.one_line: Risk YELLOW — Invest conditionally. A+ account with zero overdue and 1.7x buy acceleration is commercially compelling, but T12M DSO of 245d and unverified 2026 sell-out mean investment must be led by sell-out activation, not incremental credit extension.

summary_3lines: United Electronics carries SAR 74.2M with zero overdue and is accelerating 2026 purchases at 1.7x T12M pace entering Ramadan/summer peak — on the surface, LG's strongest OR account is fully engaged.

Beneath the clean AR, T12M DSO at 245 days means LG is financing this account 125 days beyond contracted terms; 2025 sell-out collapsed 33% leaving 6,969u NW242H stranded in channel, and 2026 consumer movement is reported at zero.

If sell-out is genuinely stalled and the 1.7x buy pace continues unchecked, peak-season invoices mature in Q3 with no channel velocity to support payment — converting today's clean AR into a concentrated SAR 80M+ collection crisis at the worst possible time.
synthesis.decision_required: Confirm within 48 hours whether 2026 sell-out at 0u is a POS reporting lag or real zero consumer movement — everything else (investment commitment, shipment continuation, credit posture) pivots on this single data point.

options.option: Option A — Verify first, then invest: hold incremental shipments for 48–72 hours pending sell-out confirmation, then deploy NW242H liquidation support and co-marketing budget in full if consumer velocity is confirmed
options.consequence: Caps Q3 collection exposure; if sell-out data is positive, investment proceeds at full throttle with markedly lower downside risk; if sell-out is genuinely zero, avoids compounding a third overhang year on a 245d DSO account

options.option: Option B — Invest at full pace now without sell-out verification: approve all pending orders and deploy co-marketing immediately on the strength of A+ grade and 1.7x buy signal
options.consequence: Maximises peak-season revenue capture if channel velocity recovers, but adds SAR 20–30M+ in Q3 exposure on an account with habitual 2× credit-term settlement and an unverified channel floor — if sell-out is zero, AR deterioration in August–September is highly probable

timeline.step: Obtain and validate 2026 sell-out data from United Electronics POS — determine definitively whether 0u is reporting lag or real zero consumer movement; escalate immediately to account team if confirmed real

If sell-out confirmed: activate NW242H liquidation plan (consumer promotions + installer cash incentive + dedicated floor display) and co-marketing deployment; set WOS guardrail at 6 weeks with weekly review through September

Set YTD DSO threshold alert at 150d — if breached by end-Q2, escalate to credit committee regardless of OVD% remaining zero; condition any supplemental Q3 order approvals on T12M DSO trending below 180d

finance.headline: Lulu Saudi is in active recovery — OVD below baseline, all aging in 0-30d, and a confirmed April payment event — but the unexplained 2024→2025 revenue collapse prevents credit expansion until trajectory solidifies.
finance.analysis:
AR health is structurally clean: the SAR 30,218 overdue sits entirely in the 0-30d bucket with zero exposure in 30-60d or 60d+, indicating a timing lag rather than structural delinquency. The 19.9% OVD ratio is materially below the YTD account average of 35.4%, and the Apr 29→30 snapshot confirms a meaningful payment event that cleared both balance (SAR 0.19M → 0.15M) and OVD ratio in a single day — a behavioral positive. YTD DSO of 71d slightly exceeds the 60d credit term but is well within the 180d DPO ceiling, and the gap between YTD DSO (71d) and T12M DSO (118d) signals genuine 2026 payment acceleration relative to historical behavior.

Collection risk is low. The sole structural concern is the 2025 revenue collapse (SAR 2.3M → SAR 696K), which reduced LG's net exposure organically but raises an unanswered question: why did Lulu sharply curtail AC purchases? If it reflected a display-space or buyer decision, the 2026 1.7x buy-pace acceleration likely signals normal peak-season resumption. If it reflected Lulu financial stress, then accelerating purchases from a Grade D account ahead of the May–Sep peak warrants closer behavioral monitoring before assuming the recovery is durable.

Credit policy should be maintained at current levels. The Q1 Safe quadrant, improving OVD trend, and clean aging bucket provide no basis for tightening. However, the 2024→2025 volume gap and Grade D classification argue against expansion until YTD DSO normalizes consistently below 60d and 2026 sell-in exceeds SAR 500K with sustained clean payment behavior — confirming recovery is structural rather than seasonal front-loading.
finance.credit_rationale: Account is Q1 Safe with improving OVD and clean aging, but the unexplained 2024→2025 revenue collapse and Grade D status require at least one more quarter of validated payment behavior before any credit expansion is justified.
action: Monitor the SAR 30,218 (0-30d overdue) for collection within the next 2 weeks — escalate immediately if it ages into the 30-60d bucket without a payment event

Investigate root cause of 2025 sell-in collapse (SAR 2.3M → SAR 696K): confirm whether driven by LG supply allocation, a Lulu buyer change, or Lulu's own financial constraint — this context is essential before the 1.7x 2026 buy pace triggers any credit limit review

Set a credit review trigger: if YTD DSO drops below 60d and holds for two consecutive months while 2026 cumulative sell-in exceeds SAR 500K, escalate to credit committee for a limit increase aligned with peak season demand
finance.risk_reason: OVD 19.9% below 35.4% YTD baseline, all aging in 0-30d, confirmed April payment event, DSO well within 180d DPO — no near-term collection threat.

sales.headline: Lulu staged a near-total exit in 2025 (-70% YoY) but is showing tentative re-engagement in 2026 with buy pace 1.7x T12M — the signal is promising yet unproven against a very low base.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is recovering from a structural collapse. Sell-in fell from SAR 2.3M (2024) to SAR 696K (2025), a 70% wipeout that is far too steep to attribute to seasonality alone. The most plausible hypothesis is a 2024 inventory overhang — Lulu likely over-bought in 2024 (possibly driven by promotional incentives or pre-Ramadan stocking) and spent all of 2025 depleting that stock without reordering. A secondary hypothesis is supplier diversification toward Samsung or Gree, which dominate the hypermarket floor-display game in Saudi OR channels. The two hypotheses are not mutually exclusive: an overhang can accelerate a supplier switch if a competitor fills the floor while LG stock sits idle.

The 2026 signals are cautiously positive. Buy pace at 1.7x T12M average suggests Lulu has cleared old stock and is actively restocking ahead of peak season (May onset). YTD DSO of 71d versus T12M DSO of 118d is a meaningful improvement — payment behavior has tightened sharply. OVD dropping from 37.6% on Apr-29 to 19.9% on Apr-30 and holding steady indicates a targeted payment cleared 0-30d overdue invoices; no aged buckets (30-60d, 60d+) exist, which is clean. Current OVD at 19.9% is well below the YTD average baseline of 35.4%, confirming financial health is not the constraint.

Despite the positive buy-pace signal, absolute 2026 volumes are still modest (SAR 267K through early May). Annualizing at current pace gets to roughly SAR 700-800K — a recovery toward 2025 levels but nowhere near 2024 peak. Grade D classification and OR_Others channel positioning limit strategic priority. The right posture is relationship recovery first: understand why 2025 collapsed before committing promotional or credit resources. If the overhang hypothesis is confirmed and Lulu is genuinely restocking for peak, a targeted sell-out support package (display incentive, installer co-op) could accelerate volume recovery through September.
sales.investment_rationale: Re-engagement pace is real but volume base remains too low and the root cause of 2025 collapse is unconfirmed — validate overhang clearance and shelf presence before committing promotional budget.
action: Schedule immediate account visit to diagnose 2025 disengagement — confirm inventory overhang cleared, map current floor share vs Samsung/Gree, and assess Lulu buyer appetite for peak-season stocking deal

Propose a conditional sell-out support package (display fee + installer co-op) contingent on minimum Q2 order commitment — use peak season urgency as leverage to accelerate the recovery from tentative to committed
Monitor buy pace weekly through May; if SAR 150K+ additional orders materialize by end-May, escalate to invest signal and consider a structured credit facility review to remove order-size friction
sales.channel_reason: Recovering from a 70% YoY collapse with buy pace acceleration, but absolute volumes remain low and disengagement root cause is unconfirmed.

synthesis.headline: Lulu's 2025 collapse (-70% YoY) is the unresolved swing variable — AR is clean and buy pace is accelerating, but until a field visit confirms the root cause, any peak-season investment commitment is a bet on a hypothesis, not a fact.

verdict.one_line: Risk Green — Hold. Collection exposure is negligible, but the 2025 disengagement root cause must be diagnosed before peak-season resources are committed.

summary_3lines: Lulu has clean AR (OVD 19.9% vs 35.4% baseline, zero aged buckets, confirmed April payment event) and a real buy-pace recovery (1.7x T12M), but absolute 2026 volumes remain modest at SAR 267K.

The 2024→2025 70% collapse is structurally too steep for seasonality — either a LG inventory overhang that Lulu spent 2025 clearing, or a competitive floor-share shift to Samsung/Gree; the two hypotheses carry opposite investment implications.

If the overhang hypothesis is correct and Lulu is genuinely restocking ahead of peak (May–Sep), inaction now sacrifices a recoverable account mid-season; if the competitive displacement hypothesis is correct, promotional spend without floor-share diagnosis subsidizes a losing position.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize an immediate field visit this week to diagnose the 2025 collapse — confirm inventory clearance status, map current floor share vs Samsung/Gree, and assess Lulu buyer appetite — before committing any peak-season sell-out support budget.

options.option: Option A — Visit First, Then Decide (Recommended)
options.consequence: Field visit by May 9 confirms overhang-cleared + LG shelf space intact → greenlight conditional sell-out package (display fee + installer co-op) tied to minimum Q2 order commitment, capturing peak season upside with validated intent. If visit reveals competitive displacement, pivot immediately to relationship rescue mode and avoid wasted promotional spend.

options.option: Option B — Watch Buy Pace Through May Without Visiting
options.consequence: Preserves budget but risks missing the peak-season window entirely — if Lulu is actively restocking, a competitor's visit during this window can lock in the floor deal. Account recovers to 2025 levels at best (SAR 700-800K annualized) with no structural upgrade, and root cause remains unresolved heading into 2027 planning.

timeline.step: Field visit to Lulu buyer — diagnose 2025 disengagement, confirm inventory clearance, map floor share vs Samsung/Gree
Sell-out support decision — if visit confirms overhang-cleared and LG shelf intact, issue conditional display + installer co-op proposal with minimum Q2 order threshold
Credit review trigger — if cumulative 2026 sell-in exceeds SAR 500K and YTD DSO holds below 60d for two consecutive months, escalate to credit committee for limit expansion

finance.headline: Grade-C account doubling purchase pace while carrying 69.7% OVD — selective payment behavior and T12M DSO of 239d signal a chronic slow-payer exploiting credit terms as free financing.
finance.analysis:
AR health is materially impaired. SAR 461K of the SAR 662K balance is overdue, and critically, SAR 406K sits in the 30–60d bucket — one ageing cycle away from breaching the 60d credit term ceiling. The absence of 60d+ exposure is the only technical positive, but it is a lagging indicator; within the next 30 days, that bucket converts to severely aged receivables unless payments accelerate. OVD has improved from 94.3% (Apr-29) to 69.7% (May-04), suggesting partial payments were made — consistent with the 'selective payment' flag — but the structural OVD level remains elevated, not resolved.

Collection risk is elevated and time-sensitive. YTD DSO of 128d against a 60d credit term means LG is effectively extending 2x the agreed financing. T12M DSO of 239d reveals this is not a seasonal cash-flow issue but a chronic behavioural pattern — this account routinely pays at 4× credit terms over the past year. The 1.9× acceleration in 2026 buy pace compounds risk: they are loading inventory ahead of peak season (May–September) while legacy balances remain unsettled. This pattern — accelerate purchases, trickle payments, age the oldest buckets — is a textbook sign of a customer using trade credit as a substitute for bank financing.

Credit policy must be tightened. The 35× revenue surge from 2024 to 2026 YTD looks like strong account development on the surface, but it has been financed by LG's increasingly distorted receivables position. A Grade C account in Q4-Watch with T12M DSO of 239d does not merit expanded credit headroom. Maintaining current limits would passively increase exposure as peak-season orders arrive. The credit limit should be decreased — or alternatively, enforced via order block until the 30–60d bucket is substantially cleared — to force payment discipline before the seasonal order surge further inflates the balance.
finance.credit_rationale: T12M DSO of 239d and 69.7% OVD on a Grade-C account demonstrate chronic non-compliance with 60d credit terms; expanding exposure into peak season without payment remediation would materially increase collection risk.
action: Place order block effective immediately: no new shipments until 30–60d bucket (SAR 406K) is reduced below 50% — target payment by May-20 before it ages into 60d+
Initiate direct finance-to-customer escalation call this week: present aged AR schedule and demand a written payment commitment with milestone dates tied to shipment release
Reduce credit limit to SAR 300K (approximately 1× monthly Sell-In pace) and require 30d clean payment history before reinstating prior level — document in SAP credit master
finance.risk_reason: SAR 406K in 30–60d bucket will breach 60d credit term within 30 days; combined with T12M DSO 239d, Grade C rating, and accelerating buy pace, collection deterioration risk is immediate and structural.

sales.headline: Explosive Sell-In growth masks a systematic payment-avoidance pattern — account is using LG as unsecured credit, not a trade partner.
sales.analysis:
채널 볼륨 수치만 보면 인상적이다: 2024 SAR 18K → 2025 SAR 361K → 2026 YTD SAR 648K, T12M 대비 1.9배 바이 페이스로 피크시즌에 진입 중이다. 그러나 성장의 '질'이 문제다. T12M DSO 239일은 60일 Credit Term의 4배이며, YTD DSO도 128일로 여전히 임계치를 훨씬 초과한다. 이 계정은 2025년 갑작스러운 19배 볼륨 점프 이후 LG를 실질적으로 무담보 재고금융 창구로 활용해왔을 가능성이 높다. OVD 94.3%→69.7% 개선이 긍정 신호처럼 읽히지만, 같은 기간 잔액은 SAR 580K→661K로 오히려 증가했다 — 부분 결제 후 신규 발주로 OVD 비율을 희석시키는 고전적 롤링 패턴이다.

30-60d 버킷에 SAR 406,453이 집중된 것은 3~4월 납품분이 아직 전혀 회수되지 않았음을 의미하며, 현 시점에서 Snapshot 한 번만 더 넘어가면 해당 전액이 60d+ 버킷으로 이전될 위험이 있다. 60d+가 현재 0인 것은 '안전'이 아니라 '임박한 위험'의 다른 표현이다. Credit Term 60일 기준으로 보면 계정은 이미 실질적으로 한계선에 서 있다.

투자 판단 측면에서, 피크시즌(5~9월) 직전이라는 점이 딜레마를 심화시킨다. 계정이 계속 발주를 늘리고 있어 영업 입장에서 볼륨 포기가 아깝지만, 신규 크레딧 공급은 미회수 잔액을 더 쌓는 구조가 되어 LG의 익스포저를 키울 뿐이다. Sell-Out 데이터가 없어 채널 실수요를 확인할 수 없다는 점도 리스크를 가중시킨다 — 이 계정이 실제 소비자에게 판매하는지, 아니면 재판매(sub-dealer arbitrage)나 재고 적치 후 대금 지연 전략인지 불분명하다.
sales.investment_rationale: OVD SAR 461K 회수 및 DSO 정상화 없이 추가 투자 집행 시 LG 익스포저만 확대되며, 피크시즌 이후 대규모 미회수 리스크로 전환될 가능성이 높다.
action: 즉시 신규 출하 조건부 동결: 30-60d 버킷 SAR 406K의 50% 이상(≈SAR 200K) 입금 확인 후 재개 — 영업팀 에스컬레이션 및 Finance 승인 요청
5월 2주차 내 계정 담당 영업 동행 방문: 결제 일정 서면 확약(Payment Commitment Letter) 징구, 잔여 Open Order 전량 재검토 후 선별 릴리즈
T12M DSO 239d 근거로 Finance팀에 Credit Limit 재산정 에스컬레이션 — 현 여신 한도 축소 또는 현금선납(COD) 조건 전환 검토 요청
sales.channel_reason: OVD 69.7%·잔액 증가 속 부분결제 희석 — 30-60d SAR 406K가 다음 스냅샷에서 60d+ 전환 임박, DSO T12M 239d는 크레딧 남용 구조를 명시.

synthesis.headline: SAR 406K in 30–60d bucket breaches credit term ceiling within days — order block must be enforced THIS WEEK or LG absorbs a peak-season collection crisis of its own making.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Hold. Account is rolling trade credit as free financing; new shipments before 50% OVD clearance directly fund their inventory, not LG's growth.

summary_3lines: SAR 661K balance with 69.7% OVD and SAR 406K concentrated in the 30–60d bucket — one aging snapshot away from breaching the 60d credit term in full.

T12M DSO 239d and the OVD%↓ / balance↑ pattern (SAR 580K→661K while OVD dropped from 94% to 70%) confirm systematic rolling: partial payments dilute the ratio while new orders rebuild exposure — this is credit arbitrage, not trade partnership.

If no action this week: SAR 406K migrates to 60d+ by mid-May, peak-season order surge inflates the balance past SAR 800K+, and LG exits the summer season with a chronic C-grade debtor holding 4× credit terms — with no seasonal leverage left to collect.
synthesis.decision_required: Order block YES or NO — enforce shipment hold until SAR 406K (30–60d) is reduced by ≥50%, or accept that new credit extended this week becomes the next overdue bucket by July.

options.option: Option A — Full Order Block (Finance-aligned)
options.consequence: No new shipments until SAR 200K+ of the 30–60d bucket is confirmed received AND a written payment milestone schedule is signed. Forces payment discipline before peak-season leverage is lost. Risk: account may source from competitor for the season — volume loss is real but bounded. Upside: prevents SAR 800K+ exposure cliff at season end.

options.option: Option B — Conditional COD Bridge (Sales-aligned)
options.consequence: New orders released only on Cash-on-Delivery or advance payment terms; zero additional credit extension. Existing OVD collection escalated in parallel. Maintains commercial relationship and captures peak-season volume without increasing credit exposure. Risk: requires strict SAP enforcement — any credit slip-through recreates the problem. Requires Finance sign-off on COD override mechanism.

timeline.step: Place SAP order block and notify account: no new shipments until 30–60d bucket reduced by ≥50% (SAR 200K+) — Finance + Sales joint communication
Account visit or escalation call: present aged AR schedule, obtain Payment Commitment Letter with milestone dates (SAR 200K by May-16, remainder by May-31)
Verify SAR 200K receipt — if confirmed, release conditional COD orders only; if not received, maintain full block and escalate to senior management
