Formal credit limit reduction in SAP (to SAR 300K or COD terms) — document in credit master regardless of payment outcome

finance.headline: SAR 138K fully overdue with zero 2026 revenue — account has gone dark while holding LG's receivable.
finance.analysis:
The entire SAR 138,061 balance sits exclusively in the 60d+ aging bucket, meaning 100% of exposure has already breached the contractual 60-day credit term. OVD has held at a static 100% for at least six consecutive days with no partial payment or settlement movement detected, indicating no active engagement from the account side. Most critically, Sama Al Khair has recorded zero Sell-In in 2026 — the commercial relationship has effectively stalled while the legacy debt remains uncollected.

Collection risk is high and structurally deteriorating. T12M DSO of 61 days historically placed this account at the very edge of the credit term, but the current state has broken past that pattern. The absence of 2026 purchases eliminates LG's primary leverage tool — blocking future shipments — since there are no future shipments to block. Grade C classification combined with Q3-Critical quadrant status confirms both weak creditworthiness and poor payment execution. Without an active commercial relationship, there is no organic cash flow pathway from which LG can expect settlement.

From a credit policy standpoint, maintaining or expanding credit to an account with 100% overdue AR and no current-year activity is indefensible. Peak season pressure (May–September) may create internal sales urgency to re-engage Sama Al Khair, but releasing new inventory on credit before clearing SAR 138,061 would compound exposure on an already-distressed account with no collection traction. Credit must be suspended immediately and reinstated only upon full clearance or a formalized installment plan with a material down payment.
finance.credit_rationale: 100% of AR is overdue past credit term, zero 2026 commercial activity removes all collection leverage, and no payment movement has been observed — further credit extension would deepen uncollectable exposure.
action: Issue formal written demand for SAR 138,061 today with a 7-day response deadline; copy legal/finance for escalation readiness
Place account on credit hold immediately — zero new orders or shipments until outstanding AR is cleared or a signed installment plan with minimum 50% down payment is executed

Assign channel manager for in-person visit before May 15 to assess account viability, collect settlement commitment, and determine whether to pursue legal recovery or negotiate a structured write-off ahead of peak season
finance.risk_reason: 100% AR overdue in 60d+ bucket, zero 2026 revenue, six-day static OVD trend — full SAR 138K exposure at risk with no active collection leverage.

sales.headline: Sama Al Khair has gone dark in 2026 — zero buys, 100% overdue balance, entering peak season without a single order.
sales.analysis:
채널 헬스 관점에서 이 계정은 사실상 동면 상태입니다. 2025년 SAR 822K의 Sell-In 실적이 있었으나 2026년 YTD 매출은 0이며, 5월 피크시즌 진입 시점에도 발주 움직임이 전혀 없습니다. SAR 138K 잔액 전액이 60일 초과 연체(60d+)로 굳어진 채 최소 1주일 이상 100% OVD 상태가 지속되고 있어, 단순 결제 지연이 아닌 유동성 경색 또는 관계 단절 신호로 해석해야 합니다.

매출 단절의 원인은 두 가지 가설이 경합합니다. 첫째, 2025년 재고 소진 실패로 인한 오버행 — 지난해 구매한 물량이 아직 창고에 남아 있어 신규 발주 여력이 없는 상태. 둘째, 경쟁사 전환 — 삼성·Gree 등이 더 유리한 결제 조건이나 판촉 지원을 제시하며 이 계정을 흡수했을 가능성. 어느 쪽이든 현 시점에서 이 계정은 피크시즌(5~9월) 기여를 기대하기 어려운 구조입니다.

투자 관점에서는 명백히 '회수 우선' 모드입니다. 신규 여신 확대나 셀아웃 지원은 미회수 채권 위험만 키웁니다. Grade C + Q3 Critical + 100% OVD + 2026 zero revenue의 조합은 추가 자원 투입의 근거를 제공하지 않습니다. 미수금 SAR 138K 회수를 선결 조건으로 설정하고, 결제 이행 후 관계 재건 여부를 평가하는 단계적 접근이 합리적입니다.
sales.investment_rationale: 100% 연체 + 2026 zero buy로 채권 회수 없이는 어떤 투자도 손실 확대로 귀결됨.
action: 즉시 SAR 138K 미수금 공식 회수 통보 발송 — 60d+ 전액, 법적 조치 예고 포함
영업 담당자 현장 방문으로 재고 보유 현황 및 영업 정상화 의지 확인 (경쟁사 전환 여부 파악)
결제 완료 시까지 신규 오더 수락 중단 — 여신 한도 0 처리 후 재개 조건 서면 합의
sales.channel_reason: 2026 YTD 매출 0 + 전액 60d+ 연체 — 피크시즌 진입에도 발주·결제 모두 멈춤.

synthesis.headline: SAR 138K 전액 60일 초과 연체, 2026년 매출 제로 — 계정이 채무만 남긴 채 사실상 이탈했으며 피크시즌 진입에도 어떤 회복 신호도 없다.

verdict.one_line: Risk RED — Exit. 채권 회수 레버리지(미래 출하)가 소멸된 상태에서 100% 연체가 고착 중이며, 추가 자원 투입은 손실 확대 외에 근거가 없다.

summary_3lines: Sama Al Khair는 SAR 138K 전액을 60일 이상 연체한 채 2026년 단 한 건의 발주도 없이 6일 이상 완전 동면 상태다.
2025년 재고 오버행 또는 경쟁사 전환으로 상업 관계가 실질 단절됐고, LG가 보유한 유일한 레버리지인 '신규 출하 차단'이 이미 무력화된 구조다.
이 상태를 방치하면 SAR 138K는 피크시즌이 끝난 3Q에도 미회수 상태로 남아 대손 처리 압력이 올라오며, 선례로 인해 동일 채널 내 유사 계정의 결제 기강도 이완될 위험이 있다.
synthesis.decision_required: 이번 주 내 SAR 138K 공식 회수 통보 발송 여부 및 법적 조치 에스컬레이션 수준을 결정하라 — 협상 유예 없이 즉시 집행할 것인가, 7일 유예 후 분할 합의를 수용할 것인가.

options.option: Option A — 즉시 강경 집행: 공식 내용증명 발송 + 여신 한도 0 처리 + 법적 조치 예고 (7일 기한)
options.consequence: SAR 138K 회수 가능성을 최대화하나, 계정 관계가 완전히 종결될 수 있음. 2025년 SAR 823K 수준의 매출 복구는 사실상 포기하는 경로.

options.option: Option B — 현장 방문 선행 후 분할 합의 협상 (5월 12일까지): 최소 50% 선납 조건으로 잔액 분할 계획 서면 체결
options.consequence: 회수 시간이 지연되나 계정 이탈 원인(재고 오버행 vs 경쟁사 전환) 확인 후 관계 재건 가능성을 열어둠. 단, 합의 불이행 시 Option A로 즉시 전환해야 하며 의사결정 일관성 유지가 필수.

timeline.step: 공식 회수 통보 발송 (내용증명) — Finance + Legal 공동 발신, 7일 응답 기한 명시
영업 담당자 현장 방문 — 재고 현황·경쟁사 전환 여부 확인, 결제 의향 및 분할 합의 가능성 판단
결과에 따라 분기 처리: 합의 시 서면 분할 계획 체결 / 무응답·거절 시 법적 조치 즉시 착수 및 대손 검토 개시

finance.headline: Zero 2026 AR exposure masks a structurally broken account — Grade D, Q3 Critical, dormant since 2024 with no sign of return.
finance.analysis:
Current AR balance is SAR 0 with no overdue aging whatsoever, which superficially reads as clean. However, this is not a sign of health — it reflects total dormancy. The account recorded SAR 532,132 in Sell-In during 2024 and has generated zero revenue in 2026 (and likely 2025 as well, given no T12M DSO is available). The absence of activity is itself a signal: this account has either self-selected out of ordering, or has been operationally constrained by prior credit restrictions. Either way, LG holds no live receivable risk today.

The deeper concern is the underlying credit quality profile. Grade D is the bottom of the risk tier, and the Q3 Critical quadrant classification — defined by DSO exceeding DPO 180d and elevated OVD — tells us this account historically failed to pay within terms, and the stress was severe enough to hit both collection and repayment simultaneously. With peak season (May–September) now beginning, there is a real possibility the account attempts to re-engage for summer inventory. If new Sell-In is granted without disciplined controls, LG risks replicating the prior overdue pattern at a point in the season when collection leverage diminishes post-peak.

Credit policy recommendation is unambiguous: do not expand credit. The combination of Grade D, Q3 Critical history, and multi-year dormancy does not support any credit limit increase. If the account resurfaces with orders, a zero-credit (prepayment or secured LC) policy should be enforced until at least two consecutive clean payment cycles are demonstrated. Maintaining a nominal limit on paper while enforcing prepayment in practice is the prudent structure here.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade D rating combined with Q3 Critical historical classification and complete 2026 dormancy justifies reducing or zeroing the credit limit to eliminate re-engagement risk without prepayment controls.
action: Flag account in credit system as 'prepayment-only' before any new order is accepted — no open credit releases regardless of season demand pressure
Confirm with Sales whether account has made any informal inquiries for Q2/Q3 2026 stock; if so, qualify solvency before engaging
Schedule formal credit review in 90 days — if still zero activity, consider reclassifying as inactive and archiving from active coverage reporting
finance.risk_reason: Zero current exposure is deceptive — Grade D + Q3 Critical history + peak-season re-engagement risk makes this account a latent high-risk exposure if credit is extended without prepayment enforcement.

sales.headline: Complete 2026 radio silence into peak season — account has effectively disengaged from LG.
sales.analysis:
The account purchased SAR 532K in 2024 but has posted zero Sell-In through May 2026 — the opening month of Saudi peak season. For an IR_Others account with a 90-day credit term, the absence of any order activity at this point in the calendar year is not a timing anomaly; it is a structural disengagement signal. The zero AR balance confirms there is no residual commercial relationship in play: no open orders, no outstanding receivables, nothing to indicate the account is warm but slow. The pipeline is empty in both directions.

Root cause hypotheses, in order of likelihood: (1) Competitor switch — a Grade D IR_Others account with modest historical volume is a prime target for Samsung or Gree aggressive pricing during pre-season loading; (2) Business model shift — the account name ('European Tents Factory') suggests a manufacturing or B2B-adjacent operation, and their AC procurement pattern may have moved to project-based or direct-sourcing channels; (3) Financial stress leading to voluntary disengagement — they may have chosen not to open credit exposure ahead of the 90-day cycle given internal cash constraints, even though current AR is clean.

With Q3-Critical quadrant designation and a Grade D rating, this account carried structural risk even when it was buying. The combination of zero 2026 revenue, no active AR, and peak season already opening without a single purchase order makes the investment case essentially nil. Deploying sell-out support, co-marketing, or credit uplift into this account would require proof of life — evidence the account is still actively retailing AC — which does not currently exist.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 Sell-In into peak season with no AR activity confirms disengagement; Grade D IR_Others account does not justify proactive resource allocation without a concrete re-engagement signal from the account.
action: Field visit within 2 weeks: confirm account is still operational and understand 2026 non-purchase reason (competitor switch, business change, or financial withdrawal)
If account is still active retailer: one-time re-engagement offer tied to a minimum order threshold (e.g., SAR 100K pre-season loading) — no credit extension without proof of sell-through
If no positive response by end of May: formally downgrade to dormant, reallocate any earmarked BTL budget to higher-grade IR accounts heading into peak June–July demand
sales.channel_reason: Zero purchases in 2026 through peak season open with no AR activity — account is effectively inactive.

synthesis.headline: Account is effectively dead — zero 2026 activity into peak season opening; the only decision is whether to formally close it or run one last field visit before archiving.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. No live AR exposure today, but Grade D + Q3 Critical history demands prepayment-only lock before any peak-season re-engagement attempt.

summary_3lines: SAR 532K buyer in 2024 has gone completely silent — zero Sell-In, zero AR, zero pipeline activity through the opening of Saudi peak season 2026.

Grade D rating and Q3 Critical quadrant confirm this account historically failed on both collection and payment simultaneously; dormancy is not recovery — it is either competitor defection, business model shift, or self-imposed credit retreat.

If no action is taken and the account spontaneously re-engages in June–July under a season-pressure sales cycle, LG risks replicating the prior overdue pattern at the worst possible collection window — when leverage disappears post-peak.
synthesis.decision_required: Enforce prepayment-only policy NOW and dispatch a field visit within 2 weeks — if no confirmed operational status and re-engagement signal by end of May, formally archive as dormant and reallocate any reserved budget.

options.option: Option A — Prepayment lock + field visit (recommended)
options.consequence: Credit system flagged prepayment-only immediately; sales rep visits within 2 weeks to confirm account is still operational; if active, a single conditional re-engagement offer (SAR 100K+ prepaid minimum) is extended; if no response by 31 May, account is archived. Cost: 1 field visit. Risk: near-zero.

options.option: Option B — Passive watch with no action
options.consequence: Account remains on standard credit terms on paper; if re-engagement occurs under peak-season urgency, sales may release an order without finance sign-off, reinstating Grade D credit exposure during June–August — the exact window where post-peak collection deteriorates fastest.

timeline.step: Flag account as 'prepayment-only' in credit system — block any order release without CFO sign-off
timeline.deadline: 2026-05-07 (this week, before any order is accepted)

timeline.step: Field visit: confirm account is still operational, identify 2026 non-purchase reason, and assess competitor/financial situation
Go/No-Go decision: if no positive re-engagement signal, formally reclassify as dormant, archive from active coverage, reallocate BTL budget to higher-grade IR accounts for peak season

finance.headline: Dormant Grade-D account with zero 2026 activity entering peak season — no live AR exposure but historically distressed payment profile warrants hard credit freeze.
finance.analysis:
AR health is superficially clean: SAR 0 balance, SAR 0 overdue as of 2026-05-05. However, this cleanliness is not a sign of financial strength — it reflects complete commercial inactivity in 2026. The account placed SAR 999,985 Sell-In in 2024 but has generated zero revenue in 2026 YTD, including the opening months of Saudi peak season (May entry). Zero orders from a channel-active IR account in peak season is itself a distress signal: the account either lost credit access, redirected volume to competitors, or is operationally impaired.

Collection risk on existing balance is technically nil — nothing to collect. But the systemic risk is the Q3 Critical quadrant designation, which is driven by DSO historically exceeding DPO (180d), combined with a 35.4% YTD OVD baseline. Grade D further confirms chronic payment stress. If LG were to extend new credit today, it would be extending into a profile that has demonstrated inability to honor even 90-day terms. The 35.4% OVD baseline means over one-third of past receivables historically aged past due — far above acceptable IR channel standards.

Credit policy is straightforward: this account must not receive incremental credit exposure without a formal credit review and settlement of any legacy obligations. The combination of Grade D + Q3 Critical + zero 2026 engagement is a pattern consistent with account deterioration, not recovery. Maintaining credit limit at current level is acceptable only if that limit effectively functions as a freeze. Any reactive credit extension to 'reactivate' this account in peak season would be high-risk capital deployment with low collection confidence.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade D, Q3 Critical quadrant, 35.4% historical OVD baseline, and full 2026 commercial dormancy collectively indicate the existing credit ceiling should be reduced to zero or near-zero until a formal credit rehabilitation review is completed.
action: Freeze new Sell-In approvals for GOLDEN OVEN .CO pending a mandatory credit review — no shipments without Finance sign-off regardless of open order requests
Conduct account status investigation via IR sales team: determine why zero orders in 2026 peak season entry (voluntary exit, LG credit block, or account insolvency)

If account requests reactivation, require: (1) signed payment commitment letter, (2) minimum 30% advance payment on first order, (3) reduce credit term from 90d to 30d COD-forward until two clean payment cycles are confirmed
finance.risk_reason: No live AR exposure today, but Grade D + Q3 Critical history + zero 2026 activity creates high probability of collection failure if credit is extended — watchlist status maintained.

sales.headline: GOLDEN OVEN has gone dark — zero 2026 sell-in entering peak season signals effective self-exit from LG
sales.analysis:
The account posted SAR ~1M in 2024 sell-in but has recorded zero revenue through May 2026, with peak season (May–September) already underway. In the IR_Others segment, a D-grade account that has not placed a single order by Week 18 of the calendar year is not exhibiting a slow-start pattern — it has disengaged. The clean AR balance (SAR 0) is not a positive signal here; it simply means there is no active trade relationship to speak of, rather than any evidence of improved financial discipline.

Root cause is most likely one of two scenarios: (1) inventory overhang — if 2024 purchases were over-stocked relative to sell-out velocity, the account may still be liquidating and has no headroom or appetite to re-order; or (2) competitor switch — Gree, Midea, or Hisense have been aggressive on price in the IR channel, and a Grade D account with tight margins is exactly the profile that migrates to lowest-cost supply. The 35.4% YTD OVD baseline on the portfolio further confirms this account has historically struggled to convert sell-in into timely cash, suggesting sell-out velocity was always weak.

With credit term already at 90 days and a Critical Q3 quadrant driven by chronic overdue patterns, extending further credit or funding promotional support into this account carries asymmetric downside. There is no current momentum signal — no open order, no recent engagement, no evidence of pipeline — that would justify unlocking additional investment. The account should be classified as effectively inactive.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 sell-in at peak season onset, Grade D classification, and chronic OVD history offer no credible return on incremental investment; resources are better redeployed to active IR accounts showing Q1/Q2 momentum.
action: Confirm account status with local sales rep — one direct call to determine if disengagement is intentional (competitor switch, business wind-down) or recoverable (cash flow pause)
Do not extend new credit or initiate any promotional outreach until account places an order and demonstrates willingness to re-engage on LG terms
If rep confirms account is actively buying from competitors, flag for formal inactive classification and reallocate 90d credit line capacity to higher-grade IR accounts
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in through peak season entry with no open order pipeline — account is functionally inactive

synthesis.headline: GOLDEN OVEN has self-exited LG at peak season — no current AR fire, but any credit extension into this Grade D / Q3 Critical profile would be capital destruction.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. Zero AR exposure today, but Grade D + 35.4% OVD history makes reactivation credit a trap; redeploy the 90d line to performing IR accounts.

summary_3lines: GOLDEN OVEN placed SAR 1M Sell-In in 2024 but has recorded zero revenue through May 2026 — entering peak season with no open orders, no pipeline, no signal of intent to re-engage.

Root cause is most likely competitor migration (Gree / Midea / Hisense price pressure on a margin-thin Grade D buyer) or uncleared 2024 inventory overhang suppressing reorder appetite — either way, the account is functionally inactive.

If LG takes no action, the account exits the ecosystem permanently and the 90d credit capacity sits idle through peak; if LG reactively re-extends credit to force reactivation, it repeats the same chronic OVD cycle with no sell-out velocity to backstop it.
synthesis.decision_required: This week: authorize one field confirmation call to determine if disengagement is permanent (competitor switch / business wind-down) or recoverable — then immediately classify as inactive or set hard rehabilitation conditions before any shipment clears Finance.

options.option: Option A — Immediate Inactive Classification
options.consequence: Freeze credit line to zero, no outreach, reallocate 90d capacity to Q1/Q2 IR accounts with demonstrated sell-out momentum. GOLDEN OVEN re-engages only if it self-initiates on LG's revised terms (30% advance + 30d COD until two clean cycles). Opportunity cost: ~SAR 1M revenue foregone if account was recoverable, but zero collection risk.

options.option: Option B — Controlled Recovery Probe
options.consequence: One sales rep call by May 9 to determine root cause. If recoverable, require (1) signed payment commitment, (2) 30% advance on first order, (3) credit term reduced from 90d to 30d. If rep confirms competitor switch or wind-down, immediately execute Option A. Limits downside while preserving a narrow reactivation window during peak season.

timeline.step: Sales rep direct call to GOLDEN OVEN — determine voluntary exit vs recoverable pause
timeline.deadline: 2026-05-09 (this week, before any order could theoretically enter the pipeline)

timeline.step: Finance credit freeze formalized in system — no Sell-In approval without CFO sign-off regardless of order request

finance.headline: Dormant D-grade account with zero 2026 activity and zero current AR — collection risk is nil today, but structural Grade D / Q3-Critical history means any peak-season reactivation must be treated as a new credit application, not a renewal.
finance.analysis:
Current AR position is clean at SAR 0, so there is no live collection risk as of 05-May-2026. However, this cleanliness is misleading: it reflects complete account dormancy, not healthy trading. The account generated SAR 668K in sell-in during 2024, implying a real commercial relationship existed — yet 2026 shows zero revenue with peak season now opening. Dormancy of this kind in IR_Others typically signals one of three scenarios: (1) LG internally froze credit due to past overdue behavior, (2) the dealer is under liquidity stress and unable to commit orders, or (3) the dealer has defected to a competing brand. Grade D classification and the Q3-Critical quadrant tag — which requires both DSO > 180d (2× credit term of 90d) and elevated OVD — confirm this account carried a structurally poor payment history prior to going dark.

Credit policy assessment is straightforward: with a Grade D rating, Q3-Critical history, and a full fiscal year of zero activity, there is no justification for maintaining or expanding a credit line. The portfolio-level YTD OVD baseline of 35.4% is already elevated; exposing additional credit to a Grade D dormant account in a high-OVD environment compounds concentration risk. If the account reactivates for peak season (May–September), the correct entry gate is Cash-in-Advance (CIA) or 30d maximum with 50% prepayment — not the original 90d credit term.

The single actionable question for sales: why did this account go silent in 2026? The answer determines whether this is a recoverable relationship worth re-engaging under tighter terms, or a de facto write-off candidate to be formally closed and replaced with a healthier IR dealer in the same region.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade D + Q3-Critical history with zero 2026 activity eliminates any basis for open credit; reactivation terms must revert to CIA or secured prepayment until a minimum 2-quarter clean payment record is established.
action: Instruct sales to conduct an immediate account status call — determine whether dormancy is LG-side (credit freeze) or dealer-side (liquidity/defection); document outcome in CRM before any peak-season order is accepted.
Flag account in ERP: block standard 90d credit term; require finance pre-approval with CIA or ≥50% prepayment condition for any new order above SAR 50K during May–September peak window.
If dealer confirms intent to reactivate, initiate formal Grade D re-evaluation: request updated bank reference, trade references, and current balance sheet before restoring any open credit line.
finance.risk_reason: Zero live AR means no immediate collection loss, but Grade D + Q3-Critical precedent and full-year dormancy create high reactivation risk if credit terms are not tightened before any new peak-season order is booked.

sales.headline: Account has gone completely dark in 2026 — zero purchases entering peak season signals probable competitor switch or terminal disengagement.
sales.analysis:
ATALLAH MAJOUL AL-HUDHALI recorded SAR 668K in sell-in during 2024 but shows zero 2026 revenue as of May 2026 — the heart of Saudi AC pre-peak loading season. AR balance is also zero, meaning the account has cleared prior obligations and simply stopped ordering. This pattern — debt settled, buying stopped — is a classic quiet exit: the account has likely either shifted its sourcing to a competitor (Gree/Midea at lower price points are common IR_Others switchers) or is sitting on unsold 2024/2025 inventory with no capacity to absorb new stock. There is no sell-out visibility to confirm which, but the complete absence of any 2026 buy activity through May, when even marginal IR accounts typically load ahead of summer, is a strong disengagement signal.

The Q3 Critical quadrant classification reflects the historical stress pattern — elevated OVD and extended DSO on prior cycles — but current financials are technically clean (SAR 0 balance). This does not rehabilitate the account; it simply means LG has no current exposure. The Grade D rating confirms this is a low-strategic-value outlet in the IR_Others tier, where switching costs for the account are minimal and LG brand pull is weakest relative to price-competitive alternatives.

With peak season (May–September) already opening and no orders placed, the window to generate meaningful 2026 volume from this account has effectively closed. Even if the account were to reactivate now, sell-through of new stock before season-end would be uncertain, and extending credit to a historically stressed D-grade account into a peak cycle carries collection risk with limited upside. Investment here diverts attention and credit headroom from higher-grade, actively buying IR accounts.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 activity entering peak season on a Grade D, historically stressed account offers no credible return on any form of LG investment.
action: Confirm no open orders or outstanding commitments — if truly clean, formally close active account management coverage and reallocate sales visit frequency to Grade B/C IR accounts with 2026 momentum.

Conduct a single reactivation call to determine root cause (inventory overhang vs. competitor switch vs. financial constraint) — log finding for future cycle review, but do not offer credit extension or promotional support.
If competitor switch is confirmed, flag to IR channel manager as a price-positioning signal for IR_Others segment — pattern may indicate a broader Gree/Midea encroachment in this sub-tier.
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 sell-in with zero AR balance entering peak season — account has quietly exited without formal notice.

synthesis.headline: Account has quietly exited — zero 2026 buys entering peak season on a Grade D / Q3-Critical history means there is no live collection risk today, but any reactivation attempt without tightened terms converts a dormant risk into an active one.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. Account cleared debt and stopped buying; reactivation on legacy 90d terms would create new exposure with near-zero upside.

summary_3lines: ATALLAH went silent in 2026 — SAR 668K buyer in 2024, zero orders in 2026 entering peak season, AR balance fully cleared to SAR 0.

Grade D + Q3-Critical classification confirms structurally stressed payment history prior to dormancy; quiet exit pattern (debt settled, buying stopped) signals competitor switch (Gree/Midea) or inventory overhang — not a temporary pause.

If management does nothing and a sales rep extends the original 90d credit line to restart the relationship during peak season, LG books new exposure on a Grade D account at the worst possible time — with no sell-out visibility and no collection leverage.
synthesis.decision_required: Decide this week whether to formally close active coverage and reallocate credit headroom, or run a single CIA-only reactivation probe — there is no middle path where legacy 90d terms are acceptable.

options.option: Option A — Controlled Exit: Close active account management, conduct one diagnostic call (competitor switch vs. inventory overhang), log finding, reallocate sales visits and credit headroom to active Grade B/C IR accounts.
options.consequence: Zero new credit exposure; intelligence captured for IR_Others channel pricing review; no peak-season volume from this account but also no collection risk.

options.option: Option B — CIA-Only Reactivation Probe: Allow a single order this peak season under Cash-in-Advance or minimum 50% prepayment terms, with a SAR 50K order cap and finance pre-approval gate.
options.consequence: Potential partial volume recovery (likely sub-SAR 200K given late-season entry); establishes whether account is genuinely reclaimable before formal exit decision; adds minimal credit risk if CIA terms are enforced.

timeline.step: Sales conducts single diagnostic call — confirm root cause (competitor switch / inventory overhang / LG-side credit freeze) and document in CRM
Finance blocks 90d credit term in ERP regardless of outcome; CIA / ≥50% prepayment flag activated on account
Based on diagnostic outcome: formally close account OR issue CIA-only proforma for peak-season order — no decision in limbo beyond this date

finance.headline: Zero current exposure masks a dormant Grade-C account that has generated no 2026 revenue entering peak season — structural risk, not active collection risk.
finance.analysis:
AR health is superficially clean: SAR 0 balance, SAR 0 overdue, all aging buckets empty. There is literally nothing to collect, which eliminates near-term collection risk. However, the zero balance must be read alongside context: this account produced SAR 1.06M in 2024 Sell-In and has placed zero orders in 2026 through May — the precise month peak season begins (May–September). The dormancy is the signal, not the clean AR. A Grade C SME account going silent at season entry typically indicates one of three scenarios: (a) cash-flow stress forcing the account to pause purchasing, (b) a relationship or service breakdown, or (c) the account has shifted wallet-share to a competitor. All three carry downstream risk if credit is extended when activity resumes.

The Q3 Critical quadrant classification is consistent with the 35.4% YTD avg OVD baseline — meaning when this account is active, more than one-third of its balance historically sits overdue. Paired with a 90-day credit term and the Critical label (DSO > 180d DPO threshold), the account's payment discipline under active trading conditions has been materially weak. The current zero-balance 'clean' state does not represent a rehabilitation of that behavior; it represents inactivity.

Credit policy should be tightened, not preserved at prior limits. A Grade C, Q3 Critical account with no 2026 activity should not carry a standing credit line that reflects 2024 peak-season volumes. If the account re-engages, any new credit exposure should be approved on a per-order basis with tighter terms (cash-in-advance or 30-day max) until the account demonstrates consistent on-time payment and earns a grade upgrade. Maintaining a legacy credit limit signals tolerance for a risk profile that the data does not support.
finance.credit_rationale: Grade C + Q3 Critical classification with 35.4% historical OVD rate and full 2026 dormancy justifies reducing credit limit to minimum or per-order cash basis until payment behavior is rehabilitated.
action: Freeze standing credit limit — require per-order approval with cash-in-advance or ≤30d terms if account re-engages during peak season (May–Sep)
Conduct outreach to determine cause of 2026 dormancy before any new order acceptance — distinguish cash-stress vs. competitor shift vs. relationship gap
Flag account for Q2 credit review: if no Sell-In by end of June, reclassify as inactive and remove from credit exposure reporting to avoid distorting SME channel metrics
finance.risk_reason: Zero current AR exposure eliminates red-level immediacy, but Grade C + 35.4% historical OVD + complete 2026 dormancy at peak-season entry creates latent credit risk if the account reactivates without tighter terms in place.

sales.headline: Dormant SME account — zero 2026 revenue entering peak season signals likely competitor defection or business contraction.
sales.analysis:
Khalid Alessa posted SAR 1.06M in 2024 but shows zero sell-in for 2026 YTD, and T12M DSO is N/A — indicating no meaningful purchase activity for at least 12–18 months. We are now at peak season entry (May), the single highest-urgency buy window for any Saudi AC account; continued silence at this juncture is not a seasonal gap but a structural signal. Root cause is most likely competitor lock-in (Samsung or Gree are aggressive in SME pricing) or business-level contraction, rather than inventory overhang — there is no open AR balance suggesting outstanding stock to work through.

The clean AR (SAR 0 balance, SAR 0 overdue) is the one constructive data point: there is no collection liability, meaning LG has no financial exposure and the account has a clean slate for potential re-engagement. However, the Grade C classification and a historical OVD baseline of 35.4% confirm this account carried above-average payment risk when it was active. The 90-day credit term is already tight for SME, which likely constrained their buy appetite versus competitors offering softer terms.

Investment priority is low. With zero 2026 momentum, no sell-out visibility, and peak season already open, deploying co-marketing or credit extension here would be speculative. The correct move is a single targeted re-engagement call — probe why they went dark, assess whether a competitor has locked them in, and determine if a small trial order is possible before end of May. If no response or confirmed competitor lock-in emerges within 2 weeks, classify as exit and reallocate resources to active SME accounts.
sales.investment_rationale: Account is dormant but AR is clean — one peak-season re-engagement attempt is warranted before writing off the SAR 1M annual run rate; no broader investment until buy intent is confirmed.
action: Sales rep direct outreach within 48 hours — confirm account is still operational and identify why purchasing stopped (competitor switch, financial issue, or business change)
If account is active, offer a limited peak-season trial order (small SKU, short credit window) to test re-engagement with low exposure
If no response or confirmed competitor lock-in by end of May, reclassify to exit and redirect SME development resources to higher-grade accounts
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 revenue with T12M DSO N/A — effectively dormant entering the most critical buy window of the year.

synthesis.headline: 2024년 SAR 1.06M 실적 계정이 피크 시즌 진입 시점에 완전 침묵 — 경쟁사 이탈 또는 사업 위축 여부를 48시간 내 확인하지 않으면 해당 물량을 영구 상실할 위험.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. AR 노출은 제로이나 Grade C + 35.4% 연체 이력 보유 계정이 피크 시즌 첫 달에 침묵 중 — 재활성화 시 신용 확대 금지, 재활성화 가능성 확인 후 투자 결정.

summary_3lines: 2024년 SAR 1.06M 매입 계정이 2026년 YTD 매입 제로, T12M DSO N/A — 최소 12~18개월간 실질 거래 없음.
Grade C + Q3 Critical + 역사적 OVD 35.4% 조합은 활성 거래 시 신용 리스크가 높았음을 증명하며, 현재 침묵은 재활성화 아닌 이탈 신호일 가능성이 높음.
48시간 내 원인 파악 없이 피크 시즌(5~9월)이 지나면 SAR 1M 연간 물량은 삼성·Gree 등 경쟁사로 영구 귀속될 수 있음.
synthesis.decision_required: 48시간 내 직접 아웃리치로 이탈 원인을 확인하고 — '재활성화 시도(소량 Trial Order + ≤30일 조건)' vs. '즉시 Exit 및 자원 재배분' 중 하나를 선택.

options.option: Option A — 제한적 재활성화 시도
options.consequence: 48시간 내 담당 영업 직접 접촉 → 계정 운영 확인 시 소량 SKU + 30일 이하 신용조건 Trial Order 제안. 5월 말까지 반응 없거나 경쟁사 고착 확인 시 즉시 Exit으로 전환. 성공 시 SAR 1M 물량 일부 회수 가능, 실패해도 추가 신용 노출 없음.

options.option: Option B — 즉시 Exit 및 자원 재배분
options.consequence: 아웃리치 없이 비활성 처리, SME 개발 자원을 고등급 활성 계정에 집중 재배분. SAR 1M 연간 물량은 포기하되, Grade C 계정 재활성화에 따른 신용 리스크 완전 차단.

timeline.step: 담당 영업 직접 아웃리치 — 2026년 침묵 원인 확인 (경쟁사 이탈 / 자금 문제 / 사업 변화)
재활성화 가능 시: 소량 Trial Order 제안 (≤30일 조건, 현금선불 또는 선결제 우선), 신용한도 동결 유지
반응 없거나 경쟁사 고착 확인 시: 비활성 재분류 + 신용 보고에서 제거 + SME 자원 재배분

finance.headline: Dormant Grade-C account with zero 2026 activity — no live AR exposure today, but re-engagement ahead of peak season without cleared Q3-Critical status creates latent credit risk.
finance.analysis:
AR Health: The account carries a zero balance as of 2026-05-05, meaning there is no outstanding receivable to collect and no immediate impairment risk. The Q3-Critical quadrant classification is therefore a legacy signal — reflecting the account's historical payment behavior profile (OVD pattern and DSO exceeding DPO of 180d in prior periods) rather than a live collection emergency. On a pure AR-health basis, collection risk today is nil.

Dormancy Signal: The complete absence of 2026 Sell-In activity against SAR 1.12M shipped in 2024 is the substantive red flag. Whether this dormancy reflects the account switching to a competitor, LG's own internal credit suspension, or the customer's financial incapacity to place orders is unresolved and must be investigated before any peak-season exposure decision. A Grade-C IR_Others account going dark at the exact start of the May–September peak window is a pattern consistent with either financial distress or active de-prioritization of LG — both scenarios demand field-level confirmation, not a desk assumption.

Credit Policy Rationale: With zero live exposure, reducing the credit limit has no practical protective value. Conversely, extending standard 90d open credit to re-engage this account without first clearing the Q3-Critical classification would be imprudent — the historical OVD structure that drove this grade has not been disproven, merely paused. The operationally sound posture is to maintain the limit on paper but require advance payment or a significantly compressed term (net-30 or net-45) for any first new order, pending one clean payment cycle that allows DSO recalculation.
finance.credit_rationale: Zero live exposure removes urgency to cut limits, but Grade-C Q3-Critical history blocks any increase until re-engagement terms are cleared and at least one full payment cycle is verified clean.
action: Assign Sales Director to conduct a field visit by 2026-05-13 to confirm root cause of 2026 dormancy — self-exit, internal credit hold, or customer financial incapacity — and report back before any new order is accepted.
If re-engagement is approved, require 40–50% advance payment on the first shipment and cap terms at net-45 (not the 90d credit line) until a clean billing cycle is logged; escalate exceptions to CFO.
Retain Q3-Critical flag and block standard 90d open credit release until end-of-June 2026 review confirms positive DSO and OVD trend from at least one completed cycle.
finance.risk_reason: No live AR today, but a Grade-C dormant account with unresolved Q3-Critical history entering peak season is a latent risk if standard credit terms are extended without conditions.

sales.headline: Complete 2026 buy-out with zero activity entering peak season — account has effectively gone dark.
sales.analysis:
Specialized Treatment Company posted SAR 1.12M in 2024 Sell-In but shows zero 2026 revenue through May — five months into the year and three months before peak-season demand peaks in July–August. The clean AR balance (SAR 0 outstanding, SAR 0 overdue) tells only half the story: the account has settled prior debt but stopped reordering entirely. This is the most dangerous disengagement pattern — it looks financially clean on the ledger but masks a relationship rupture or competitor switch. With no 2026 orders and the peak window now open, any inventory they are selling is either prior-cycle stock or sourced from a competitor like Samsung or Gree.

The Q3 Critical quadrant designation — driven by DSO > DPO 180d and a 35.4% YTD average overdue baseline — reflects how the account behaved when it was actually buying. The fact that they cleared their balance without reordering in 2026 suggests either: (a) financial stress preventing new commitment, (b) a deliberate switch to a competing brand that offered better credit terms or margins, or (c) structural business change (downsizing, pivot away from AC). IR_Others channel placement and Grade C classification further reinforce limited strategic weight — this was never a tier-1 account.

The investment calculus is straightforward: there is no active channel to support. Injecting promotional support, co-marketing, or credit expansion into an account that hasn't placed a single order in 2026 would be capital misallocation during peak season, when those same resources could drive velocity in active Q1/Q2 accounts. The priority action is a diagnostic visit to understand whether the account is salvageable before peak closes in September.
sales.investment_rationale: Zero 2026 revenue entering peak season disqualifies active investment — relationship triage must establish disengagement cause before any resource commitment.
action: Assign KAM for in-person visit within 2 weeks: determine root cause of zero 2026 orders — financial freeze, competitor switch, or business contraction
If account confirms intent to reorder: offer limited 60d credit (down from 90d) contingent on minimum SAR 300K peak-season order by end of June, with overdue rate clause
If no reorder intent confirmed by mid-June: flag for commercial team as dormant and reallocate any reserved credit line to active IR_Others accounts
sales.channel_reason: Zero 2026 Sell-In across 5 months including pre-peak April; account appears fully disengaged with no forward buy signals.

synthesis.headline: Account went completely dark in 2026 (SAR 0 vs SAR 1.12M in 2024) — no live AR risk today, but root cause of disengagement is unknown with peak season now open; extending standard 90d credit without a field diagnosis would be a policy error.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Hold. Zero AR exposure removes urgency, but unresolved Q3-Critical history plus zero 2026 activity means no resource commitment until a KAM visit confirms the account is alive and on what terms.

summary_3lines: Specialized Treatment Company cleared all prior debt but placed zero orders in 2026 — five months of silence entering the May–September peak window.
The Q3-Critical flag reflects a historical OVD and DSO-over-DPO pattern; the account is financially quiet now only because it stopped buying, not because behavior improved.

If we do nothing: the peak window closes in September with this account either fully in a competitor's pocket or permanently dormant, and the SAR 1.12M revenue base is written off with no deliberate decision ever made.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize a KAM field visit by 2026-05-13 to determine whether this account is recoverable — competitor switch, financial freeze, or structural exit — before committing any credit, promotional support, or peak-season inventory allocation.

options.option: Option A — Diagnostic-first re-engagement
options.consequence: KAM visits within 7 days, confirms intent and capacity; if positive, offer 60d (not 90d) credit with 40–50% advance on first shipment and SAR 300K minimum order by end of June; first clean cycle clears Q3-Critical flag for H2 review. Upside: recover SAR 1M+ revenue in peak season on controlled terms. Downside: KAM time cost if account is unsalvageable.

options.option: Option B — Formal dormancy declaration
options.consequence: Flag account as commercially inactive by mid-June if no reorder intent confirmed; reallocate reserved credit line to active IR_Others accounts; remove from peak-season planning. Upside: capital and attention redirected to accounts with proven 2026 velocity. Downside: permanently cedes the account to a competitor with no recovery attempt on record.

timeline.step: KAM field visit — diagnose root cause of zero 2026 activity (competitor switch / financial freeze / business contraction) and report findings
Re-engagement decision gate — if account confirms intent to reorder, issue conditional credit offer (net-60, 40–50% advance, SAR 300K minimum); if no intent, trigger Option B dormancy flag
Q3-Critical status review — if first shipment billed and payment received clean, recalculate DSO and consider grade upgrade; if no activity, close out peak-season allocation and reallocate

finance.headline: Dormant account with zero 2026 activity and 2025 net returns — no live AR exposure, but Q3 Critical history demands a hard hold on any new credit extension.
finance.analysis: On the surface, AR looks clean: zero balance, zero overdue, all aging buckets empty. But this reflects account dormancy, not financial health. There has been zero sell-in in 2026, and 2025 closed at SAR -15,334 — a net negative, meaning the year ended with returns or unresolved credit notes rather than revenue. That is a structural red flag: the commercial relationship either deteriorated or was deliberately wound down, and the negative close leaves open the question of whether all claims have been properly settled. The Q3 Critical quadrant assignment — requiring DSO to exceed DPO of 180d AND elevated OVD — confirms this account had severe payment stress when it was active, with a 35.4% YTD average OVD baseline as the underlying chronic pattern.
finance.analysis_2: Collection risk in the immediate term is negligible precisely because there is nothing to collect. However, zero AR on a dormant account is not equivalent to a healthy account with zero AR — the distinction matters for policy. Grade C classification, Q3 Critical quadrant, and a 2025 net-return close together describe an account that absorbed product, delayed payment beyond DPO, and ultimately sent goods back. The pipeline into peak season (May–September) creates sales-side pressure to re-engage IR_Others channel accounts, and that commercial urgency is the primary risk vector here: a rushed shipment to fill peak-season volume targets would recreate Q3 Critical conditions within 90–180 days.
finance.analysis_3: Credit policy should be maintained at its current de facto restricted state. Decreasing the limit is technically moot since exposure is already zero, but the policy signal must be explicit and documented: no new open-credit orders approved without joint finance and sales leadership sign-off. Any re-engagement requires first resolving the 2025 negative balance (confirm returns fully credited, no open claims), then establishing a cash-in-advance or short prepayment structure before any credit terms are reinstated. The 90d credit term on file is academic until that rehabilitation process is completed.
finance.credit_rationale: Zero current exposure makes a decrease moot, but the Q3 Critical history and 2025 net-return close prohibit any credit expansion — hold at current restricted level until account formally rehabilitated.
action: Audit the 2025 SAR -15,334 figure immediately: confirm whether returns are fully credited, pricing disputes closed, and no residual open claims — do not consider re-engagement until this is clean
Issue internal credit block flag on this account so no shipment order can be processed without explicit finance sign-off, preventing peak-season commercial pressure from bypassing controls

Define written rehabilitation criteria for reactivation (minimum: 1 cash-in-advance order, 30d credit maximum for second order, upgrade from Grade C) and communicate to sales team before peak season kicks in
finance.risk_reason: No live AR eliminates immediate collection exposure, but dormant Q3 Critical account with 2025 net returns is a reactivation trap — credit extension without controls would recreate the same stressed position within one credit cycle.

sales.headline: Account has effectively ceased all purchasing — 2025 net returns wiped prior year revenue and 2026 shows zero activity entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated. The 2024 sell-in of SAR 1.53M collapsed to a net negative SAR -15,334 in 2025, meaning product returns or credit notes exceeded any new purchases — a clear sign the account was unwinding its LG inventory position, not building it. With zero sell-in and zero AR balance in 2026, the account has gone completely dark heading into the May–September peak window. A Grade C IR_Others account that has already missed the pre-season stocking cycle (typically March–April) is almost certainly not going to buy meaningfully for 2026.

The buy/sell dynamics point to structural disengagement rather than a seasonal delay. The -2025 figure is the critical signal: it is not a slow year, it is a net unwind. Root cause hypotheses in order of likelihood: (1) inventory overhang from 2024 that did not sell through, forcing returns; (2) a competitor switch — Samsung, Gree, or Midea gaining share in this sub-channel with better margin or credit terms; (3) financial stress causing the dealer to reduce exposure to any single supplier. The 35.4% YTD OVD baseline on this grade of account corroborates a pattern of financial strain even in periods when they were buying.

From an investment standpoint, there is no current financial exposure (SAR 0 balance) so the risk is already contained. However, committing sell-out support, credit extension, or co-marketing to an account that has net returned product and gone silent in 2026 would be capital misallocation. The opportunity cost — redirecting those resources to active or growing IR accounts within the same channel — is substantially higher. The only marginal value in engaging this account now is a low-cost diagnostic call to confirm whether the disengagement is recoverable in 2027 or permanent.
sales.investment_rationale: Net negative 2025 sell-in and zero 2026 activity entering peak season confirm the account has already exited the LG purchasing relationship; no investment vehicle can generate return on a dormant channel partner at this stage of the season.
action: One diagnostic call (sales rep, not KAM) to confirm whether account is permanently switching suppliers or has a specific grievance addressable in 2027 planning
Do not extend credit, approve promotions, or assign display support until account places a confirmed purchase order
Flag account for 2027 grade review — if zero sell-in persists through year-end, recommend formal delisting from active coverage
sales.channel_reason: Net returns in 2025 plus zero 2026 buy-in at peak season onset = confirmed channel disengagement

synthesis.headline: Account has net-unwound the LG relationship — 2025 ended in returns, 2026 is dark entering peak season; the only urgent action is a credit block to prevent peak-season pressure from forcing a bad reinvestment.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. Zero live AR eliminates collection urgency, but absent a hard internal block the peak-season pipeline will recreate Q3 Critical conditions within one credit cycle.

summary_3lines: 2024 SAR 1.53M sell-in collapsed to a net negative SAR -15,334 in 2025 (returns exceeded purchases), and 2026 is zero — the account has already exited the LG purchasing relationship heading into peak season.

Root cause is structural disengagement, not a seasonal delay: Q3 Critical quadrant, 35.4% chronic OVD baseline, and a net-return close in 2025 together describe an account that over-stocked on LG product, could not service the debt, and unwound its position.

If no action is taken, the default risk is not collection loss today — it is that a rep ships peak-season stock under commercial pressure without finance sign-off, the account re-enters Q3 Critical by Q3 2026, and we are recovering SAR 1M+ overdue in September.

finance.credit_rationale: Grade D dormant account with negative 2025 net sell-in and zero 2026 revenue entering peak season — formal suspension of credit limit prevents inadvertent exposure if account is reactivated without proper review.
action: Formally set credit limit to SAR 0 in the system and flag account status as 'Suspended — Reactivation Review Required' to block accidental order processing on open terms
Dispatch AR/field sales inquiry to establish root cause of SAR –1,987 in 2025: dispute, product rejection, or voluntary exit — document findings to determine if account rehabilitation is viable
If account is to be retained for 2026 peak season, require 100% cash-in-advance on a pilot order (minimum SAR 50K) and a minimum Grade C upgrade before any credit terms are restored
finance.risk_reason: Zero current AR eliminates immediate collection risk, but Grade D classification, negative 2025 sell-in, and full commercial dormancy create high reactivation risk if credit terms are applied without controls.

sales.headline: Account has effectively churned — 2025 net negative (returns), zero 2026 activity entering peak season.
sales.analysis:
Channel health is critically deteriorated. The 2024 sell-in of SAR 603K suggests the account was once a meaningful SME buyer, but 2025 recorded SAR -1,987 — net returns with no new purchases — and 2026 shows zero revenue as of early May, the threshold of peak season. This is not a slow buyer; this is a disengaged account. The return activity in 2025 implies the channel held excess inventory it could not sell through, likely a combination of overhang from 2024 loading and possible competitor displacement at the end-user level.

Root cause hierarchy: (1) Inventory overhang from 2024 pushed the account into return mode — Grade D SME buyers typically lack the financial buffer to absorb unsold stock and will return rather than carry. (2) Competitor switch is a strong secondary hypothesis — Samsung, Gree, and Midea are all active in SME/project channels with aggressive pricing; without sell-out data, we cannot confirm, but zero re-order in 6+ months strongly implies sourcing has moved. (3) Business contraction or project pipeline dry-up is a tertiary possibility given the SME nature of the account.

Investment case is weak. The only positive data point is a clean AR (SAR 0 balance, zero overdue) — but this is a consequence of inactivity, not discipline. The 35.4% YTD OVD baseline suggests historical payment stress when the account was active. Investing credit or promotional support into an account that returned product and has gone silent for 6+ months, entering peak season, carries high risk of zero utilization or repeat return cycle. Relationship recovery (a direct visit to diagnose departure) is the only justifiable action before any resource commitment.
sales.investment_rationale: Account has churned with net returns in 2025 and zero 2026 activity — no investment vehicle makes sense until root cause of disengagement is confirmed via direct contact.
action: Sales visit / call within 1 week: diagnose whether account switched supplier, faced business contraction, or is open to re-engagement — do not offer terms before understanding the departure reason
If competitor switch confirmed: assess whether a targeted peak-season promotional offer (discounted split unit bundle, short 30d credit) can recover a trial order before June heat peak
If no credible re-engagement signal within 2 weeks: deprioritize account for 2026 peak allocation; redirect SME channel focus to active Grade B/C accounts with positive buy momentum
sales.channel_reason: Net return in 2025, zero purchase in 2026, silent entering peak season — effective churn with no recovery signal yet.

synthesis.headline: SAR 0 AR is a tombstone, not a clean bill of health — this account churned in 2025 and is silent entering peak season; the only open question is whether a 1-week rescue visit changes the math.

verdict.one_line: Risk Yellow — Exit. Zero AR reflects commercial death, not creditworthiness; no investment is justified until a sales visit confirms re-engagement is real.

summary_3lines: A once-meaningful SAR 603K SME buyer returned product in 2025 (net SAR –1,987) and has placed zero orders in 2026 entering the peak May–September window — effective churn.

Root cause is most likely 2024 inventory overhang that forced returns, compounded by probable competitor displacement (Samsung/Gree/Midea); Grade D profile with 35.4% portfolio OVD baseline confirms this account historically underperformed on payment when active.

If no action is taken this week, the peak season window closes without resolution — the account will either self-exit permanently, or worse, be reactivated informally on 90-day credit terms with no controls, recreating the exact exposure profile that earned it a Grade D in the first place.
synthesis.decision_required: Authorize a mandatory sales diagnostic visit within 5 business days — the outcome of that visit is the gating condition for every subsequent resource decision on this account for 2026 peak.

options.option: Option A — Rescue Attempt: Conduct sales visit, diagnose departure, offer cash-in-advance pilot (SAR 50K minimum) with 30-day terms if competitor switch is reversible
options.consequence: If credible re-engagement signal exists, you recover a historically SAR 600K+ SME account before June heat peak at controlled risk (cash-first); if no signal, you formally close the loop with documented evidence and move on cleanly.

options.option: Option B — Formal Exit: Suspend credit limit to SAR 0 in-system today, deprioritize for 2026 peak allocation, redirect SME channel capacity to active Grade B/C accounts
options.consequence: Eliminates any reactivation exposure immediately and frees field sales bandwidth for accounts with positive buy momentum — but forfeits any chance of recovering a historically significant SME buyer before the highest-volume quarter ends.

timeline.step: Finance formally sets credit limit to SAR 0 in-system and flags 'Suspended — Reactivation Review Required' to block accidental order processing on open terms
Sales visit or direct call to diagnose: did account switch supplier, face business contraction, or is re-engagement possible — document outcome, do not offer terms before root cause is confirmed
Decision gate: if credible re-engagement signal → approve cash-in-advance pilot order only; if no signal → formally deprioritize and reallocate peak season SME focus to active accounts
